```
    {
      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/",
      "title": "ocrmypdf · PyPI",
      "publish_date": "2026-06-17",
      "excerpts": [
        "* **License Expression:** MPL-2.0  \n  _[SPDX](https://spdx.org/licenses/) [License Expression](https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/annexes/spdx-license-expressions/)_\n* **Author:** James R. Barlow\n* Tags OCR , PDF , PDF/A , optical character recognition , scanning\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.11\n* **Provides-Extra:** `watcher` , `webservice`\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\nOCRmyPDF\n\n[Build Status](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/actions/workflows/build.yml) [PyPI version](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/) Homebrew version ReadTheDocs Python versions\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted.\n\n```\nocrmypdf # it's a scriptable command line program \n -l eng+fra # it supports multiple languages \n --rotate-pages # it can fix pages that are misrotated\n\n...\n\nocrmypdf -l eng+fra Bilingual-English-French.pdf Bilingual-English-French.pdf\n\n # Deskew (straighten crooked pages) \nocrmypdf --deskew input.pdf output.pdf\n```\n\nFor more features, see the [documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) .\n\n##  Requirements\n\nIn addition to the required Python version, OCRmyPDF requires external program installations of Ghostscript and Tesseract OCR. OCRmyPDF is pure Python, and runs on pretty much everything: Linux, macOS, Windows and FreeBSD.\n\n##  Plugins\n\nOCRmyPDF provides a plugin interface allowing its capabilities to be extended or replaced. Here are some plugins we are aware of:\n\n* [OCRmyPDF-AppleOCR](https://github.com/mkyt/ocrmypdf-AppleOCR) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with Apple Vision Framework. Requires macOS.\n* [OCRmyPDF-EasyOCR](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF-EasyOCR) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with EasyOCR, a newer OCR engine based on PyTorch. GPU strongly recommended.\n* [OCRmyPDF-PaddleOCR](https://github.com/clefru/ocrmypdf-paddleocr) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with PaddleOCR, a powerful GPU accelerated OCR engine.\n\n[paperless-ngx](https://docs.paperless-ngx.com/) provides integration of OCRmyPDF into a searchable document management system.\n\n##  Press & Media\n\n* [Going paperless with OCRmyPDF](https://medium.com/@ikirichenko/going-paperless-with-ocrmypdf-e2f36143f46a)\n* [Converting a scanned document into a compressed searchable PDF with redactions](https://medium.com/@treyharris/converting-a-scanned-document-into-a-compressed-searchable-pdf-with-redactions-63f61c34fe4c)\n* [c't 1-2014, page 59](https://heise.de/-2279695) : Detailed presentation of OCRmyPDF v1.0 in the leading German IT magazine c't\n* [heise Open Source, 09/2014: Texterkennung mit OCRmyPDF](https://heise.de/-2356670)\n* [heise Durchsuchbare PDF-Dokumente mit OCRmyPDF erstellen](https://www.heise.de/ratgeber/Durchsuchbare-PDF-Dokumente-mit-OCRmyPDF-erstellen-4607592.\nhtml)\n* [Excellent Utilities: OCRmyPDF](https://www.linuxlinks.com/excellent-utilities-ocrmypdf-add-ocr-text-layer-scanned-pdfs/)\n* [LinuxUser Texterkennung mit OCRmyPDF und Scanbd automatisieren](https://www.linux-community.de/ausgaben/linuxuser/2021/06/texterkennung-mit-ocrmypdf-und-scanbd-automatisieren/)\n* [Y Combinator discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=32028752)\n\n##  Business enquiries\n\nOCRmyPDF would not be the software that it is today without companies and users choosing to provide support for feature development and consulting enquiries. We are happy to discuss all enquiries, whether for extending the existing feature set, or integrating OCRmyPDF into a larger system.\n\n##  License\n\nThe OCRmyPDF software is licensed under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL-2.0). This license permits integration of OCRmyPDF with other code, included commercial and closed source, but asks you to publish source-level modifications you make to OCRmyPDF.\nSome components of OCRmyPDF have other licenses, as indicated by standard SPDX license identifiers or the DEP5 copyright and licensing information file. Generally speaking, non-core code is licensed under MIT, and the documentation and test files are licensed under Creative Commons ShareAlike 4.0 (CC-BY-SA 4.0).\n\n##  Disclaimer\n\nThe software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n\n## Project details\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Changelog](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/tree/main/docs/releasenotes)\n* [Source](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n* [Tracker](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/issues)\n\n###### GitHub Statistics\n\n* [**Repository**](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n* **Stars:**\n* **Forks:**\n* **Open issues:**\n* **Open PRs:**\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for jrbarlow from gravatar.\ncom jrbarlow\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/)\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License Expression:** MPL-2.0  \n  _[SPDX](https://spdx.org/licenses/) [License Expression](https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/annexes/spdx-license-expressions/)_\n* **Author:** James R. Barlow\n* Tags OCR , PDF , PDF/A , optical character recognition , scanning\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.11\n* **Provides-Extra:** `watcher` , `webservice`\n\n## Download files\n\nDownload the file for your platform. If you're not sure which to choose, learn more about [installing packages](https://packaging.python.org/tutorials/installing-packages/ \"External link\") .\n\n### Source Distribution\n\n[ocrmypdf-17.7.0.tar.gz](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/0e/f4/7d8fcd1f255ce098ecb8daf40ae20eb3fd940ac0f40c5bc22f10711352f6/ocrmypdf-17.7.0.tar.gz) (7.4 MB view details )\n\nUploaded Jun 18, 2026 `Source`\n\n### Built Distribution"
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced.html",
      "title": "Advanced features — ocrmypdf 17.6.0 documentation",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "<!DOCTYPE html> \n\n* [](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html)\n* Advanced features\n* [ View page source](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/%5Fsources/advanced.md.txt)\n\n---\n\n# Advanced features[](#advanced-features \"Link to this heading\")\n\n## Control of unpaper[](#control-of-unpaper \"Link to this heading\")\n\nOCRmyPDF uses `unpaper` to provide the implementation of the `--clean` and `--clean-final` arguments. [unpaper](https://github.com/Flameeyes/unpaper/blob/main/doc/basic-concepts.md)provides a variety of image processing filters to improve images.\n\nBy default, OCRmyPDF uses only `unpaper` arguments that were found to be safe to use on almost all files without having to inspect every page of the file afterwards. This is particularly true when only `--clean`is used, since that instructs OCRmyPDF to only clean the image before OCR and not the final image.\n\nHowever, if you wish to use the more aggressive options in `unpaper`, you may use `--unpaper-args '...\n\n...\n\nthen run ocrmypdf as follows (along with any other desired arguments):\n\nocrmypdf --tesseract-config no-dict.cfg input.pdf output.pdf\n\nWarning\n\nSome combinations of control parameters will break Tesseract or break assumptions that OCRmyPDF makes about Tesseract’s output.\n\n### Changing page segmentation mode[](#changing-page-segmentation-mode \"Link to this heading\")\n\nThe directive `--tesseract-pagesegmode Nmode` forwards the desired page segmentation mode to Tesseract OCR. The default is 3.\n\nPage segmentation can improve OCR results when you know that a PDF ought to be analyzed a particular way, such as PDFs whose pages contain only a single line of text. For the vast majority of users, changing the page segmentation mode will only make things worse.\n\nAs of June 2024, the Tesseract page segmentation modes are:\n\n| ID | Description                                                                                   |\n\n...\n\n|\n| 9  | Treat the image as a single word in a circle.                                                 |\n| 10 | Treat the image as a single character.                                                        |\n| 11 | Sparse text. Find as much text as possible in no particular order.                            |\n| 12 | Sparse text with OSD.                                                                         |\n| 13 | Raw line. Treat the image as a single text line, bypassing hacks that are Tesseract-specific. |\n\nModes 0, 1, 2, and 12 (all of those that enable orientation and script detection) are not compatible with OCRmyPDF, which performs OSD in a separate step from OCR. Their use may interfere with `--rotate-pages` and other features.\n\nIt is currently not possible to use advanced Tesseract OCR features, such as creating OCR information, when using Tesseract through OCRmyPDF.\n\n...\n\nSuppose that you have a document that was prepared for professional printing in a Separation or CMYK color space, and text was converted to curves. In this case, you may want to use a different color conversion strategy. The `--color-conversion-strategy` option allows you to select a different strategy, such as `RGB`.\n\n## Advanced Ghostscript tuning[](#advanced-ghostscript-tuning \"Link to this heading\")\n\nAdded in version 17.5.0.\n\nOCRmyPDF intentionally hides most Ghostscript controls because Ghostscript is a legacy code path. The preferred PDF/A pipeline in v17+ uses pypdfium2 as the rasterizer and verapdf to validate speculative PDF/A output, with Ghostscript reserved as a fallback for PDFs that cannot be made compliant without it. OCRmyPDF’s separate optimizer (controlled by `--optimize`, `--jpeg-quality`, `--png-quality`, etc.\n) is the supported way to shrink output PDFs: it gives consistent results across input files, and isolates Ghostscript so it can focus on producing a PDF/A with as few image transformations as possible.\n\nThe two options below are exposed for advanced users who want to tune Ghostscript’s intermediate PDF/A output directly. Most users will get more predictable results from the optimizer.\n\n### `--ghostscript-jpeg-quality Q`[](#ghostscript-jpeg-quality-q \"Link to this heading\")\n\nSets Ghostscript’s `-dJPEGQ` switch for images that Ghostscript chooses to recompress to JPEG while building a PDF/A. `Q=0` requests maximum compression and `Q=100` requests best quality; if the flag is omitted, OCRmyPDF passes `95` (the historical default). This only affects images Ghostscript transcodes — existing JPEGs pass through unchanged on modern Ghostscript releases.\nFor end-to-end JPEG quality tuning, prefer `--jpeg-quality`, which is implemented by the OCRmyPDF optimizer and is applied independently of whatever Ghostscript decides to do.\n\nNote: setting both `--ghostscript-jpeg-quality` and `--jpeg-quality` can result in double JPEG recompression, since the optimizer may re-encode images that Ghostscript already recompressed. This can degrade quality in subtle ways.\n\n### `--ghostscript-jpeg-maxdpi DPI`[](#ghostscript-jpeg-maxdpi-dpi \"Link to this heading\")\n\nEnables Ghostscript’s image downsampling and caps color, grayscale, and monochrome image resolution to `DPI`. The downsample threshold is set to `1.0`, so any image whose effective DPI exceeds the cap will be downsampled.\n\nReducing JPEG quality is almost always a better trade than downsampling at the same compression budget: a 400 DPI JPEG at modest quality usually looks much better than a 200 DPI JPEG, because the JPEG codec can spend bits where they count."
      ]
    },
```

{
  "search_id": "search_3d716d12b4df4ceeb1e3e8e15ba75d82",

    {
      "url": "https://github.com/LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr",
      "title": "GitHub - LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr: A free tool to OCR a PDF and ...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nLeoFCardoso / **pdf2pdfocr** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 35\n* Star 303\n\n# LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr\n\nmaster\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* Apache-2.0 license\n\n# pdf2pdfocr\n\nA tool to OCR a PDF (or supported images) and add a text \"layer\" (a \"pdf sandwich\") in the original file making it a searchable PDF.\nThe script uses only open source tools.\n\n# donations\n\nThis software is free, but if you like it, please donate to support new features.\n\n[paypal](https://www.paypal.com/cgi-bin/webscr?cmd=_donations&business=PZZU5APJGSWVA&lc=GB&item_name=pdf2pdfocr%20development&currency_code=USD)\nBitcoin (BTC) address: [bc1qqd4pj0pymptwg523ems8pxgjwdslh7ujv5egqg](https://blockchair.com/bitcoin/address/bc1qqd4pj0pymptwg523ems8pxgjwdslh7ujv5egqg)\n\n# tips\n\nTips are also welcome!\n\nDogecoin (DOGE) address: [D94hD2qPnkxmZk8qa1b6F1d7NfUrPkmcrG](https://blockchair.com/dogecoin/address/D94hD2qPnkxmZk8qa1b6F1d7NfUrPkmcrG)\n\nPIX (Brazilian Instant Payments): d3222ec1-74e6-40e0-a2d6-8f2d8e9f1568\n\nchave PIX\n\nPlease contact for donations and tips in other cryptocurrencies.\n\n# installation\n\nIn Linux, please try these instructions (thanks to @nicoursi).\n\n```\n# 1. Create the virtual environment\npython3 -m venv ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv\n# 2. Activate the virtual environment\nsource ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/activate\n# 3. Install Python dependencies\npip install -r requirements.txt\n# 4. Copy Python files to the virtual environment bin directory\ncp *.py ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/\n# 5. Copy executables to /usr/local/bin\ncp pdf2pdfocr pdf2pdfocr_gui /usr/local/bin\n```\n\nIn macOS, you will need macports.\n\n```\n# First install Xcode from Mac App Store, then:\nxcode-select --install\nsudo xcodebuild -license\n# Install Macports from https://www.macports.org/install.php\nsudo port selfupdate\n# Install tesseract as main ocr engine (Portuguese included below - please add your preferred languages)\nsudo port install git tesseract tesseract-por tesseract-osd tesseract-eng\n# Install qpdf\nsudo port install qpdf\n# Install python 3 and other dependencies\nsudo port install python314 py314-pip poppler poppler-data ImageMagick7 ghostscript\n# Configure default python3 installer\nsudo port select --set python python314\nsudo port select --set python3 python314\nsudo port select --set pip pip314\nsudo port select --set pip3 pip314\n# Configure venv and python deps in fixed home directory\npython3 -m venv ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv\n~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip\n~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/pip3 install --upgrade setuptools\n~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/pip3 install -r requirements.txt\n\n...\n\nIn some cases, you will want to deal with option flags. Please use:\n\n```\npdf2pdfocr.py --help\n```\n\nto view all the options.\n\nIt's also possible to use GUI.\n\n```\npdf2pdfocr_gui.py <<optional input file>>\n```\n\n# fun\n\nCaseiro com orgulho! ;-)\n\n## About\n\nA free tool to OCR a PDF and add a text \"layer\" in the original file, making a searchable PDF. Use only open source tools.       "title": "GUIs and Other Projects using Tesseract OCR | tessdoc",

      "excerpts": [
        "# GUIs and Other Projects using Tesseract OCR\n\n## Tesseract documentation\n\n[View on GitHub](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdoc)\n\n# GUIs and Other Projects using Tesseract OCR\n\n## 1\\. GUIs\n\n|**Name** |**Linux** |**Mac** |**Windows** |**License** |**Description** |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|[Scribe OCR](https://github.com/scribeocr/scribeocr) |X |X |X |AGPL-3.0 license |Scribe OCR is a free and open-source web application for recognizing text, proofreading OCR data, and creating fully-digitized documents. Live site at [scribeocr.com](https://scribeocr.com/) . |\n|[Rescribe](https://rescribe.xyz/rescribe) |X |X |X |GPL v3 |Easy to use Tesseract frontend with no install needed, including PDF processing, and Google Book downloading, and extra pre- and post-processing capabilities. |\n|[normcap](https://github.com/dynobo/normcap) |X |X |X |GPL v3 |OCR powered screen-capture tool to capture information instead of images. |\n|[gImageReader](https://github.\ncom/manisandro/gImageReader) |X | |X |GPL v3 |A graphical GTK frontend to tesseract-ocr |\n|[VietOCR](https://vietocr.sourceforge.net/) |X |X |X |Apache 2.0 |A GUI frontend for Tesseract OCR engine. Supports optical character recognition for Vietnamese and other languages supported by Tesseract |\n|[NeOCR](https://sourceforge.net/projects/ne-ocr/) | | |X |Freeware |A GUI frontend for Tesseract 4.0 OCR engine. |\n|[Free-Ocr-Windows-Desktop](https://github.com/A9T9/Free-Ocr-Windows-Desktop/releases) | | |X |[GNU AGPL v3](https://github.com/A9T9/Free-Ocr-Windows-Desktop/blob/master/LICENSE) |Free OCR application for the Windows Desktop - Essentially a graphical user interface (GUI) for the Tesseract OCR engine. The application also includes support for reading and scanned PDF files |\n|[YAGF](https://sourceforge.net/projects/yagf-ocr/) |X | | |GPL v3 |A graphical front-end for cuneiform and tesseract |\n|[OCR2Text](https://github.\n\n...\n\nnet) |X | | |GPL v2 |Tesseract-GUI is not a front-end for tesseract-ocr, it is just a graphical way to use it with simple image manipulation through ImageMagick |\n|[QTesseract](https://code.google.com/p/qtesseract/) |X | | |LGPL v3 |QT GUI for the Tesseract OCR |\n|[dpScreenOCR](https://danpla.github.io/dpscreenocr/) |X | |X |zlib |Program to recognize text on screen |\n|[pmOCR](https://github.com/deajan/pmocr/) |X | | |BSD |Batch OCR tool, also file monitor event OCR with tesseract |\n|[tesseract4java](https://github.com/tesseract4java/tesseract4java) |X |X |X |GPLv3 |A cross-platform GUI for training and running Tesseract with advanced features like batch recognition and accuracy evaluation |\n|[Linux-Intelligent-OCR-Solution(lios)](https://github.com/Nalin-x-Linux/lios-3.git) |X | | |GPLv3 |A GUI for scanning, running and training Tesseract with total accessibility for visually impaired and advanced features like Scanner Brightness optimizer, Text-Cleaner, etc |\n\n...\n\ncom/trufanov-nok/scantailor-universal) , [ScanTailor Advanced](https://github.com/4lex4/scantailor-advanced) and [ScanTailor-Experimental](https://github.com/ImageProcessing-ElectronicPublications/scantailor-experimental)\n* [ClearText](https://github.com/ajinkya933/ClearText) \\- An AI-powered text detection and enhancement tool that helps make text in images more readable and clearer. Perfect for improving the legibility of text in scanned documents, photos, and other images.\n\n### A. PDF to Searchable PDF tools\n\n(ie: any tool which can also handle a non-searchable PDF as an input):\n\n1. [OCRmyPDF](https://github.com/jbarlow83/OCRmyPDF) \\- Adds OCR text layer to scanned PDF files and images, allowing them to be searched. Processes pages in parallel on multi-core CPUs. Keeps exact resolution of original embedded images without recompressing JPEGs, when possible. Includes image several preprocessing options, detailed documentation, and support for many exotic PDFs.\n2.\n[pdf2pdfocr](https://github.com/LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr) is a tool to OCR a PDF (or supported images) and add a text layer in the original file making it a searchable PDF. It is a python script that uses tesseract and other open source tools. Linux, macOS and Windows supported.\n3. [pdf2searchablepdf](https://github.com/ElectricRCAircraftGuy/PDF2SearchablePDF) \\- a tool which allows converting any non-searchable PDF, OR any entire directory of images, to a searchable PDF\n\n### B. Others:\n\n1. [Tessract\\_path\\_finder](https://github.com/MrSpideyNihal/Tessract_path_finder) \\- A comprehensive, cross-platform Python utility that automatically discovers Tesseract OCR tessdata directories.\n2. [Docling](https://ds4sd.github.io/docling/) \\- Docling parses documents and exports them to the desired format with ease and speed.\n3. [Scantools](https://gitlab.com/kebekus/scantools) \\- A library and a matching set of command line applications for\ngraphics manipulation, written with a view toward handling scanned documents and generating high-quality PDF files. **image2pdf** converts images to PDF. HOCR files, produced by optical character recognition programs such as Tesseract, can optionally be\n   specified to make the PDF file searchable. The resulting file complies with the ISO PDF/A standard for long-term archiving of digital documents. **hocr2any** converts HOCR files to text, or renders them as raster graphics or PDF files **ocrPDF** uses the Tesseract OCR engine to detect text and generate a text layer in the PDF file.\n4. [ocr-fileformat](https://github.com/UB-Mannheim/ocr-fileformat) \\- Validate and transform between OCR file formats (hOCR, ALTO, PAGE, FineReader)\n5. [Tess4J](https://github.com/nguyenq/tess4j) \\- A Java JNA wrapper for Tesseract OCR API.\n6. [Traineddata inspector](https://mazoea.com/te/traineddata/) \\- to inspect some of the internals of traineddata files\n7. [TopOCR](https://www.topocr."

**Output:**

```
{
  "search_id": "search_217f8e764fa7487fb11c5f35406821b6",
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools",
      "title": "GitHub - internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools: Fast PDF generation and compression. Deals with millions of pages daily. · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "|README |README | | |\n|README.rst |README.rst | | |\n|pyproject.toml |pyproject.toml | | |\n|requirements.txt |requirements.txt | | |\n|run-all-tools.sh |run-all-tools.sh | | |\n|setup.cfg |setup.cfg | | |\n|setup.py |setup.py | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* AGPL-3.0 license\n* License\n\n# Internet Archive PDF tools\n\n|Authors: |Merlijn Wajer < merlijn@archive.org > |\n| --- | --- |\n|Date: |2021-11-14 18:00 |\n\nThis repository contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[\\*]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[†]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and\nPDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the \"scandata.\nxml\" files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don't\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\n  offerings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)\n* Creation of 1 bit (black and white) PDFs\n\n...\n\nCreating a PDF from a set of images is pretty straightforward:\n\n```\nrecode_pdf --from-imagestack 'sim_english-illustrated-magazine_1884-12_2_15_jp2/*' \\\n    --hocr-file sim_english-illustrated-magazine_1884-12_2_15_hocr.html \\\n    --dpi 400 --bg-downsample 3 \\\n    -m 2 -t 10 --mask-compression jbig2 \\\n    -o /tmp/example.pdf\n[...]\nProcessed 9 pages at 1.16 seconds/page\nCompression ratio: 7.144962\n```\n\nOr, to scan a document, OCR it with Tesseract and save the result as a compressed PDF\n(JPEG2000 compression with OpenJPEG, background downsampled three times), with\ntext layer:\n\n```\nscanimage --resolution 300 --mode Color --format tiff | tee /tmp/scan.tiff | tesseract - - hocr > /tmp/scan.hocr ; recode_pdf -v -J openjpeg --bg-downsample 3 --from-imagestack /tmp/scan.tiff --hocr-file /tmp/scan.hocr -o /tmp/scan.pdf\n[...]\nProcessed 1 pages at 11.40 seconds/page\nCompression ratio: 249.876613\n```\n\n### Examining the results\n`mrcview` (tools/mrcview) is shipped with the package and can be used to turn a\nMRC-compressed PDF into a PDF with each layer on a separate page, this is the\neasiest way to inspect the resulting compression. Run it like so:\n\n> mrcview /tmp/compressed.pdf /tmp/mrc.pdf\n\nThere is also `maskview` , which just renders the masks of a PDF to another PDF.\n\nAlternatively, one could use `pdfimages` to extract the image layers of a\nspecific page and then view them with your favourite image viewer:\n\n```\npageno=0; pdfimages -f $pageno -l $pageno -png path_to_pdf extracted_image_base\nfeh extracted_image_base*.png\n```\n\ntools/pdfimagesmrc can be used to check how the size of the PDF\nis broken down into the foreground, background, masks and text layer.\n\n## License\n\nLicense for all code (minus `internetarchivepdf/pdfrenderer.py` ) is AGPL 3.0.\n\n`internetarchivepdf/pdfrenderer.py` is Apache 2.0, which matches the Tesseract\nlicense for that file.\n\n|[[\\*]]() |[https://en.wikipedia.\norg/wiki/Mixed\\_raster\\_content](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mixed_raster_content) |\n| --- | --- |\n |[[†]]() |<http://kba.cloud/hocr-spec/1.2/> |\n| --- | --- |\n\n## About\n\nFast PDF generation and compression. Deals with millions of pages daily.\n\n[archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/](https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/ \"https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/\")\n\n### Topics\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nAGPL-3.0, Unknown licenses fo"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest",
      "title": "Internet Archive PDF Tools — archive-pdf-tools 1.2.0 documentation",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# Internet Archive PDF Tools ¶\n\nThis project contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[ \\* ]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[ † ]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and PDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the “scandata.xml” files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don’t\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features ¶\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\n  offerings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)\n* Creation of 1 bit (black and white) PDFs\n\n## Dependencies ¶\n\n* Python 3.x\n* See requirements.txt in the source repo\n\nOne-of:\n\n* [Kakadu JPEG2000 binaries](https://kakadusoftware.com/)\n* Pillow\n* Open source OpenJPEG2000 tools (opj\\_compress and opj\\_decompress)\n* [Grok](https://github.com/GrokImageCompression/grok/) (grk\\_compress and grk\\_decompress)\n* [jpegoptim](https://github.\ncom/tjko/jpegoptim) (when using JPEG instead of JPEG2000)\n\nFor JBIG2 compression:\n\n* [jbig2enc](https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc) for JBIG2 compression (and PyMuPDF 1.19.0 or higher)\n\n## Installation ¶\n\nFirst install dependencies. For example, in Ubuntu:\n\n```\nsudo apt install libleptonica - dev libopenjp2 - tools libxml2 - dev libxslt - dev python3 - dev python3 - pip \n git clone https : // github . com / agl / jbig2enc \n cd jbig2enc \n ./ autogen . sh \n ./ configure && make \n sudo make install\n```\n\nBecause archive-pdf-tools is on the [Python Package Index](https://pypi.org/project/archive-pdf-tools/) (PyPI), you can use pip (the\nPython 3 version is often called pip3 ) to install the latest version:\n\n```\n# Latest version \n pip3 install archive - pdf - tools \n\n # Specific version \n pip3 install archive - pdf - tools == 1.4.14\n```\n\nAlternatively, if you want a specific commit or unreleased version, check out\nthe master branch or a [tagged release](https://github.\ncom/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools/tags) and use pip to\ninstall:\n\n```\ngit clone https : // github . com / internetarchive / archive - pdf - tools . git \n cd archive - pdf - tools \n pip3 install .\n```\n\nFinally, if you’ve downloaded a wheel to test a specific commit, you can also\ninstall it using pip :\n\n```\npip3 install --force-reinstall -U --no-deps ./archive_pdf_tools-${version}.whl\n```\n\nTo see if archive-pdf-tools is installed correctly for your user, run:\n\n```\nrecode_pdf -- version\n```\n\n## Not well tested features ¶\n\n* “Recoding” an existing PDF, extracting the images and creating a new PDF with\n  the images from the existing PDF is not well tested. This works OK if every\n  PDF page just has a single image.\n\n## Known issues ¶\n\n* Using `--image-mode 0` and `--image-mode 1` is currently broken, so only\n  MRC or no images is supported.\n* It is not possible to recode/compress a PDF without hOCR files. This will be\n\n...\n\nThere is also `maskview` , which just renders the masks of a PDF to another PDF.\n\nAlternatively, one could use `pdfimages` to extract the image layers of a\nspecific page and then view them with your favourite image viewer:\n\n```\npageno=0; pdfimages -f $pageno -l $pageno -png path_to_pdf extracted_image_base\nfeh extracted_image_base*.png\n```\n\ntools/pdfimagesmrc can be used to check how the size of the PDF\nis broken down into the foreground, background, masks and text layer.\n\n## License ¶\n\nLicense for all code (minus `internetarchive/pdfrende"
      ]
    },

        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# archive-pdf-tools 1.5.7\n\npip install archive-pdf-tools Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: Aug 20, 2025\n\nInternet Archive PDF compression tools\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for merlijn from gravatar.com merlijn\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Homepage](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools)\n* [Download](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools/archive/1.5.7.tar.gz)\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License:** GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPL-3.0)\n* **Author:** Merlijn Boris Wolf Wajer\n* Tags PDF , MRC , hOCR , Internet Archive\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.6\n* **Provides-Extra:** `docs`\n\n###### Classifiers\n\n* **Development Status**\n  \n    + 3 - Alpha\n* **Intended Audience**\n+ Developers\n* **License**\n  \n    + OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3\n* **Programming Language**\n  \n    + Python :: 3\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/archive-pdf-tools/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\nAuthors :\n    Merlijn Wajer < merlijn @ archive . org >\nDate :\n    2021-11-14 18:00\n\nThis repository contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[ \\* ]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[ † ]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and PDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the “scandata.\nxml” files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don’t\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\n  offerings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)\n\n...\n\nmrcview (tools/mrcview) is shipped with the package and can be used to turn a\nMRC-compressed PDF into a PDF with each layer on a separate page, this is the\neasiest way to inspect the resulting compression. Run it like so:\n\n> mrcview /tmp/compressed.pdf /tmp/mrc.pdf\n> \n> \n\nThere is also maskview , which just renders the masks of a PDF to another PDF.\n\nAlternatively, one could use pdfimages to extract the image layers of a\nspecific page and then view them with your favourite image viewer:\n\n```\npageno=0; pdfimages -f $pageno -l $pageno -png path_to_pdf extracted_image_base\nfeh extracted_image_base*.png\n```\n\ntools/pdfimagesmrc can be used to check how the size of the PDF\nis broken down into the foreground, background, masks and text layer.\n\n## License\n\nLicense for all code (minus internetarchivepdf/pdfrenderer.py ) is AGPL 3.0.\n\ninternetarchivepdf/pdfrenderer.py is Apache 2.0, which matches the Tesseract\nlicense for that file.\n\n [\\* ]\n\n[https://en.wikipedia."
      ]

Pdf Optimization Series Part6 Mrc Compression\n\nPDF PDF optimization\n\n# Methods with Losses: Mixed Raster Content (MRC) Compression\n\nJonathan D. Rhyne\n\nJonathan D. Rhyne\n\nUpdated: February 25, 2025\n\nCONTENTS\n\n## Table of contents\n\nMRC pdf compression -- methods with losses\n\n_Previously in our PDF Optimization In-depth Series:_\n\n1. [1\\. Introduction to the Optimization of Existing PDF files: Methods (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/pdf-optimization-series-part1-methods/)\n2. [2\\. Lossless Methods: Optimization of Document Content (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/pdf-optimization-series-part2-lossless-methods/)\n3. [3\\. Lossless Methods: Compression of Streams and Fonts (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/pdf-optimization-series-part3-compression-streams-fonts/)\n4. [4\\.\nMethods with Losses: JPEG 2000 and JBIG2 (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/pdf-optimization-series-part4-jpeg2000-jbig2/)\n5. [5\\. Methods with losses: resizing, color detection, and other techniques (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/pdf-optimization-series-part5-resizing-color-detection/)\n\n* * *\n\n**MRC compression** in PDF documents is highly efficient in file size reduction without visible degradation of document rendering.\n\nMRC, also known as **hyper-compression** , is an image compression benefiting from image segmentation methods. It is particularly useful for images containing text and continuous-tone graphics.\n\n\\*\\*\\*This method reduces the size up to 8 to 10 times compared to JPEG. \\*\\*\\*\n\nIn some cases, it also improves the quality of the [rendering of documents (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/advanced-mrc-compression/) .\n\n###  MRC briefly\nThe method combines many techniques, among which **segmentation** plays a key role.\n\nThe crucial point is to separate certain regions of an image into three separate images, so-called **layers** . Each of these layers is subsequently altered and optimally compressed. The specification of the PDF format allows reconstructing the original document using specific rendering instructions.\n\nLet’s describe the mentioned layers:\n\n* **The binary layer**\n\nThe text and graphic elements are placed in their uniforms, like lines, flat areas, etc. This layer is ideally saved in high resolution, and it is compressed with the JBIG2 algorithm.\n\n* **The background layer**\n\nIt remains from the original image once the content of the previous layer is removed. The resolution of this layer is defined by the user or by using decision algorithms. Then it is saved ideally with the JPEG 2000 compression algorithm.\n\n* **The foreground layer**\n\n...\n\nOur recent blog article about [MRC (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/advanced-mrc-compression/) shows more very interesting examples of file size reduction.\n\nWith the help of our **[PDF Reducer SDK (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/blog/pdf-optimization/)** as a part of **[GdPicture.NET (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/products/hyper-compression/)** , you can directly try this feature.\n\nYou can and find an example of usage [here (opens in a new tab)](http://guides.gdpicture.com/content/Optimize%20PDF%20MRC.html) .\n\n* * *\n\n_We’re reaching the end of our PDF Optimization In-depth Series!_\n\n_We hope you found these posts useful and that they helped you with the never-ending task of PDF compression..._\n\n\\*You can download the original article on which this series was based [here (opens in a new tab)](https://www.gdpicture.com/wp-content/uploads/pdf_optimization_orpalis.pdf) \\*"
      ]
```

Let me update the summary with all the new information from this conversation. Key changes to incorporate:

1. The invertBinaryImage bug in PDFAssembler.java that caused black background in MRC output - FIXED
2. Temp dir restructuring - `temp/{inputName}-{nanotime}/` for intermediate files
3. OCREngine changed from `ocr(BufferedImage)` to `ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)`
4. `--threads N` CLI flag now overrides both segmentation and OCR threads
5. Added stdin redirect to /dev/null, destroyForcibly in InterruptedException catch
6. Similar projects analysis requested and completed
7. Comparison document saved to ./docs/

</details>

## Goal
Maintain a commercially-safe OCR pipeline that produces fully searchable, MRC-compressed PDFs from any input PDF.

## Constraints & Preferences
- All dependencies must be business-friendly (Apache 2.0, BSD, MIT, EPL 2.0 test-only, GPL+CPE for JDK) — no GPL, no AGPL, no copyleft that would force source disclosure.
- All dependencies install locally into project subdirectories; entire stack is self-contained and portable by copying the folder.
- Pure-Java segmentation (no Leptonica native dependency for the core algorithm).
- `./gradlew build` must NOT run tests; tests run separately via `./gradlew test`.
- `temp/` directory in project root added to `.gitignore`.

## Progress
### Done
- Fixed `TestPdfGenerator` compilation: `PDAnnotationTextMarkup` constructor is `protected` in PDFBox 3.0.6, replaced with `PDAnnotationUnderline()`; removed unused imports.
- Added `generateTestPdfs` Gradle task to `build.gradle.kts`.
- Deleted `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py` (PyMuPDF) and purged all PyMuPDF references from `install-deps.sh`, `README.md`, `.gitignore`, `tests/TESTING.md`.
- Verified all runtime dependencies: PDFBox 3.0.6 (Apache 2.0), picocli 4.7.6 (Apache 2.0), commons-logging (Apache 2.0). Test-only: JUnit Jupiter (EPL 2.0), opentest4j (EPL 2.0). Native: Tesseract (Apache 2.0), Leptonica (BSD 2-Clause), jbig2enc (Apache 2.0), tessdata (Apache 2.0), OpenJDK 21 Adoptium (GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception). No AGPL, no GPL (without exception), no proprietary components.
- Cleaned up `NOTICE.md` and `README.md` with accurate dependency list and restructured sections.
- Cleaned up `bootstrap.sh`: removed dead Tess4J/lept4j JNA symlinks and unnecessary `libtesseract-dev` package.
- Expanded language bundles in `bootstrap.sh` from 7 to 20 (added ita, por, rus, ara, hin, kor, nld, swe, pol, tur, heb, vie, tha).
- Added PDF/A-2b output support (`--pdfa` flag, XMP metadata, sRGB OutputIntent, optional font embedding via `pdf.pdfa.fontPath` setting).
- Added `--threads` CLI flag and `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` setting for concurrency control.
- Fixed OCR subprocess deadlock in `OCREngine.ocr()`: replaced blocking `readLine()` stdout loop with a daemon thread drainer, ensuring `process.waitFor(120s)` timeout is always reachable.
- Increased OCR subprocess timeout from 30s to 120s.
- Default `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` set to 1 (sequential OCR).
- `build.gradle.kts`: removed `check` from `build` task dependencies — tests no longer run during `./gradlew build`.
- Updated settings.jsonc, CLI help text, and README for all new settings.
- Fixed `--threads N` CLI flag to override OCR threads too: `ocrMax = threads != null ? segThreads : Math.min(segThreads, settings.getInt(...))`.
- Added `stdin` redirect to `/dev/null` in `ProcessBuilder` (precaution against subprocess deadlock).
- Added `process.destroyForcibly()` in `InterruptedException` catch block to prevent orphaned Tesseract zombies when `Future.cancel(true)` interrupts a worker thread.
- Restructured temp directory: `temp/{inputName}-{nanotime}/` holds grayscale page PNGs and Tesseract TSV output. Cleans up in `finally`.
- Changed `OCREngine.ocr(BufferedImage)` → `OCREngine.ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)`: reads pre-saved grayscale PNG from disk, no in-memory conversion or temp file creation per call.
- **FIXED** `PDFAssembler.java` black-background bug: removed `invertBinaryImage()` call on foreground mask. Original mask (text=black, background=white) maps correctly to CCITT G4 (0=white, 1=black) and ImageMask (1=draw black, 0=transparent). Inversion caused background pixels to draw black over everything.
- Searched GitHub for similar projects and saved comparison analysis to `docs/similar-projects-comparison.md`.

### In Progress
- (none)

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- PDFBox 3.0.6 over iText (AGPL) to keep license business-friendly.
- Tesseract CLI via subprocess over Tess4J/JNA to avoid native-library version mismatches.
- OCR concurrency defaults to 1 (sequential) because Tesseract is I/O- and memory-bound; concurrent instances slow each other even with sufficient RAM (16 GB) and small traineddata (4 MB).
- `--threads N` CLI flag overrides both segmentation and OCR threads; `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` setting only applies when `--threads` is omitted.
- Segmentation uses a separate thread pool from OCR (CPU-bound vs subprocess-bound, different optimal concurrency).
- `process.waitFor(timeout)` used with a daemon thread draining stdout via `InputStream.read()` (not `readLine()`), so the timeout is always reachable even if the subprocess hangs.
- MRC mode uses Lossless PNG background + CCITT G4 foreground mask (no inversion) + invisible text. Non-MRC uses JPEG background + invisible text only.
- Pages saved as grayscale PNGs to `temp/` immediately after extraction — OCR threads read from disk instead of sharing mutable `BufferedImage` objects.
- Adoptium API URL set to `eclipse` (Temurin) distribution.
- `./gradlew build` excludes tests; `./gradlew test` runs tests separately.

## Next Steps
- Resolve latent inconsistency in `SettingsTest.java`: test expects `jbig2enc.flags` key with default `"-p -s"`, but the code ignores this key — the test only passes because the key exists in `settings.jsonc`.

## Critical Context
- `PDFAssemblerTest` and `PipelineIntegrationTest` hang/time out due to slow OCR in full pipeline integration tests (pre-existing, not introduced by recent changes).
- `OCREngine.ocr()` blocking `readLine()` loop was the root cause of the perceived concurrent deadlock — the 30s `waitFor` timeout was never reached because the main thread was stuck draining stdout.
- Concurrent Tesseract processes from the shell work correctly (verified: 2, 3, 4 parallel instances all succeed). The hang was caused entirely by Java's blocking I/O on stdout, not by Tesseract itself.
- **MRC black-background bug** was a CCITT/ImageMask inversion: `invertBinaryImage()` swapped text and background pixel roles. Original mask (text=black=CCITT 1, background=white=CCITT 0) maps correctly without inversion — CCITT 1 → ImageMask 1 → draws black (text), CCITT 0 → transparent (background).
- On the test system: 16 GB RAM, Intel Core i7-5600U (2 cores / 4 threads), `eng.traineddata` is 4.0 MB. Sequential OCR takes ~10-12 seconds per page under low system load; under high load (opencode, VS Code, Firefox, Gradle daemon) it degrades to 26-106s per page.
- System load of 8+ on 2-core CPU causes Tesseract slowdowns that can exceed the 120s timeout.
- `OCREngine` default tesseract path: `native/linux/tesseract`; `Settings.java` default: `./native/linux/tesseract` — both resolve to the same path functionally.

## Relevant Files
- `build.gradle.kts`: Java 21, PDFBox 3.0.6, picocli 4.7.6, Shadow plugin; `build` task excludes tests.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TrulyFreeOCR.java`: CLI entry point with `--threads`, `--pdfa`, `--no-mrc` flags; creates `temp/{inputName}-{nanotime}/`, saves grayscale page PNGs, concurrent pipeline with separate thread pools for segmentation and OCR.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/OCREngine.java`: Subprocess-based OCR `ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)` with daemon thread stdout drainer, stdin redirect, 120s timeout, `destroyForcibly()` on both timeout and interrupt.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssembler.java`: MRC assembly — Lossless PNG background + CCITT G4 foreground mask (no inversion, uses original binary mask directly) + invisible text layer.
- `bootstrap.sh`: Downloads 20 language bundles via loop; installs system Tesseract/Leptonica; builds jbig2enc from source.
- `settings.jsonc`: Key settings — `pipeline.mrc.enabled`, `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` (default 1), `pdf.pdfa.enabled` (default false), `pdf.pdfa.fontPath`.
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TestPdfGenerator.java`: Java replacement for PyMuPDF PDF generator.
- `docs/similar-projects-comparison.md`: Comparison of OCRmyPDF, pdf2pdfocr, Internet Archive archive-pdf-tools, and TrulyFreeOCR.

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
```

```
3: import java.io.IOException;

6: import java.nio.file.Paths;

8: import java.util.Map;
9: 
10: public class Settings {
11: 
12:     private static Settings instance;
13: 
14:     private final Map<String, String> values;
15: 
16:     private static final Path DEFAULT_PATH = Paths.get("settings.jsonc");
17: 
18:     // System property key for overriding the settings file location.
19:     private static final String PROP_KEY = "tfocr.settings";
20: 
21:     public Settings() {
22:         this.values = new HashMap<>();
23:     }
24: 
25:     public Settings(Map<String, String> values) {
26:         this.values = new HashMap<>(values);
27:     }
28: 
29:     /**
30:      * Loads settings from the default location (settings.jsonc in CWD),
31:      * or from the path specified by the "tfocr.settings" system property.
32:      * Does nothing if the file does not exist — all getters fall back to
33:      * their supplied defaults.
34:      */
35:     public static Settings load() {
36:         Settings s = new Settings();
37:         Path path = findPath();
38:         if (path != null && Files.exists(path)) {
39:             try {
40:                 String raw = Files.readString(path);
41:                 s.values.putAll(parseJsonc(raw));
42:             } catch (IOException e) {
43:                 // file missing or unreadable — use defaults
44:             }
45:         }
46:         instance = s;
47:         return s;
48:     }
49: 
50:     /**
51:      * Returns the singleton instance.  Must be preceded by a call to load().
52:      */
53:     public static Settings getInstance() {
54:         if (instance == null) {
55:             instance = new Settings();
56:         }
57:         return instance;
58:     }
59: 
60:     // ---- typed accessors with defaults ----
61: 
62:     public String getString(String key, String defaultValue) {
63:         return values.getOrDefault(key, defaultValue);
64:     }
65: 
66:     public int getInt(String key, int defaultValue) {
67:         String v = values.get(key);
68:         if (v == null) return defaultValue;
69:         try { return Integer.parseInt(v); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { return defaultValue; }
70:     }
71: 
72:     public double getDouble(String key, double defaultValue) {
73:         String v = values.get(key);
74:         if (v == null) return defaultValue;
75:         try { return Double.parseDouble(v); } catch (NumberFormatException e) { return defaultValue; }
76:     }
77: 
78:     public boolean getBoolean(String key, boolean defaultValue) {
79:         String v = values.get(key);
80:         if (v == null) return defaultValue;
81:         return "true".equalsIgnoreCase(v);
82:     }
83: 
84:     // ---- path resolution ----
85: 
86:     private static Path findPath() {
87:         String prop = System.getProperty(PROP_KEY);
88:         if (prop != null && !prop.isEmpty()) {
89:             return Paths.get(prop);
90:         }
91:         return DEFAULT_PATH;
92:     }
93: 
94:     // ---- minimal JSONC parser (flat objects only) ----
95: 
96:     static Map<String, String> parseJsonc(String raw) {
97:         String stripped = stripComments(raw);
98:         return parseJson(stripped);
99:     }
100: 
101:     private static String stripComments(String input) {
102:         StringBuilder out = new StringBuilder(input.length());
103:         int i = 0;
104:         while (i < input.length()) {
105:             if (i + 1 < input.length() && input.charAt(i) == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/') {
106:                 // line comment — skip to end of line
107:                 i += 2;
108:                 while (i < input.length() && input.charAt(i) != '\n') i++;
109:                 continue;
110:             }
111:             if (i + 1 < input.length() && input.charAt(i) == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '*') {
112:                 // block comment — skip to */
113:                 i += 2;
114:                 while (i + 1 < input.length() && !(input.charAt(i) == '*' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/')) i++;
115:                 i += 2; // skip */
116:                 continue;
117:             }
118:             out.append(input.charAt(i));
119:             i++;
120:         }
121:         return out.toString();
122:     }
123: 
124:     private static Map<String, String> parseJson(String input) {
125:         Map<String, String> result = new HashMap<>();
126:         int i = skipWhitespace(input, 0);
127:         if (i >= input.length() || input.charAt(i) != '{') return result;
128:         i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1);
129: 
130:         while (i < input.length()) {
131:             if (input.charAt(i) == '}') break;
132:             if (input.charAt(i) == ',') { i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1); continue; }
133: 
134:             // parse key
135:             if (input.charAt(i) != '"') break;
136:             int keyStart = i + 1;
137:             int keyEnd = findStringEnd(input, keyStart);
138:             if (keyEnd < 0) break;
139:             String key = input.substring(keyStart, keyEnd);
140:             i = skipWhitespace(input, keyEnd + 1);
141: 
142:             if (i >= input.length() || input.charAt(i) != ':') break;
143:             i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1);
144: 
145:             // parse value
146:             if (i >= input.length()) break;
147:             char c = input.charAt(i);
148:             String value;
149:             if (c == '"') {
150:                 int valStart = i + 1;
151:                 int valEnd = findStringEnd(input, valStart);
152:                 if (valEnd < 0) break;
153:                 value = input.substring(valStart, valEnd);
154:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, valEnd + 1);
155:             } else if (c == 't' && input.startsWith("true", i)) {
156:                 value = "true";
157:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 4);
158:             } else if (c == 'f' && input.startsWith("false", i)) {
159:                 value = "false";
160:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 5);
161:             } else if (c == 'n' && input.startsWith("null", i)) {
162:                 value = "null";
163:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 4);
164:             } else {
165:                 // number
166:                 int valEnd = i;
167:                 while (valEnd < input.length() && (Character.isDigit(input.charAt(valEnd))
168:                         || input.charAt(valEnd) == '.' || input.charAt(valEnd) == '-'
169:                         || input.charAt(valEnd) == '+' || input.charAt(valEnd) == 'e'
170:                         || input.charAt(valEnd) == 'E')) {
171:                     valEnd++;
172:                 }
173:                 if (valEnd == i) break;
174:                 value = input.substring(i, valEnd);
175:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, valEnd);
176:             }
177:             result.put(key, value);
178:         }
179:         return result;
180:     }
181: 
182:     private static int skipWhitespace(String s, int pos) {
183:         while (pos < s.length() && Character.isWhitespace(s.charAt(pos))) pos++;
184:         return pos;
185:     }
186: 
187:     private static int findStringEnd(String s, int start) {
188:         int i = start;
189:         while (i < s.length()) {
190:             char c = s.charAt(i);
191:             if (c == '\\') {
192:                 i += 2; // skip escaped char
193:             } else if (c == '"') {
194:                 return i;
195:             } else {
196:                 i++;
197:             }
198:         }
199:         return -1;
200:     }
201: }

(End of file - total 201 lines)
</content>
```

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: 
7: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;

11: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils;
12: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils.Os;
13: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.SubprocessRunner;
14: 
15: /**
16:  * Compresses a binary foreground mask via jbig2enc subprocess,
17:  * falling back to CCITT Group 4 fax encoding via PDFBox when
18:  * the native binary is unavailable.
19:  *
20:  * jbig2enc flags:
21:  *   -p  — Produce a global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).
22:  *   -s  — Refine smearing to improve symbol matching.
23:  */
24: public class JBIG2Compressor {
25: 
26:     private final String nativeDir;
27: 
28:     public JBIG2Compressor() {
29:         this("native");
30:     }
31: 
32:     public JBIG2Compressor(String nativeDir) {
33:         this.nativeDir = nativeDir;
34:     }
35: 
36:     /**
37:      * Holds compressed foreground-mask data.
38:      *
39:      * isJbig2  true = JBIG2 format (from jbig2enc); false = CCITT G4 fallback.
40:      * data     The compressed byte stream.
41:      * width    Original mask width  in pixels.
42:      * height   Original mask height in pixels.
43:      */
44:     public static class CompressionResult {
45:         private final boolean isJbig2;
46:         private final byte[] data;
47:         private final int width;
48:         private final int height;
49: 
50:         public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {
51:             this.data = data;
52:             this.width = width;
53:             this.height = height;
54:             this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;
55:         }
56: 
57:         public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }
58:         public byte[] getData() { return data; }
59:         public int getWidth() { return width; }
60:         public int getHeight() { return height; }
61:     }
62: 
63:     /**
64:      * Attempts JBIG2 compression via jbig2enc; falls back to CCITT G4 on failure.
65:      *
66:      * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, black=foreground).
67:      * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or CCITT G4 data.
68:      */
69:     public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {
70:         File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();
71:         if (jbig2enc != null) {
72:             try {
73:                 return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);
74:             } catch (IOException e) {
75:                 // jbig2enc failed → fall through to fallback
76:             }
77:         }
78:         return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);
79:     }
80: 
81:     /**
82:      * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current OS.
83:      *
84:      * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).
85:      * The base path can be overridden via system property "tfocr.native.dir"
86:      * (defaults to "native").
87:      *
88:      * @return The executable File, or null if not found.
89:      */
90:     private File findJbig2enc() {
91:         Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();
92:         String dirName;
93:         switch (os) {
94:             case WINDOWS: dirName = "win"; break;
95:             case MAC: dirName = "mac"; break;
96:             default: dirName = "linux";
97:         }
98:         File exe = new File(nativeDir + "/" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? "jbig2enc.exe" : "jbig2enc");
99:         if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;
100:         return null;
101:     }
102: 
103:     /**
104:      * Runs jbig2enc and concatenates global sym + page jbig2 into one byte array.
105:      *
106:      * PDF JBIG2Decode streams require global segments (symbol dictionary)
107:      * followed by page data — exactly the .sym + .jbig2 layout that
108:      * jbig2enc produces.
109:      */
110:     private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
111:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-jbig2-", ".png");
112:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
113:         javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, "png", tempPng);
114: 
115:         try {
116:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
117: 
118:             SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,
119:                     "-p", "-s", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
120:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
121:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
122:             }
123: 
124:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
125:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
126: 
127:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
128:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
129: 
130:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
131:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
132:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
133: 
134:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
135:         } finally {
136:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
137:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
138:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
139:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
140:             }
141:         }
142:     }
143: 
144:     /**
145:      * Fallback: encodes the mask as CCITT Group 4 fax using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.
146:      */
147:     private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
148:         // Create a throwaway PDDocument to use CCITTFactory
149:         try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {
150:             PDImageXObject ccitt = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, mask);
151:             return new CompressionResult(extractStreamBytes(ccitt), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);
152:         }
153:     }
154: 
155:     private static byte[] extractStreamBytes(PDImageXObject image) throws IOException {
156:         try (java.io.InputStream is = image.createInputStream()) {
157:             return is.readAllBytes();
158:         }
159:     }
160: }
```

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now I see the issue clearly.

In `JBIG2Compressor.java` at line 118-119, the jbig2enc flags are hardcoded:
```java
SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc,
        "-p", "-s", tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
```

The test (`SettingsTest.java` line 34) expects:
```java
assertEquals("-p -s", s.getString("jbig2enc.flags", "DEFAULT"));
```

This test passes because `settings.jsonc` does contain `"jbig2enc.flags": "-p -s"`. The test is correct - it just validates that the settings file loads properly.

The inconsistency is that `JBIG2Compressor.java` doesn't actually read the `jbig2enc.flags` setting from `Settings.java` - it hardcodes `"-p", "-s"`. So changing the value in `settings.jsonc` would have no effect on the actual behavior.

So the fix is to make `JBIG2Compressor` read the flags from `Settings.getInstance()` instead of hardcoding them.

Let me also check how JBIG2Compressor is instantiated in the pipeline to see where nativeDir comes from.

</details>

Now I see the issue. `JBIG2Compressor.java:118-119` hardcodes `"-p", "-s"` instead of reading the `jbig2enc.flags` setting. The test passes because `settings.jsonc` happens to have the same value, but changing the setting would have no effect. Let me check how the compressor is created in the pipeline.

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.util;
2: 
3: import java.io.BufferedReader;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.io.InputStreamReader;
7: import java.util.ArrayList;
8: import java.util.List;
9: import java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit;
10: 
11: /**
12:  * Utility for running external processes and capturing stdout/stderr.
13:  *
14:  * Two convenience modes:
15:  *   run(executable, args...)      — blocks until completion (no timeout).
16:  *   run(executable, args, ms)     — kills the process after <ms> milliseconds.
17:  *
18:  * Stderr is NOT merged with stdout (redirectErrorStream = false) so
19:  * callers can distinguish normal output from error diagnostics.
20:  */
21: public class SubprocessRunner {
22: 
23:     /**
24:      * Executes a command with no timeout.
25:      *
26:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
27:      * @param args        Variable-length argument list.
28:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
29:      */
30:     public static Result run(File executable, String... args) throws IOException {
31:         return run(executable, List.of(args), -1);
32:     }
33: 
34:     /**
35:      * Executes a command with an optional timeout.
36:      *
37:      * @param executable  The binary to run.
38:      * @param args        Argument list.
39:      * @param timeoutMs   Max wall-clock time in milliseconds (&le;0 = no timeout).
40:      * @return Result containing exit code, stdout, and stderr.
41:      */
42:     public static Result run(File executable, List<String> args, long timeoutMs) throws IOException {
43:         List<String> command = new ArrayList<>();
44:         command.add(executable.getAbsolutePath());
45:         command.addAll(args);
46: 
47:         ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(command);
48:         pb.redirectErrorStream(false);
49: 
50:         Process process = pb.start();
51: 
52:         if (timeoutMs > 0) {
53:             try {
54:                 boolean finished = process.waitFor(timeoutMs, TimeUnit.MILLISECONDS);
55:                 if (!finished) {
56:                     process.destroyForcibly();
57:                     throw new IOException("Process timed out after " + timeoutMs + "ms: " + executable.getName());
58:                 }
59:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
60:                 process.destroyForcibly();
61:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
62:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
63:             }
64:         } else {
65:             try {
66:                 process.waitFor();
67:             } catch (InterruptedException e) {
68:                 process.destroyForcibly();
69:                 Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
70:                 throw new IOException("Process was interrupted", e);
71:             }
72:         }
73: 
74:         byte[] stdout = process.getInputStream().readAllBytes();
75:         byte[] stderr = process.getErrorStream().readAllBytes();
76:         int exitCode = process.exitValue();
77: 
78:         return new Result(exitCode, stdout, stderr);
79:     }
80: 
81:     /**
82:      * Holds the subprocess output.
83:      *
84:      * exitCode  Process exit status (0 = success).
```

```
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: 
7: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
8: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;
9: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
10: 
11: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils;
12: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.PlatformUtils.Os;
13: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.Settings;
14: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.SubprocessRunner;
15: 
16: /**
17:  * Compresses a binary foreground mask via jbig2enc subprocess,
18:  * falling back to CCITT Group 4 fax encoding via PDFBox when
19:  * the native binary is unavailable.
20:  *
21:  * jbig2enc flags:
22:  *   -p  — Produce a global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).
23:  *   -s  — Refine smearing to improve symbol matching.
24:  */
25: public class JBIG2Compressor {
26: 
27:     private final String nativeDir;
28: 
29:     public JBIG2Compressor() {
30:         this("native");
31:     }
32: 
33:     public JBIG2Compressor(String nativeDir) {
34:         this.nativeDir = nativeDir;
35:     }
36: 
37:     /**
38:      * Holds compressed foreground-mask data.
39:      *
40:      * isJbig2  true = JBIG2 format (from jbig2enc); false = CCITT G4 fallback.
41:      * data     The compressed byte stream.
42:      * width    Original mask width  in pixels.
43:      * height   Original mask height in pixels.
44:      */
45:     public static class CompressionResult {
46:         private final boolean isJbig2;
47:         private final byte[] data;
48:         private final int width;
49:         private final int height;
50: 
51:         public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {
52:             this.data = data;
53:             this.width = width;
54:             this.height = height;
55:             this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;
56:         }
57: 
58:         public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }
59:         public byte[] getData() { return data; }
60:         public int getWidth() { return width; }
61:         public int getHeight() { return height; }
62:     }
63: 
64:     /**
65:      * Attempts JBIG2 compression via jbig2enc; falls back to CCITT G4 on failure.
66:      *
67:      * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, black=foreground).
68:      * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or CCITT G4 data.
69:      */
70:     public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {
71:         File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();
72:         if (jbig2enc != null) {
73:             try {
74:                 return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);
75:             } catch (IOException e) {
76:                 // jbig2enc failed → fall through to fallback
77:             }
78:         }
79:         return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);
80:     }
81: 
82:     /**
83:      * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current OS.
84:      *
85:      * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).
86:      * The base path can be overridden via system property "tfocr.native.dir"
87:      * (defaults to "native").
88:      *
89:      * @return The executable File, or null if not found.
90:      */
91:     private File findJbig2enc() {
92:         Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();
93:         String dirName;
94:         switch (os) {
95:             case WINDOWS: dirName = "win"; break;
96:             case MAC: dirName = "mac"; break;
97:             default: dirName = "linux";
98:         }
99:         File exe = new File(nativeDir + "/" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? "jbig2enc.exe" : "jbig2enc");
100:         if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;
101:         return null;
102:     }
103: 
104:     /**
105:      * Runs jbig2enc and concatenates global sym + page jbig2 into one byte array.
106:      *
107:      * PDF JBIG2Decode streams require global segments (symbol dictionary)
108:      * followed by page data — exactly the .sym + .jbig2 layout that
109:      * jbig2enc produces.
110:      */
111:     private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
112:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-jbig2-", ".png");
113:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
114:         javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, "png", tempPng);
115: 
116:         try {
117:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
118: 
119:             String flags = Settings.getInstance().getString("jbig2enc.flags", "-p -s");
120:             java.util.List<String> args = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
121:             for (String f : flags.split("\\s+")) {
122:                 if (!f.isEmpty()) args.add(f);
123:             }
124:             args.add(tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
125: 
126:             SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc, args, -1);
127:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
128:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
129:             }
130: 
131:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
132:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
133: 
134:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
135:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
136: 
137:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
138:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
139:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
140: 
141:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
142:         } finally {
143:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
144:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
145:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
146:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
147:             }
148:         }
149:     }
150: 
151:     /**
152:      * Fallback: encodes the mask as CCITT Group 4 fax using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.
153:      */
154:     private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
155:         // Create a throwaway PDDocument to use CCITTFactory
156:         try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {
157:             PDImageXObject ccitt = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, mask);
158:             return new CompressionResult(extractStreamBytes(ccitt), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     private static byte[] extractStreamBytes(PDImageXObject image) throws IOException {
163:         try (java.io.InputStream is = image.createInputStream()) {
164:             return is.readAllBytes();
165:         }
166:     }
167: }

(End of file - total 167 lines)
</content>
```

```
23: /**

25:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler).

29:  * (US Letter = 612×792 pt).
30:  */
31: class PDFAssemblerTest {
32: 
33:     static PageExtractor extractor;
34:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
35:     static OCREngine engine;
36:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
37: 
38:     @BeforeAll
39:     static void setup() {
40:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
41:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
42:         engine = new OCREngine();
43:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
44:     }
45: 
46:     @Test
47:     void assemble_simpleText_createsSearchablePdf() throws IOException {
48:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
49:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
50:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
51: 
52:         // Segment and OCR each page
53:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()
54:                 .map(segmenter::segment)
55:                 .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
56:                 .toList();
57:         var ocrResults = pages.stream()
58:                 .map(p -> {
59:                     try { return engine.ocr(p); }
60:                     catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
61:                 })
62:                 .toList();
63: 
64:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, null, ocrResults, false)) {
65:             assertNotNull(output);
66:             assertEquals(1, output.getNumberOfPages());
67: 
68:             // Verify text is extractable (searchable)
69:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
70:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
71:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
72:                     "Output PDF should contain 'quick' but got: " + text);
73:         }
74:     }
75: 
76:     @Test
77:     void assemble_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {

81: 
82:         var backgrounds = pages.stream()

171: 
172:             // Verify page resources include a CCITT-encoded image (stencil mask)
173:             PDPage page = output.getPage(0);

178:                     var xObj = resources.getXObject(name);

180:                         if (img.getCOSObject().getBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, false)) {
181:                             hasStencil = true;
182:                             break;
183:                         }
184:                     }
185:                 } catch (IOException e) {
186:                     // skip
187:                 }
188:             }
189:             assertTrue(hasStencil, "Output PDF should contain an ImageMask stencil");

191:     }
192: }
```
