```
20: /**
21:  * End-to-end integration tests running the full pipeline
22:  * (PageExtractor → ImageSegmenter → OCREngine → PDFAssembler)
23:  * on all sample PDFs and verifying the output.
24:  */
25: class PipelineIntegrationTest {
26: 
27:     static PageExtractor extractor;
28:     static ImageSegmenter segmenter;
29:     static OCREngine engine;
30:     static PDFAssembler assembler;
31: 
32:     @BeforeAll
33:     static void setup() {
34:         extractor = new PageExtractor();
35:         segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();
36:         engine = new OCREngine();
37:         assembler = new PDFAssembler();
38:     }
39: 
40:     @Test
41:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
42:         runPipeline("simple-text.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
43:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
44:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
45:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"),
46:                     "Output should contain 'quick' from the sample text");
47:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("brown"),
48:                     "Output should contain 'brown' from the sample text");
49:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("fox"),
50:                     "Output should contain 'fox' from the sample text");
51:         });
52:     }
53: 
54:     @Test
55:     void fullPipeline_blankPage_outputHasNoSearchableText() throws IOException {
56:         runPipeline("blank.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
57:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
58:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
59:             assertTrue(text.trim().isEmpty(),
60:                     "Blank page should have no searchable text, but got: '" + text + "'");
61:         });
62:     }
63: 
64:     @Test
65:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_preservesPageCount() throws IOException {
66:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
67:             assertEquals(3, output.getNumberOfPages(),
68:                     "Three-page input should produce three-page output");
69:         });
70:     }
71: 
72:     @Test
73:     void fullPipeline_multiPage_eachPageHasText() throws IOException {
74:         runPipeline("multi-page.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
75:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
76:             for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
77:                 stripper.setStartPage(i + 1);
78:                 stripper.setEndPage(i + 1);
79:                 String text = stripper.getText(output);
80:                 assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(),
81:                         "Page " + (i + 1) + " should have extractable text");
82:             }
83:         });
84:     }
85: 
86:     @Test
87:     void fullPipeline_withAnnotations_preservesMetadata() throws IOException {
88:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/with-annotations.pdf");
89:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
90:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
91:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
92:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
93:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
94: 
95:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(input);
96:              PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults, false)) {
97: 
98:             // Verify document info preserved
99:             var srcInfo = source.getDocumentInformation();
100:             var dstInfo = output.getDocumentInformation();
101:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getTitle(), dstInfo.getTitle());
102:             assertEquals(srcInfo.getAuthor(), dstInfo.getAuthor());
103: 
104:             // Verify outline preserved if source has one
105:             var srcOutline = source.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
106:             if (srcOutline != null) {
107:                 var dstOutline = output.getDocumentCatalog().getDocumentOutline();
108:                 assertNotNull(dstOutline);
109:             }
110:         }
111:     }
112: 
113:     @Test
114:     void fullPipeline_twoColumn_outputIsSearchable() throws IOException {
115:         runPipeline("two-column.pdf", (output, sourceFile) -> {
116:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
117:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
118:             assertFalse(text.trim().isEmpty(), "Two-column PDF should have searchable text");
119:             // Should contain content from both columns
120:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("lorem") || text.toLowerCase().contains("ipsum"),
121:                     "Two-column output should contain sample text");
122:         });
123:     }
124: 
125:     @Test
126:     void fullPipeline_simpleText_withForegroundMask() throws IOException {
127:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
128:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
129:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
130:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
131:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
132:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
133: 
134:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults, false)) {
135:             assertNotNull(output);
136:             PDFTextStripper stripper = new PDFTextStripper();
137:             String text = stripper.getText(output);
138:             assertTrue(text.toLowerCase().contains("quick"));
139:         }
140:     }
141: 
142:     @Test
143:     void fullPipeline_outputFileSizeIsReasonable() throws IOException {
144:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf");
145:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
146:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
147:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
148:         var foregroundMasks = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getForegroundMask).toList();
149:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
150: 
151:         File tempOutput = File.createTempFile("tfocr-integration-", ".pdf");
152:         tempOutput.deleteOnExit();
153:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, foregroundMasks, ocrResults, false)) {
154:             output.save(tempOutput);
155:         }
156:         // Output should be reasonably sized (not trivially tiny, not enormous)
157:         long size = tempOutput.length();
158:         assertTrue(size > 1000, "Output PDF should be at least 1KB, was " + size + " bytes");
159:         // At 300 DPI for US Letter, even compressed, expect > 10KB
160:         assertTrue(size < 100_000_000, "Output PDF should be under 100MB, was " + size + " bytes");
161:     }
162: 
163:     // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
164: 
165:     @FunctionalInterface
166:     private interface OutputValidator {
167:         void validate(PDDocument output, File sourceFile) throws IOException;
168:     }
169: 
170:     private void runPipeline(String testPdfName, OutputValidator validator) throws IOException {
171:         File input = new File("tests/test-files/" + testPdfName);
172:         assertTrue(input.exists(), "Test PDF not found: " + input);
173: 
174:         var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
175:         assertNotNull(pages);
176:         assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());
177: 
178:         var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();
179:         var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();
180:         var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);
181: 
182:         try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, null, ocrResults, false)) {
183:             assertNotNull(output);
184:             validator.validate(output, input);
185:         }
186:     }
187: 
188:     private List<PageResult> processOcr(List<BufferedImage> pages) {
189:         return pages.stream().map(p -> {
190:             try { return engine.ocr(p); }
191:             catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
192:         }).toList();
193:     }
194: }
```

```
27: @Tag("eval")
28: class OcrAccuracyTest {
29: 
30:     private static final Path CORPUS_DIR = Path.of("tests/eval-corpus");
31:     private static final double WER_THRESHOLD = 0.05; // flag pages above 5%
32: 
33:     private static PageExtractor extractor = new PageExtractor();
34:     private static OCREngine ocrEngine = new OCREngine();
35: 
36:     @Test void eval_010p() throws IOException { runEval("sherlock-holmes-010p", 10); }
37:     @Test void eval_050p() throws IOException { runEval("sherlock-holmes-050p", 50); }
38:     @Test void eval_200p() throws IOException { runEval("sherlock-holmes-200p", 200); }
39:     @Test void eval_500p() throws IOException { runEval("sherlock-holmes-500p", 500); }
40: 
41:     private void runEval(String stem, int expectedPages) throws IOException {
42:         Path pdfPath = CORPUS_DIR.resolve(stem + ".pdf");
43:         Path jsonPath = CORPUS_DIR.resolve(stem + ".json");
44: 
45:         if (!pdfPath.toFile().exists()) {
46:             System.out.println("SKIP: " + pdfPath + " not found (run generate_eval_corpus.py first)");
47:             return;
48:         }
49: 
50:         System.out.println("\n=== " + stem + ".pdf (" + expectedPages + " pages) ===");
51:         GroundTruth gt = GroundTruth.load(jsonPath);
52:         evaluateDocument(pdfPath.toFile(), gt);
53:     }
54: 
55:     private void evaluateDocument(File pdf, GroundTruth gt) throws IOException {
56:         List<BufferedImage> pages;
57:         try (PageExtractor pe = extractor) {
58:             pages = pe.extractPages(pdf);
59:         }
60: 
61:         int n = Math.min(pages.size(), gt.getPageCount());
62:         List<PageResult> results = new ArrayList<>(n);
63: 
64:         long totalStart = System.currentTimeMillis();
65: 
66:         for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
67:             long start = System.currentTimeMillis();
68:             PageResult result = ocrEngine.ocr(pages.get(i));
69:             long elapsed = System.currentTimeMillis() - start;
70:             results.add(result);
71: 
72:             List<String> gtWords = normalize(gt.getWords(i));
73:             List<String> ocrWords = normalize(result.getTextBlocks().stream()
74:                     .map(tb -> tb.getWord()).collect(Collectors.toList()));
75: 
76:             double pageWer = Levenshtein.wer(ocrWords, gtWords);
77:             String gtText = String.join(" ", gtWords);
78:             String ocrText = String.join(" ", ocrWords);
79:             double pageCer = Levenshtein.cer(ocrText, gtText);
80:             double recall = computeRecall(ocrWords, gtWords);
81:             double precision = computePrecision(ocrWords, gtWords);
82:             double meanConf = result.getTextBlocks().stream()
83:                     .mapToDouble(tb -> tb.getConfidence()).average().orElse(0);
84: 
85:             String flag = pageWer > WER_THRESHOLD ? " ***" : "";
86:             System.out.printf("  Page %3d: WER=%.1f%%  CER=%.1f%%  recall=%.1f%%  prec=%.1f%%  conf=%.1f  time=%.2fs%s%n",
87:                     i + 1, pageWer * 100, pageCer * 100, recall * 100, precision * 100,
88:                     meanConf, elapsed / 1000.0, flag);
89:         }
90: 
91:         long totalTime = System.currentTimeMillis() - totalStart;
92: 
93:         // Aggregate metrics
94:         List<String> allGt = new ArrayList<>();
95:         List<String> allOcr = new ArrayList<>();
96:         int totalGtWords = 0, totalOcrWords = 0;
97:         double sumConf = 0;
98:         int confCount = 0;
99:         int flaggedPages = 0;
100: 
101:         for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
102:             List<String> gw = normalize(gt.getWords(i));
103:             List<String> ow = normalize(results.get(i).getTextBlocks().stream()
104:                     .map(tb -> tb.getWord()).collect(Collectors.toList()));
105:             allGt.addAll(gw);
106:             allOcr.addAll(ow);
107:             totalGtWords += gw.size();
108:             totalOcrWords += ow.size();
109:             for (var tb : results.get(i).getTextBlocks()) {
110:                 sumConf += tb.getConfidence();
111:                 confCount++;
112:             }
113: 
114:             double pageWer = Levenshtein.wer(ow, gw);
115:             if (pageWer > WER_THRESHOLD) flaggedPages++;
116:         }
117: 
118:         String gtFull = String.join(" ", allGt);
119:         String ocrFull = String.join(" ", allOcr);
120: 
121:         double aggregateWer = Levenshtein.wer(allOcr, allGt);
122:         double aggregateCer = Levenshtein.cer(ocrFull, gtFull);
123:         double aggregateRecall = computeRecall(allOcr, allGt);
124:         double aggregatePrecision = computePrecision(allOcr, allGt);
125:         double meanConf = confCount > 0 ? sumConf / confCount : 0;
126: 
127:         if (flaggedPages > 0) {
128:             System.out.println("\n  Pages with WER > " + (WER_THRESHOLD * 100) + "%: " + flaggedPages + " / " + n);
129:         } else {
130:             System.out.println("\n  Pages with WER > " + (WER_THRESHOLD * 100) + "%: none");
131:         }
132: 
133:         System.out.println("\n  Document summary:");
134:         System.out.printf("    Pages:                %d%n", n);
135:         System.out.printf("    Total GT words:       %d%n", totalGtWords);
136:         System.out.printf("    Total OCR words:      %d%n", totalOcrWords);
137:         System.out.printf("    Aggregate WER:        %.1f%%%n", aggregateWer * 100);
138:         System.out.printf("    Aggregate CER:        %.1f%%%n", aggregateCer * 100);
139:         System.out.printf("    Word recall:          %.1f%%%n", aggregateRecall * 100);
140:         System.out.printf("    Word precision:       %.1f%%%n", aggregatePrecision * 100);
141:         System.out.printf("    Mean confidence:      %.1f%n", meanConf);
142:         System.out.printf("    Total time:           %.1fs%n", totalTime / 1000.0);
143:         System.out.printf("    Time per page:        %.2fs%n", totalTime / 1000.0 / n);
144:     }
145: 
146:     // ── helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
147: 
148:     private List<String> normalize(List<String> words) {
149:         return words.stream()
150:                 .map(w -> w.replaceAll("^[^a-zA-Z0-9]+", ""))
151:                 .map(w -> w.replaceAll("[^a-zA-Z0-9]+$", ""))
152:                 .map(String::toLowerCase)
153:                 .filter(w -> !w.isEmpty())
154:                 .collect(Collectors.toList());
155:     }
156: 
157:     private double computeRecall(List<String> ocrWords, List<String> gtWords) {
158:         if (gtWords.isEmpty()) return 1.0;
159:         long found = gtWords.stream().filter(w -> ocrWords.contains(w)).count();
160:         return (double) found / gtWords.size();
161:     }
162: 
163:     private double computePrecision(List<String> ocrWords, List<String> gtWords) {
164:         if (ocrWords.isEmpty()) return 1.0;
165:         long found = ocrWords.stream().filter(w -> gtWords.contains(w)).count();
166:         return (double) found / ocrWords.size();
167:     }
168: }
```

1. What PageExtractor.extractPages returns (should be List<BufferedImage> or something else)
2. What OCREngine.ocr(int, File) returns
3. How the main TrulyFreeOCR uses these
4. How PageResult stores page index

From the summary:
- `OCREngine.ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)` — reads pre-saved grayscale PNG from disk
- Pages are saved as grayscale PNGs to `temp/{inputName}-{nanotime}/` after extraction

So the key question is: these tests use `extractor.extractPages()` which returns `List<BufferedImage>`, then try to OCR them. But the new OCREngine.ocr() needs a pageIndex and a tempDir where the grayscale page PNGs have already been saved.

For the tests, the simplest approach is to:
1. Save pages to a temp directory as PNGs first
2. Then call engine.ocr(i, tempDir)

OR, we could change the tests to save the pages first and then OCR them.

Actually, looking at the TrulyFreeOCR.java main code, the page saving happens in the main pipeline, not in extractPages(). Let me look at the main pipeline code to understand the flow.

Let me read the current OCREngine and the main pipeline method.

```
1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.Rectangle;
4: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
5: import java.io.BufferedReader;
6: import java.io.File;
7: import java.io.IOException;
8: import java.io.InputStream;
9: import java.util.ArrayList;
10: import java.util.List;
11: 
12: import javax.imageio.ImageIO;
13: 
14: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
15: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
16: 
17: public class OCREngine {
18: 
19:     private final File tessdataDir;
20:     private final String tesseractPath;
21:     private final String language;
22:     private final String psm;
23: 
24:     public OCREngine() {
25:         this("tessdata", "native/linux/tesseract", "eng", "1");
26:     }
27: 
28:     public OCREngine(String tessdataDir, String tesseractPath, String language, String psm) {
29:         this.tesseractPath = tesseractPath;
30:         this.language = language;
31:         this.psm = psm;
32: 
33:         if (tessdataDir != null && !tessdataDir.isEmpty()) {
34:             this.tessdataDir = new File(tessdataDir);
35:         } else {
36:             String env = System.getenv("TESSDATA_PREFIX");
37:             if (env != null && !env.isEmpty()) {
38:                 this.tessdataDir = new File(env);
39:             } else {
40:                 this.tessdataDir = new File("/usr/share/tesseract-ocr/5/tessdata");
41:             }
42:         }
43:     }
44: 
45:     public PageResult ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir) throws IOException {
46:         File pngFile = new File(tempDir, "page-" + pageIndex + ".png");
47:         BufferedImage tmp = ImageIO.read(pngFile);
48:         int width = tmp.getWidth();
49:         int height = tmp.getHeight();
50:         tmp = null;
51: 
52:         String prefix = pngFile.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
53:         Process process = null;
54: 
55:         try {
56:             ProcessBuilder pb = new ProcessBuilder(
57:                     tesseractPath,
58:                     pngFile.getAbsolutePath(),
59:                     prefix,
60:                     "-l", language,
61:                     "--psm", psm,
62:                     "tsv"
63:             );
64:             pb.environment().put("TESSDATA_PREFIX", tessdataDir.getAbsolutePath());
65:             pb.redirectErrorStream(true);
66:             pb.redirectInput(ProcessBuilder.Redirect.from(new File("/dev/null")));
67: 
68:             process = pb.start();
69:             Process proc = process;
70:             Thread drainer = new Thread(() -> {
71:                 try (InputStream is = proc.getInputStream()) {
72:                     byte[] buf = new byte[4096];
73:                     while (is.read(buf) != -1) { }
74:                 } catch (IOException ignored) { }
75:             }, "tesseract-stdout-drain");
76:             drainer.setDaemon(true);
77:             drainer.start();
78:             boolean exited = process.waitFor(120, java.util.concurrent.TimeUnit.SECONDS);
79:             if (!exited) {
80:                 process.destroyForcibly();
81:                 throw new IOException("Tesseract timed out after 120 seconds");
82:             }
83:             int exitCode = process.exitValue();
84:             if (exitCode != 0) {
85:                 throw new IOException("Tesseract exited with code " + exitCode);
86:             }
87: 
88:             File tsvFile = new File(prefix + ".tsv");
89:             if (!tsvFile.exists()) {
90:                 return new PageResult(pageIndex + 1, width, height, List.of());
91:             }
92: 
93:             List<TextBlock> blocks = parseTsv(tsvFile);
94:             return new PageResult(pageIndex + 1, width, height, blocks);
95: 
96:         } catch (InterruptedException e) {
97:             if (process != null) process.destroyForcibly();
98:             Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
99:             throw new IOException("Tesseract was interrupted", e);
100:         } finally {
101:             for (String ext : new String[]{".tsv"}) {
102:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
103:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
104:             }
105:         }
106:     }
107: 
108:     private List<TextBlock> parseTsv(File tsv) throws IOException {
109:         List<TextBlock> blocks = new ArrayList<>();
110:         try (BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new java.io.FileReader(tsv))) {
111:             String header = reader.readLine();
112:             if (header == null) return blocks;
113: 
114:             String line;
115:             while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) {
116:                 if (line.trim().isEmpty()) continue;
117:                 String[] parts = line.split("\t");
118:                 if (parts.length < 12) continue;
119: 
120:                 String level = parts[0];
121:                 String text = parts[11].trim();
122:                 if (!"5".equals(level) || text.isEmpty()) continue;
123: 
124:                 try {
125:                     int left = Integer.parseInt(parts[6]);
126:                     int top = Integer.parseInt(parts[7]);
127:                     int w = Integer.parseInt(parts[8]);
128:                     int h = Integer.parseInt(parts[9]);
129:                     double conf = Double.parseDouble(parts[10]);
130: 
131:                     blocks.add(new TextBlock(text, new Rectangle(left, top, w, h), conf));
132:                 } catch (NumberFormatException e) {
133:                 }
134:             }
135:         }
136:         return blocks;
137:     }
138: }
```

The key pattern from `runPipeline`:
```java
// Save each page as a grayscale PNG
for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
    BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);
    if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {
        BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);
        Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();
        g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);
        g.dispose();
        page = gray;
    }
    ImageIO.write(page, "png", new File(tempDir, "page-" + i + ".png"));
}
```

Then: `ocrEngine.ocr(pageIdx, tempDir)`

For each test, the approach will be:
1. Create a temp directory (e.g., `File tempDir = new File("temp/test-ocr-" + System.nanoTime()); tempDir.mkdirs()` or use `Files.createTempDirectory`)
2. Extract pages → `List<BufferedImage>`
3. Save pages as grayscale PNGs to tempDir
4. For each page index i, call `engine.ocr(i, tempDir)`
5. Clean up tempDir in finally

```
13: /**
14:  * Renders each page of a PDF to a BufferedImage at 300 DPI
15:  * using PDFBox's PDFRenderer.
16:  *
17:  * 300 DPI is the standard scanning resolution for OCR:
18:  *   - US Letter (8.5"x11")  → 2550 × 3300 px
19:  *   - A4 (210×297 mm)        → 2480 × 3508 px
20:  *
21:  * The renderer uses PDFBox's built-in SFF (subpixel font finalizer)
22:  * for high-quality text rendering.
23:  *
24:  * Implements AutoCloseable so the underlying PDDocument is released.
25:  * Callers should use try-with-resources.
26:  */
27: public class PageExtractor implements AutoCloseable {
28: 
29:     private PDDocument document;
30:     private final float dpi;
31: 
32:     public PageExtractor() {
33:         this(300f);
34:     }
35:

37:         this.dpi = dpi;

39: 
40:     /**

42:      *
43:      * @param pdf  Input PDF file.

50:         List<BufferedImage> pages = new ArrayList<>(pageCount);
51:         for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
52:             BufferedImage img = renderer.renderImageWithDPI(i, dpi);
53:             pages.add(img);
54:         }
55:         return pages;
56:     }
57:
```

Let me write a common static helper:

```java
private static List<PageResult> ocrPages(OCREngine engine, List<BufferedImage> pages) throws IOException {
    File tempDir = File.createTempFile("tfocr-test-", "");
    tempDir.delete();
    tempDir.mkdirs();
    try {
        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
            BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);
            if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {
                BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);
                java.awt.Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();
                g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);
                g.dispose();
                page = gray;
            }
            javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(page, "png", new File(tempDir, "page-" + i + ".png"));
        }
        List<PageResult> results = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());
        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
            results.add(engine.ocr(i, tempDir));
        }
        return results;
    } finally {
        // clean up
        for (File f : tempDir.listFiles()) f.delete();
        tempDir.delete();
    }
}
```

I think a clean helper approach for the stream-based code is:

```java
private static List<PageResult> runOcr(OCREngine engine, List<BufferedImage> pages) throws IOException {
    Path tempDir = Files.createTempDirectory("tfocr-test-");
    try {
        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
            BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);
            if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {
                BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);
                Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();
                g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);
                g.dispose();
                page = gray;
            }
            ImageIO.write(page, "png", tempDir.resolve("page-" + i + ".png").toFile());
        }
        List<PageResult> results = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());
        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
            results.add(engine.ocr(i, tempDir.toFile()));
        }
        return results;
    } finally {
        for (File f : tempDir.toFile().listFiles()) f.delete();
        tempDir.toFile().delete();
    }
}
```

But this is going to be a lot of repeated code... Let me think about whether it's worth creating a shared test utility. Actually, looking at the test code, there's no shared test utility class, and adding one would be introducing a new file which we should avoid unless necessary.

For **OCREngineTest.java**, I'll replace `engine.ocr(pages.get(0))` with a helper that saves and OCRs. Let me write the code.

```java
package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;

import static org.junit.jupiter.api.Assertions.*;

import java.awt.Graphics2D;
import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
import java.io.File;
import java.io.IOException;
import java.nio.file.Files;
import java.nio.file.Path;
import java.util.ArrayList;
import java.util.List;

import javax.imageio.ImageIO;

import org.junit.jupiter.api.BeforeAll;
import org.junit.jupiter.api.Test;

import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;

class OCREngineTest {

    static PageExtractor extractor;
    static OCREngine engine;

    @BeforeAll
    static void setup() {
        extractor = new PageExtractor();
        engine = new OCREngine();
    }

    private static PageResult ocrPage(OCREngine engine, List<BufferedImage> pages, int pageIndex) throws IOException {
        Path tempDir = Files.createTempDirectory("tfocr-test-");
        try {
            for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
                BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);
                if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {
                    BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);
                    Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();
                    g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);
                    g.dispose();
                    page = gray;
                }
                ImageIO.write(page, "png", tempDir.resolve("page-" + i + ".png").toFile());
            }
            return engine.ocr(pageIndex, tempDir.toFile());
        } finally {
            File dir = tempDir.toFile();
            File[] files = dir.listFiles();
            if (files != null) {
                for (File f : files) f.delete();
            }
            dir.delete();
        }
    }

    @Test
    void ocr_simpleText_returnsWordBlocks() throws IOException {
        var pages = extractor.extractPages(new File("tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf"));
        var result = ocrPage(engine, pages, 0);
        ...
```

Wait, the problem is that `ocrPage` creates a temp dir for each call. For tests that call it once, that's fine. For the stream-based map patterns in the other tests, it's wasteful but still correct.

Actually, for the stream-based tests (PDFAssemblerTest, etc.), I'll create a separate helper that returns `List<PageResult>`:

```java
private static List<PageResult> ocrPages(OCREngine engine, List<BufferedImage> pages) throws IOException {
    Path tempDir = Files.createTempDirectory("tfocr-test-");
    try {
        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
            BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);
            if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {
                BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);
                Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();
                g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);
                g.dispose();
                page = gray;
            }
            ImageIO.write(page, "png", tempDir.resolve("page-" + i + ".png").toFile());
        }
        List<PageResult> results = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());
        for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {
            results.add(engine.ocr(i, tempDir.toFile()));
        }
        return results;
    } finally {
        File dir = tempDir.toFile();
        File[] files = dir.listFiles();
        if (files != null) {
            for (File f : files) f.delete();
        }
        dir.delete();
    }
}
```

OK, let me write out all the changes now. I'll do them one file at a time using edit operations.

Actually, let me think about this differently. The test pattern in PDFAssemblerTest, MetadataPreserverTest, and PipelineIntegrationTest is:

```java
var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);
var backgrounds = pages.stream()
    .map(segmenter::segment)
    .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)
    .toList();
var ocrResults = pages.stream()
    .map(p -> {
        try { return engine.ocr(p); }
        catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
    })
    .toList();
```

I need to change this to save pages first, then OCR them. The `.stream().map()` pattern won't work directly since I need the index. So I'll need to change the approach - save pages first, then iterate with index.

Actually, I can keep using streams. Instead of mapping `pages` to OCR, I map the index range:

```java
var ocrResults = IntStream.range(0, pages.size())
    .mapToObj(i -> {
        try { return engine.ocr(i, tempDir); }
        catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }
    })
    .toList();
```

But I still need the tempDir from somewhere. Let me create a helper method.

Actually the simplest clean approach: I'll create a helper method in each file. For the stream-heavy files, I'll change the approach to use a helper that takes `OCREngine` + pages and returns `List<PageResult>` directly, replacing the `.stream().map(...)` block entirely.

Let me now write all the edits.

For OCREngineTest:
- Add imports for Graphics2D, Files, Path, ArrayList, ImageIO
- Add a `ocrPage` helper method
- Replace all `engine.ocr(pages.get(0))` with `ocrPage(engine, pages, 0)`

For PDFAssemblerTest:
- Add imports
- Add `ocrPages` helper method
- Replace the `.stream().map(p -> { try { return engine.ocr(p); } catch ... })` blocks with `ocrPages(engine, pages)`

For MetadataPreserverTest:
- Same pattern as PDFAssemblerTest

For PipelineIntegrationTest:
- Change `processOcr` helper method

For OcrAccuracyTest:
- Change the `evaluateDocument` method

Let me start.

Actually wait, let me think about whether there's a simpler approach. For the stream-based patterns, I could save pages first then use IntStream. Let me check if IntStream is already imported.

I think the cleanest approach is:
1. Create a shared test utility file (but we said "ALWAYS prefer editing existing files")
2. Add a private helper to each test file

Since creating a shared utility file would be a new file, let me add private helpers in each test file. It's some duplication but it keeps each test file self-contained.

Let me write the edits now.

</details>

Now I have a clear picture. The OCREngine API changed from `ocr(BufferedImage)` to `ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)`. The tests need to save pages to a temp directory first, then OCR by index. Let me fix all five files.

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/MetadataPreserverTest.java",
```
```json
  "oldString": "    static PageExtractor extractor;\n    static ImageSegmenter segmenter;\n    static OCREngine engine;\n    static PDFAssembler assembler;\n    static MetadataPreserver preserver;\n\n    @BeforeAll\n    static void setup() {\n        extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();\n        engine = new OCREngine();\n        assembler = new PDFAssembler();\n        preserver = new MetadataPreserver();\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void preserve_copiesDocumentInfo() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = pages.stream()\n                .map(p -> { try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n                    catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); } })\n                .toList();",
  "newString": "    static PageExtractor extractor;\n    static ImageSegmenter segmenter;\n    static OCREngine engine;\n    static PDFAssembler assembler;\n    static MetadataPreserver preserver;\n\n    @BeforeAll\n    static void setup() {\n        extractor = new PageExtractor();\n        segmenter = new ImageSegmenter();\n        engine = new OCREngine();\n        assembler = new PDFAssembler();\n        preserver = new MetadataPreserver();\n    }\n\n    private static List<PageResult> ocrPages(OCREngine engine, List<BufferedImage> pages) throws IOException {\n        Path tempDir = Files.createTempDirectory(\"tfocr-test-\");\n        try {\n            for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n                BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);\n                if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {\n                    BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);\n                    Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();\n                    g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);\n                    g.dispose();\n                    page = gray;\n                }\n                ImageIO.write(page, \"png\", tempDir.resolve(\"page-\" + i + \".png\").toFile());\n            }\n            List<PageResult> results = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n            for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n                results.add(engine.ocr(i, tempDir.toFile()));\n            }\n            return results;\n        } finally {\n            File dir = tempDir.toFile();\n            File[] files = dir.listFiles();\n            if (files != null) {\n                for (File f : files) f.delete();\n            }\n            dir.delete();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Test\n    void preserve_copiesDocumentInfo() throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/simple-text.pdf\");\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        var backgrounds = pages.stream()\n                .map(segmenter::segment)\n                .map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground)\n                .toList();\n        var ocrResults = ocrPages(engine, pages);"
}
```

```json
{
  "filePath": "/home/bgu/GitHub/msmarkgu/TrulyFreeOCR/src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PipelineIntegrationTest.java",
  "oldString": "    // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    @FunctionalInterface\n    private interface OutputValidator {\n        void validate(PDDocument output, File sourceFile) throws IOException;\n    }\n\n    private void runPipeline(String testPdfName, OutputValidator validator) throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/\" + testPdfName);\n        assertTrue(input.exists(), \"Test PDF not found: \" + input);\n\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        assertNotNull(pages);\n        assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());\n\n        var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();\n        var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();\n        var ocrResults = processOcr(pages);\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, null, ocrResults, false)) {\n            assertNotNull(output);\n            validator.validate(output, input);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private List<PageResult> processOcr(List<BufferedImage> pages) {\n        return pages.stream().map(p -> {\n            try { return engine.ocr(p); }\n            catch (IOException e) { throw new RuntimeException(e); }\n        }).toList();\n    }\n}",
  "newString": "    // ── Helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    @FunctionalInterface\n    private interface OutputValidator {\n        void validate(PDDocument output, File sourceFile) throws IOException;\n    }\n\n    private void runPipeline(String testPdfName, OutputValidator validator) throws IOException {\n        File input = new File(\"tests/test-files/\" + testPdfName);\n        assertTrue(input.exists(), \"Test PDF not found: \" + input);\n\n        var pages = extractor.extractPages(input);\n        assertNotNull(pages);\n        assertFalse(pages.isEmpty());\n\n        var segmented = pages.stream().map(segmenter::segment).toList();\n        var backgrounds = segmented.stream().map(SegmentedImage::getCleanedBackground).toList();\n        var ocrResults = processOcr(engine, pages);\n\n        try (PDDocument output = assembler.assemble(input, backgrounds, null, ocrResults, false)) {\n            assertNotNull(output);\n            validator.validate(output, input);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private List<PageResult> processOcr(OCREngine engine, List<BufferedImage> pages) throws IOException {\n        Path tempDir = Files.createTempDirectory(\"tfocr-test-\");\n        try {\n            for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n                BufferedImage page = pages.get(i);\n                if (page.getType() != BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY) {\n                    BufferedImage gray = new BufferedImage(page.getWidth(), page.getHeight(), BufferedImage.TYPE_BYTE_GRAY);\n                    Graphics2D g = gray.createGraphics();\n                    g.drawImage(page, 0, 0, null);\n                    g.dispose();\n                    page = gray;\n                }\n                ImageIO.write(page, \"png\", tempDir.resolve(\"page-\" + i + \".png\").toFile());\n            }\n            List<PageResult> results = new ArrayList<>(pages.size());\n            for (int i = 0; i < pages.size(); i++) {\n                results.add(engine.ocr(i, tempDir.toFile()));\n            }\n            return results;\n        } finally {\n            File dir = tempDir.toFile();\n            File[] files = dir.listFiles();\n            if (files != null) {\n                for (File f : files) f.delete();\n            }\n            dir.delete();\n        }\n    }\n}"
}
```

```
166:     private List<String> normalize(List<String> words) {
167:         return words.stream()
168:                 .map(w -> w.replaceAll("^[^a-zA-Z0-9]+", ""))
169:                 .map(w -> w.replaceAll("[^a-zA-Z0-9]+$", ""))
170:                 .map(String::toLowerCase)
171:                 .filter(w -> !w.isEmpty())
172:                 .collect(Collectors.toList());
173:     }
174: 
175:     private double computeRecall(List<String> ocrWords, List<String> gtWords) {
176:         if (gtWords.isEmpty()) return 1.0;
177:         long found = gtWords.stream().filter(w -> ocrWords.contains(w)).count();
178:         return (double) found / gtWords.size();
179:     }
180: 
181:     private double computePrecision(List<String> ocrWords, List<String> gtWords) {
182:         if (ocrWords.isEmpty()) return 1.0;
183:         long found = ocrWords.stream().filter(w -> gtWords.contains(w)).count();
184:         return (double) found / ocrWords.size();
185:     }
186: }

(End of file - total 186 lines)
</content>
```

Fixed all 5 test files. Summary of changes:

- **OCREngineTest.java** — Added `ocrPage()` helper that saves `BufferedImage` pages as grayscale PNGs to a temp directory, then calls `engine.ocr(pageIndex, tempDir)`. All 6 test methods updated.

- **PDFAssemblerTest.java** — Added `ocrPages()` helper (batch version). Replaced all 5 stream-based `engine.ocr(p)` blocks with `ocrPages(engine, pages)`.

- **MetadataPreserverTest.java** — Same pattern: added `ocrPages()` helper, replaced 3 OCR stream blocks.

- **PipelineIntegrationTest.java** — Changed `processOcr()` to accept `OCREngine` parameter and use the save-to-temp-dir pattern. Updated 3 call sites.

- **OcrAccuracyTest.java** — Added temp directory management to `evaluateDocument()`, saving all pages as grayscale PNGs before the OCR loop, then calling `ocrEngine.ocr(i, tempDir.toFile())` with cleanup in `finally`.

It is based on Python development , the use of Tesseract OCR engine , can accurately recognize the text in the image and embed it into the PDF , maintaining the layout of the original document and image quality . Tools support multi-language, for Linux, Windows, macOS and other platforms, but also provides a Docker image to facilitate cross-platform deployment.OCRmyPDF default generated PDF/A format, suitable for long-term archiving, while supporting page correction, image optimization and other features, widely used in document digitization and archiving scenarios.\n\n## Function List\n\n* Add searchable OCR text layers to scanned PDFs with copy and paste support.\n* Default generation of PDF/A format, suitable for long-term document archiving.\n* Supports text recognition in 39 languages, covering English, German, Chinese and more.\n* Automatic correction of page skew (deskew) and rotation (rotate-pages).\n\n...\n\nutilization `--optimize 1` Or install the JBIG2 encoder to compress the file size.\n4. **How to verify OCR results?**  \n   utilization `--verbose 2` Generate detailed logs, or check the output PDF for reproducible text.\n\n[AI open source project](https://www.kdjingpai.com/en/aikaiyuanxiangmu/) [Document Extraction and Cleaning](https://www.kdjingpai.com/en/markdown/)\n\n[AI productivity tools](https://www.kdjingpai.com/en) » [OCRmyPDF: scanned PDF into searchable text of the open source tool](https://www.kdjingpai.com/en/ocrmypdf/) Posted on 2025-08-04, please contact us if you find the URL is out of date, or inaccessible.\n\n__ __ _0_ Bookmarked\n\n__ __ 0 _kudos_\n\nShare\n\n[RAGLight: Lightweight Retrieval Augmentation Generation Python Library](https://www.kdjingpai.com/en/raglight/)\n\n[YCursor: an automated trial renewal tool for Cursor software](https://www.kdjingpai.com/en/ycursor/)\n\n### Recommended"

      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "#  OCR multilingual documents \nocrmypdf -l eng+fra Bilingual-English-French.pdf Bilingual-English-French.pdf\n\n #  Deskew (straighten crooked pages) \nocrmypdf --deskew input.pdf output.pdf\n```\n\nFor more features, see the [documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) .\n\n## Requirements\n\nIn addition to the required Python version, OCRmyPDF requires external program installations of Ghostscript and Tesseract OCR. OCRmyPDF is pure Python, and runs on pretty much everything: Linux, macOS, Windows and FreeBSD.\n\n## Plugins\n\nOCRmyPDF provides a plugin interface allowing its capabilities to be extended or replaced. Here are some plugins we are aware of:\n\n* [OCRmyPDF-AppleOCR](https://github.com/mkyt/ocrmypdf-AppleOCR) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with Apple Vision Framework. Requires macOS.\n* [OCRmyPDF-EasyOCR](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF-EasyOCR) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with EasyOCR, a newer OCR engine based on PyTorch.\nGPU strongly recommended.\n* [OCRmyPDF-PaddleOCR](https://github.com/clefru/ocrmypdf-paddleocr) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with PaddleOCR, a powerful GPU accelerated OCR engine.\n\n[paperless-ngx](https://docs.paperless-ngx.com/) provides integration of OCRmyPDF into a searchable document management system.\n\n## Press & Media\n\n* [Going paperless with OCRmyPDF](https://medium.com/@ikirichenko/going-paperless-with-ocrmypdf-e2f36143f46a)\n* [Converting a scanned document into a compressed searchable PDF with redactions](https://medium.com/@treyharris/converting-a-scanned-document-into-a-compressed-searchable-pdf-with-redactions-63f61c34fe4c)\n* [c't 1-2014, page 59](https://heise.de/-2279695) : Detailed presentation of OCRmyPDF v1.0 in the leading German IT magazine c't\n* [heise Open Source, 09/2014: Texterkennung mit OCRmyPDF](https://heise.de/-2356670)\n* [heise Durchsuchbare PDF-Dokumente mit OCRmyPDF erstellen](https://www.heise.\nde/ratgeber/Durchsuchbare-PDF-Dokumente-mit-OCRmyPDF-erstellen-4607592.html)\n* [Excellent Utilities: OCRmyPDF](https://www.linuxlinks.com/excellent-utilities-ocrmypdf-add-ocr-text-layer-scanned-pdfs/)\n* [LinuxUser Texterkennung mit OCRmyPDF und Scanbd automatisieren](https://www.linux-community.de/ausgaben/linuxuser/2021/06/texterkennung-mit-ocrmypdf-und-scanbd-automatisieren/)\n* [Y Combinator discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=32028752)\n\n## Business enquiries\n\nOCRmyPDF would not be the software that it is today without companies and users choosing to provide support for feature development and consulting enquiries. We are happy to discuss all enquiries, whether for extending the existing feature set, or integrating OCRmyPDF into a larger system.\n\n## License\n\nThe OCRmyPDF software is licensed under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL-2.0)."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://dev.to/githubopensource/ocrmypdf-the-magic-wand-for-your-scanned-pdfs-36fb",
      "title": "OCRmyPDF: The Magic Wand for Your Scanned PDFs",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "* ✅ Supports multiple languages and creates PDF/A files for long-term archiving.\n* ✅ Easy to use command-line interface and available on multiple platforms (Linux, Windows, macOS, FreeBSD).\n\n##  Project Statistics: 📊\n\n* ⭐ **Stars:** 28797\n* 🍴 **Forks:** 1962\n* ❗ **Open Issues:** 128\n\n##  Tech Stack: 💻\n\n* ✅ Python\n\nEver struggled with scanned PDFs that you can't search or copy-paste from? Frustrating, right? Meet OCRmyPDF, a command-line tool that's a game-changer for anyone working with scanned documents. It takes your messy, unsearchable PDFs and transforms them into easily searchable, copy-pasteable, and even better quality PDFs. Think of it as a magic wand for your scanned documents!\n\nSo, how does this magic work? OCRmyPDF uses a powerful OCR (Optical Character Recognition) engine called Tesseract. Tesseract analyzes the images in your PDF and extracts the text. But here's the clever part: OCRmyPDF doesn't just slap the text onto the page anywhere.\nIt carefully places the text layer _underneath_ the image, preserving the original document's layout. This means you can copy-paste text accurately and easily, without the frustration of misaligned characters.\n\nBut OCRmyPDF does more than just basic OCR. It also optimizes your PDF images, often resulting in smaller file sizes. It can even automatically correct rotated or skewed pages, saving you the tedious manual work. It supports multiple languages and produces PDF/A files, which are ideal for long-term archiving. The best part? It's incredibly fast, using multiple CPU cores to process your PDFs efficiently, even those with thousands of pages.\n\nWhy should you, a developer, care? Imagine the time you'll save when dealing with scanned documents. No more manual typing, no more struggling with unsearchable PDFs. OCRmyPDF seamlessly integrates into your workflow, whether you're building a document processing pipeline or simply need a quick way to make scanned documents usable.\nIts simple command-line interface makes it easy to automate, and its open-source nature ensures transparency and community support.\n\nThe project is battle-tested on millions of PDFs, meaning it's reliable and robust. It's available on Linux, Windows, macOS, and FreeBSD, with easy installation options via package managers or Docker. It's truly a powerful tool that simplifies a common pain point for developers and anyone who works with scanned documents. Give it a try; you won't be disappointed!\n\n##  Learn More: 🔗\n\n[View the Project on GitHub](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n\n* * *\n\n##  🌟 Stay Connected with GitHub Open Source!\n\n> 📱 **Join us on Telegram**  \n>   \n> Get daily updates on the best open-source projects  \n>   \n> [GitHub Open Source](https://t.me/GitHub_Open_Source)\n> \n> 👥 **Follow us on Facebook**  \n>   \n> Connect with our community and never miss a discovery  \n>   \n> [GitHub Open Source](https://www.facebook.com/people/GitHub-Open-Source/61571925474856/)\n> \n>\n\n...\n\nDEV Community — A space to discuss and keep up software development and manage your software career\n\nBuilt on [Forem](https://www.forem.com) — the [open source](https://dev.to/t/opensource) software that powers [DEV](https://dev.to) and other inclusive communities.\n\nDEV Community\n\nWe're a place where coders share, stay up-to-date and grow their careers."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF",
      "title": "OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files ... - GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "pre-commit-config.yaml | | |\n|.readthedocs.yaml |.readthedocs.yaml | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|README\\_ZH.md |README\\_ZH.md | | |\n|REUSE.toml |REUSE.toml | | |\n|pyproject.toml |pyproject.toml | | |\n|snapcraft.yaml |snapcraft.yaml | | |\n|uv.lock |uv.lock | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* Contributing\n* MPL-2.0 license\n\nOCRmyPDF\n\n[Build Status](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/actions/workflows/build.yml) [PyPI version](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/) [Homebrew version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c77bc15e95c6f2333ec096806d0eb2fb4008f71576ff066602d84d308c3dc93d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f686f6d65627265772f762f6f63726d797064662e737667) [ReadTheDocs](https://camo.githubusercontent.\ncom/6ab9ef6df0c5144bb402ef9318e32ffe87e1ab35b2d3a9f8755d2c248a1e0bc2/68747470733a2f2f72656164746865646f63732e6f72672f70726f6a656374732f6f63726d797064662f62616467652f3f76657273696f6e3d6c6174657374) [Python versions](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0cb43ab967c987d2ff0a625c849aab4a820f2094748f7299ed9821d5cd849fee/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f707970692f707976657273696f6e732f6f63726d79706466)\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted.\n\n```\nocrmypdf                      #  it's a scriptable command line program \n   -l eng+fra                 #  it supports multiple languages \n   --rotate-pages             #  it can fix pages that are misrotated \n   --deskew                   #  it can deskew crooked PDFs! \n   --title \" My PDF \"           #  it can change output metadata \n   --jobs 4                   #  it uses multiple cores by default \n   --output-type pdfa         #  it produces PDF/A by default\ninput_scanned.pdf          #  takes PDF input (or images) \n   output_searchable.pdf      #  produces validated PDF output\n```\n\n[See the release notes for details on the latest changes](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/release_notes.html) .\n\n## Main features\n\n* Generates a searchable [PDF/A](https://en.wikipedia.org/?title=PDF/A) file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Validates input and output files\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores\n* Uses [Tesseract OCR](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract) engine to recognize more than [100 languages](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdata)"
      ]
    },

        "SourceForge logo\n\n For Vendors Help Create Join \n\nSourceForge logo\n\nBusiness Software\n\nOpen Source Software\n\n[SourceForge Podcast](https://sourceforge.net/articles/category/sourceforge-podcast/)\n\nResources\n\n* Articles\n* Case Studies\n* Blog\n\nMenu\n\n* Help\n* Create\n* Join\n* \n\n* Home\n* Open Source Software\n* Formats and Protocols\n* Data Formats\n* PDF\n* OCRmyPDF\n\nOCRmyPDF\n\n# OCRmyPDF\n\n## OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files\n\n**This is an exact mirror of the OCRmyPDF project, hosted at <https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF> . SourceForge is not affiliated with OCRmyPDF.**\n\nAdd a Review\n\n**Downloads:** 112 This Week\n\n**Last Update:** 2026-06-18\n\nDownload\n\nShare This\n\n[](https://x.com/share?url=https://sourceforge.net/projects/ocrmypdf.mirror/&text=Download%20OCRmyPDF%20on%20SourceForge%20for%20free%21%20OCRmyPDF%20adds%20an%20OCR%20text%20layer%20to%20scanned%20PDF%20files \"Share OCRmyPDF on SourceForge on X\") [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://sourceforge.\nnet/projects/ocrmypdf.mirror/&title=OCRmyPDF%20on%20SourceForge \"Share OCRmyPDF on SourceForge on facebook\") [](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://sourceforge.net/projects/ocrmypdf.mirror/&title=OCRmyPDF%20on%20SourceForge&source=SourceForge.net \"Share OCRmyPDF on SourceForge on LinkedIn\")\n\nEmail in envelope\n\n#### Get an email when there's a new version of OCRmyPDF\n\nNext\n\n* Summary\n* Files\n* Reviews\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an optical character recognition (OCR) text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched. PDF is the best format for storing and exchanging scanned documents. Unfortunately, PDFs can be difficult to modify. OCRmyPDF makes it easy to apply image processing and OCR (recognized, searchable text) to existing PDFs.\n\n### Features\n\n* Generates a searchable PDF/A file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores\n\n### Project Samples\n\nOCRmyPDF Screenshot 1\n\n### Project Activity\n\n[](https://sourceforge.net/auth/?return_to=%2Fprojects%2Focrmypdf.mirror%2F \"Follow OCRmyPDF - Requires login\") \n\nSee All Activity >\n\n### Categories\n\nPDF , OCR\n\n### License\n\nMozilla Public License 1.0 (MPL)\n\n### Follow OCRmyPDF\n\n[OCRmyPDF Web Site](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF \"OCRmyPDF Web Site\")\n\nOther Useful Business Software\n\nEnterprise-grade ITSM, for every business Icon\n\nEnterprise-grade ITSM, for every business\n\nGive your IT, operations, and business teams the ability to deliver exceptional services—without the complexity."

        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# ocrmypdf 17.7.0\n\npip install ocrmypdf Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: Jun 18, 2026\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Changelog](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/tree/main/docs/releasenotes)\n* [Source](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n* [Tracker](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/issues)\n\n###### GitHub Statistics\n\n* [**Repository**](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n* **Stars:**\n* **Forks:**\n* **Open issues:**\n* **Open PRs:**\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for jrbarlow from gravatar.com jrbarlow\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/)\n\n###### Meta\n* **License Expression:** MPL-2.0  \n  _[SPDX](https://spdx.org/licenses/) [License Expression](https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/annexes/spdx-license-expressions/)_\n* **Author:** James R. Barlow\n* Tags OCR , PDF , PDF/A , optical character recognition , scanning\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.11\n* **Provides-Extra:** `watcher` , `webservice`\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\nOCRmyPDF\n\n[Build Status](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/actions/workflows/build.yml) [PyPI version](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/) Homebrew version ReadTheDocs Python versions\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted.\n\n```\nocrmypdf # it's a scriptable command line program \n -l eng+fra # it supports multiple languages \n --rotate-pages # it can fix pages that are misrotated\n--deskew # it can deskew crooked PDFs! \n --title \"My PDF\" # it can change output metadata \n --jobs 4 # it uses multiple cores by default \n --output-type pdfa # it produces PDF/A by default \n input_scanned.pdf # takes PDF input (or images) \n output_searchable.pdf # produces validated PDF output\n```\n\n[See the release notes for details on the latest changes](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/release_notes.html) .\n\n##  Main features\n\n* Generates a searchable [PDF/A](https://en.wikipedia.org/?title=PDF/A) file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Validates input and output files\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores"
      ]
    },

      "title": "OCRmyPDF documentation — ocrmypdf 17.5.0 documentation",

      "excerpts": [
        "<!DOCTYPE html> \n\n* [](#)\n* OCRmyPDF documentation\n* [ Edit on GitHub](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/blob/main/docs/index.md)\n\n---\n\n# OCRmyPDF documentation[](#ocrmypdf-documentation \"Link to this heading\")\n\n![_images/logo.svg](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_images/logo.svg) \n\nOCRmyPDF adds an optical character recognition (OCR) text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched.\n\nPDF is the best format for storing and exchanging scanned documents. Unfortunately, PDFs can be difficult to modify. OCRmyPDF makes it easy to apply image processing and OCR (recognized, searchable text) to existing PDFs.\n\n* [Introduction](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/introduction.html)\n* [Release notes](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/releasenotes/index.html)\n* [Installing OCRmyPDF](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/installation.html)\n* [Installing additional language packs](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/languages.html)\n* [Installing the JBIG2 encoder](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/jbig2.html)\n\nUsage\n\n* [Cookbook](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html)\n  * [Basic examples](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#basic-examples)\n  * [Image processing](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#image-processing)\n  * [Don’t actually OCR my PDF](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#don-t-actually-ocr-my-pdf)\n  * [Using v17 features](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#using-v17-features)\n  * [Redo existing OCR](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#redo-existing-ocr)\n  * [Improving OCR quality](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#improving-ocr-quality)\n  * [PDF optimization](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#pdf-optimization)\n  * [Digitally signed PDFs](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cookbook.html#digitally-signed-pdfs)"

      "title": "GitHub - ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF: OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "com/4a9ee25a3dc8438e565f9ecf097565d2305d3dc22735115899f98ae01632f732/68747470733a2f2f72656164746865646f63732e6f72672f70726f6a656374732f6f63726d797064662f62616467652f3f76657273696f6e3d6c6174657374) [](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/41e3f37a3a53cbd6a40e4c06b883806cf7c867eb2b2f88a7aac1a460bfaa6fe0/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f707970692f707976657273696f6e732f6f63726d79706466)\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted.\n\n```\nocrmypdf                      #  it's a scriptable command line program \n   -l eng+fra                 #  it supports multiple languages \n   --rotate-pages             #  it can fix pages that are misrotated \n   --deskew                   #  it can deskew crooked PDFs! \n   --title \" My PDF \"           #  it can change output metadata \n   --jobs 4                   #  it uses multiple cores by default \n   --output-type pdfa         #  it produces PDF/A by default \n   input_scanned.\npdf          #  takes PDF input (or images) \n   output_searchable.pdf      #  produces validated PDF output\n```\n\n[See the release notes for details on the latest changes](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/release_notes.html) .\n\n## Main features\n\n* Generates a searchable [PDF/A](https://en.wikipedia.org/?title=PDF/A) file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Validates input and output files\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores\n* Uses [Tesseract OCR](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract) engine to recognize more than [100 languages](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdata)\n* Keeps your private data private."

        "favicon\n\n🔥\n\nGLM-5.2 免费领\n\noperation-entry\n\n登录 注册\n\nSign up free\n\nDiscover high-quality open-source projects easily and host them with one click\n\n首页\n\n工作台\n\nAI社区\n\n大赛平台\n\n应用市场\n\n项目\n\n组织\n\n企业\n\n偏好设置\n\nThis is not the web page you are looking for\n\nBack to Home\n\n## Selected\n\ngo-iot-platform\n\n34\n\nGo IoT 平台，这是一个高效、可扩展的物联网解决方案，使用 Go 语言开发。本平台专注于提供稳定、可靠的 MQTT 客户端管理，以及对 MQTT上报数据的全面处理和分析。\n\nGo\n\n34\n\n11\n\nnote-gen\n\n129\n\n一款跨平台的 Markdown AI 笔记软件，致力于使用 AI 建立记录和写作的桥梁。\n\nTSX\n\n129\n\n11\n\ntoday 8\n\nWxJava\n\n933\n\n微信开发 Java SDK，支持微信支付、开放平台、公众号、视频号、企业微信、小程序等的后端开发，记得关注公众号及时接受版本更新信息，以及加入微信群进行深入讨论\n\nJava\n\n933\n\n36\n\nGLM-5.2\n\nModel\n\n113\n\n智谱开源 GLM-5.2，这是针对长文本任务的最新旗舰模型。相较于前代产品 GLM-5.1，它在长文本任务处理能力上实现了显著飞跃，并且首次在稳定的 100 万 token 上下文中提供这一能力。\n\nJinja\n\n113\n\ntoday 6\n\nWebStack-Hugo\n\n64\n\nWebStack 网址导航 Hugo 主题，无需服务器，支持导航一键配置的纯静态网址导航网站。\n\nSCSS\n\n64\n\n17\n\ncann-learning-hub\n\n505\n\nCANN 学习中心仓，支持在线互动运行、边学边练，提供教程、示例与优化方案，一站式助力昇腾开发者快速上手。\n\nJupyter Notebook\n\n505\n\n190\n\ntoday 9\n\nran\n\n3\n基于web components打造的原生组件，一些函数库，构建工具等等\n\nJavaScript\n\n3\n\natomcode\n\n2\\.56 K\n\nClaude Code 的开源替代方案。连接任意大模型，编辑代码，运行命令，自动验证 — 全自动执行。用 Rust 构建，极致性能。 ｜ An open-source alternative to Claude Code.       "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nLeoFCardoso / **pdf2pdfocr** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 35\n* Star 303\n\n# LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr\n\nmaster\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* Apache-2.0 license\n\n# pdf2pdfocr\n\nA tool to OCR a PDF (or supported images) and add a text \"layer\" (a \"pdf sandwich\") in the original file making it a searchable PDF.\nThe script uses only open source tools.\n\n# donations\n\nThis software is free, but if you like it, please donate to support new features.\n\n[paypal](https://www.paypal.com/cgi-bin/webscr?cmd=_donations&business=PZZU5APJGSWVA&lc=GB&item_name=pdf2pdfocr%20development&currency_code=USD)\nBitcoin (BTC) address: [bc1qqd4pj0pymptwg523ems8pxgjwdslh7ujv5egqg](https://blockchair.com/bitcoin/address/bc1qqd4pj0pymptwg523ems8pxgjwdslh7ujv5egqg)\n\n# tips\n\nTips are also welcome!\n\nDogecoin (DOGE) address: [D94hD2qPnkxmZk8qa1b6F1d7NfUrPkmcrG](https://blockchair.com/dogecoin/address/D94hD2qPnkxmZk8qa1b6F1d7NfUrPkmcrG)\n\nPIX (Brazilian Instant Payments): d3222ec1-74e6-40e0-a2d6-8f2d8e9f1568\n\nchave PIX\n\nPlease contact for donations and tips in other cryptocurrencies.\n\n# installation\n\nIn Linux, please try these instructions (thanks to @nicoursi).\n\n```\n# 1. Create the virtual environment\npython3 -m venv ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv\n# 2. Activate the virtual environment\nsource ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/activate\n# 3. Install Python dependencies\npip install -r requirements.txt\n# 4. Copy Python files to the virtual environment bin directory\ncp *.py ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/\n# 5. Copy executables to /usr/local/bin\ncp pdf2pdfocr pdf2pdfocr_gui /usr/local/bin\n```\n\nIn macOS, you will need macports.\n\n```\n# First install Xcode from Mac App Store, then:\nxcode-select --install\nsudo xcodebuild -license\n# Install Macports from https://www.macports.org/install.php\nsudo port selfupdate\n# Install tesseract as main ocr engine (Portuguese included below - please add your preferred languages)\nsudo port install git tesseract tesseract-por tesseract-osd tesseract-eng\n# Install qpdf\nsudo port install qpdf\n# Install python 3 and other dependencies\nsudo port install python314 py314-pip poppler poppler-data ImageMagick7 ghostscript\n# Configure default python3 installer\nsudo port select --set python python314\nsudo port select --set python3 python314\nsudo port select --set pip pip314\nsudo port select --set pip3 pip314\n# Configure venv and python deps in fixed home directory\npython3 -m venv ~/pdf2pdfocr-venv\n~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/python3 -m pip install --upgrade pip\n~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/pip3 install --upgrade setuptools\n~/pdf2pdfocr-venv/bin/pip3 install -r requirements.txt\n\n...\n\nIn some cases, you will want to deal with option flags. Please use:\n\n```\npdf2pdfocr.py --help\n```\n\nto view all the options.\n\nIt's also possible to use GUI.\n\n```\npdf2pdfocr_gui.py <<optional input file>>\n```\n\n# fun\n\nCaseiro com orgulho! ;-)\n\n## About\n\nA free tool to OCR a PDF and add a text \"layer\" in the original file, making a searchable PDF. Use only open source tools. Please tip!\n\n### Topics\n\npython docker pdf ocr tesseract pdftk\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**303** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**11** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**35** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\nNo releases published\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 94\\.1%\n* VBScript 4\\.0%\n* Dockerfile 1\\.6%\n* Shell 0\\.3%\n\n## Footer"

      "url": "https://olmocr.allenai.org/",
      "title": "olmOCR – Open-Source OCR for Accurate Document Conversion",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Demo Blog\n\n**olmOCR 2** is an open-source tool designed for high-throughput conversion of PDFs and other documents into plain text while preserving natural reading order. It supports tables, equations, handwriting, and more.\n\nolmOCR 2 has been trained on highly curated set academic papers, technical documentation, and other reference content; the latest version uses synthetic data and unit tests as verifiable rewards for reinforcement learning to further decrease hallucinations. For full details on the recipe, read our technical report. The current model was fine-tuned on **English** documents using a multilingual base VLM; other languages may work.\n\nTry the demo on your own documents below. You can then deploy the full olmOCR toolkit on your own GPUs for efficient, scalable document processing—at an estimated cost of just $178 USD per million pages converted.\n\n⚠️ This demo processes pages sequentially; for full throughput, use batch mode in our toolkit. ⚠️\n[Technical Report](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr/blob/main/olmOCR-2-Unit-Test-Rewards-for-Document-OCR.pdf) [Toolkit Code](https://github.com/allenai/olmocr) [Hugging Face Icon Dataset and Checkpoints](https://huggingface.co/collections/allenai/olmocr-67af8630b0062a25bf1b54a1) [Demo Model: `allenai/olmOCR-2-7B-1025-FP8`](https://huggingface.co/allenai/olmOCR-2-7B-1025-FP8)\n\n## Analyze any PDF, JPG, or PNG\n\nDrag and drop a file here or click to browse the file system\n\n## Or try a sample document\n\nOlmo 2 PDP Sample\n\nAcademic Papers\n\nTextbook Sample\n\nMath Textbooks\n\nHandwriting Sample\n\nHandwriting\n\nHistorical Document\n\nHistorical Documents\n\n## Notice & Consent\n\nAs a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, Ai2 offers olmOCR at no cost to educate the general public and advance scientific research in AI. Ai2 may collect user logs for diagnostic purposes according to our [Privacy Policy](https://allenai.org/terms) , as permitted by law, but will not retain your content submitted to olmoCR.\nNever submit personal, sensitive, or confidential information.\n\nBy selecting “Accept” below, you agree to Ai2’s [Terms of Use](https://allenai.org/terms) and [Responsible Use Guidelines](https://allenai.org/terms) .\n\nIf you do not wish to agree to these terms, select “Decline” and exit this page.\n\nAccept Decline"
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://tesseract-ocr.github.io/tessdoc/User-Projects-%E2%80%93-3rdParty.html",
      "title": "GUIs and Other Projects using Tesseract OCR | tessdoc",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# GUIs and Other Projects using Tesseract OCR\n\n## Tesseract documentation\n\n[View on GitHub](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdoc)\n\n# GUIs and Other Projects using Tesseract OCR\n\n## 1\\. GUIs\n\n|**Name** |**Linux** |**Mac** |**Windows** |**License** |**Description** |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|[Scribe OCR](https://github.com/scribeocr/scribeocr) |X |X |X |AGPL-3.0 license |Scribe OCR is a free and open-source web application for recognizing text, proofreading OCR data, and creating fully-digitized documents. Live site at [scribeocr.com](https://scribeocr.com/) . |\n|[Rescribe](https://rescribe.xyz/rescribe) |X |X |X |GPL v3 |Easy to use Tesseract frontend with no install needed, including PDF processing, and Google Book downloading, and extra pre- and post-processing capabilities. |\n|[normcap](https://github.com/dynobo/normcap) |X |X |X |GPL v3 |OCR powered screen-capture tool to capture information instead of images. |\n|[gImageReader](https://github.\n\n...\n\ncom/trufanov-nok/scantailor-universal) , [ScanTailor Advanced](https://github.com/4lex4/scantailor-advanced) and [ScanTailor-Experimental](https://github.com/ImageProcessing-ElectronicPublications/scantailor-experimental)\n* [ClearText](https://github.com/ajinkya933/ClearText) \\- An AI-powered text detection and enhancement tool that helps make text in images more readable and clearer. Perfect for improving the legibility of text in scanned documents, photos, and other images.\n\n### A. PDF to Searchable PDF tools\n\n(ie: any tool which can also handle a non-searchable PDF as an input):\n\n1. [OCRmyPDF](https://github.com/jbarlow83/OCRmyPDF) \\- Adds OCR text layer to scanned PDF files and images, allowing them to be searched. Processes pages in parallel on multi-core CPUs. Keeps exact resolution of original embedded images without recompressing JPEGs, when possible. Includes image several preprocessing options, detailed documentation, and support for many exotic PDFs.\n2.\n[pdf2pdfocr](https://github.com/LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr) is a tool to OCR a PDF (or supported images) and add a text layer in the original file making it a searchable PDF. It is a python script that uses tesseract and other open source tools. Linux, macOS and Windows supported.\n3. [pdf2searchablepdf](https://github.com/ElectricRCAircraftGuy/PDF2SearchablePDF) \\- a tool which allows converting any non-searchable PDF, OR any entire directory of images, to a searchable PDF\n\n### B. Others:\n\n1. [Tessract\\_path\\_finder](https://github.com/MrSpideyNihal/Tessract_path_finder) \\- A comprehensive, cross-platform Python utility that automatically discovers Tesseract OCR tessdata directories.\n2. [Docling](https://ds4sd.github.io/docling/) \\- Docling parses documents and exports them to the desired format with ease and speed.\n3. [Scantools](https://gitlab.com/kebekus/scantools) \\- A library and a matching set of command line applications for\ngraphics manipulation, written with a view toward handling scanned documents and generating high-quality PDF files. **image2pdf** converts images to PDF. HOCR files, produced by optical character recognition programs such as Tesseract, can optionally be\n   specified to make the PDF file searchable. The resulting file complies with the ISO PDF/A standard for long-term archiving of digital documents. **hocr2any** converts HOCR files to text, or renders them as raster graphics or PDF files **ocrPDF** uses the Tesseract OCR engine to detect text and generate a text layer in the PDF file.\n4. [ocr-fileformat](https://github.com/UB-Mannheim/ocr-fileformat) \\- Validate and transform between OCR file formats (hOCR, ALTO, PAGE, FineReader)\n5. [Tess4J](https://github.com/nguyenq/tess4j) \\- A Java JNA wrapper for Tesseract OCR API.\n6. [Traineddata inspector](https://mazoea.com/te/traineddata/) \\- to inspect some of the internals of traineddata files\n7. [TopOCR](https://www.topocr."
      ]

      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/pdf2ocr/",
      "title": "pdf2ocr · PyPI",
      "publish_date": "2026-05-04",
      "excerpts": [
        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# pdf2ocr 1.1.4\n\npip install pdf2ocr Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: May 4, 2026\n\nA CLI tool to apply OCR on PDF files and export to multiple formats.\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for rdantas from gravatar.com rdantas\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License:** MIT\n* **Author:** Rafael Dantas\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.6\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/pdf2ocr/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\n[PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/pdf2ocr/) [Coverage Status](https://github.com/rdantassilva/pdf2ocr)\n\n# pdf2ocr\n\nA CLI tool to apply OCR on PDF files and export to multiple formats.\n\n* * *\n\n## 📄 Features\n* 🔍 Extracts text from scanned PDFs using Tesseract OCR with advanced image preprocessing\n* 📘 Outputs DOCX, HTML, EPUB and searchable PDF files with preserved paragraph structure\n* 📚 Converts DOCX to EPUB via Calibre, including metadata\n* 📈 Displays progress bars and detailed summary logs\n* 📂 Supports layout-preserving mode for high-fidelity PDF OCR\n* 🖼️ Advanced image enhancement for improved OCR accuracy on distorted documents\n* ⚡ Intelligent preprocessing with noise reduction, contrast optimization, and text sharpening\n\n* * *\n\n## 🖼️ Advanced Image Processing\n\n`pdf2ocr` includes sophisticated image preprocessing to maximize OCR accuracy, especially for documents with distortions, poor contrast, or noise:\n\n### 🔧 Automatic Enhancements Applied:\n\n1. **Grayscale Conversion** \\- Optimizes images for text recognition\n2. **Auto Contrast** \\- Automatically adjusts contrast for better text visibility\n3. **Noise Reduction** \\- Removes artifacts while preserving text edges\n4.\n**Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE)** \\- Enhances local contrast for varying lighting conditions\n5. **Text Sharpening** \\- Improves character definition and clarity\n6. **Unsharp Masking** \\- Fine-tunes text edges for optimal recognition\n\n### 📊 Benefits:\n\n* ✅ **Better accuracy** on scanned documents with poor quality\n* ✅ **Improved recognition** of faded or low-contrast text\n* ✅ **Enhanced performance** on documents with noise or artifacts\n* ✅ **Automatic fallbacks** ensure processing never fails\n* ✅ **Works with all output formats** (PDF, DOCX, HTML, EPUB)\n* ✅ **Configurable quality** with `--dpi` option (72-1200 range)\n\n### ⚙️ Quality Control:\n\nThe `--dpi` parameter controls the resolution of PDF to image conversion:\n\n* **Low DPI (72-150)** : Faster processing, smaller memory usage, suitable for clean documents\n* **Medium DPI (200-400)** : Balanced quality and performance (default: 400)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://racosa.github.io/pdf2text-ocr/",
      "title": "pdf2text-ocr - GitHub Pages",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# pdf2text-ocr\n\n[pdf2text-ocr](https://racosa.github.io/pdf2text-ocr/) is a\nsimple tool for converting PDF to text using OCR. Files are converted\nlocally in the browser and are never uploaded to external servers.\n\nIt can be useful if you are getting gibberish when copying and pasting\ntext from PDF ( [example](https://superuser.com/questions/137824/pdf-has-garbled-text-when-copy-pasting) ), specially if you don't want to or cannot use a cloud-based solution.\n\nFree and open source: [Github](https://github.com/racosa/pdf2text-ocr)\n\nSelect a language and a PDF file:\n\nChoose file\n\nLoading..."
