        "[Productivity](https://medevel.com/tag/productivity/)\n\n# 13 Best Open Source Free PDF OCR Text Extractors\n\nHamza Musa\n\n#### Hamza Musa\n\n30 Mar 2023 — 9 min read\n\n13 Best Open Source Free PDF OCR Text Extractors Photo by Ingo Stiller / Unsplash\n\nPDF file formats are a compact format widely used to create portable documents, reports, e-books, and more. Originally developed by Adobe in 1992, it has become a world standard.\n\nPDF files can contain text, images, and tables, and can be generated by many office suites, document editors, apps, web services, and more.\n\nMany users may need to extract and edit PDF content, such as text, images, and tables, or extract text highlights and annotations. If you are one of these users, this post is for you.\n\nHowever, if you are looking for a free PDF editor programs, we got you covered in the following post:\n\n[11 Best Free Open Source PDF Editors Some teachers and students require editing their PDF to add annotations, and study notes.\nEditing a PDF file is not an easy task, sometimes because of lack of a proper software. And it gets worse when you want to edit a badly encoded PDF file. While there are many commercial MEDevel.com Hamza Mousa](https://medevel.com/11-os-free-pdf-editors/)\n\nIn this post, we present the best free and open-source PDF OCR solutions. These alternatives can save you the cost of commercial PDF programs while still offering high-quality OCR capabilities.\n\nNote that most of these tools require a fair amount of knowledge on how to run command-line applications.\n\n## 1\\. [OCRmyPDF](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF) : Search your PDFs with ease\n\nOCRmyPDF is a free open-source command-line tool that adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted. It is already being used to scan and search millions of heavy PDF files.\n\n### **Features**\n\n* Generates a searchable PDF/A file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Validates input and output files\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores\n* Uses Tesseract OCR engine to recognize more than 100 languages\n* Keeps your private data private.\n* Scales properly to handle files with thousands of pages.\n\n[GitHub - ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF: OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched - GitHub - ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF: OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched GitHub ocrmypdf](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n\n## 2\\."

        "v5.3.0 Available Now\n\n# The world's best free  \nOCR engine.\n\n**Tesseract OCR is the industry-standard free, open-source Optical Character Recognition engine.** It leverages advanced LSTM neural networks to extract text from images with up to 99%+ accuracy across 100+ languages. Fully offline and secure, it is the foundation of global document analysis, text extraction, and **tesseract ocr download** .\n\nInstall / Download [GitHub](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract) Docs\n\nOriginally developed at HP (1985), sponsored by Google (2006–2018). Trusted by academia, enterprises, and open source developers globally.\n\nGoogle Hewlett-Packard Ubuntu Debian Python\n\n60 k+\n\nGitHub Stars\n\n100 \\+\n\nLanguages Supported\n\n40 yrs\n\nProven Heritage\n\n99 %+\n\nRecognition Accuracy\n\n$0\n\nCost — Forever Free\n\ntesseract\\_demo.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ \\# Extract text from a scanned document\n\n$ tesseract document.tiff outputbase -l eng --psm 3\n\nTesseract Open Source OCR Engine v5.3.0 with Leptonica\n\nPage 1\nEstimating resolution as 300\n\n  \n$ cat outputbase.txt\n\nThe quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.\nThis text was successfully extracted with 99.8% confidence.\n\n## Three Steps to Structured Data.\n\nIntegrate Tesseract into your pipeline to turn arbitrary image data into structured text and searchable PDFs instantly.\n\n01\n\n### Provide Image\n\nPass TIFF, JPEG, PNG, or WebP inputs. Tesseract leverages Leptonica for robust image processing, binarization, and scaling.\n\n02\n\n### Layout Analysis\n\nThe engine segments the page into blocks of text, lines, and words. Choose from 14 Page Segmentation Modes (PSM) for perfect targeting.\n\n03\n\n### Execute LSTM\n\nNeural networks evaluate the character lines continuously. Tesseract supports various output formats: plain text, hOCR (HTML), standard PDF, invisible-text-only PDF, TSV, ALTO, and PAGE.\n\n## 📥 How to install Tesseract OCR\n\nZero to OCR in minutes. Select your operating system below for the official **tesseract ocr download** guide.\nmacOS Ubuntu / Debian Windows\n\nStep 1\n\n### Install Library\n\nTesseract is natively compiled. Install the core CLI engine via Homebrew.\n\nos\\_install.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ brew install tesseract\n\nStep 2\n\n### Download Languages\n\nFetch `.traineddata` files for non-English languages.\n\nlang\\_install.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ brew install tesseract-lang\n\nStep 3\n\n### Test Installation\n\nVerify exactly which version is currently active.\n\ncheck\\_version.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ tesseract --version\n\nStep 1\n\n### Install Library\n\nTesseract is available directly in the apt repository.\n\napt\\_install.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ sudo apt install tesseract-ocr\n\nStep 2\n\n### Download Languages\n\nFetch `.traineddata` files for non-English languages.\n\napt\\_lang.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ sudo apt install tesseract-ocr-all\n\nStep 3\n\n### Test Installation\n\nVerify exactly which version is currently active.\n\nverify\\_tess.sh\n\nCopy\n\n$ tesseract --version\n\nStep 1\n\n### Tesseract OCR Windows Installer\n\nTesseract cannot be natively compiled easily on Windows.\n\n...\n\nThe industry standard in open-source OCR infrastructure and deployment.\n\nReleased under the Apache 2.0 License. Maintained by the open source community.\n\n**Disclaimer:** TesseractOCR.org is an independent, community-driven documentation project and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to the official Tesseract OCR project or its maintainers."

      "publish_date": "2021-11-14",
      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\ninternetarchive / **archive-pdf-tools** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 22\n* Star 141\n\n# internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools\n\nmaster\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[377 Commits](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools/commits/master/)\n\n 377 Commits |\n|.github/ workflows |.github/ workflows | | |\n|bin |bin | | |\n|cython |cython | | |\n|docs |docs | | |\n|internetarchivepdf |internetarchivepdf | | |\n|tools |tools | | |\n|.gitignore |.gitignore | | |\n|.readthedocs.yaml |.readthedocs.yaml | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|LICENSE.txt |LICENSE.txt | | |\n|MANIFEST.in |MANIFEST.in | | |\n|README |README | | |\n|README.rst |README.rst | | |\n|pyproject.toml |pyproject.toml | | |\n|requirements.txt |requirements.txt | | |\n|run-all-tools.sh |run-all-tools.sh | | |\n|setup.cfg |setup.cfg | | |\n|setup.py |setup.py | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* AGPL-3.0 license\n* License\n\n# Internet Archive PDF tools\n\n|Authors: |Merlijn Wajer < merlijn@archive.org > |\n| --- | --- |\n|Date: |2021-11-14 18:00 |\n\nThis repository contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[\\*]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[†]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and\nPDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the \"scandata.\nxml\" files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don't\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\n  offerings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)\n* Creation of 1 bit (black and white) PDFs\n\n...\n\n`mrcview` (tools/mrcview) is shipped with the package and can be used to turn a\nMRC-compressed PDF into a PDF with each layer on a separate page, this is the\neasiest way to inspect the resulting compression. Run it like so:\n\n> mrcview /tmp/compressed.pdf /tmp/mrc.pdf\n\nThere is also `maskview` , which just renders the masks of a PDF to another PDF.\n\nAlternatively, one could use `pdfimages` to extract the image layers of a\nspecific page and then view them with your favourite image viewer:\n\n```\npageno=0; pdfimages -f $pageno -l $pageno -png path_to_pdf extracted_image_base\nfeh extracted_image_base*.png\n```\n\ntools/pdfimagesmrc can be used to check how the size of the PDF\nis broken down into the foreground, background, masks and text layer.\n\n## License\n\nLicense for all code (minus `internetarchivepdf/pdfrenderer.py` ) is AGPL "
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest",
      "title": "Internet Archive PDF Tools — archive-pdf-tools 1.2.0 documentation",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# Internet Archive PDF Tools ¶\n\nThis project contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[ \\* ]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[ † ]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and PDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the “scandata.xml” files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don’t\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features ¶\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\n  offerings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)\n* Creation of 1 bit (black and white) PDFs\n\n## Dependencies ¶\n\n* Python 3.x\n* See requirements.txt in the source repo\n\nOne-of:\n\n* [Kakadu JPEG2000 binaries](https://kakadusoftware.com/)\n* Pillow\n* Open source OpenJPEG2000 tools (opj\\_compress and opj\\_decompress)\n* [Grok](https://github.com/GrokImageCompression/grok/) (grk\\_compress and grk\\_decompress)\n* [jpegoptim](https://github.\ncom/tjko/jpegoptim) (when using JPEG instead of JPEG2000)\n\nFor JBIG2 compression:\n\n* [jbig2enc](https://github.com/agl/jbig2enc) for JBIG2 compression (and PyMuPDF 1.19.0 or higher)\n\n## Installation ¶\n\nFirst install dependencies. For example, in Ubuntu:\n\n```\nsudo apt install libleptonica - dev libopenjp2 - tools libxml2 - dev libxslt - dev python3 - dev python3 - pip \n git clone https : // github . com / agl / jbig2enc \n cd jbig2enc \n ./ autogen . sh \n ./ configure && make \n sudo make install\n```\n\nBecause archive-pdf-tools is on the [Python Package Index](https://pypi.org/project/archive-pdf-tools/) (PyPI), you can use pip (the\nPython 3 version is often called pip3 ) to install the latest version:\n\n```\n# Latest version \n pip3 install archive - pdf - tools \n\n # Specific version \n pip3 install archive - pdf - tools == 1.4.14\n```\n\nAlternatively, if you want a specific commit or unreleased version, check out\nthe master branch or a [tagged release](https://github.\n\n...\n\n- o / tmp / example . pdf \n [ ... ] \n Processed 9 pages at 1.16 seconds / page \n Compression ratio : 7.144962\n```\n\nOr, to scan a document, OCR it with Tesseract and save the result as a compressed PDF\n(JPEG2000 compression with OpenJPEG, background downsampled three times), with\ntext layer:\n\n```\nscanimage -- resolution 300 -- mode Color -- format tiff | tee / tmp / scan . tiff | tesseract - - hocr > / tmp / scan . hocr ; recode_pdf - v - J openjpeg -- bg - downsample 3 -- from - imagestack / tmp / scan . tiff -- hocr - file / tmp / scan . hocr - o / tmp / scan . pdf \n [ ... ] \n Processed 1 pages at 11.40 seconds / page \n Compression ratio : 249.876613\n```\n\n### Examining the results ¶\n\n`mrcview` (tools/mrcview) is shipped with the package and can be used to turn a\nMRC-compressed PDF into a PDF with each layer on a separate page, this is the\neasiest way to inspect the resulting compression. Run it li"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_modules/internetarchivepdf/mrc.html",
      "title": "internetarchivepdf.mrc — archive-pdf-tools 1.2.0 documentation",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "### Navigation\n\n* [index](https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/genindex.html \"General Index\")\n* [modules](https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/py-modindex.html \"Python Module Index\") |\n* [archive-pdf-tools 1.2.0 documentation](https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) »\n* [Module code](https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/%5Fmodules/index.html) »\n* internetarchivepdf.mrc\n\n# Source code for internetarchivepdf.mrc\n\n\n# archive-pdf-tools\n# Copyright (C) 2020-2021, Internet Archive\n#\n# This program is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\n# it under the terms of the GNU Affero General Public License as published by\n# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\n# (at your option) any later version.\n#\n# This program is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.\nSee the\n# GNU Affero General Public License for more details.\n#\n# You should have received a copy of the GNU Affero General Public License\n# along with this program.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n#\n# Author: Merlijn Boris Wolf Wajer <merlijn@archive.org>\n\nimport sys\nfrom os import close, remove\n\nfrom glob import glob\nfrom tempfile import mkstemp\nimport subprocess\nfrom time import time\n\nimport warnings\n\nfrom PIL import Image, ImageOps\nfrom skimage.filters import threshold_local, threshold_otsu\nfrom skimage.restoration import denoise_tv_bregman, estimate_sigma\n\nfrom scipy import ndimage\nimport numpy as np\n\nfrom optimiser import optimise_gray, optimise_rgb, optimise_gray2, optimise_rgb2, fast_mask_denoise\nfrom sauvola import binarise_sauvola\n\nimport fitz\n\nfitz.TOOLS.set_icc(True) # For good measure, not required\n\nfrom internetarchivepdf.jpeg2000 import encode_jpeg2000\nfrom internetarchivepdf.const import (RECODE_RUNTIME_WARNING_TOO_SMALL_TO_DOWNSAMPLE, COMPRESSOR_JPEG,\nCOMPRESSOR_JPEG2000, DENOISE_NONE, DENOISE_FAST, DENOISE_BREGMAN)\n\n\n\"\"\"\n\"\"\"\n\n# skimage throws useless UserWarnings in various functions\ndef mean_estimate_sigma(arr):\n    with warnings.catch_warnings():\n        warnings.simplefilter('ignore')\n        return np.mean(estimate_sigma(arr))\n\n\n[[docs]](https://archive-pdf-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/mrc.html#internetarchivepdf.mrc.threshold%5Fimage)\ndef threshold_image(img, dpi, k=0.34):\n    \"\"\"\n    Perform Sauvola binarisation on the given image\n\n    Args:\n\n    * img (np.ndarray): input image array\n    * dpi (int): dpi for Sauvola, used to calculate window size if not None\n    * k (float): k parameter, defaults to 0.34\n\n    Returns binarised numpy.ndarray\n    \"\"\"\n    window_size = 51\n\n    if dpi is not None:\n        window_size = int(dpi / 4)\n        if window_size % 2 == 0:\n            window_size += 1\n\n    h, w = img.shape\n    out_img = np.ndarray(img.shape, dtype=bool)\n    out_img = np.reshape(out_img, w*h)\n    in_img = np."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://archive.org/developers/ocr.html",
      "title": "OCR at the Internet Archive with Tesseract and hOCR",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Back to top\n\n`Ctrl` \\+ `K`\n\n* .rst\n* .pdf\n\n# OCR at the Internet Archive with Tesseract and hOCR \\#\n\nauthors :\n    Merlijn Wajer < merlijn @ archive . org >\ndate :\n    2020-01-29\nlast-updated :\n    2023-02-23\nversion :\n    1\\.2\n\n## Introduction \\#\n\nThis document outlines the OCR (Optical Character Recognition) module and its\nfeatures as used to perform optical text recognition on Internet Archive items\nand elaborates on design decisions and how various solutions were picked.\n\n## Motivation \\#\n\nThe Internet Archive had been using proprietary OCR technology for many years,\nbut decided to move to an entirely open source stack after evaluating the\nvarious open source software OCR offerings, settling on [Tesseract](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr) but keeping an eye out for alternative\nengines.\n\nThis transition to Tesseract was completed near the end of 2020.\n\n### OCR format \\#\n\nThere are a few open standards when it comes to defining OCR results, with the\nmain contenders being:\n* [hOCR](https://kba.github.io/hocr-spec/1.2/)\n* [PAGE XML](https://github.com/PRImA-Research-Lab/PAGE-XML)\n* [ALTO XML](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ALTO_\\(XML\\))\n\nThe Internet Archive settled on using [hOCR](https://kba.github.io/hocr-spec/1.2/) . At the time of writing, Tesseract\ndoes support outputting ALTO XML, but PAGE XML was not yet supported. hOCR was\ndeemed sufficiently simple and flexible, with the added advantage that it is\nXHTML, which allows for viewing the documents in a browser. Various hOCR tools\nand libraries exist, as do hOCR viewers, such as [hocrviewer-miradoc](https://github.com/jbaiter/hocrviewer-mirador) and [hocrjs](https://github.com/kba/hocrjs) .\n\nWe have also created our own tooling to work on (large) hOCR files, as some of\nthe existing tooling ran out of memory rather quickly; see [archive-hocr-tools](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-hocr-tools) ( [documentation hosted here](https://archive-hocr-tools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) ).\nWe intend to keep around the older (pre-tesseract) OCR results, but will attempt\nto convert them to hOCR as well, providing a hOCR file for each item with OCR\nresults, no matter the OCR engine. The code to convert those files can also be\nfound in [archive-hocr-tools](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-hocr-tools) .\n\n## Basic workflow \\#\n\nAfter an [Internet Archive Item](https://archive.org/services/docs/api/items.html) has been\nuploaded, various processes kick in to analyze the content and provide\nderivative files, one of those being the OCR file. The output OCR format was changed from the old proprietary format to hOCR, as explained earlier.\n\n### hOCR files \\#\n\nBarring any failures in the OCR process, after upload, every item will get one\nor more `*_hocr.html` files which represent the results of OCR jobs. Each `*_hocr.html` file contains results for all pages in one set of images (book,\nPDF, or otherwise), with text, bounding boxes, and confidence at the word level.\n\n...\n\nprevious Reviews API next PDF analysis, generation and compression at the Internet Archive\n\nBy Internet Archive"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://libraries.io/pypi/archive-pdf-tools",
      "title": "archive-pdf-tools 1.4.29 on PyPI - Libraries.io - security ...",
      "publish_date": "2021-11-14",
      "excerpts": [
        "**[Big news!](https://blog.tidelift.com/tidelift-joins-sonar?utm_source=libraries&utm_medium=referral) Sonar has entered a definitive agreement to acquire Tidelift!**\n\nToggle navigation \n\n* \n\n    + GitHub\n    + GitLab\n    + Bitbucket\n    + By logging in you accept  \n        our terms of service  \n        and privacy policy\n\n# archive-pdf-tools  \nRelease\n1\\.4.32\n\n# Release 1.4.32 Toggle Dropdown\n\n* 1\\.5.1\n* 1\\.4.16\n* 1\\.5.2\n* 1\\.4.17\n* 1\\.4.18\n* 1\\.5.3\n* 1\\.4.20\n* 1\\.4.21\n* 1\\.5.4\n* 1\\.4.22\n\nInternet Archive PDF compression tools\n\n[Homepage](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/archive-pdf-tools/1.4.32/) Python\n\n* * *\n\nKeywords License\n    AGPL-3.0\nInstall\n    `pip install archive-pdf-tools==1.4.32`\n\n* * *\n\n### Documentation\n\n# Internet Archive PDF tools\n\n|Authors: |Merlijn Wajer < merlijn@archive.org > |\n| --- | --- |\n|Date: |2021-11-14 18:00 |\n\nThis repository contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[\\*]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[†]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and\nPDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the \"scandata.xml\" files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don't\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\nofferings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)\n* Creation of 1 bit (black and white) PDFs\n\n## Dependencies\n\n* Python 3.x\n* Python packages (also see requirements.txt):\n        + PyMuPDF\n    + lxml\n    + scikit-image\n    + Pillow\n    + roman\n    + [archive-hocr-tools](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-hocr-tools)\n\nOne-of:\n\n* [Kakadu JPEG2000 binaries](https://kakadusoftware.com/)\n* Open source OpenJPEG2000 tools (opj\\_compress and opj\\_decompress)\n* [Grok](https://github.com/GrokImageCompression/grok/) (grk\\_compress and grk\\_decompress)"
      ]
    },

      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/archive-pdf-tools/",
      "title": "archive-pdf-tools · PyPI",
      "publish_date": "2025-08-20",
      "excerpts": [
        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# archive-pdf-tools 1.5.7\n\npip install archive-pdf-tools Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: Aug 20, 2025\n\nInternet Archive PDF compression tools\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for merlijn from gravatar.com merlijn\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Homepage](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools)\n* [Download](https://github.com/internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools/archive/1.5.7.tar.gz)\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License:** GNU Affero General Public License v3 (AGPL-3.0)\n* **Author:** Merlijn Boris Wolf Wajer\n* Tags PDF , MRC , hOCR , Internet Archive\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.6\n* **Provides-Extra:** `docs`\n\n###### Classifiers\n\n* **Development Status**\n  \n    + 3 - Alpha\n* **Intended Audience**\n+ Developers\n* **License**\n  \n    + OSI Approved :: GNU Affero General Public License v3\n* **Programming Language**\n  \n    + Python :: 3\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/archive-pdf-tools/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\nAuthors :\n    Merlijn Wajer < merlijn @ archive . org >\nDate :\n    2021-11-14 18:00\n\nThis repository contains a library to perform MRC (Mixed Raster Content)\ncompression on images [[ \\* ]]() , which offers lossy high compression of images, in\nparticular images with text.\n\nAdditionally, the library can generate MRC-compressed PDF files with hOCR [[ † ]]() text layers mixed into to the PDF, which makes searching and copy-pasting of the\nPDF possible. PDFs generated by bin/recode\\_pdf should be PDF/A 3b and PDF/UA compatible.\n\nSome of the tooling also supports specific Internet Archive file formats (such\nas the “scandata.\nxml” files, but the tooling should work fine without those\nfiles, too.\n\nWhile the code is already being used internally to create PDFs at the Internet\nArchive, the code still needs more documentation and cleaning up, so don’t\nexpect this to be super well documented just yet.\n\n## Features\n\n* Reliable: has produced over 6 million PDFs in 2021 alone (each with many\n  hundreds of pages)\n* Fast and robust compression: Competes directly with the proprietary software\n  offerings when it comes to speed and compressibility (often outperforming in\n  both)\n* MRC compression of images, leading to anywhere from 3-15x compression ratios,\n  depending on the quality setting provided.\n* Creates PDF from a directory of images\n* Improved compression based on OCR results (hOCR files)\n* Hidden text layer insertion based on hOCR files, which makes a PDF searchable\n  and the text copy-pasteable.\n* PDF/A 3b compatible.\n* Basic PDF/UA support (accessibility features)"

      "url": "https://github.com/Mobasheera/SearchablePDF",
      "title": "GitHub - Mobasheera/SearchablePDF: Convert scanned PDFs into fully searchable, text-layered documents using open-source tools. A complete Windows 11 workflow with Tesseract OCR and OCRmyPDF for fast, accurate results. · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nMobasheera / **SearchablePDF** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 0\n* Star 2\n\n# Mobasheera/SearchablePDF\n\nmain\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[2 Commits](https://github.com/Mobasheera/SearchablePDF/commits/main/)\n\n 2 Commits |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* MIT license\n\n# SearchablePDF\n\nConvert scanned PDFs into fully searchable, text-layered documents using open-source tools.  \nA complete Windows 11 workflow with **Tesseract OCR** and **OCRmyPDF** for fast, accurate results.\n\n* * *\n\n## 📌 Features\n\n...\n\nIf your PDF already has a text layer but you want to **replace** it:\n\n```\nocrmypdf -- force - ocr - l eng \" C:\\Users\\YourName\\Downloads\\1.pdf \" \" C:\\Users\\YourName\\Downloads\\output.pdf \"\n```\n\n* * *\n\n## 🌍 Language Support\n\n* Download additional Tesseract language packs from the installer or manually from:\n  👉 <https://tesseract-ocr.github.io/tessdoc/Data-Files.html>\n* Use multiple languages by separating with `+` :\n\n```\nocrmypdf - l eng + fra input.pdf output.pdf\n```\n\n* * *\n\n## 🛠 Troubleshooting\n\n`tesseract : The term 'tesseract' is not recognized`\n\n* Tesseract is not in your PATH. Follow Step 1.4 above.\n\n`PriorOcrFoundError: page already has text!`\n\n* Use `--force-ocr` to replace existing text.\n\n### OCRmyPDF runs but text is inaccurate\n\n* Use higher-quality scans (300 DPI recommended).\n* Try installing additional language packs for better recognition.\n\n* * *\n\n## 📜 License\n\nThis project is licensed under the MIT License .\n\n* * *\n\n## About\nConvert scanned PDFs into fully searchable, text-layered documents using open-source tools. A complete Windows 11 workflow with Tesseract OCR and OCRmyPDF for fast, accurate results.\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nMIT license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**2** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\nNo releases published\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Footer"
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF",
      "title": "GitHub - ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF: OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "pre-commit-config.yaml | | |\n|.readthedocs.yaml |.readthedocs.yaml | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|README\\_ZH.md |README\\_ZH.md | | |\n|REUSE.toml |REUSE.toml | | |\n|pyproject.toml |pyproject.toml | | |\n|snapcraft.yaml |snapcraft.yaml | | |\n|uv.lock |uv.lock | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* Contributing\n* MPL-2.0 license\n\nOCRmyPDF\n\n[Build Status](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/actions/workflows/build.yml) [PyPI version](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/) [Homebrew version](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/c77bc15e95c6f2333ec096806d0eb2fb4008f71576ff066602d84d308c3dc93d/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f686f6d65627265772f762f6f63726d797064662e737667) [ReadTheDocs](https://camo.githubusercontent.\ncom/6ab9ef6df0c5144bb402ef9318e32ffe87e1ab35b2d3a9f8755d2c248a1e0bc2/68747470733a2f2f72656164746865646f63732e6f72672f70726f6a656374732f6f63726d797064662f62616467652f3f76657273696f6e3d6c6174657374) [Python versions](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/0cb43ab967c987d2ff0a625c849aab4a820f2094748f7299ed9821d5cd849fee/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f707970692f707976657273696f6e732f6f63726d79706466)\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted.\n\n```\nocrmypdf                      #  it's a scriptable command line program \n   -l eng+fra                 #  it supports multiple languages \n   --rotate-pages             #  it can fix pages that are misrotated \n   --deskew                   #  it can deskew crooked PDFs! \n   --title \" My PDF \"           #  it can change output metadata \n   --jobs 4                   #  it uses multiple cores by default \n   --output-type pdfa         #  it produces PDF/A by default\ninput_scanned.pdf          #  takes PDF input (or images) \n   output_searchable.pdf      #  produces validated PDF output\n```\n\n[See the release notes for details on the latest changes](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/release_notes.html) .\n\n## Main features\n\n* Generates a searchable [PDF/A](https://en.wikipedia.org/?title=PDF/A) file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Validates input and output files\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores\n* Uses [Tesseract OCR](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract) engine to recognize more than [100 languages](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tessdata)"
      ]

      "title": "Free Java OCR Library for TIFF, JPEG, GIF, PNG, & BMP Image ...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "1. [Products](https://products.fileformat.com/)\n2. [OCR](https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/)\n3. [Java](https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/java/)\n4. Tess4J\n\n# Free Java Library for OCR Text Extraction & Document Analysis\n\n## Open Source Java OCR Library for Incorporating OCR capabilities into Java apps and allows Extracting Text from Images as well as Scanned Documents.\n\nFor software developers seeking a powerful and accessible Java OCR API, Tess4J emerges as a premier open source Java OCR library that seamlessly integrates the renowned Tesseract engine into Java applications. This robust solution is specifically designed to perform OCR operations on JPEG, BMP & PNG files, as well as a wide range of other image formats. It empowers applications to recognize text from images with high accuracy, converting static pictures into machine-readable data.\nThis capability is fundamental for automating data entry, digitizing archives, and building intelligent systems that require Java optical character recognition to process scanned documents and digital images efficiently.\n\nA key strength of this free Java OCR library is its extensive format support and versatility. Developers can leverage its straightforward API to extract text from PNG images, perform OCR operations on GIF files, and handle numerous other formats with ease. Built as a Java wrapper for Google's Tesseract engine, Tess4J provides exceptional cross-platform compatibility and supports advanced features like image preprocessing and multi-language recognition to ensure reliable text extraction from even low-quality scans. This makes it an indispensable tool for any project that requires accurate and efficient conversion of visual information into actionable textual data.\n\n* At a Glance\n* Platform Independence\n* Supported File Formats\n\n### At A Glance\nAn overview of Tess4J features.\n\nFeatures Overview\n\n* Perform OCR\n* Add OCR Capabilities\n* Recognize Image text\n* Convet images of text\n* Recognized Font text\n* Searc PDF\n* Over 100 Languages\n* Create OCR apps\n* Save to browser\n* Extract Text\n* Multi-threading Support\n\nTess4J\n\nTess4J supports popular image file formats listed below.\n\nReader\n\n* [PNG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/png/) , [JPEG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/jpeg/) , [BMP](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/bmp/) , [TIFF](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/tiff/) , TGA, DICOM\n\nWriter\n\n* [PNG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/png/) , [JPEG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/jpeg/) , [BMP](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/bmp/) , [TIFF](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/tiff/)\n\nTess4J\n\n### Platform Independence\n\nTess4J can work with any Java-based programming language\n\n* Java\n\nTess4J\n\nPrevious Next\n\n## Getting Started with Tess4J\n\nThe recommend way to install Tess4J is using Maven.\nPlease use the following command for a smooth installation.\n\n### **Maven Dependency for Tess4J**\n\n```\n<dependencies>\n\t<dependency>\n\t<groupId>net.sourceforge.tess4j</groupId>\n\t<artifactId>tess4j;/artifactId>\n\t<version>X.X.X</version>\n    </dependency>\n</dependencies>\n```\n\n### Install Tess4J via GitHub\n\n```\n git clone https://github.com/nguyenq/tess4j.git\n```\n\nYou can also install it manually; download the latest release files directly from [GitHub](https://github.com/nguyenq/tess4j) repository.\n\n## Content Extraction via Java API\n\nThe open source Tess4J library allows software developers to extract text from various types of images inside Java applications. The library enables the extraction of text from images, enabling applications to analyze and process the textual content. This capability finds applications in areas such as sentiment analysis, text summarization, and information retrieval."
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF",
      "title": "GitHub - ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF: OCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "* Keeps your private data private.\n* Scales properly to handle files with thousands of pages.\n* Battle-tested on millions of PDFs.\n\nDemo of OCRmyPDF in a terminal session\n\nFor details: please consult the [documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) .\n\n## Motivation\n\nI searched the web for a free command line tool to OCR PDF files: I found many, but none of them were really satisfying:\n\n* Either they produced PDF files with misplaced text under the image (making copy/paste impossible)\n* Or they did not handle accents and multilingual characters\n* Or they changed the resolution of the embedded images\n* Or they generated ridiculously large PDF files\n* Or they crashed when trying to OCR\n* Or they did not produce valid PDF files\n* On top of that none of them produced PDF/A files (format dedicated for long time storage)\n\n...so I decided to develop my own tool.\n\n## Installation\n\nLinux, Windows, macOS and FreeBSD are supported.\n\n...\n\n#  OCR multilingual documents \nocrmypdf -l eng+fra Bilingual-English-French.pdf Bilingual-English-French.pdf\n\n #  Deskew (straighten crooked pages) \nocrmypdf --deskew input.pdf output.pdf\n```\n\nFor more features, see the [documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/index.html) .\n\n## Requirements\n\nIn addition to the required Python version, OCRmyPDF requires external program installations of Ghostscript and Tesseract OCR. OCRmyPDF is pure Python, and runs on pretty much everything: Linux, macOS, Windows and FreeBSD.\n\n## Plugins\n\nOCRmyPDF provides a plugin interface allowing its capabilities to be extended or replaced. Here are some plugins we are aware of:\n\n* [OCRmyPDF-AppleOCR](https://github.com/mkyt/ocrmypdf-AppleOCR) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with Apple Vision Framework. Requires macOS.\n* [OCRmyPDF-EasyOCR](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF-EasyOCR) : replaces the standard Tesseract OCR engine with EasyOCR, a newer OCR engine based on PyTorch.\n\n...\n\nde/ratgeber/Durchsuchbare-PDF-Dokumente-mit-OCRmyPDF-erstellen-4607592.html)\n* [Excellent Utilities: OCRmyPDF](https://www.linuxlinks.com/excellent-utilities-ocrmypdf-add-ocr-text-layer-scanned-pdfs/)\n* [LinuxUser Texterkennung mit OCRmyPDF und Scanbd automatisieren](https://www.linux-community.de/ausgaben/linuxuser/2021/06/texterkennung-mit-ocrmypdf-und-scanbd-automatisieren/)\n* [Y Combinator discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=32028752)\n\n## Business enquiries\n\nOCRmyPDF would not be the software that it is today without companies and users choosing to provide support for feature development and consulting enquiries. We are happy to discuss all enquiries, whether for extending the existing feature set, or integrating OCRmyPDF into a larger system.\n\n## License\n\nThe OCRmyPDF software is licensed under the Mozilla Public License 2.0 (MPL-2.0).\nThis license permits integration of OCRmyPDF with other code, included commercial and closed source, but asks you to publish source-level modifications you make to OCRmyPDF.\n\nSome components of OCRmyPDF have other licenses, as indicated by standard SPDX license identifiers or the DEP5 copyright and licensing information file. Generally speaking, non-core code is licensed under MIT, and the documentation and test files are licensed under Creative Commons ShareAlike 4.0 (CC-BY-SA 4.0).\n\n## Disclaimer\n\nThe software is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\n\n## About\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched\n\n[ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/](http://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/ \"http://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/\")\n\n### Topics\n\npython pdf ocr image-processing tesseract\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nMPL-2.0 license\n\n### Contributing\n\nContributing\n\nActiv"

        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nSearch\n\nClear\n\n[Search syntax tips](https://docs.github.com/search-github/github-code-search/understanding-github-code-search-syntax)\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nYou signed in with another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You signed out in another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. You switched accounts on another tab or window. Reload to refresh your session. Dismiss alert\n\nsenurah / **java-tess4J-ocr** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 1\n* Star 6\n\n## Files Expand file tree\n\nmain\n\n## Breadcrumbs\n\n1. java-tess4J-ocr\n\n/\n\n# README.md\n\nCopy path\n\nBlame More file actions\n\nBlame More file actions\n\n## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[History](https://github.com/senurah/java-tess4J-ocr/commits/main/README.md)\n\n History\n\n129 lines (87 loc) · 3.85 KB\n\nmain\n\n## Breadcrumbs\n\n1. java-tess4J-ocr\n\n/\n# README.md\n\nTop\n\n## File metadata and controls\n\n* Preview\n* Code\n* Blame\n\n129 lines (87 loc) · 3.85 KB\n\n[Raw](https://github.com/senurah/java-tess4J-ocr/raw/refs/heads/main/README.md)\n\nCopy raw file\n\nDownload raw file\n\nOutline\n\nEdit and raw actions\n\n# Java OCR CLI Tool using Tess4J\n\nThis project is a **Command-Line Interface (CLI)** application for extracting text from images and PDFs using the **Tess4J** library, which provides Tesseract-based Optical Character Recognition (OCR) functionality. The extracted text can be copied to the clipboard for easy use.\n\n## Features\n\n* Extract text from images (PNG, JPG, etc.)\n* Extract text from PDFs\n* Supports multiple languages (e.g., English, Sinhala)\n* Automatically copies extracted text to the clipboard\n\n## Prerequisites\n\nBefore you begin, ensure you have the following installed:\n\n* **Java 17** or higher\n* **Maven** (for building the project)\n* **Tesseract-OCR** trained data files ( `.traineddata` files) for the required languages.\nPlace these files in the `tessdata/` directory.\n\n## Installation\n\n#### 1\\. Clone the Repository\n\n```\ngit clone https://github.com/senurah/java-tess4J-ocr.git\n cd java-tess4J-ocr\n```\n\n#### 2\\. Download and Setup Tess4J\n\nEnsure that the `tessdata` folder contains the correct `.traineddata` files for the languages you need (e.g., English, Sinhala).\n\n#### 3\\. Build the Project\n\n```\nmvn clean package\n```\n\nThis will create a JAR file in the `target/` directory.\n\n#### 4\\. **Configure Tesseract Data Path**\n\nIn the `OCRProcessor.java` file, you need to set the correct path to the `tessdata` directory, which contains the trained language data files.\n\nUpdate the following line:\n\n```\nthis . tesseract . setDatapath ( \"C:/Users/YourUsername/Project/java-ocr-tess4j/tessdata\" );\n```\n\nReplace the path with the location of your `tessdata` folder. For example:\n\n* For Windows: `C:/Users/YourUsername/Project/tessdata`\n* For Linux/Mac: `/home/YourUsername/Project/tessdata`\nThis step is crucial to ensure that the correct language data is used for OCR.\n\n### Running the Application\n\nTo run the application, use the following command:\n\n```\njava -jar target/java-ocr-tess4j-1.0-SNAPSHOT-shaded.jar < image_or_pdf_path > < language >\n```\n\n* `<image_or_pdf_path>` : Path to the image or PDF file you want to process.\n* `<language>` : Language for OCR (e.g., `eng` for English, `sin` for Sinhala).\n\nExample:\n\n```\njava -jar target/java-ocr-tess4j-1.0-SNAPSHOT-shaded.jar ./src/images/testocr.png eng\n```\n\nThis will extract the text from the image and copy it to the clipboard.\n\n## Project Structure\n\nThe project structure is as follows:\n\n```\n.\n├── src\n│   ├── main\n│   │   ├── java\n│   │   │   └── ocr\n│   │   │       ├── ReadImagesCLI.java\n│   │   │       ├── OCRProcessor.java\n│   "

        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# ocrmypdf 17.7.0\n\npip install ocrmypdf Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: Jun 18, 2026\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Changelog](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/tree/main/docs/releasenotes)\n* [Source](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n* [Tracker](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/issues)\n\n###### GitHub Statistics\n\n* [**Repository**](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n* **Stars:**\n* **Forks:**\n* **Open issues:**\n* **Open PRs:**\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for jrbarlow from gravatar.com jrbarlow\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Documentation](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/)\n\n###### Meta\n* **License Expression:** MPL-2.0  \n  _[SPDX](https://spdx.org/licenses/) [License Expression](https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/annexes/spdx-license-expressions/)_\n* **Author:** James R. Barlow\n* Tags OCR , PDF , PDF/A , optical character recognition , scanning\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.11\n* **Provides-Extra:** `watcher` , `webservice`\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\nOCRmyPDF\n\n[Build Status](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/actions/workflows/build.yml) [PyPI version](https://pypi.org/project/ocrmypdf/) Homebrew version ReadTheDocs Python versions\n\nOCRmyPDF adds an OCR text layer to scanned PDF files, allowing them to be searched or copy-pasted.\n\n```\nocrmypdf # it's a scriptable command line program \n -l eng+fra # it supports multiple languages \n --rotate-pages # it can fix pages that are misrotated\n--deskew # it can deskew crooked PDFs! \n --title \"My PDF\" # it can change output metadata \n --jobs 4 # it uses multiple cores by default \n --output-type pdfa # it produces PDF/A by default \n input_scanned.pdf # takes PDF input (or images) \n output_searchable.pdf # produces validated PDF output\n```\n\n[See the release notes for details on the latest changes](https://ocrmypdf.readthedocs.io/en/latest/release_notes.html) .\n\n##  Main features\n\n* Generates a searchable [PDF/A](https://en.wikipedia.org/?title=PDF/A) file from a regular PDF\n* Places OCR text accurately below the image to ease copy / paste\n* Keeps the exact resolution of the original embedded images\n* When possible, inserts OCR information as a \"lossless\" operation without disrupting any other content\n* Optimizes PDF images, often producing files smaller than the input file\n* If requested, deskews and/or cleans the image before performing OCR\n* Validates input and output files\n* Distributes work across all available CPU cores"

Extract, analyze, and interact with documents using AI capabilities.\n\n* * *\n\n## Overview\n\n`docling-pdf-processor` is a cutting-edge PDF processing solution that integrates multiple AI-powered tools to extract, analyze, and interact with PDF documents in an intuitive and user-friendly manner.\n\n### Key Features:\n\n* **Document Processing** : Powered by **IBM Docling** , the platform processes PDF files, extracting structured data, tables, and text content.\n* **Data Structuring** : **LlamaIndex** is used to organize the extracted data, making it easier to search, analyze, and manipulate.\n* **User Interface** : Built with **Streamlit** , the platform provides a robust and easy-to-use web interface for interacting with documents. Users can upload, view, and analyze PDF files, as well as explore extracted content and document metrics.\n\n### How It Works:\n\n1. **Upload PDF** : Users upload PDF files to the platform for processing.\n2. **Extract and Analyze** : **IBM Docling** is used to extract text, tables, and images, and apply advanced analysis such as OCR (Optical Character Recognition).\n3. **Structure Data** : The extracted data is organized with **LlamaIndex** , which makes it easy to query and analyze.\n4. **Interactive UI** : Users interact with the document and extracted data via a **Streamlit** interface. The platform visualizes text content, tables, images, and document analysis metrics for easy exploration.\n\n### Setup\nTo get started with the project, follow these steps:\n\n1. **Clone the repository** :\n   \n   ```\n   git clone https://github.com/AhmedNazeh2/docling-pdf-processor.git\n    cd docling-pdf-processor\n   ```\n\n## About\n\nNo description, website, or topics provided.\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nMIT license\n\n### Uh oh!\n\nThere was an error while loading. semantic search across all document types\n\n* * *\n\n## 🔗 Additional Resources\n\n* **Docling Documentation** : <https://docling-project.github.io/docling/>\n* **Docling GitHub** : [https://docling-project.github.\nio/docling/examples/minimal\\_asr\\_pipeline/](https://docling-project.github.io/docling/examples/minimal_asr_pipeline/)\n* **Koa Research Group** : <https://koaresearch.group>\n\n* * *\n\n## About\n\nDocling is an open-source library for processing a wide range of document formats, including PDFs, Word documents (DOCX), Excel spreadsheets (XLSX), PowerPoint presentations (PPTX), and even scanned images with OCR capabilities. Its primary function is to extract structured data such as text, tables, figures, and metadata from these documents\n\n[KoaResearch.Group](https://KoaResearch.Group \"https://KoaResearch.Group\")\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\nActivity\n\nCustom properties\n\n### Stars\n\n**1** star\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 100\\.0%\n\n## Footer"

        "IBM announced a managed SaaS - [try Docling for IBM watsonx for free!](https://www.ibm.com/account/reg/us-en/signup?formid=urx-54322&utm_source=docling.ai&utm_medium=banner&utm_campaign=free_trial&utm_content=site_banner)\n\n### Docling converts messy documents into structured data and simplifies downstream document and AI processing by detecting tables, formulas, reading order, OCR, and much more.\n\n[GitHub](https://docling-project.github.io/docling) [HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/docling-project) Slack [LinkedIn](https://linkedin.com/company/docling) [YouTube](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLt0drfpBaTa1ywCtPwJGLYg-t0UmxhQP4)\n\n### Read all about it\n\nDocling for IBM watsonx: A Managed Service, Built on Open Source ##### Docling for IBM watsonx: A Managed Service, Built on Open Source Jun 15, 2026 All blog posts\n[Moving Beyond Sparse Grounding with Complete Screen Parsing Supervision ##### Moving Beyond Sparse Grounding with Complete Screen Parsing Supervision](https://scholar.google.com/citations?view_op=view_citation&hl=en&user=sPuvIfgAAAAJ&cstart=20&pagesize=80&citation_for_view=sPuvIfgAAAAJ:9vf0nzSNQJEC) All papers\n\n### Start\n\n**Install** Docling as a [Python library](https://pypi.org/project/docling) with your favorite package manager:\n\n```\npip install docling\n```\n\n**Run** the CLI directly from your terminal:\n\n```\ndocling https://arxiv.org/pdf/2206.01062\n```\n\n**Code** a document conversion as part of a Python application:\n\n```\nfrom docling.document_converter import DocumentConverter\n                        \n                            source = \"https://arxiv.org/pdf/2408.09869\"\n                            converter = DocumentConverter()\n                            doc = converter.convert(source).document\n                            print(doc.export_to_markdown())\n```\n**Deploy** it as [Docling Serve](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-serve)\n\n**Enable** an agent via [Docling MCP](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-mcp)\n\n### Features\n\n**Import** many document formats into a unified and structured [Docling Document](https://docling-project.github.io/docling/concepts/docling_document) , including scanned pages via an OCR engine of your choice.\n\n**Export** a parsed document to formats that simplify processing and ingestion into AI, RAG, and agentic systems.\n\n**Extract** document components and their properties from the Docling Document.\n\n|Rich |Markup |Tabular |Image |Audio |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Import |PDF |Markdown |XLSX |PNG |MP3 |\n|DOCX |HTML |CSV |JPEG |WAV |\n|PPTX |AsciiDoc |TIFF |\n|WebVTT |BMP |\n|WEBP |\n|Export |JSON |Text |\n|Doctags |Markdown |\n|HTML |\n|Extract |Page |Component |\n|Text |Table |Picture |\n|Image |Header |Structure |Image |\n|Number |Paragraph |Cell |Class |\n|Header |List item |Description |"

    {
      "url": "https://idp-software.com/vendors/docling",
      "title": "Docling: IBM Open-Source Document Processing",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Staar identifies two forward priorities: schema-driven structured extraction and agentic document generation. His framing confirms the repositioning is deliberate, not feature drift: _\"It's not just conversion anymore. We're thinking through it. We're generating and manipulating documents.\"_\n\n[The Register](https://www.theregister.com/2026/01/30/agnetic_ai_protocols_mcp_utcp_a2a_etc/) classifies Docling as a framework and tool alongside Goose and BeeAI, not as a protocol alongside MCP and A2A. This editorial distinction places its competitive frame against other document-processing and agentic orchestration frameworks rather than the protocol layer.\n\nDocling operates under the MIT License, granting unrestricted commercial and personal use rights with no copyleft restrictions. All repositories under the docling-project GitHub organization maintain MIT licensing, enabling integration into proprietary systems without licensing conflicts.\nThe companion Granite-Docling-258M model is separately licensed under Apache 2.0.\n\n**Open-source release date:** IBM open-sourced Docling in July 2024 under MIT license. The project was identified as the top trending repository worldwide in November 2024 before its first formal PyPI release in August 2025.\n\n## How Docling processes documents\n\nDocling's architecture spans eight public repositories: `docling` (main package), `docling-core` (types, transforms, serializers; home of `DoclingDocument` ), `docling-parse` (PDF backend), `docling-serve` (FastAPI REST wrapper), `docling-ibm-models` (AI models), `docling-sdg` (synthetic data generation for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and fine-tuning), `docling-mcp` (Model Context Protocol tool definitions for document agents), and `docling-java` (Java API built on `docling-serve` ). The `docling-mcp` repository is the most forward-looking integration point for agentic workflows.\n**Document ingestion** handles PDF, DOCX, PPTX, XLSX, HTML, audio (WAV, MP3), and video (MP4, AVI, MOV) through a unified pipeline. The [TableFormer model, trained on 1M+ tables](https://pypi.org/project/docling-ibm-models/3.10.2/) from scientific, financial, and general datasets, handles complex table structures including partial or missing borderlines, empty cells, cell spans, and hierarchical headers. The Heron layout model, introduced in [December 2025](https://pypi.org/project/docling/2.66.0/) , improves PDF parsing speed while maintaining accuracy. All output flows through the `DoclingDocument` unified representation format, which exports to Markdown, JSON, HTML, or DocTags.\n\nThe underlying Layout Analysis Model uses RT-DETR architecture trained on the DocLayNet dataset for page element classification."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://research.ibm.com/blog/docling-generative-AI",
      "title": "IBM is open-sourcing a new toolkit for document conversion",
      "publish_date": "2024-11-12",
      "excerpts": [
        "12 Nov 2024 News 3 minute read\n\n# A new tool to unlock data from enterprise documents for generative AI\n\nIBM’s new open-source toolkit, Docling, allows developers to more easily convert PDFs, manuals, and slide decks into specialized data for customizing enterprise AI models and grounding them on trusted information.\n\nVisit Docling’s [GitHub page](https://github.com/DS4SD/docling) to find out how to contribute.\n\nToday’s foundation models have been trained on just about every scrap of publicly available information on the internet — text, images, and video. But much of the data that’s valuable to enterprises is stashed away in PDFs, annual reports, slide decks, and other complex business documents.\n\n[Docling](https://github.com/DS4SD/docling) , IBM’s new open-source toolkit, is designed to more easily unearth that information for generative AI applications.\nThe toolkit streamlines the process of turning unstructured documents into JSON and Markdown files that are easy for large language models (LLMs) and other [foundation models](https://research.ibm.com/blog/what-are-foundation-models) to digest. Once machine-readable, this data can be used to train and customize AI models and AI agents for enterprise tasks and to ground them on the latest, most accurate facts through [retrieval-augmented generation](https://research.ibm.com/blog/retrieval-augmented-generation-RAG) (RAG).\n\nDocling features a command-line interface, a Python API, and is small enough to run on a standard laptop. It takes just five lines of code to set up, and it integrates seamlessly with open-source LLM frameworks like LlamaIndex and LangChain for RAG and question-answering applications. Its permissive, open-source MIT license allows developers to collaborate and expand the project to meet their needs.\nSince it was open-sourced in July, Docling has received more than 8,000 stars on GitHub and praise in social forums across the internet. “The output quality is the best of all the open-source solutions,” one developer recently wrote on Reddit.\n\n## Moving beyond OCR for efficient document conversion\n\nTraditionally, developers have relied on optical character recognition (OCR) for digitizing documents. It’s a technology that can be error prone and slow, because of its heavy computational processing. Docling sidesteps OCR when it can, in favor of computer vision models trained to recognize and categorize the visual elements on a page.\n\n“Avoiding OCR reduces errors, and it also speeds up the time-to-solution by 30 times,” says Peter Staar, an IBM researcher who helped build Docling.\n\nDocling is built on top of two models designed by IBM researchers."
      ]

We like to get continuous feedback from the community: take the [poll](https://www.feedback.redhat.com/jfe/form/SV_eSaELOTnOafy0Vo) !\n\n## Docling\n\n[](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/docling-project/docling/refs/heads/main/docs/assets/logo.svg)\n\n**[Docling](https://github.com/docling-project/docling)** is our main open-source package. It is a powerful library which simplifies document processing, parsing diverse formats — including advanced PDF understanding — and providing seamless integrations with the gen AI ecosystem.\nWe support an amazing community which helps us driving forward the adoption of Docling.\nGive it a try and join the community!\n\nThe key repositories of Docling are:\n\n* [docling](https://github.com/docling-project/docling) \\- The home of the main `docling` package.\n* [docling-core](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-core) \\- The definition of types, transforms, serializers, etc. If it has to do with the `DoclingDocument` you will find it here.\n* [docling-parse](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-parse) \\- The backend PDF parser used by Docling.\n* [docling-serve](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-serve) \\- The FastAPI wrappers for running Docling as REST API and distribute large jobs.\n* [docling-ibm-models](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-ibm-models) \\- The AI models powering Docling.\n* [docling-sdg](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-sdg) \\- Synthetic data generation (SDG) on documents for dataset generation for RAG, finetuning, etc.\n* [docling-mcp](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-mcp) \\- The definition of tools with the Model Context Protocol for document conversion, manipulation and generation agents.\n* [docling-java](https://github.com/docling-project/docling-java) \\- A Java API for interacting with Docling, currently based on       "url": "https://research.ibm.com/publications/docling-an-efficient-open-source-toolkit-for-ai-driven-document-conversion",
      "title": "Docling: An Efficient Open-Source Toolkit for AI-driven ...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "AAAI 2025\n\nWorkshop paper\n\n25 Feb 2025\n\n# Docling: An Efficient Open-Source Toolkit for AI-driven Document Conversion\n\n[Download paper](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.17887)\n\n[](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.17887) [](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.17887) [](https://github.com/DS4SD/docling) [](https://github.com/DS4SD/docling)\n\n## Abstract\n\nWe introduce Docling, an easy-to-use, self-contained, MIT-licensed, open-source toolkit for document conversion, that can parse several types of popular document formats into a unified, richly structured representation. It is powered by state-of-the-art specialized AI models for layout analysis (DocLayNet) and table structure recognition (TableFormer), and runs efficiently on commodity hardware in a small resource budget. Docling is released as a Python package and can be used as a Python API or as a CLI tool. Docling's modular architecture and efficient document representation make it easy to implement extensions, new features, models, and customizations.\nDocling has been already integrated in other popular open-source frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, spaCy), making it a natural fit for the processing of documents and the development of high-end applications. The open-source community has fully engaged in using, promoting, and developing for Docling, which gathered 10k stars on GitHub in less than a month and was reported as the No. 1 trending repository in GitHub worldwide in November 2024.\n\n## Related\n\nConference paper\n\n### Social navigation through the Spoken Web: Improving audio access through collaborative filtering in Gujarat, India\n\nRobert Farrell, Rajarshi Das, et al.\n\nAAAI-SS 2010\n\nConference paper\n\n### Replacing Paths with Connection-Biased Attention for Knowledge Graph Completion\n\nSharmishtha Dutta, Alex Gittens, et al.\n\nAAAI 2025\n\nWorkshop paper\n\n### SPRIG: Stackelberg Perception-Reinforcement Learning with Internal Game Dynamics\n\nFernando Martinez, Juntao Chen, et al.\n\nAAAI 2025\n\nWorkshop paper\n### TDiMS : A Topological Distance based Intra-Molecular Substructure Descriptor for Improved Machine Learning Predictions\n\nLisa Hamada, Indra Priyadarsini S, et al.\n\nAAAI 2025\n\nView all publications\n\n1. Home\n2. ↳ Publications\n\n## Date\n\n25 Feb 2025\n\n## Publication\n\nAAAI 2025\n\n## Resources\n\n* [Download](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2501.17887)\n* [Publication](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.17887)\n* [Code](https://github.com/DS4SD/docling)\n* [Website](https://github.com/DS4SD/docling)\n\n## Share\n\n* \n*"

        "IDP-Software IDP-Software\n\nAbout Vendor Finder Newsletter\n\nIDP-Software IDP-Software\n\nVendor Finder\n\nVendors Capabilities Evaluations Guides News Vendor Finder\n\n# Docling Guide: IDP Guide\n\n14 min read\n\nOn This Page\n\n* Explosive Open Source Adoption and Market Impact\n* GitHub Success and Community Growth\n* Enterprise Integration and Production Deployments\n* Understanding Docling Architecture and Capabilities\n* Core Technology Components\n* Performance Benchmarks and Accuracy\n* Multi-Format Document Support\n* Installation and Basic Usage\n* Environment Setup and Dependencies\n* Python API Fundamentals\n* Command-Line Interface Usage\n* Advanced Features and Integrations\n* Vision-Language Model Integration\n* Framework Integrations for AI Applications\n* Structured Information Extraction\n* Building Document Intelligence Applications\n* RAG System Implementation\n* Document Intelligence Workflows\n* Agent-Based Document Processing\n* Performance Optimization and Best Practices\n* Processing Optimization Strategies\n* Quality Assurance and Validation\n* Enterprise Deployment Considerations\n* Future Development and Community Engagement\n* Open Source Community and Contributions\n* Integration with Emerging Technologies\n* Enterprise Adoption and Support\n\nDocling transforms complex document processing for AI applications through open-source Python libraries that preserve document structure, tables, and images while converting diverse formats into AI-ready data. Developed by [IBM Research Zurich](https://research.ibm.com/blog/docling-generative-AI) and hosted by the [LF AI & Data Foundation](https://lfaidata.foundation/projects/) , Docling processes documents up to [30 times faster than traditional OCR-based methods](https://research.ibm.com/blog/docling-generative-AI) by using computer vision models to understand document layouts rather than treating every page as an image requiring character recognition."

Not every healthy project tags releases.\n* **Open issues:** unresolved reports and requests. A high number is normal for a popular project and is not a warning on its own.\n* **Stars:** how many people bookmarked the project on its forge. A rough popularity signal, not a measure of quality.\n\n* * *\n\n### 🏗️ Profile\n\n* **Official:** [docling-project.github.io](https://docling-project.github.io/docling/?ref=opentechhub.io)\n* **Source:** [github.com/docling-project/docling](https://github.com/docling-project/docling?ref=opentechhub.io)\n* **License:** [MIT](https://www.opentechhub.io/resource/license-mit)\n* **Deployment:** [Python Library](https://www.opentechhub.io/resource/deployment-runtime) | [CLI](https://www.opentechhub.io/resource/deployment-native)\n* **Data Model:** Unstructured (PDF, DOCX, HTML) -> Structured (JSON, Markdown)\n* **Jurisdiction:** Global Community 🌐 (IBM Research / LF AI & Data)\n* **Compliance (SaaS):** N/A (Local-first)\n* **Compliance (Self-Hosted):** [HIPAA Eligible](https://www.opentechhub.io/resource/compliance-hipaa) | [GDPR Ready](https://www.opentechhub.io/resource/compliance-gdpr) | [ISO 27001 Ready](https://www.opentechhub.io/resource/compliance-iso27001)\n* **Complexity:** Low (2/5) - Standard Python library integration\n* **Maintenance:** Low (2/5) - Stateless parsing utility\n* **Enterprise Ready:** High (4/5) - Vision-based layout models & IBM Research roots\n\n## 1\\. The Executive Summary\n\n**What is it?** Docling is an advanced document parsing engine born out of IBM Research.   "content": "# Similar Projects Comparison\n\n## Overview\n\nA survey of open-source tools that produce searchable, compressed PDFs from scanned\ndocuments or input PDFs.  The focus is on license compatibility, MRC compression,\nself-contained deployment, and Java portability.\n\n---\n\n## Project Matrix\n\n| Feature | TrulyFreeOCR | OCRmyPDF | archive-pdf-tools | pdf2pdfocr | Docling | PaddleOCR |\n|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **Language** | Java 21 (pure) | Python | Python | Python | Python | Python / C++ |\n| **License** | Apache 2.0 | MPL-2.0 | AGPL-3.0 | Apache 2.0 | MIT | Apache 2.0 |\n| **GitHub Stars** | — | ~29k | ~140 | ~300 | ~62k | ~84k |\n| **OCR Engine** | Tesseract 5 CLI | Tesseract 5 | Tesseract 5 | Tesseract | Tesseract / pluggable | PaddleOCR (own) |\n| **MRC Compression** | Yes (PNG bg + JBIG2/G4 fg) | Yes (via `--optimize`) | Yes (JPEG2000 + JBIG2) | No | No (extraction only) | No |\n| **Self-Contained** | Yes (single JAR) | No (Python + Ghostscript + Tesseract) | No (Python + system deps) | No | No (Python + models) | No (Python + GPU) |\n| **PDF/A** | Yes (2b) | Yes (1b/2b/3b) | Yes (3b) | No | Focused on extraction | No |\n| **No Native Deps** | Only Tesseract subprocess | Ghostscript + Tesseract | Kakadu/OpenJPEG + MuPDF | pdftk + qpdf + ghostscript | Models downloaded at runtime | PyTorch / GPU |\n| **Image Preprocessing** | Otsu + background normalization | Deskew, clean, deskew | Sauvola binarization | None | Vision-based layout models | DL-based |\n| **Concurrent OCR** | Optional (configurable threads) | Yes (multi-core) | Yes | Yes | Yes (batching) | Yes (GPU batch) |\n| **Language Support** | 20 languages (tessdata) | 100+ languages | 100+ languages | 100+ languages | 100+ languages | 100+ languages |\n| **CLI Interface** | picocli | argparse | Custom | argparse | Typer | Custom |\n| **Active Development** | Yes | Yes (v17.7.0, Jun 2026) | Stale (last release 2021) | Low (last commit 2023) | Yes (v2.x, weekly) | Yes (v3.7.0, Jun 2026) |\n\n---\n\n## Detailed Analysis\n\n### OCRmyPDF (ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)\n- **License**: MPL-2.0 — business-friendly, allows closed-source integration if\n  modifications to OCRmyPDF itself are published.\n- **Strengths**: Gold standard for PDF OCR.  Battle-tested on millions of PDFs.\n  Rich preprocessing (deskew, clean, deskew, rotate).  Full PDF/A support.\n  100+ languages.  Plugin architecture (AppleOCR, EasyOCR, PaddleOCR backends).\n- **Weaknesses**: Python + system dependencies (Ghostscript, Tesseract).  Not\n  self-contained.  MPL-2.0 requires source disclosure of any modifications to\n  OCRmyPDF code itself.  Ghostscript is AGPL (used as subprocess, not linked).\n- **Relevance**: Directly comparable in output quality and feature set.  Our\n  advantage is Java portability (single JAR) and Apache 2.0 license (no\n  publication requirement).\n\n### archive-pdf-tools (internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools)\n- **License**: AGPL-3.0 — **not compatible** with commercial use without\n  disclosing entire application source.\n- **Strengths**: Mature MRC compression with JPEG2000.  Proven at scale (6M PDFs\n  in 2021).  PDF/A-3b and PDF/UA support.  Mask viewing tools.\n- **Weaknesses**: AGPL-3.0 is a non-starter for commercial use.  Requires\n  system JPEG2000 codec (Kakadu or OpenJPEG).  Python + complex dependency tree.\n  Stale (last release 2021).\n- **Relevance**: Shows that MRC + OCR at Internet Archive scale is feasible.\n  Our MRC approach (PNG bg + JBIG2/G4 fg) is simpler and avoids JPEG2000\n  licensing issues.\n\n### pdf2pdfocr (LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr)\n- **License**: Apache 2.0 — same as ours.\n- **Strengths**: Simple, straightforward.  No re-encoding of source images when\n  possible.  GUI available.  Cross-platform (Windows batch script included).\n- **Weaknesses**: No MRC compression.  No PDF/A.  No image preprocessing.\n  Low community (303 stars).  1.2.0 \"marapurense\" release never formalized.\n- **Relevance**: Demonstrates the simplest possible OCR sandwich approach.\n  Our tool adds MRC compression and full PDF/A for a material improvement.\n\n### Docling (docling-project/docling)\n- **License**: MIT — permissive.\n- **Strengths**: IBM Research quality.  Vision-based layout analysis (avoids\n  pixel-level OCR for clean PDFs).  TableFormer for table extraction.  30x faster\n  than OCR for born-digital PDFs.  Huge ecosystem (LangChain, LlamaIndex).\n  61k+ stars.\n- **Weaknesses**: Focused on *extraction* to Markdown/JSON, not on producing\n  compressed searchable PDFs.  Heavy model downloads (GBs).  Python-only.\n  Requires PyTorch for local inference.\n- **Relevance**: Complementary — Docling is for RAG pipelines, not for\n  producing archive-quality PDF output.  If we ever add a structure-preserving\n  extraction mode, Docling's approach (vision models for layout) would be the\n  direction.\n\n### PaddleOCR (PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR)\n- **License**: Apache 2.0.\n- **Strengths**: SOTA accuracy (reported better than Tesseract for many\n  scripts).  GPU-accelerated.  84k+ stars.  LLM-ready output (JSON/Markdown).\n  Mature document parsing pipeline.\n- **Weaknesses**: Python + PyTorch/PaddlePaddle runtime.  GPU strongly\n  recommended for practical throughput.  Model downloads are large.  No\n  searchable PDF output (focused on text extraction).\n- **Relevance**: PaddleOCR is the best OCR *engine* available today.  If we\n  ever add a pluggable OCR backend, PaddleOCR CLI (via subprocess, like\n  Tesseract) would be a natural second option for higher accuracy.\n\n### olmOCR (allenai/olmOCR)\n- **License**: Apache 2.0 (model weights, code).\n- **Strengths**: VLM-based OCR that preserves reading order, handles tables,\n  equations, handwriting.  Designed for high-throughput (estimated $178/1M\n  pages).\n- **Weaknesses**: Requires GPU (FP8 inference).  7B parameter model.  Very new\n  (2025/2026).  No searchable PDF output — text-only.\n- **Relevance**: VLM-based OCR is the emerging paradigm.  Once these models\n  become efficient enough to run on CPU, a subprocess-based integration would\n  be straightforward.\n\n---\n\n## Why TrulyFreeOCR Exists\n\n| Problem | Existing Tools | Our Solution |\n|---|---|---|\n| Commercial-safe license | OCRmyPDF (MPL-2.0) requires publishing modifications; archive-pdf-tools is AGPL | Apache 2.0 — no disclosure obligations |\n| Self-contained deployment | All alternatives require Python + system libs + model downloads | Single fat JAR + bundled native binaries + bootstrap script |\n| Java/enterprise integration | Python tools need subprocess calls from Java code | Native Java API, embeddable in Maven/Gradle projects |\n| MRC compression | Only OCRmyPDF and archive-pdf-tools offer it, with complex deps | PNG bg + JBIG2/G4 fg with clean fallback |\n| No Ghostscript dependency | OCRmyPDF requires Ghostscript (AGPL) | Pure PDFBox — no AGPL anywhere in the stack |\n\n## Potential Future Directions\n\n1. **Pluggable OCR backends** — Add PaddleOCR CLI as an alternative to\n   Tesseract (subprocess-based, same pattern).\n2. **Deskew/clean preprocessing** — Add Leptonica-based deskew and text\n   cleaning via subprocess (similar to OCRmyPDF's `--deskew`).\n3. **JPEG2000 background** — Add optional JPEG2000 background compression\n   via OpenJPEG subprocess for better compression ratios than PNG.\n4. **Docling-style extraction** — A `--extract-json` mode that outputs\n   structured Markdown/JSON for RAG pipelines.\n"
}

        "jDai\n\nEasyOCR About [GitHub](https://github.com/JaidedAI) Contact\n\n### EasyOCR\n\nOverview Installation Tutorial Model Hub API Documentation [GitHub ↗](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR)\n\n# API Documentation\n\n## `Reader` class\n\nBase class for EasyOCR.\n\n#### Parameters\n\n* **lang\\_list** (list) — list of language codes to recognize, e.g., `['ch_sim','en']` . See the supported language codes .\n* **gpu** (bool or string, default `True` ) — enable GPU.\n* **model\\_storage\\_directory** (string, default `None` ) — path to directory for model data. If not specified, models are read from `EASYOCR_MODULE_PATH` (preferred), `MODULE_PATH` , or `~/.EasyOCR/` .\n* **download\\_enabled** (bool, default `True` ) — enable automatic model download.\n* **user\\_network\\_directory** (string, default `None` ) — path to user-defined recognition network. Default: `MODULE_PATH + '/user_network'` .\n* **recog\\_network** (string, default `'standard'` ) — name of recognition network.\n* **detector** (bool, default `True` ) — load detection model into memory.\n* **recognizer** (bool, default `True` ) — load recognition model into memory.\n\n#### Attributes\n\n* **lang\\_char** — all available characters in the current model.\n\n## `readtext` method\n\nMain method for a Reader object. Parameters are grouped into General, Contrast, Text Detection, and Bounding Box Merging.\n\n#### General\n\n* **image** (string, numpy array, or bytes) — input image.\n* **decoder** (string, default `'greedy'` ) — options: `'greedy'` , `'beamsearch'` , `'wordbeamsearch'` .\n* **beamWidth** (int, default `5` ) — how many beams to keep when using beam search.\n* **batch\\_size** (int, default `1` ) — larger batch is faster but uses more memory.\n* **workers** (int, default `0` ) — number of dataloader threads.\n* **allowlist** (string) — force EasyOCR to recognize only a subset of characters.\n* **blocklist** (string) — block a subset of characters. Ignored if `allowlist` is given.\n* **detail** (int, default `1` ) — set to `0` for simple output.\n* **paragraph** (bool, default `False` ) — combine result into paragraphs.\n* **min\\_size** (int, default `10` ) — filter text boxes smaller than this (pixels).\n* **rotation\\_info** (list, default `None` ) — rotate each text box and keep the best result. E.g., `[90, 180, 270]` .\n\n#### Contrast\n\n* **contrast\\_ths** (float, default `0.1` ) — low-contrast boxes are processed twice.\n* **adjust\\_contrast** (float, default `0.5` ) — target contrast for low-contrast boxes.\n\n#### Text Detection (from CRAFT)\n\n* **text\\_threshold** (float, default `0.7` ) — text confidence threshold.\n* **low\\_text** (float, default `0.4` ) — text low-bound score.\n* **link\\_threshold** (float, default `0.4` ) — link confidence threshold.\n* **canvas\\_size** (int, default `2560` ) — maximum image size.\n* **mag\\_ratio** (float, default `1` ) — image magnification ratio.\n\n#### Bounding Box Merging\n\nControls when adjacent bounding boxes merge."
