      "url": "https://ironsoftware.com/csharp/ocr/blog/ocr-tools/easyocr-vs-tesseract",
      "title": "Easyocr vs Tesseract (In-Depth OCR Features Comparison) | IronOCR",
      "publish_date": "2026-04-22",
      "excerpts": [
        "OCR scans the document, recognizes the characters, such as letters, numbers, or symbols, and translates it into a machine-readable format. Its uses include book digitization, form processing, automation of document workflow, and improvement in accessibility to blind people. With the development of deep learning and AI, OCR engines became very accurate in recognizing complex formats, multi-language documents, and even poor-quality images.\n\nPopular OCR tools and libraries, like EasyOCR, Tesseract OCR, Keras-OCR, and IronOCR, are commonly employed to integrate this functionality into modern applications.\n\n## EasyOCR\n\n[EasyOCR](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR) is an open-source Python library that aims to make text extraction from images simple and efficient. It uses deep learning techniques and supports over 80 languages, including Latin, Chinese, Arabic, and many others.\n\n...\n\n// Display the extracted text\n            Console.WriteLine(\"Text:\");\n            Console.WriteLine(result.Text);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n```\nusing IronOcr;\n\nclass Program\n{\n    static void Main(string[] args)\n    {\n        // Initialize IronTesseract engine\n        var Ocr = new IronTesseract();\n\n        // Add languages to the OCR engine\n        Ocr.Language = OcrLanguage.English;\n\n        // Define the path to the input image\n        var inputFile = @\"path\\to\\your\\image.png\";\n\n        // Read the image and perform OCR\n        using (var input = new OcrInput(inputFile))\n        {\n            var result = Ocr.Read(input);\n\n            // Display the extracted text\n            Console.WriteLine(\"Text:\");\n            Console.WriteLine(result.Text);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n```\nImports IronOcr\n\nFriend Class Program\n\tShared Sub Main(ByVal args() As String)\n\t\t' Initialize IronTesseract engine\n\t\tDim Ocr = New IronTesseract()\n\n\t\t' Add languages to the OCR engine\n\t\tOcr.\nLanguage = OcrLanguage.English\n\n\t\t' Define the path to the input image\n\t\tDim inputFile = \"path\\to\\your\\image.png\"\n\n\t\t' Read the image and perform OCR\n\t\tUsing input = New OcrInput(inputFile)\n\t\t\tDim result = Ocr.Read(input)\n\n\t\t\t' Display the extracted text\n\t\t\tConsole.WriteLine(\"Text:\")\n\t\t\tConsole.WriteLine(result.Text)\n\t\tEnd Using\n\tEnd Sub\nEnd Class\n```\n\n$vbLabelText $csharpLabel\n\n### Comparative Assessment\n\n#### High Accuracy\n\nIronOCR stands out for its accuracy with complex layouts, noisy images, and low-resolution texts when compared to Tesseract or EasyOCR. Its built-in image preprocessing tools, such as noise reduction and contrast adjustments, contribute to achieving high accuracy in real-world applications.\n\n#### Multi-format and Layout Preservation\n\nIronOCR excels in processing various image formats, PDF files, and multi-column layouts while preserving original document structure and formatting. It is well-suited for projects where layout preservation is paramount.\nIts capability to directly convert images and scanned documents into fully searchable PDFs without relying on additional tools or libraries gives it an advantage over Tesseract and EasyOCR.\n\n#### IronOCR Provides Advanced Preprocessing\n\nEven poor-quality images can achieve high OCR accuracy using IronOCR’s advanced preprocessing features, which reduce the need for additional libraries like OpenCV, making it a comprehensive solution for text extraction.\n\n#### Scalability and Performance\n\nOptimized for high-speed, resource-efficient OCR, IronOCR supports scalability for large document processing tasks, a priority for enterprise applications.\n\n#### Support and Updates\n\nWith commercial support, IronOCR benefits from regular updates, bug fixes, and dedicated assistance, offering long-term reliability and the latest advancements in OCR, unlike open-source options such as Tesseract and EasyOCR.\n\n## Concl"

In folder [easyocr/character](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR/tree/master/easyocr/character) ,\n   we need 'yourlanguagecode\\_char.txt' that contains list of all characters. Please see format examples from other files in that folder.\n2. In folder [easyocr/dict](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR/tree/master/easyocr/dict) ,\n   we need 'yourlanguagecode.txt' that contains list of words in your language.\n   On average, we have ~30000 words per language with more than 50000 words for more popular ones.\n   More is better in this file.\n\nIf your language has unique elements (such as 1. Arabic: characters change form when attached to each other + write from right to left 2.\nThai: Some characters need to be above the line and some below), please educate us to the best of your ability and/or give useful links. It is important to take care of the detail to achieve a system that really works.\n\nLastly, please understand that our priority will have to go to popular languages or sets of languages that share large portions of their characters with each other (also tell us if this is the case for your language).         "# Making sure you're not a bot!\n\nLoading...\n\nYou are seeing this because the administrator of this website has set up Anubis to protect the server against the scourge of AI companies aggressively scraping websites. This can and does cause downtime for the websites, which makes their resources inaccessible for everyone.\n\nAnubis is a compromise. Anubis uses a Proof-of-Work scheme in the vein of Hashcash, a proposed proof-of-work scheme for reducing email spam. The idea is that at individual scales the additional load is ignorable, but at mass scraper levels it adds up and makes scraping much more expensive.\n\nUltimately, this is a placeholder solution so that more time can be spent on fingerprinting and identifying headless browsers (EG: via how they do font rendering) so that the challenge proof of work page doesn't need to be presented to users that are much more likely to be legitimate.\nPlease note that Anubis requires the use of modern JavaScript features that plugins like JShelter will disable. Please disable JShelter or other such plugins for this domain.\n\nProtected by [Anubis](https://github.com/TecharoHQ/anubis) From [Techaro](https://techaro.lol) . We are currently supporting 80+ languages and expanding.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```\npip install easyocr\n```\n\n```\nimport easyocr\nreader = easyocr.Reader(['ch_sim','en'])\nresult = reader.readtext('image.jpg')\n```\n\nFor more details, see the installation guide , tutorial , and API documentation .\n\n## Supported Languages\n\n|Language |Code |\n| --- | --- |\n|Abaza |abq |\n|Adyghe |ady |\n|Afrikaans |af |\n|Albanian |sq |\n|Angika |ang |\n|Arabic |ar |\n|Assamese |as |\n|Avar |ava |\n|Azerbaijani |az |\n|Belarusian |be |\n|Bengali |bn |\n|Bhojpuri |bho |\n|Bihari |bh |\n|Bosnian |bs |\n|Bulgarian |bg |\n|Chechen |che |\n|Chinese (simplified) |ch\\_sim |\n|Chinese (traditional) |ch\\_tra |\n|Croatian |hr |\n|Czech |cs |\n|Danish |da |\n|Dargwa |dar |\n|Dutch |nl |\n|English |en |\n|Estonian |et |\n|French |fr |\n|German |de |\n|Goan Konkani |gom |\n|Hindi |hi |\n|Hungarian |hu |\n|Icelandic |is |\n|Indonesian |id |\n|Ingush |inh |\n|Irish |ga |\n|Italian |it |\n|Japanese |ja |\n|Kabardian |kbd |\n|Kannada |kn |\n|Korean |ko |\n|Kurdish |ku |\n|Lak |lbe |\n|Latin |la |\n|Latvian |lv |\n|Lezghian |lez |\n|Lithuanian |lt |\n|Magahi |mah |\n|Maithili |mai |\n|Malay |ms |\n|Maltese |mt |\n|Maori |mi |\n|Marathi |mr |\n|Mongolian |mn |\n|Nagpuri |sck |\n|Nepali |ne |\n|Newari |new |\n|Norwegian |no |\n|Occitan |oc |\n|Pali |pi |\n|Persian (Farsi) |fa |\n|Polish |pl |\n|Portuguese |pt |\n|Romanian |ro |\n|Russian |ru |\n|Serbian (cyrillic) |rs\\_cyrillic |\n|Serbian (latin) |rs\\_latin |\n|Slovak |sk |\n|Slovenian |sl |\n|Spanish |es |\n|Swahili |sw |\n|Swedish |sv |\n|Tabassaran |tab |\n|Tagalog |tl |\n|Tajik |tjk |\n|Tamil |ta |\n|Telugu |te |\n|Thai |th |\n|Turkish |tr |\n|Ukrainian |uk |\n|Urdu |ur |\n|Uyghur |ug |\n|Uzbek |uz |\n|Vietnamese |vi |\n|Welsh |cy |\n\nLanguages sharing the same written script (e.g., Latin) can be used together. README\n* License\n\n# Surya\n\nSurya is a document OCR toolkit that does:\n\n* OCR in 90+ languages that benchmarks favorably vs cloud services\n* Line-level text detection in any language\n* Layout analysis (table, image, header, etc detection)\n* Reading order detection\n\nIt works on a range of documents (see usage and benchmarks for more details).\n\n|Detection |OCR |\n| --- | --- |\n|New York Times Article Detection |New York Times Article Recognition |\n |Layout |Reading Order |\n| --- | --- |\n|New York Times Article Layout |New York Times Article Reading Order |\n\nSurya is named for the [Hindu sun god](https://en.wikipedia.\norg/wiki/Surya) , who has universal vision.\n\n## Community\n\n[Discord](https://discord.gg//KuZwXNGnfH) is where we discuss future development.\n\n## Examples\n\n|Name |Detection |OCR |Layout |Order |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Japanese |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Chinese |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Hindi |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Arabic |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Chinese + Hindi |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Presentation |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scientific Paper |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scanned Document |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|New York Times |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scanned Form |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Textbook |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n\n# Hosted API\n\nThere is a hosted API for all surya models available [here](https://www.datalab.to/) :\n\n* Works with PDF, images, word docs, and powerpoints\n* Consistent speed, with no latency spikes\n* High reliability and uptime\n\n# Commercial usage\nI want surya to be as widely accessible as possible, while still funding my development/training costs. Research and personal usage is always okay, but there are some restrictions on commercial usage.\n\nThe weights for the models are licensed `cc-by-nc-sa-4.0` , but I will waive that for any organization under $5M USD in gross revenue in the most recent 12-month period AND under $5M in lifetime VC/angel funding raised. If you want to remove the GPL license requirements (dual-license) and/or use the weights commercially over the revenue limit, check out the options [here](https://www.datalab.to) .\n\n# Installation\n\nYou'll need python 3.9+ and PyTorch. You may need to install the CPU version of torch first if you're not using a Mac or a GPU machine. See [here](https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/) for more details.\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\n\nModel weights will automatically download the first time you run surya.\n\n# Usage\n\n* Inspect the settings in `surya/settings.\npy` . You can override any settings with environment variables.\n* Your torch device will be automatically detected, but you can override this. For example, `TORCH_DEVICE=cuda` .\n\n## Interactive App\n\nI've included a streamlit app that lets you interactively try Surya on images or PDF files. Run it with:\n\n```\npip install streamlit\nsurya_gui\n```\n\n## OCR (text recognition)\n\nThis command will write out a json file with the detected text and bboxes:\n\n```\nsurya_ocr DATA_PATH --images --langs hi,en\n```\n\n* `DATA_PATH` can be an image, pdf, or folder of images/pdfs\n* `--langs` specifies the language(s) to use for OCR. You can comma separate multiple languages (I don't recommend using more than `4` ). Use the language name or two-letter ISO code from [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639_language_codes) . Surya supports the 90+ languages found in `surya/languages.py` .\n* `--lang_file` if you want to use a different language for different PDFs/images, you can specify languages here."

      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# Commercial usage\n\nI want surya to be as widely accessible as possible, while still funding my development/training costs. Research and personal usage is always okay, but there are some restrictions on commercial usage.\n\nThe weights for the models are licensed `cc-by-nc-sa-4.0` , but I will waive that for any organization under $5M USD in gross revenue in the most recent 12-month period AND under $5M in lifetime VC/angel funding raised. You also must not be competitive with the [Datalab API](https://www.datalab.to/) . If you want to remove the GPL license requirements (dual-license) and/or use the weights commercially over the revenue limit, check out the options [here](https://www.datalab.to) .\n\n# Installation\n\nYou'll need python 3.10+ and PyTorch. You may need to install the CPU version of torch first if you're not using a Mac or a GPU machine. See [here](https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/) for more details.\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\nModel weights will automatically download the first time you run surya.\n\n# Usage\n\n* Inspect the settings in `surya/settings.py` . You can override any settings with environment variables.\n* Your torch device will be automatically detected, but you can override this. For example, `TORCH_DEVICE=cuda` .\n\n## Interactive App\n\nI've included a streamlit app that lets you interactively try Surya on images or PDF files. Run it with:\n\n```\npip install streamlit pdftext\nsurya_gui\n```\n\n## OCR (text recognition)\n\nThis command will write out a json file with the detected text and bboxes:\n\n```\nsurya_ocr DATA_PATH\n```\n\n* `DATA_PATH` can be an image, pdf, or folder of images/pdfs\n* `--langs` is an optional (but recommended) argument that specifies the language(s) to use for OCR. You can comma separate multiple languages. Use the language name or two-letter ISO code from [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639_language_codes) . Surya supports the 90+ languages found in `surya/languages.py` .\n* `--lang_file` if you want to use a different language for different PDFs/images, you can optionally specify languages in a file. The format is a JSON dict with the keys being filenames and the values as a list, like `{\"file1.pdf\": [\"en\", \"hi\"], \"file2.pdf\": [\"en\"]}` .\n* `--images` will save images of the pages and detected text lines (optional)\n* `--output_dir` specifies the directory to save results to instead of the default\n* `--page_range` specifies the page range to process in the PDF, specified as a single number, a comma separated list, a range, or comma separated ranges - example: `0,5-10,20` .\n\nThe `results.json` file will contain a json dictionary where the keys are the input filenames without extensions. Each value will be a list of dictionaries, one per page of the input document. Each page dictionary contains:\n\n* `text_lines` \\- the detected text and bounding boxes for each line\n  \n    + `text` \\- the text in the line"

      "url": "https://tasarim.ai/en/models/surya-ocr",
      "title": "Surya OCR | tasarim.ai",
      "publish_date": "2024-01-01",
      "excerpts": [
        "tasarim .ai\n\nTools Models Top 10 Find Tool Content\n\n🇬🇧EN 🇬🇧\n\nHome Models OCR Surya OCR\n\nSurya OCR icon\n\n# Surya OCR\n\nOpen Source\n\n4\\.5\n\nVikParuchuri\n\nSurya OCR is a modern AI-powered optical character recognition model developed by Vik Paruchuri that supports over 90 languages with impressive accuracy across diverse document types. Built on a Vision Transformer architecture inspired by the Donut framework, Surya takes an encoder-decoder approach that processes document images directly without requiring traditional text detection as a separate preprocessing step. The model extracts text content along with precise bounding box coordinates, enabling both full-text extraction and position-aware document understanding. Beyond basic character recognition, Surya includes a comprehensive document layout analysis module that identifies structural elements such as headers, paragraphs, tables, figures, lists, and captions, providing a complete understanding of document organization.\nThe model handles complex document layouts including multi-column pages, academic papers with equations, invoices with tabular data, and historical documents with non-standard typography. Surya achieves competitive or superior accuracy compared to commercial OCR services on many benchmarks while running locally without requiring cloud API calls, making it suitable for privacy-sensitive document processing. Released under the GPL-3.0 license, the model is open source and actively maintained with regular updates. It provides a Python API and command-line interface for batch processing. Key applications include digitizing printed and handwritten documents, extracting structured data from invoices and receipts, converting scanned books and academic papers to searchable text, processing legal and medical documents, archival document preservation, and building document understanding pipelines for enterprise content management systems.\nSurya is particularly valued for its strong multilingual support covering Latin, Cyrillic, CJK, Arabic, Devanagari, and many other scripts.\n\nOCR\n\n[Visit Website](https://github.com/VikParuchuri/surya?ref=tasarim-ai) Save\n\n[](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftasarim.ai%2Fen%2Fmodels%2Fsurya-ocr&text=Surya%20OCR) [](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftasarim.ai%2Fen%2Fmodels%2Fsurya-ocr) [](https://wa.me/?text=Surya%20OCR%20https%3A%2F%2Ftasarim.ai%2Fen%2Fmodels%2Fsurya-ocr) [](https://t.me/share/url?url=https%3A%2F%2Ftasarim.ai%2Fen%2Fmodels%2Fsurya-ocr&text=Surya%20OCR)\n\n## Key Highlights\n\n### Support for 90+ Languages\n\nMeets multilingual document processing needs with text detection and recognition in over 90 languages.\n\n### Advanced Layout Analysis\n\nDetermines correct reading order by automatically detecting document structure, columns, headings, and paragraphs.\n\n### Table Detection and Extraction"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://awesome.ecosyste.ms/projects/github.com/datalab-to/surya",
      "title": "\n      https://github.com/datalab-to/surya | Ecosyste.ms: Awesome\n    ",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "- `bbox` - the axis-aligned rectangle for the text line in (x1, y1, x2, y2) format.  (x1, y1) is the top left corner, and (x2, y2) is the bottom right corner.\n  \n  - `polygon` - the polygon for the text line in (x1, y1), (x2, y2), (x3, y3), (x4, y4) format.  The points are in clockwise order from the top left.\n  \n  - `position` - the reading order of the box.\n  \n  - `label` - the label for the bbox.  One of `Caption`, `Footnote`, `Formula`, `List-item`, `Page-footer`, `Page-header`, `Picture`, `Figure`, `Section-header`, `Table`, `Form`, `Table-of-contents`, `Handwriting`, `Text`, `Text-inline-math`.\n  \n  - `top_k` - the top-k other potential labels for the box.  A dictionary with labels as keys and confidences as values.\n  \n- `page` - the page number in the file\n  \n- `image_bbox` - the bbox for the image in (x1, y1, x2, y2) format.  (x1, y1) is the top left corner, and (x2, y2) is the bottom right corner.  All line bboxes will be contained within this bbox.\n\n**Performance tips**\nSetting the `LAYOUT_BATCH_SIZE` env var properly will make a big difference when using a GPU.  Each batch item will use `220MB` of VRAM, so very high batch sizes are possible.  The default is a batch size `32`, which will use about 7GB of VRAM.  Depending on your CPU core count, it might help, too - the default CPU batch size is `4`.\n\n### From python\n\n```python\n  \nfrom PIL import Image\n  \nfrom surya.foundation import FoundationPredictor\n  \nfrom surya.layout import LayoutPredictor\n  \nfrom surya.settings import settings\n\nimage = Image.open(IMAGE_PATH)\n  \nlayout_predictor = LayoutPredictor(FoundationPredictor(checkpoint=settings.LAYOUT_MODEL_CHECKPOINT))\n\n# layout_predictions is a list of dicts, one per image\n  \nlayout_predictions = layout_predictor([image])\n  \n```\n\n## Table Recognition\n\nThis command will write out a json file with the detected table cells and row/column ids, along with row/column bounding boxes.\nIf you want to get cell positions and text, along with nice formatting, check out the [marker](https://www.github.com/VikParuchuri/marker) repo.  You can use the `TableConverter` to detect and extract tables in images and PDFs.  It supports output in json (with bboxes), markdown, and html.\n\n```shell\n  \nsurya_table DATA_PATH\n  \n```\n\n- `DATA_PATH` can be an image, pdf, or folder of images/pdfs\n  \n- `--images` will save images of the pages and detected table cells + rows and columns (optional)\n  \n- `--output_dir` specifies the directory to save results to instead of the default\n  \n- `--page_range` specifies the page range to process in the PDF, specified as a single number, a comma separated list, a range, or comma separated ranges - example: `0,5-10,20`.\n  \n- `--detect_boxes` specifies if cells should be detected.  By default, they're pulled out of the PDF, but this is not always possible.\n  \n- `--skip_table_detection` tells table recognition not to detect tables first."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/surya-ocr",
      "title": "surya-ocr · PyPI",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "**State of the Art models for Document Intelligence**\n\n[Code License](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) [Model License](https://www.datalab.to/pricing) [Discord](https://discord.gg/KuZwXNGnfH)\n\n[Homepage](https://www.datalab.to) [Docs](https://documentation.datalab.to) [Datalab Playground](https://www.datalab.to/playground)\n\n* * *\n\n# Surya\n\nSurya is a 650M param OCR model with these features:\n\n* Accuracy - scores 83.3% on [olmOCR-bench](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/olmOCR-bench) (top under 3B params)\n* Speed - throughput of 5 pages/s on an RTX 5090\n* Multilingual - scores 87.2% on an internal benchmark set of 91 languages (more here )\n* Layout analysis (table, image, header, etc.) with reading order\n* Table recognition (rows + columns)\n\nWe also ship smaller models for line-level text detection and ocr error detection. It works on a range of documents (see usage and benchmarks ).\n\n## Try Datalab's Managed Platform\nOur managed platform runs both Surya, and variants of our highest accuracy model, [Chandra](https://github.com/datalab-to/chandra) .\n\nGet started with **$5 in free credits** —  (takes under 30 seconds) or try our free [public playground](https://www.datalab.to/playground?utm_source=gh-surya) .\n\n## Model Information\n\n|Detection |OCR |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n\n|Layout |Table Recognition |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n\nSurya is named for the [Hindu sun god](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surya) , who has universal vision.\n\n## Examples\n\nEach row links to five annotated views of the same page: text-line detection, OCR, layout, reading order, and (when present) table recognition.\n\n|Name |Detection |OCR |Layout |Order |Table Rec |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Newspaper |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Textbook |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Tax Form |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Handwritten Notes |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Corporate Doc |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n# Commercial usage\n\nThe Surya code is licensed under Apache 2.0. The model weights use a modified AI Pubs Open Rail-M license (free for research, personal use, and startups under $5M funding/revenue). For broader commercial licensing of the model weights, visit our pricing page [here](https://www.datalab.to/pricing?utm_source=gh-surya) .\n\n# Installation\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\n\n## Inference backend prerequisites\n\nSurya auto-spawns the server on first use, and you need `vllm` (NVIDIA GPU) or `llama.cpp` (CPU / Apple Silicon):\n\n* **NVIDIA GPU:** [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) plus the [NVIDIA Container Toolkit](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/container-toolkit/latest/install-guide.html) .\n* **CPU / Apple Silicon:** the `llama-server` binary from llama.cpp:\n  \n  ```\n  brew install llama.cpp # macOS \n   # or grab a release from https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases\n  ```\n\n## Upgrading from Surya v1"

It works on a range of documents (see usage and benchmarks ).\n\n## Try Datalab's Managed Platform\n\nOur managed platform runs both Surya, and variants of our highest accuracy model, [Chandra](https://github.com/datalab-to/chandra) .\n\nGet started with **$5 in free credits** —  (takes under 30 seconds) or try our free [public playground](https://www.datalab.to/playground?utm_source=gh-surya) .\n\n## Model Information\n\n|Detection |OCR |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n |Layout |Table Recognition |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n\nSurya is named for the [Hindu sun god](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surya) , who has universal vision.\n\n## Examples\nEach row links to five annotated views of the same page: text-line detection, OCR, layout, reading order, and (when present) table recognition.\n\n|Name |Detection |OCR |Layout |Order |Table Rec |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Newspaper |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Textbook |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Tax Form |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Handwritten Notes |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Corporate Doc |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n\n# Commercial usage\n\nThe Surya code is licensed under Apache 2.0. The model weights use a modified AI Pubs Open Rail-M license (free for research, personal use, and startups under $5M funding/revenue). For broader commercial licensing of the model weights, visit our pricing page [here](https://www.datalab.to/pricing?utm_source=gh-surya) .\n\n# Installation\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\n\n## Inference backend prerequisites\n\nSurya auto-spawns the server on first use, and you need `vllm` (NVIDIA GPU) or `llama.\ncpp` (CPU / Apple Silicon):\n\n* **NVIDIA GPU:** [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) plus the [NVIDIA Container Toolkit](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/container-toolkit/latest/install-guide.html) .\n* **CPU / Apple Silicon:** the `llama-server` binary from llama.cpp:\n  \n  ```\n  brew install llama.cpp     #  macOS \n   #  or grab a release from https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases\n  ```\n\n## Upgrading from Surya v1\n\nIf you have v1 code, you can migrate to this:\n\n```\n# v2 \n from surya . inference import SuryaInferenceManager \n from surya . recognition import RecognitionPredictor \n\n manager = SuryaInferenceManager ()              # auto-spawns vllm or llama-server \n rec = RecognitionPredictor ( manager )\n predictions = rec ([ image ])\n```\n\nWhat's different:\n\n* `SuryaInferenceManager` replaces `FoundationPredictor` . Same manager instance is shared across `LayoutPredictor` , `RecognitionPredictor` , `TableRecPredictor` ."

      "url": "https://github.com/ThinkCode/surya-pdf-ocr",
      "title": "GitHub - ThinkCode/surya-pdf-ocr: OCR, layout analysis, reading order, table recognition in 90+ languages · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## Community\n\n[Discord](https://discord.gg//KuZwXNGnfH) is where we discuss future development.\n\n## Examples\n\n|Name |Detection |OCR |Layout |Order |Table Rec |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Japanese |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Chinese |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Hindi |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Arabic |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Chinese + Hindi |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Presentation |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scientific Paper |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scanned Document |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|New York Times |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Scanned Form |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Textbook |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n\n# Hosted API\n\nThere is a hosted API for all surya models available [here](https://www.datalab.to/) :\n\n* Works with PDF, images, word docs, and powerpoints\n* Consistent speed, with no latency spikes\n* High reliability and uptime\n\n# Commercial usage\nI want surya to be as widely accessible as possible, while still funding my development/training costs. Research and personal usage is always okay, but there are some restrictions on commercial usage.\n\nThe weights for the models are licensed `cc-by-nc-sa-4.0` , but I will waive that for any organization under $5M USD in gross revenue in the most recent 12-month period AND under $5M in lifetime VC/angel funding raised. You also must not be competitive with the [Datalab API](https://www.datalab.to/) . If you want to remove the GPL license requirements (dual-license) and/or use the weights commercially over the revenue limit, check out the options [here](https://www.datalab.to) .\n\n# Installation\n\nYou'll need python 3.9+ and PyTorch. You may need to install the CPU version of torch first if you're not using a Mac or a GPU machine. See [here](https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/) for more details.\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\nModel weights will automatically download the first time you run surya.\n\n# Usage\n\n* Inspect the settings in `surya/settings.py` . You can override any settings with environment variables.\n* Your torch device will be automatically detected, but you can override this. For example, `TORCH_DEVICE=cuda` .\n\n## Interactive App\n\nI've included a streamlit app that lets you interactively try Surya on images or PDF files. Run it with:\n\n```\npip install streamlit\nsurya_gui\n```\n\n## OCR (text recognition)\n\nThis command will write out a json file with the detected text and bboxes:\n\n```\nsurya_ocr DATA_PATH\n```\n\n* `DATA_PATH` can be an image, pdf, or folder of images/pdfs\n* `--langs` is an optional (but recommended) argument that specifies the language(s) to use for OCR. You can comma separate multiple languages. Use the language name or two-letter ISO code from [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639_language_codes) . Surya supports the 90+ languages found in `surya/languages.py` ."

Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/mindee/notebooks/blob/main/doctr/quicktour.ipynb) [Gurubase](https://gurubase.io/g/doctr)\n\n**Optical Character Recognition made seamless & accessible to anyone, powered by PyTorch**\n\nWhat you can expect from this repository:\n\n* efficient ways to parse textual information (localize and identify each word) from your documents\n* guidance on how to integrate this in your current architecture\n\n[OCR_example](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/raw/main/docs/images/ocr.png)\n\n## Quick Tour\n\n### Getting your pretrained model\n\nEnd-to-End OCR is achieved in docTR using a two-stage approach: text detection (localizing words), then text recognition (identify all characters in the word).\nAs such, you can select the architecture used for [text detection](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/latest/modules/models.html) , and the one for [text recognition](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/latest//modules/models.\nhtml) from the list of available implementations.\n\n```\nfrom doctr . models import ocr_predictor \n\n model = ocr_predictor ( det_arch = \"db_resnet50\" , reco_arch = \"crnn_vgg16_bn\" , pretrained = True )\n```\n\n### Reading files\n\nDocuments can be interpreted from PDF or images:\n\n```\nfrom doctr . io import DocumentFile \n\n # PDF \n pdf_doc = DocumentFile . from_pdf ( \"path/to/your/doc.pdf\" )\n # Image \n single_img_doc = DocumentFile . from_images ( \"path/to/your/img.jpg\" )\n # Webpage (requires `weasyprint` to be installed) \n webpage_doc = DocumentFile . from_url ( \"https://www.yoursite.com\" )\n # Multiple page images \n multi_img_doc = DocumentFile . from_images ([ \"path/to/page1.jpg\" , \"path/to/page2.jpg\" ])\n```\n\n### Putting it together\n\nLet's use the default pretrained model for an example:\n\n```\nfrom doctr . io import DocumentFile \n from doctr . models import ocr_predictor \n\n model = ocr_predictor ( pretrained = True )\n # PDF \n doc = DocumentFile . from_pdf ( \"path/to/your/doc.pdf\" )\n\n...\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\n### Code of conduct\n\nCode of conduct\n\n### Contributing\n\nContributing\n\nActivity\n\nCustom [Pypi](https://pypi.org/project/python-doctr/) [Hugging Face Spaces](https://huggingface.\nco/spaces/mindee/doctr) [Open In Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/mindee/notebooks/blob/main/doctr/quicktour.ipynb) [Gurubase](https://gurubase.io/g/doctr)\n\n**Optical Character Recognition made seamless & accessible to anyone, powered by PyTorch**\n\nWhat you can expect from this repository:\n\n* efficient ways to parse textual information (localize and identify each word) from your documents\n* guidance on how to integrate this in your current architecture\n\n[OCR_example](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/raw/main/docs/images/ocr.png)\n\n## Quick Tour\n\n### Getting your pretrained model\n\nEnd-to-End OCR is achieved in docTR using a two-stage approach: text detection (localizing words), then text recognition (identify all characters in the word).\nAs such, you can select the architecture used for [text detection](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/latest/modules/models.html) , and the one for [text recognition](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/latest//modules/models.\nhtml) from the list of available implementations.\n\n```\nfrom doctr . models import ocr_predictor \n\n model = ocr_predictor ( det_arch = 'db_resnet50' , reco_arch = 'crnn_vgg16_bn' , pretrained = True )\n```\n\n### Reading files\n\nDocuments can be interpreted from PDF or images:\n\n```\nfrom doctr . io import DocumentFile \n # PDF \n pdf_doc = DocumentFile . from_pdf ( \"path/to/your/doc.pdf\" )\n # Image \n single_img_doc = DocumentFile . from_images ( \"path/to/your/img.jpg\" )\n # Webpage (requires `weasyprint` to be installed) \n webpage_doc = DocumentFile . from_url ( \"https://www.yoursite.com\" )\n # Multiple page images \n multi_img_doc = DocumentFile . from_images ([ \"path/to/page1.jpg\" , \"path/to/page2.jpg\" ])\n```\n\n### Putting it together\n\nLet's use the default pretrained model for an example:\n\n```\nfrom doctr . io import DocumentFile \n from doctr . models import ocr_predictor \n\n model = ocr_predictor ( pretrained = True )\n # PDF \n doc = DocumentFile . from_pdf ( \"path/to/your/doc.pdf\" )\n # Analyze\n\n...\n\n### Minimal API integration\n\nLooking to integrate docTR into your API? Here is a template to get you started with a fully working API using the wonderful [FastAPI](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi) framework.\n\n#### Deploy your API locally\n\nSpecific dependencies are required to run the API template, which you can install as follows:\n\n```\ncd api/\npip install poetry\nmake lock\npip install -r requirements.txt\n```\n\nYou can now run your API locally:\n\n```\nuvicorn --reload --workers 1 --host 0.0.0.0 --port=8002 --app-dir api/ app.main:app\n```\n\nAlternatively, you can run the same server on a docker container if you prefer using:\n\n```\nPORT=8002 docker-compose up -d --build\n```\n\n#### What you have deployed\n\nYour API should now be running locally on your port 8002. Access your automatically-built documentation at <http://localhost:8002/redoc> and enjoy your three functional routes (\"/detection\", \"/recognition\", \"/ocr\", \"/kie\"). Here is an example with Python to send a request to the OCR route:"
      ]
    },

      "url": "https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2025/08/31/doctr-the-fast-open-source-way-to-extract-text-from-documents-and-images-using-ocr/",
      "title": "docTR: The Fast, Open-Source Way to Extract Text from ...",
      "publish_date": "2025-08-31",
      "excerpts": [
        "* **Real-time video OCR** (> 30 FPS) → look at dedicated streaming pipelines.\n* **Handwriting-heavy workflows** → consider specialized HTR (handwriting-text recognition) models.\n\n* * *\n\n1. Roadmap & Community\n\n* * *\n\n* **v0.10** (planned Q4 2025) will add table-structure recognition and on-device CoreML export.\n* **Contributions welcome** : the repo has a “good first issue” label, and the maintainers actively review PRs within 48 h.\n\n* * *\n\n1. TL;DR Cheat Sheet\n\n* * *\n\nI want to…Copy-paste snippetOCR one image `DocumentFile.from_images([\"receipt.jpg\"])` OCR a PDF `DocumentFile.from_pdf(\"contract.pdf\")` Switch to GPU `ocr_predictor(pretrained=True).cuda()` Get plain text `result.render()` Visualize `result.show()` Deploy behind RESTSee FastAPI example above---\n\n\\*\\* [docTR](https://github.com/mindee/doctr) \\*\\*brings **research-grade OCR** into the realm of “pip install and go”.\nWhether you are indexing archives, building a compliance pipeline, or simply need to search inside scanned lecture notes, docTR gives you Google-level quality without the vendor lock-in **all in three lines of Python** .\n\nRepository: [github.com/mindee/doctr](https://github.com/mindee/doctr)  \nDocumentation: [mindee.github.io/doctr](https://mindee.github.io/doctr)\n\n[GitHub - mindee/doctr: docTR (Document Text Recognition) - a seamless, high-performing & accessible library for OCR-related tasks powered by Deep Learning.](https://github.com/mindee/doctr)\n\nM\n\n### Written by Mohamed IDBRAHIM\n\nTech enthusiast and web developer. Sharing knowledge about Laravel, JavaScript, and modern software architecture.\n\n  \n\n### Read Next AI Picks\n\n[Zero-Shot Text-to-Speech with Voice Cloning for English and Chinese: An In-Depth Look at IndexTTS](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2025/09/15/zero-shot-text-to-speech-with-voice-cloning-for-english-and-chinese-an-in-depth-look-at-indextts)\n#### [Zero-Shot Text-to-Speech with Voice Cloning for English and Chinese: An In-Depth Look at IndexTTS](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2025/09/15/zero-shot-text-to-speech-with-voice-cloning-for-english-and-chinese-an-in-depth-look-at-indextts)\n\nSep 15, 2025 Read\n\n[The Ultimate Guide to WhatsApp REST API Service in Go: Build Scalable, Multi-Device Solutions with WuzAPI 🚀](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2025/11/23/the-ultimate-guide-to-whatsapp-rest-api-service-in-go-build-scalable-multi-device-solutions-with-wuzapi-%F0%9F%9A%80)\n\n#### [The Ultimate Guide to WhatsApp REST API Service in Go: Build Scalable, Multi-Device Solutions with WuzAPI 🚀](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2025/11/23/the-ultimate-guide-to-whatsapp-rest-api-service-in-go-build-scalable-multi-device-solutions-with-wuzapi-%F0%9F%9A%80)\n\nNov 23, 2025 Read\n\n[The Open-Source FinanceToolkit Revolution: Transparent Analysis in an Age of $7B Data Breaches](https://www.blog.brightcoding."
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://mindee.github.io/doctr/using_doctr/using_models.html",
      "title": "Choosing the right model - docTR documentation - GitHub Pages",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "For a comprehensive comparison, we have compiled a detailed benchmark on publicly available datasets:\n\n| |FUNSD |CORD |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|**Architecture** |**Recall** | **Precision** |**Recall** |**Precision** |\n|db\\_resnet50 + crnn\\_vgg16\\_bn |73\\.37 |76\\.11 |84\\.80 |79\\.09 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + crnn\\_mobilenet\\_v3\\_small |73\\.06 |75\\.79 |84\\.64 |78\\.94 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + crnn\\_mobilenet\\_v3\\_large |73\\.17 |75\\.90 |84\\.96 |79\\.25 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + master |73\\.90 |76\\.66 |85\\.84 |80\\.07 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + sar\\_resnet31 |73\\.58 |76\\.33 |85\\.64 |79\\.88 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + vitstr\\_small |73\\.06 |75\\.79 |85\\.95 |80\\.17 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + vitstr\\_base |73\\.70 |76\\.46 |85\\.76 |79\\.99 |\n|db\\_resnet50 + parseq |73\\.52 |76\\.27 |85\\.91 |80\\.13 |\n|Gvision text detection |59\\.50 |62\\.50 |75\\.30 |59\\.03 |\n|Gvision doc. text detection |64\\.00 |53\\.30 |68\\.90 |61\\.10 |\n|AWS textract |78\\.10 |83\\.00 |87\\.50 |66\\.00 |\n|Azure Form Recognizer (v3.2) |79\\.42 |85\\.89 |89\\.62 |88\\.93 |\nAll OCR models above have been evaluated using both the training and evaluation sets of FUNSD and CORD (cf. doctr.datasets ).\nExplanations about the metrics being used are available in Task evaluation .\n\n_Disclaimer: both FUNSD subsets combine have 199 pages which might not be representative enough of the model capabilities_\n\n### Two-stage approaches ¶\n\nThose architectures involve one stage of text detection, and one stage of text recognition. The text detection will be used to produces cropped images that will be passed into the text recognition block. Everything is wrapped up with `ocr_predictor` .\n\n```\nimport numpy as np \n from doctr.models import ocr_predictor \n model = ocr_predictor ( 'db_resnet50' , 'crnn_vgg16_bn' , pretrained = True ) \n input_page = ( 255 * np . random . rand ( 800 , 600 , 3 )) . astype ( np . uint8 ) \n out = model ([ input_page ])\n```\n\nYou can pass specific boolean arguments to the predictor:\n* assume\\_straight\\_pages : if you work with straight documents only, it will fit straight bounding boxes to the text areas.\n* preserve\\_aspect\\_ratio : if you want to preserve the aspect ratio of your documents while resizing before sending them to the model.\n* symmetric\\_pad : if you choose to preserve the aspect ratio, it will pad the image symmetrically and not from the bottom-right.\n\nThose 3 are going straight to the detection predictor, as mentioned above (in the detection part).\n\nAdditional arguments which can be passed to the ocr\\_predictor are:\n\n* export\\_as\\_straight\\_boxes : If you work with rotated and skewed documents but you still want to export straight bounding boxes and not polygons, set it to True.\n* straighten\\_pages : If you want to straighten the pages before sending them to the detection model, set it to True."
      ]
    },

(Document AI, OCR, and Chart Understanding / Models and tools)\n\n## [README](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/blob/main/README.md)\n\n```\n          \n\n[![Slack Icon](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-Community-4A154B?style=flat-square&logo=slack&logoColor=white)](https://slack.mindee.com) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE) ![Build Status](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/workflows/builds/badge.svg) [!\n[Docker Images](https://img.shields.io/badge/Docker-4287f5?style=flat&logo=docker&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/pkgs/container/doctr) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/mindee/doctr/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=577MO567NM)](https://codecov.io/gh/mindee/doctr) [![CodeFactor](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/mindee/doctr/badge?s=bae07db86bb079ce9d6542315b8c6e70fa708a7e)](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/mindee/doctr) [![Codacy Badge](https://api.codacy.com/project/badge/Grade/340a76749b634586a498e1c0ab998f08)](https://app.codacy.com/gh/mindee/doctr?utm_source=github.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=mindee/doctr&utm_campaign=Badge_Grade) [![Doc Status](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/workflows/doc-status/badge.svg)](https://mindee.github.io/doctr) [![Pypi](https://img.shields.io/badge/pypi-v0.11.0-blue.svg)](https://pypi.org/project/python-doctr/) [![Hugging Face Spaces](https://img.shields."

      "url": "https://mindee.github.io/doctr/",

      "excerpts": [
        "docTR documentation\n\nBack to top\n\nView this page\n\n# docTR: Document Text Recognition ¶\n\nState-of-the-art Optical Character Recognition made seamless & accessible to anyone, powered by PyTorch\n\nDocTR provides an easy and powerful way to extract valuable information from your documents:\n\n* 🧾 **for automation** : seamlessly process documents for Natural Language Understanding tasks: we provide OCR predictors to parse textual information (localize and identify each word) from your documents.\n* 👩‍🔬 **for research** : quickly compare your own architectures speed & performances with state-of-art models on public datasets.\n\n## Main Features ¶\n\n* 🤖 Robust 2-stage (detection + recognition) OCR predictors with pretrained parameters\n* ⚡ User-friendly, 3 lines of code to load a document and extract text with a predictor\n* 🚀 State-of-the-art performance on public document datasets, comparable with GoogleVision/AWS Textract\n* ⚡ Optimized for inference speed on both CPU & GPU\n* 🐦 Light package, minimal dependencies\n* 🛠️ Actively maintained by Mindee\n* 🏭 Easy integration (available templates for browser demo & API deployment)\n\n### Supported datasets ¶\n\n* FUNSD from [“FUNSD: A Dataset for Form Understanding in Noisy Scanned Documents”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1905.13538.pdf) .\n* CORD from [“CORD: A Consolidated Receipt Dataset forPost-OCR Parsing”](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=SJl3z659UH) .\n* SROIE from [ICDAR 2019](https://rrc.cvc.uab.es/?ch=13) .\n* IIIT-5k from [CVIT](https://cvit.iiit.ac.in/research/projects/cvit-projects/the-iiit-5k-word-dataset) .\n* Street View Text from [“End-to-End Scene Text Recognition”](http://vision.ucsd.edu/~kai/pubs/wang_iccv2011.pdf) .\n* SynthText from [Visual Geometry Group](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/scenetext/) .\n* SVHN from [“Reading Digits in Natural Images with Unsupervised Feature Learning”](http://ufldl.stanford.edu/housenumbers/nips2011_housenumbers.pdf) .\n* IC03 from [ICDAR 2003](http://www.iapr-tc11.\norg/mediawiki/index.php?title=ICDAR_2003_Robust_Reading_Competitions) .\n* IC13 from [ICDAR 2013](http://dagdata.cvc.uab.es/icdar2013competition/) .\n* IMGUR5K from [“TextStyleBrush: Transfer of Text Aesthetics from a Single Example”](https://github.com/facebookresearch/IMGUR5K-Handwriting-Dataset) .\n* MJSynth from [“Synthetic Data and Artificial Neural Networks for Natural Scene Text Recognition”](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/text/) .\n* IIITHWS from [“Generating Synthetic Data for Text Recognition”](https://github.com/kris314/hwnet) .\n* WILDRECEIPT from [“Spatial Dual-Modality Graph Reasoning for Key Information Extraction”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2103.14470v1.pdf) .\n* COCO-Text dataset from [“COCO-Text: Dataset and Benchmark for Text Detection and Recognition in Natural Images”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1601.07140v2) .\n\nNext Installation\n\nMade with [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/) and [@pradyunsg](https://pradyunsg.me) 's [Furo](https://github.com/pradyunsg/furo)\n\nOn this page\n* docTR: Document Text Recognition\n  \n    + Main Features\n        \n        - Model zoo\n                  \n            * Text detection models\n            * Text recognition models\n        - Supported datasets"
      ]

        "docTR documentation\n\nBack to top\n\nView this page\n\n# docTR: Document Text Recognition ¶\n\nState-of-the-art Optical Character Recognition made seamless & accessible to anyone, powered by PyTorch\n\nDocTR provides an easy and powerful way to extract valuable information from your documents:\n\n* 🧾 **for automation** : seamlessly process documents for Natural Language Understanding tasks: we provide OCR predictors to parse textual information (localize and identify each word) from your documents.\n* 👩‍🔬 **for research** : quickly compare your own architectures speed & performances with state-of-art models on public datasets.\n\n## Main Features ¶\n\n* 🤖 Robust 2-stage (detection + recognition) OCR predictors with pretrained parameters\n* ⚡ User-friendly, 3 lines of code to load a document and extract text with a predictor\n* 🚀 State-of-the-art performance on public document datasets, comparable with GoogleVision/AWS Textract\n* ⚡ Optimized for inference speed on both CPU & GPU\n* 🐦 Light package, minimal dependencies\n* 🛠️ Actively maintained by Mindee\n* 🏭 Easy integration (available templates for browser demo & API deployment)\n\n### Supported datasets ¶\n\n* FUNSD from [“FUNSD: A Dataset for Form Understanding in Noisy Scanned Documents”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1905.13538.pdf) .\n* CORD from [“CORD: A Consolidated Receipt Dataset forPost-OCR Parsing”](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=SJl3z659UH) .\n* SROIE from [ICDAR 2019](https://rrc.cvc.uab.es/?ch=13) .\n* IIIT-5k from [CVIT](https://cvit.iiit.ac.in/research/projects/cvit-projects/the-iiit-5k-word-dataset) .\n* Street View Text from [“End-to-End Scene Text Recognition”](http://vision.ucsd.edu/~kai/pubs/wang_iccv2011.pdf) .\n* SynthText from [Visual Geometry Group](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/scenetext/) .\n* SVHN from [“Reading Digits in Natural Images with Unsupervised Feature Learning”](http://ufldl.stanford.edu/housenumbers/nips2011_housenumbers.pdf) .\n* IC03 from [ICDAR 2003](http://www.iapr-tc11.\norg/mediawiki/index.php?title=ICDAR_2003_Robust_Reading_Competitions) .\n* IC13 from [ICDAR 2013](http://dagdata.cvc.uab.es/icdar2013competition/) .\n* IMGUR5K from [“TextStyleBrush: Transfer of Text Aesthetics from a Single Example”](https://github.com/facebookresearch/IMGUR5K-Handwriting-Dataset) .\n* MJSynth from [“Synthetic Data and Artificial Neural Networks for Natural Scene Text Recognition”](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/text/) .\n* IIITHWS from [“Generating Synthetic Data for Text Recognition”](https://github.com/kris314/hwnet) .\n* WILDRECEIPT from [“Spatial Dual-Modality Graph Reasoning for Key Information Extraction”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2103.14470v1.pdf) .\n* COCO-Text dataset from [“COCO-Text: Dataset and Benchmark for Text Detection and Recognition in Natural Images”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1601.07140v2) .\n\nNext Installation\n\nMade with [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/) and [@pradyunsg](https://pradyunsg.me) 's [Furo](https://github.com/pradyunsg/furo)\n\nOn this page\n* docTR: Document Text Recognition\n  \n    + Main Features\n        \n        - Model zoo\n                  \n            * Text detection models\n            * Text recognition models\n        - Supported datasets"

        "ReadmeX\n\nmindee/doctr\n\nToggle theme\n\n# API\n\nRelevant source files\n\nThe API component of the `mindee/doctr` repository provides a web server interface to utilize the document text recognition capabilities of the docTR library. Built with FastAPI, it offers endpoints for text detection, recognition, end-to-end OCR, and Key Information Extraction (KIE). This allows users to integrate docTR into their applications via simple HTTP requests without needing to interact directly with the docTR Python library.\n\n## Architecture\n\nThe API follows a standard web application structure using FastAPI. It consists of the main application entry point, separate routers for different functionalities, utility functions, schema definitions for data validation, and configuration settings.\n\n```\nmermaid\n```\n\n## Core Functionality and Modules\n\nThe API exposes several endpoints, each handled by a dedicated router module:\n\n### Main Application ( `api/app/main.py` )\nThis file initializes the FastAPI application, sets up middleware (e.g., for tracking processing time), and includes the routers for different API functionalities. It also configures the OpenAPI documentation.\n\n```\n`# api/app/main.py # Lines 13-16 app . include_router ( recognition . router ,  prefix = \"/recognition\" ,  tags = [ \"recognition\" ] ) app . include_router ( detection . router ,  prefix = \"/detection\" ,  tags = [ \"detection\" ] ) app . include_router ( ocr . router ,  prefix = \"/ocr\" ,  tags = [ \"ocr\" ] ) app . include_router ( kie . router ,  prefix = \"/kie\" ,  tags = [ \"kie\" ] )`\n```\n\n### OCR Route ( `api/app/routes/ocr.py` )\n\nHandles requests to the `/ocr` endpoint. It receives uploaded files, processes them using the docTR OCR predictor, and returns the structured OCR results.\n\n```\n`# api/app/routes/ocr.py # Lines 17-20 @router . post ( \"/\" ,  response_model = list [ OCROut ] ,  status_code = status .\nHTTP_200_OK ,  summary = \"Perform OCR\" ) async def perform_ocr ( request :  OCRIn  =  Depends ( ) ,  files : list [ UploadFile ] = [ File ( . . . ) ] ) : \"\"\"Runs docTR OCR model to analyze the input image\"\"\" # ... (rest of the function)`\n```\n\n### Vision Logic ( `api/app/vision.py` )\n\nContains the `init_predictor` function responsible for loading and configuring the appropriate docTR model ( `ocr_predictor` or `kie_predictor` ) based on the request parameters. It also handles moving the model to the available device (CPU or GPU).\n\n```\n`# api/app/vision.py # Lines 24-43 def init_predictor ( request :  KIEIn  |  OCRIn  |  RecognitionIn  |  DetectionIn ) - >  Callable : \"\"\"Initialize the predictor based on the request\"\"\" # ... (logic to initialize and configure predictor)`\n```\n\n### Schemas ( `api/app/schemas.py` )\n\nDefines the data structures for incoming requests and outgoing responses using Pydantic models."
      ]
    }
  ],

      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "* HTTP接口\n* 构建项目（Windows、Linux）\n\n## 使用源码\n\n开发者请务必阅读 构建项目 。\n\n## 下载发行版\n\n以下发布链接均长期维护，提供稳定版本的下载。\n\n* **蓝奏云** <https://hiroi-sora.lanzoul.com/s/umi-ocr> （国内推荐，免注册/无限速）\n* **GitHub** <https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases/latest>\n* **Source Forge** <https://sourceforge.net/projects/umi-ocr>\n\n**•  Scoop Installer** （点击展开）\n\n[Scoop](https://scoop.sh/) 是一款Windows下的命令行安装程序，可方便地管理多个应用。您可以先安装 Scoop ，再使用以下指令安装 `Umi-OCR` ：\n\n* 添加 `extras` 桶：\n\n```\nscoop bucket add extras\n```\n\n* （可选1）安装 Umi-OCR（自带 `Rapid-OCR` 引擎，兼容性好）：\n\n```\nscoop install extras/umi-ocr\n```\n\n* （可选2）安装 Umi-OCR（自带 `Paddle-OCR` 引擎，速度稍快）：\n\n```\nscoop install extras/umi-ocr-paddle\n```\n\n* 不要同时安装二者，快捷方式可能会被覆盖。但您可以额外导入 [插件](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR_plugins) ，随时切换不同OCR引擎。\n  \n## 开始使用\n\n软件发布包下载为 `.7z` 压缩包或 `.7z.exe` 自解压包。自解压包可在没有安装压缩软件的电脑上，解压文件。\n\n本软件无需安装。解压后，点击 `Umi-OCR.exe` 即可启动程序。\n\n遇到任何问题，请提 [Issue](https://github.\ncom/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/issues) ，我会尽可能帮助你。\n\n## 界面语言\n\nUmi-OCR 支持的界面多国语言。在第一次打开软件时，将会按照你的电脑的系统设置，自动切换语言。\n\n如果需要手动切换语言，请参考下图， `全局设置` → `语言/Language` 。\n\n[1-标题-1.png](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/559d04478eb5a5039dd3ec75d153458854ea942aefcde43cba0d5fa64b67e198/68747470733a2f2f74757069616e2e6c692f696d616765732f323032332f31312f31392f363535393963336639653630302e706e67)\n\n## 标签页\n\nUmi-OCR v2 由一系列灵活好用的 **标签页** 组成。您可按照自己的喜好，打开需要的标签页。\n\n标签栏左上角可以切换 **窗口置顶** 。右上角能够 **锁定标签页** ，以防止日常使用中误触关闭标签页。\n\n### 截图OCR\n\n[2-截图-1.png](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/2ee49cac7824b7018c9db7662fad68d100334a4e62c8149ac8c86d78d0ecfc0d/68747470733a2f2f74757069616e2e6c692f696d616765732f323032332f31312f31392f363535393930393761626138652e706e67)\n\n**截图OCR** ：打开这一页后，就可以用快捷键唤起截图，识别图中的文字。\n\n* 左侧的图片预览栏，可直接用鼠标划选复制。\n* 右侧的识别记录栏，可以编辑文字，允许划选多个记录复制。\n* 也支持在别处复制图片，粘贴到Umi-OCR进行识别。\n* 关于 [公式识别](https://github.\ncom/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/issues/254) 功能\n\n#### 文本后处理\n\n[2-截图-2.png](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/4a797e96b102866b93bcbe91fa778445da2c994e860a3526ba281389ee0aa4ff/68747470733a2f2f74757069616e2e6c692f696d616765732f323032332f31312f31392f363535393930396633653337382e706e67)\n\n关于 **OCR文本后处理 - 排版解析方案** ： 可以整理OCR结果的排版和顺序，使文本更适合阅读和使用。预设方案：\n\n* `多栏-按自然段换行` ：适合大部分情景，自动识别多栏布局，按自然段规则进行换行。\n* `多栏-总是换行` ：每段语句都进行换行。\n* `多栏-无换行` ：强制将所有语句合并到同一行。\n* `单栏-按自然段换行` / `总是换行` / `无换行` ：与上述类似，不过 不区分多栏布局。\n* `单栏-保留缩进` ：适用于解析代码截图，保留行首缩进和行中空格。\n* `不做处理` ：OCR引擎的原始输出，默认每段语句都进行换行。\n\n上述方案，均能自动处理横排和竖排（从右到左）的排版。（竖排文字还需要OCR引擎本身支持）\n\n* * *\n\n### 批量OCR\n\n[3-批量-1.png](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/a4816e536159297a56ec04068d59b33ce8c0145b2bea9969b8d5935e4f1b2edf/68747470733a2f2f74757069616e2e6c692f696d616765732f323032332f31312f31392f363535393930613235313165302e706e67)\n\n**批量OCR** ：这一页用于批量导入本地图片进行识别。"
      ]

        "# Umi-OCR:免费离线OCR工具，支持截图/批量识别/PDF/二维码/公式，多语言高效解析\n\nfavicon\n\n## \n\nhendelyson /\n\nUmi-OCR\n\n按下 `/` 快速开启搜索\n\noperation-entry\n\n登录 注册\n\nForked from： [GitHub推荐项目精选 / um / Umi-OCR](https://gitcode.com/GitHub_Trending/um/Umi-OCR)\n\nStar 0\n\nFork\n\n0\n\nCode [Issues](https://gitcode.com/hendelyson/Umi-OCR/issues) [Pull Requests](https://gitcode.com/hendelyson/Umi-OCR/pulls) [Discussion](https://gitcode.com/hendelyson/Umi-OCR/discussions) Analysis\n\nStar 0\n\nFork\n\n0\n\nSign up free\n\nDiscover high-quality open-source projects easily and host them with one click\n\n首页\n\n工作台\n\nAI社区\n\n大赛平台\n\n项目\n\n组织\n\n企业\n\n偏好设置\n\n邀请\n\n客\n\n服"

    {
      "url": "https://grokipedia.com/page/Umi-OCR",
      "title": "Umi-OCR — Grokipedia",
      "publish_date": "2026-03-24",
      "excerpts": [
        "Search `⌘K`\n\nSuggest Edit \n\n \n\nFact-checked by Grok 2 months ago\n\n# Umi-OCR\n\nAra Eve Leo Sal\n\n1x\n\n**Umi-OCR** is a free and open-source optical character recognition (OCR) software that operates entirely offline, enabling users to extract text from screenshots, images, PDFs, and other documents without any internet connection or data upload, thereby prioritizing privacy and local processing. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() [[[3]]()]() Developed primarily in Python and QML by hiroi-sora and hosted on GitHub under the MIT license, Umi-OCR features a tabbed interface in its current major version 2 series (latest release v2.1.5 as of March 2025) and supports platforms including Windows 7 x64 and later, as well as Linux x64 through dedicated runtime environments. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() The project has gained significant popularity, with over 42,700 stars and 4,200 forks on GitHub.\n[[[1]]()]() Core capabilities include screenshot capture with hotkey-based OCR, batch processing of unlimited images in formats such as JPG, PNG, and TIFF, PDF document recognition with options to generate searchable dual-layer PDFs while ignoring headers, footers, or watermarks, and QR code/barcode scanning and generation across multiple protocols. [[[2]]()]() [[[3]]()]() Additional tools encompass formula recognition (convertible to LaTeX), text post-processing for layout parsing, and multilingual support covering over 100 languages with built-in engines such as PaddleOCR (higher accuracy) and RapidOCR (faster performance). [[[3]]()]() [[[2]]()]() The software provides flexible integration options, including command-line and HTTP interfaces for external use, and emphasizes no installation requirement—users can unzip and run it directly—along with GPU-accelerated rendering and customizable settings for themes, fonts, and OCR plugins.\n[[[2]]()]() While macOS compatibility remains a long-term development goal without current official support, the project focuses on Windows and Linux users seeking efficient, privacy-focused text extraction. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]()\n\n## Overview\n\n### Description\n\nUmi-OCR is a free and open-source optical character recognition (OCR) software that operates entirely offline without requiring an internet connection. [[[2]]()]() This design prioritizes user privacy by keeping all processing local and enables fast performance on sufficient hardware, which can surpass online OCR services in speed. [[[2]]()]() The software focuses on efficient batch text extraction from images, screenshots, and PDF documents, supporting common document handling needs such as excluding watermarks."
      ]
    },

      "url": "https://umi-ocr.com/",
      "title": "Umi-OCR | Offline OCR Software for Windows & Linux",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Umi-OCR\n\nHome Download Blog About [GitHub](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n\nEnglish\n\n# Umi-OCR Offline OCR Software for Windows & Linux\n\nUmi-OCR turns screenshots, images, and PDFs into text with 100% offline recognition - no uploads, no data leakage.\n\nDownload Umi-OCR [View on GitHub](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n\n41K+\n\nGitHub Stars\n\n100+\n\nLanguages\n\n100%\n\nOffline\n\n## Powerful Features\n\nEverything you need for text recognition\n\n### Screenshot OCR\n\nInstantly capture and recognize text from any area of your screen with a simple hotkey\n\n### Batch Processing\n\nProcess hundreds of images at once with high accuracy and no quantity limits\n\n### PDF Scanning\n\nExtract text from scanned PDFs and create searchable double-layer PDF documents\n\n### QR Code Recognition\n\nScan and decode QR codes and 19 types of barcodes from images\n\n### Math Formula\n\nConvert mathematical formulas in images to LaTeX format\n\n### 100% Offline\nAll processing happens locally on your computer - your data never leaves your device\n\n## Frequently Asked Questions\n\nIs Umi-OCR really free?\n\nYes, Umi-OCR is completely free and open-source under the MIT license. There are no hidden fees, subscriptions, usage limits, or watermarks.\n\nDoes it require an internet connection?\n\nNo, Umi-OCR works entirely offline. All text recognition is performed locally on your computer, ensuring your documents and images remain private.\n\nWhat's the difference between Paddle and Rapid versions?\n\nPaddle version offers higher accuracy with a larger model (~128MB), ideal for complex documents. Rapid version provides faster processing with a smaller footprint (~97MB), perfect for quick tasks.\n\nWhat languages does it support?\n\nUmi-OCR supports over 100 languages for text recognition, including English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, Russian, German, French, and most European languages.\n\nCan it recognize handwritten text?\nYes, Umi-OCR can recognize both printed and handwritten text, though accuracy may vary based on handwriting clarity and style.\n\nWhat are the system requirements?\n\nUmi-OCR runs on Windows 7 or later and Linux x64 (Debian). It requires approximately 200MB of disk space and works on both high-end and low-CPU systems.\n\n## Download Umi-OCR\n\nChoose your preferred version and start recognizing text today\n\n[Paddle Version (~128MB)](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases/download/v2.1.5/Umi-OCR_Paddle_v2.1.5.7z.exe) [Rapid Version (~98MB)](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases/download/v2.1.5/Umi-OCR_Rapid_v2.1.5.7z.exe)\n\nLatest Version: v2.1.5\n\nUmi-OCR\n\nFree, open-source offline OCR software\n\n[](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n\n### Quick Links\n\n* Home\n* Download\n* Blog\n\n### Legal\n\n* Privacy Policy\n* Terms of Use\n\n### Resources\n\n* [GitHub](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n* [Releases](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases)\n* [Issues](https://github.\ncom/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/issues)\n\nThis website is not affiliated with the official Umi-OCR project.\n\nUmi-OCR | Offline OCR Software for Windows & Linux"

需要什么电脑配置？\n\n👉 最低配置：i5 处理器 + 8GB 内存即可流畅运行。NVIDIA 显卡（可选）可开启 GPU 加速。\n\n#### 🤔 支持哪些操作系统？\n\n👉 Windows 7 及以上（x64）、macOS、Linux。\n\n#### 🤔 截图识别的快捷键是什么？能改吗？\n\n👉 PaddleOCR 是什么关系？\n\n👉 Umi-OCR 封装了 PaddleOCR 的 C++ 推理引擎，并提供了图形界面和批量处理能力，可看作是“PaddleOCR 的易用版套壳”。\n\n#### 🤔 在哪里下载？GitHub 地址是什么？\n\n👉 GitHub： `github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR` 。提供 `.zip` 绿色版，解压即用。\n\n### Say hello to productivity, goodbye to frustration!\n\n* * *\n\n## **What’s the Fuss About OCR?**\nEver tried to copy text from a photo of a whiteboard, a scanned receipt, or a PDF document only to realize it’s “just an image”? That’s where Optical Character Recognition (OCR) comes in. OCR tools _scan visuals_ and _magically extract text_ , letting you edit, format, or save it for later. But not all OCR tools are created equal. Enter **Umi-OCR** , the open-source superhero that works _offline_ , respects your privacy, and puts you in control.\n\n* * *\n\n### **Why Umi-OCR Stands Out: Key Features**\n\n1. **Works Offline** : No internet? No problem. Perfect for handling sensitive documents.\n2. **Open-Source** : Customizable, transparent, and community-driven geeks and non-geeks alike love it.\n3. **Supports PDFs, Images, & Screenshots** : Extract text from JPEGs, PNGs, scanned PDFs, and even your phone’s screenshots.\n\n* * *\n\n\\*\\*Real-Life Use Cases That’ll Make You \\* _”Finally! This Works!”_ \\* 🎉\\*\\*\n* **Convert Receipts to Spreadsheets** : Snap a photo of a receipt, extract text, and auto-populate expenses in Excel.\n* **Rescue Scanned Documents** : Turn a decade-old scanned PDF into a searchable Word file.\n* **Extract Quotes from Screenshots** : Save inspirational quotes from social media posts as plain text.\n* **Study Smarter** : Extract text from lecture slides or diagrams for notes.\n\n* * *\n\n### **How to Install Umi-OCR: 3-Minute Guide 🚀**\n\n1. **Download** : Head to Umi-OCR’s GitHub and grab the installer.\n2. **Install** : Follow the prompts no coding skills required!\n3. **Launch** : Drag & drop your image/PDF, hit “Extract,” and watch the magic happen.\n\n* * *\n\n### **Pro Tips for Power Users**\n\n* **Batch Processing** : Extract text from multiple files at once.\n* **Customize Output** : Save text as .txt, .docx, or even .csv.\n* **Combine with Tools** : Pipe extracted data into Notion, Excel, or your favorite app.\n\n* * *\n\n**The Offline Advantage: Privacy First!**"

Create model instance \n llm = LLM (\n    model = \"deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR\" ,\n    enable_prefix_caching = False ,\n    mm_processor_cache_gb = 0 ,\n    logits_processors = [ NGramPerReqLogitsProcessor ]\n)\n\n # Thanks to the [vLLM](https://github.\ncom/vllm-project/vllm) team for their help.\n* [2025/10/20]🚀🚀🚀 We release DeepSeek-OCR, a model to investigate the role of vision encoders from an LLM-centric viewpoint.\n\n## Contents\n\n* Install\n* vLLM Inference\n* Transformers Inference\n\n## Install\n\n> Our environment is cuda11.8+torch2.6.0.\n> \n> \n\n1. Clone this repository and navigate to the DeepSeek-OCR folder\n\n```\ngit clone https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR.git\n```\n\n1. Conda\n\n```\nconda create -n deepseek-ocr python=3.12.9 -y\nconda activate deepseek-ocr\n```\n\n1. Packages\n\n* download the vllm-0.8.5 [whl](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/tag/v0.8.5)\n\n```\npip install torch==2.6.0 torchvision==0.21.0 torchaudio==2.6.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118\npip install vllm-0.8.5+cu118-cp38-abi3-manylinux1_x86_64.whl\npip install -r requirements.txt\npip install flash-attn==2.7.3 --no-build-isolation\n```"
      ]

We plan to extend this to the full dataset and custom datasets, plus comprehensive comparisons with other OCR solutions including reasoning-based models.\n> \n> \n\n## 🎯 Project Overview\n\nThis repository provides a complete testing pipeline for evaluating DeepSeek OCR model against the OmniDocBench benchmark. Our current evaluation focuses on a representative subset to establish baseline performance and methodology before scaling to larger datasets.\n\n## Quick Start (3 commands)\n\n1. **Get DeepSeek API Key** : Visit [api.deepseek.com](https://api.deepseek.\ncom) and get your API key\n2. **Install Dependencies** :\n   \n   ```\n   pip install requests pillow\n   ```\n3. **Run Complete Test** :\n   \n   ```\n   python run_complete_test.py --api-key YOUR_API_KEY_HERE\n   ```\n\n## What This Does\n\n* ✅ **OCR Processing** : Converts 15-18 document images to markdown using DeepSeek API\n* ✅ **Evaluation** : Compares results against OmniDocBench ground truth\n* ✅ **Metrics** : Word overlap, structure detection, success rates\n\n## Files Overview\n\n* `run_complete_test.py` \\- Main orchestrator script\n* `deepseek_ocr_test.py` \\- DeepSeek API integration and batch processing\n* `evaluate_results.py` \\- Evaluation against ground truth\n* `OmniDocBench/` \\- Cloned evaluation repository with demo data\n\n## Evaluation Results\n\n**Performance on 18-image subset:**\n\n* **Overall Score** : 82.34 (vs 87.01 full benchmark)\n* **Text Edit Distance** : 0.089 (vs 0.073 full benchmark)\n* **Table TEDS** : 81.45 (vs 84.97 full benchmark)\n* **Formula CDM** : 79.12 (vs 83."

      "url": "https://deepseek-ocr.io/",
      "title": "DeepSeek OCR | Next-Gen Document Intelligence",
      "publish_date": "2025-10-21",
      "excerpts": [
        "16× convolutional compression to 64–400 tokens\n\nContext optical compression\n\n3\\. DeepSeek OCR MoE decoding (~570M active)\n\nFlashAttention acceleration\n\n4\\. Output structured HTML, Markdown, or captions\n\nLayout-preserving results\n\n## DeepSeek OCR Benchmark Comparisons\n\nBenchmark studies indicate DeepSeek OCR delivers state-of-the-art accuracy on structured documents while maintaining low token budgets.\n\n|OCR System |Accuracy Snapshot |Speed / Throughput |Core Strengths |Deployment |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|DeepSeek OCR |~97% exact match at 10× compression |~200k pages/day per NVIDIA A100 |Layout-rich OCR, tables, formulas, diagrams, multilingual |Open-source (MIT); Local GPU or DeepSeek API |\n|Google Cloud Vision |~98% on mixed benchmarks |Elastic cloud throughput |Enterprise support, multilingual APIs |Proprietary pay-per-use API |\n|AWS Textract |~97–99% on forms |Managed cloud scaling |Invoice & form extraction with JSON output |Proprietary pay-per-use API |\n|Azure OCR |~99.\n8% on clean typed text |Azure ecosystem integrations |Strong for printed pages; handwriting variance |Proprietary pay-per-use API |\n|Tesseract OSS |~90–95% depending on scans |Local CPU/GPU |Open-source, handwriting friendly |Open-source (Apache 2.0) |\n\nSources: Fox compression benchmark, OmniDocBench, AI Multiple accuracy reviews, DeepSeek documentation.\n\n## How to Use DeepSeek OCR\n\n01\n\n### Deploy DeepSeek OCR locally with GPUs\n\nClone the DeepSeek OCR GitHub repo, download the 6.7 GB safetensors checkpoint, and configure PyTorch 2.6+ with FlashAttention. Base mode runs on 8–10 GB GPUs, while Gundam tiling benefits from 40 GB A100s.\n\n02\n\n### Call DeepSeek OCR via API\n\nUtilize DeepSeek’s OpenAI-compatible API endpoints to submit images and receive structured text. Pricing mirrors the platform’s token billing (~$0.028 per million input tokens for cache hits).\n\n03\n\n### Integrate DeepSeek OCR into workflows\nConvert OCR outputs to JSON, link SMILES strings to cheminformatics pipelines, or auto-caption diagrams for bilingual publishing—all using DeepSeek OCR’s structured results.\n\n### DeepSeek OCR Operational Guardrails\n\n* Schedule latency-sensitive jobs on Base or Large modes; queue archival batches in Tiny mode to stretch GPU hours.\n* Pair DeepSeek OCR with retrieval-augmented generation pipelines to summarize lengthy documents while respecting layout context.\n* Review regional compliance when leveraging DeepSeek’s hosted API; local deployments avoid cross-border data exposure.\n* Combine with handwriting-focused engines (e.g., Tesseract) when cursive accuracy is a requirement.\n\n## DeepSeek OCR Use Cases\n\n### DeepSeek OCR for Scanned Books & Reports\n\nCompress thousands of words per page into compact tokens for downstream search, summarization, and knowledge graph pipelines.\n\n### DeepSeek OCR for Technical Diagrams & Formulas"
      ]

      "url": "https://deepseekocr.io/",
      "title": "DeepSeek-OCR - Free, Open Source, High Performance OCR Tool",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Effortlessly extract tables, charts, and key figures from dense financial reports, invoices, and bank statements. The high accuracy and layout-aware parsing reduce manual data entry, minimize errors, and accelerate financial workflows and analysis.\n\nFinancial Report Analysis with DeepSeek-OCR\n\nLLM Data Pipelines\n\n## Powering Next-Gen AI with Quality Data\n\nThe ultimate data source for LLMs.\n\nGenerate vast, high-quality datasets for training Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs). DeepSeek-OCR's efficiency makes it economically viable to process millions of documents, creating diverse and rich training corpora.\n\nDiverse Document Processing for LLMs\n\nDigital Archiving\n\n## Creating Efficient Digital Libraries\n\nArchive, search, and manage documents.\n\nTransform physical documents into a lightweight, searchable digital archive.\nThe optical compression ensures that storage requirements are minimized while the high-quality text extraction makes every document instantly discoverable.\n\nDigital Archiving with DeepSeek-OCR\n\n## A Breakthrough in Efficiency and Accuracy\n\nDeepSeek-OCR isn't just another text recognition tool. It's a fundamental rethinking of how machines process visual information, built to be both powerful and lightweight.\n\n📦\n\n### Contexts Optical Compression\n\nThe core innovation. We represent high-resolution documents with a fraction of the vision tokens, dramatically reducing computational costs and increasing processing speed.\n\n🎯\n\n### State-of-the-Art Accuracy\n\nAchieve up to 97% OCR precision on complex benchmarks. DeepSeek-OCR surpasses leading models while using significantly fewer resources.\n\n📄\n\n### Versatile Document Parsing\n\nGo beyond plain text. Intelligently extract data from complex layouts, including academic papers, financial charts, chemical formulas, and geometric figures.\n\n🚀\n### Built for Massive Scale\n\nEngineered for real-world deployment, capable of processing over 200,000 pages per day on a single GPU, making it ideal for large-scale data annotation and LLM training.\n\n🌍\n\n### Global Language Recognition\n\nTrained on a vast dataset covering nearly 100 languages, allowing for accurate text extraction from international documents without changing models.\n\n🧠\n\n### Low-Memory Architecture\n\nThe novel DeepEncoder design processes high-resolution images while maintaining low activation memory, enabling deployment on less powerful hardware and reducing operational costs.\n\n## Frequently Asked Questions\n\nWhat is DeepSeek-OCR? ▼\n\nDeepSeek-OCR is a new vision-language model that specializes in recognizing and extracting text and data from documents using an innovative technique called 'Contexts Optical Compression'. What makes 'Contexts Optical Compression' different? ▼\n\n...\n\nWe are developing an enhanced suite of tools built on DeepSeek-OCR, including API access and advanced features. Join the waitlist to get notified when we launch.\n\nJoin Waitlist\n\nDeepseek Ocr\n\ndeepseekocr.io\n\nAnalyze More. Compute Less. A showcase for the DeepSeek-OCR project.\n\nPROJECT\n\n[Official GitHub](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) [Research Paper](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR/blob/main/DeepSeek_OCR_paper.pdf) [DeepSeek-AI](https://www.deepseek.com/)\n\nLEGAL\n\nAbout This Site Contact Terms of Service Privacy Policy\n\nFRIENDS\n\n[Runpod](https://runpod.io?ref=5kdp9mps)"

      "title": "DeepSeek-OCR: New Open-source AI Model Goes Viral On GitHub - Dataconomy",
      "publish_date": "2025-10-21",
      "excerpts": [
        "[](https://dataconomy.com/)\n\nNo Result\n\nView All Result\n\n# DeepSeek-OCR: New open-source AI model goes viral on GitHub\n\n## DeepSeek-OCR's power lies in its ability to compress information. According to its creators, the model can take a 1,000-word article and compress it into just 100 visual tokens.\n\nby [Kerem Gülen](https://dataconomy.com/author/kerem-gulen/)\n\n[October 21, 2025](https://dataconomy.com/2025/10/21/deepseek-ocr-new-open-source-ai-model-goes-viral-on-github/)\n\nin [Artificial Intelligence](https://dataconomy.com/category/news/artificial-intelligence/) , [News](https://dataconomy.com/category/news/)\n\nA new open-source model named DeepSeek-OCR has been released, disrupting the traditional paradigm of large models. The model, which was open-sourced yesterday afternoon, has seen a meteoric rise in the AI community, gaining over 4,000 stars on GitHub overnight.\nThe core focus of DeepSeek-OCR is a novel visual approach to handling text, which promises to solve one of the biggest challenges in AI: long-context efficiency.\n\n## How DeepSeek-OCR changes the game\n\nThe new DeepSeek-OCR model is not just another text-reading tool. Its power lies in its ability to compress information. According to its creators, the model can take a 1,000-word article and compress it into just 100 visual tokens. This represents a staggering tenfold compression ratio with 97% accuracy. This efficiency is remarkable; a single NVIDIA A100 GPU can process 200,000 pages of data per day using the DeepSeek-OCR method. This new processing approach could signal a significant shift in the input methods used for large models.\n\nThe rapid traction of DeepSeek-OCR was amplified by high-profile endorsements. Andrej Karpathy, the co-founder of OpenAI and former Director of Autopilot at Tesla, shared his excitement about the paper.\nHe called DeepSeek-OCR a “good OCR model” and highlighted its more “interesting part”: the concept of a computer vision AI “masquerading as a natural language person.”\n\n### Stay Ahead of the Curve!\n\nDon't miss out on the latest insights, trends, and analysis in the world of data, technology, and startups.         "Search articles, topics, and guides Search\n\nDeepSeek\n\n# Run DeepSeek OCR Locally: Complete 2026 Guide\n\nPublished 23 Oct 2025 • Updated 30 May 2026 • 13 min read\n\nRun DeepSeek OCR Locally: Complete 2026 Guide\n\n**Quick answer.** DeepSeek-OCR runs locally on CPU or modest GPU via Ollama (ollama run deepseek-ocr, requires Ollama v0.13.0+) or direct PyTorch. 16 GB RAM minimum, 32 GB recommended; no GPU is required for small documents. The MIT-licensed model compresses pages roughly 10x while keeping about 97% accuracy on standard benchmarks.\n\n_Last updated: May 22, 2026._\n\nDeepSeek-OCR represents a revolutionary breakthrough in optical character recognition technology, introducing a paradigm shift from traditional text-based processing to visual token compression.\n\nReleased in October 2025, this open-source model achieves unprecedented efficiency by compressing documents up to 10 times while maintaining 97% accuracy.\nUnlike conventional OCR systems that process text sequentially, DeepSeek-OCR employs a vision-language approach that \"looks\" at entire documents, making it capable of processing over 200,000 pages per day on a single NVIDIA A100 GPU.​\n\n> **What changed in this 2026 refresh:** _Updated current-year references to 2026, noted DeepSeek V4's April 2026 release as broader context, and refreshed deployment guidance while preserving the original installation steps, benchmarks, and historical release dates._\n\nAs of 2026, DeepSeek-OCR remains the gold standard for self-hosted document OCR even after the release of DeepSeek V4 (April 2026), since DeepSeek V4 is a general-purpose LLM and does not replace the dedicated DeepSeek-OCR vision-language model described below. **Successor note:** On January 27, 2026 DeepSeek released [**DeepSeek-OCR-2**](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR-2?ref=codersera.com) , a 3B vision-language model with the new DeepEncoder V2 encoder that scores [91\\."

        "# Making sure you're not a bot!\n\nLoading...\n\nYou are seeing this because the administrator of this website has set up Anubis to protect the server against the scourge of AI companies aggressively scraping websites. This can and does cause downtime for the websites, which makes their resources inaccessible for everyone.\n\nAnubis is a compromise. Anubis uses a Proof-of-Work scheme in the vein of Hashcash, a proposed proof-of-work scheme for reducing email spam. The idea is that at individual scales the additional load is ignorable, but at mass scraper levels it adds up and makes scraping much more expensive.\n\nUltimately, this is a placeholder solution so that more time can be spent on fingerprinting and identifying headless browsers (EG: via how they do font rendering) so that the challenge proof of work page doesn't need to be presented to users that are much more likely to be legitimate.\nPlease note that Anubis requires the use of modern JavaScript features that plugins like JShelter will disable. Please disable JShelter or other such plugins for this domain.\n\nProtected by [Anubis](https://github.com/TecharoHQ/anubis) From [Techaro](https://techaro.lol) .
