        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nLingyvKong / **GOT** Public\n\nforked from Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 0\n* Star 0\n\n# LingyvKong/GOT\n\nmain\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[49 Commits](https://github.com/LingyvKong/GOT/commits/main/)\n\n 49 Commits |\n|GOT-OCR-2.0-master |GOT-OCR-2.0-master | | |\n|assets |assets | | |\n|GOT-OCR-2.0-paper.pdf |GOT-OCR-2.0-paper.pdf | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n\n### General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model\n\n[](https://huggingface.co/ucaslcl/GOT-OCR2_0) [](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.\n\n...\n\n* [2024/9/13]🔥🔥🔥 We release the [Huggingface](https://huggingface.co/ucaslcl/GOT-OCR2_0) deployment.\n* [2024/9/03]🔥🔥🔥 We open-source the codes, weights, and benchmarks. The paper can be found in this [repo](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/blob/main/GOT-OCR-2.0-paper.pdf) . We also have submitted it to Arxiv.\n* [2024/9/03]🔥🔥🔥 We release the OCR-2.0 model GOT!\n\n[Code License](https://github.com/tatsu-lab/stanford_alpaca/blob/main/LICENSE) [Data License](https://github.com/tatsu-lab/stanford_alpaca/blob/main/DATA_LICENSE)\n\n**Usage and License Notices** : The data, code, and checkpoint are intended and licensed for research use only. They are also restricted to use that follow the license agreement of Vary.\n\n## Community contributions\n\nWe encourage everyone to develop GOT applications based on this repo. Thanks for the following contributions :\n\n[Colab of GOT](https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1nmiNciZ5ugQVp4rFbL9ZWpEPd92Y9o7p?\n\n...\n\nThe first and second WeChat groups are full, please join the [third](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/blob/main/assets/wechat3.jpg) group if you are interested.\n\n## Acknowledgement\n\n* [Vary](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/Vary/) : the codebase we built upon!\n* [Qwen](https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen) : the LLM base model of Vary, which is good at both English and Chinese!\n\n## Citation\n\n```\n@article { wei2024general ,\n  title = { General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model } ,\n  author = { Wei, Haoran and Liu, Chenglong and Chen, Jinyue and Wang, Jia and Kong, Lingyu and Xu, Yanming and Ge, Zheng and Zhao, Liang and Sun, Jianjian and Peng, Yuang and others } ,\n  journal = { arXiv preprint arXiv:2409.01704 } ,\n  year = { 2024 } \n}\n @article { wei2023vary ,\n  title = { Vary: Scaling up the Vision Vocabulary for Large Vision-Language Models } ,\nauthor = { Wei, Haoran and Kong, Lingyu and Chen, Jinyue and Zhao, Liang and Ge, Zheng and Yang, Jinrong and Sun, Jianjian and Han, Chunrui and Zhang, Xiangyu } ,\n  journal = { arXiv preprint arXiv:2312.06109 } ,\n  year = { 2023 } \n}\n```\n\n## About\n\nOfficial code implementation of General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**0** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\nNo releases published\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 98\\.6%\n* HTML 1\\.4%\n\n## Footer"

    {
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/v4.57.0/en/model_doc/got_ocr2",
      "title": "GOT-OCR2 - Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# GOT-OCR2\n\n<div class=\"flex flex-wrap space-x-1\">\n<img alt=\"PyTorch\" src=\"https://img.shields.io/badge/PyTorch-DE3412?style=flat&logo=pytorch&logoColor=white\">\n</div>\n\n## Overview\n\nThe GOT-OCR2 model was proposed in [General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model](https://huggingface.co/papers/2409.01704) by Haoran Wei, Chenglong Liu, Jinyue Chen, Jia Wang, Lingyu Kong, Yanming Xu, Zheng Ge, Liang Zhao, Jianjian Sun, Yuang Peng, Chunrui Han, Xiangyu Zhang.\n\nThe abstract from the paper is the following:\n\n*Traditional OCR systems (OCR-1.0) are increasingly unable to meet people’snusage due to the growing demand for intelligent processing of man-made opticalncharacters. In this paper, we collectively refer to all artificial optical signals (e.g., plain texts, math/molecular formulas, tables, charts, sheet music, and even geometric shapes) as \"characters\" and propose the General OCR Theory along with an excellent model, namely GOT, to promote the arrival of OCR-2.0.\nThe GOT, with 580M parameters, is a unified, elegant, and end-to-end model, consisting of a high-compression encoder and a long-contexts decoder. As an OCR-2.0 model, GOT can handle all the above \"characters\" under various OCR tasks. On the input side, the model supports commonly used scene- and document-style images in slice and whole-page styles. On the output side, GOT can generate plain or formatted results (markdown/tikz/smiles/kern) via an easy prompt. Besides, the model enjoys interactive OCR features, i.e., region-level recognition guided by coordinates or colors. Furthermore, we also adapt dynamic resolution and multipage OCR technologies to GOT for better practicality. In experiments, we provide sufficient results to prove the superiority of our model.*\n\n<img src=\"https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/transformers/model_doc/got_ocr_overview.png\"\nalt=\"drawing\" width=\"600\"/>\n\n<small> GOT-OCR2 training stages.\nTaken from the <a href=\"https://huggingface.co/papers/2409.01704\">original paper.</a> </small>\n\nTips:\n\nGOT-OCR2 works on a wide range of tasks, including plain document OCR, scene text OCR, formatted document OCR, and even OCR for tables, charts, mathematical formulas, geometric shapes, molecular formulas and sheet music. While this implementation of the model will only output plain text, the outputs can be further processed to render the desired format, with packages like `pdftex`, `mathpix`, `matplotlib`, `tikz`, `verovio` or `pyecharts`.\nThe model can also be used for interactive OCR, where the user can specify the region to be recognized by providing the coordinates or the color of the region's bounding box.\n\nThis model was contributed by [yonigozlan](https://huggingface.co/yonigozlan).\nThe original code can be found [here](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0).\n\n## Usage example\n\n### Plain text inference\n\n```python\n>>> import torch\n\n...\n\nto(device)\n\n>>> generate_ids = model.generate(\n...     **inputs,\n...     do_sample=False,\n...     tokenizer=processor.tokenizer,\n...     stop_strings=\"<|im_end|>\",\n...     max_new_tokens=4096,\n... )\n\n>>> processor.decode(generate_ids[0, inputs[\"input_ids\"].shape[1]:], skip_special_tokens=True)\n\"on developing architectural improvements to make learnable matching methods generalize.\\nMotivated by the above observations, (...)\"\n```\n\n### Inference on a specific region\n\nGOT supports interactive OCR, where the user can specify the region to be recognized by providing the coordinates or the color of the region's bounding box. Here is an example of how to process a specific region:\n\n```python\n>>> import torch\n>>> from transformers import AutoProcessor, AutoModelForImageTextToText\n"
      ]

      "url": "https://blog.csdn.net/j8267643/article/details/152419074",
      "title": "【GitHub项目推荐--GOT-OCR 2.0：通用OCR理论，通过统一端到端模型实...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "【GitHub项目推荐--GOT-OCR 2.0：通用OCR理论，通过统一端到端模型实...\n（General OCR Theory）是一个革命性的开源OCR（光学字符识别）系统，代表了OCR技术的下一代发展。 该项目通过统一的端到端模型实现了OCR 2.0的愿景，将传统OCR、文档理解、格式恢复和多模态处理融合到一个统一的框架中。"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.aimodels.fyi/models/huggingFace/got-ocr20-ucaslcl",
      "title": "GOT-OCR2_0: Image-to-Text model — overview, use cases ...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## 🤖 AIModels.fyi\n\nCreators Models Papers Researchers [Notes](https://notes.aimodels.fyi) Sign in / sign up\n\n## AIModels.fyi\n\nCreators Models Papers Researchers Notes Sign in / sign up\n\n# [GOT-OCR2\\_0](https://huggingface.co/ucaslcl/GOT-OCR2_0)\n\nMaintainer: ucaslcl \\- Last updated 10/13/2024\n\n[Run on HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/ucaslcl/GOT-OCR2_0)\n\n🔍\n\nGet notified when new models like this one come out!\n\nStart Free Trial →\n\nHave an account? We'll apply the trial to it\n\n## Model overview\n\nThe `GOT-OCR2_0` model, created by maintainer `ucaslcl` , is an end-to-end optical character recognition (OCR) model that can handle a wide range of text formats, including plain text, formatted text, fine-grained OCR, and multi-crop OCR. This model is an advancement in OCR technology, building upon the previous \"OCR 1.0\" approaches by providing a more unified and robust solution.\nThe `GOT-OCR2_0` model is trained on a large dataset of cultural heritage archives, allowing it to accurately recognize and correct text from historical documents. It can handle a variety of input types, including images with noisy or degraded text, and provides high-quality output in markdown format. The model's capabilities are highlighted in its strong performance on benchmarks like [TextVQA](https://arxiv.org/abs/1904.08920) , [DocVQA](https://arxiv.org/abs/2007.00398) , [ChartQA](https://arxiv.org/abs/2103.04800) , and [OCRbench](https://arxiv.org/abs/2302.06627) , where it outperforms other open-source and commercial models.\n\n## Model inputs and outputs\n\n### Inputs\n\n* **Image file** : The model takes an image file as input, which can contain text in various formats, such as plain text, formatted text, or a mixture of text and other elements.\n\n### Outputs\n\n* **Markdown-formatted text** : The model's primary output is the text content of the input image, formatted in Markdown syntax.\nThis includes:\n  \n    + Detected text, with headers marked by `##`\n    + Mathematical expressions wrapped in `\\( inline math \\)` and `\\[ display math \\]`\n    + Formatting elements like bold, italic, and code blocks\n\nThe model can also provide additional outputs, such as:\n\n* **Fine-grained OCR** : Bounding boxes and text annotations for individual text elements in the image.\n* **Multi-crop OCR** : Detection and recognition of multiple text regions within the input image.\n* **Rendered HTML** : The formatted text output can be rendered as an HTML document for easy visualization.\n\n## Capabilities\n\nThe `GOT-OCR2_0` model excels at handling a wide range of text formats, including plain text, formatted text, mathematical expressions, and mixed-content documents. It can accurately detect and recognize text, even in noisy or degraded images, and provide high-quality Markdown-formatted output.\n\nOne of the key strengths of the `GOT-OCR2_0` model is its ability to handle historical documents.\n\n...\n\nOverall, the `GOT-OCR2_0` model represents a significant advancement in OCR technology, delivering robust and versatile text recognition capabilities that can benefit a wide range of industries and applications.\n\n* * *\n\nThis summary was produced with help from an AI and may contain inaccuracies—check the original source!\n\n1\\.0K\n\nLoading...\n\nAdd to bookmarks\n\n[Follow @aimodelsfyi on 𝕏 →](https://twitter.com/aimodelsfyi?ref_src=aimodelsfyi)\n\nOur Privacy Policy Terms of Service"
      ]

      "excerpts": [
        "GOT-OCR2_0: Image-to-Text model — overview, use cases ...\nGOT-OCR2_0 is a unified end-to-end optical character recognition model that performs plain text OCR, formatted text OCR with layout preservation, and fine-grained text detection with bounding boxes and color information in a single architecture."

      "url": "https://gigagpu.com/ocr-speed-benchmark-update-april-2026",
      "title": "description: Updated April 2026 OCR speed benchmarks for self-hosted models across GPUs. Covers PaddleOCR, Surya, GOT-OCR 2.0, and DocTR with pages-per-minute throughput and accuracy data.",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "---\ndescription: Updated April 2026 OCR speed benchmarks for self-hosted models across GPUs. Covers PaddleOCR, Surya, GOT-OCR 2.0, and DocTR with pages-per-minute throughput and accuracy data.\n---\n\n \n\n RTX 3050 - **[Order Now ](https://portal.gigagpu.com/index.php?rp=/store/dedicated-gpu-hosting/rtx-3050)** \n\n[Home](https://gigagpu.com) / [Blog](https://gigagpu.com/blog/) / [Benchmarks](https://gigagpu.com/blog/benchmarks/) / OCR Speed Benchmark Update: April 2026 \n\n Benchmarks \n\n# OCR Speed Benchmark Update: April 2026\n\nUpdated April 2026 OCR speed benchmarks for self-hosted models across GPUs. Covers PaddleOCR, Surya, GOT-OCR 2.0, and DocTR with pages-per-minute throughput and accuracy data.\n\nBenchmarks  April 16, 2026  2 min read  gigagpu \n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. [OCR Benchmark Update Overview](#ocr-benchmark-update)\n2. [Speed Results by Model and GPU](#speed-results)\n3. [Accuracy vs Speed Trade-offs](#accuracy-vs-speed)\n4. [Batch Processing Throughput](#batch-processing)\n5.\n\n...\n\n| Surya        | 42 pg/min | 72 pg/min  | 98 pg/min  | 65 pg/min    |\n| GOT-OCR 2.0  | 35 pg/min | 58 pg/min  | 82 pg/min  | 52 pg/min    |\n| DocTR        | 48 pg/min | 80 pg/min  | 110 pg/min | 72 pg/min    |\n| EasyOCR      | 55 pg/min | 92 pg/min  | 125 pg/min | 82 pg/min    |\n\nPaddleOCR v4 remains the speed leader, processing 140 pages per minute on an RTX 5090\\. The RTX 5090 provides a meaningful 35-40% speed uplift across all models.\n\n## Accuracy vs Speed Trade-offs\n\n| Model        | Speed (RTX 5090) | Overall F1 | Table Accuracy | VRAM Used |\n| ------------ | ---------------- | ---------- | -------------- | --------- |\n| PaddleOCR v4 | 140 pg/min       | 93.5%      | 89.8%          | 2.8 GB    |\n| GOT-OCR 2.0  | 58 pg/min        | 96.1%      | 94.5%          | 8.5 GB    |\n| Surya        | 72 pg/min        | 94.8%      | 91.2%          | 4.2 GB    |\n| DocTR        | 80 pg/min        | 92.0%      | 87.3%          | 3.5 GB    |\n\nGOT-OCR 2.\n0 delivers the highest accuracy but at the cost of speed and VRAM. For high-volume processing where 93%+ accuracy suffices, PaddleOCR v4 provides the best throughput. For accuracy-critical applications like financial documents, GOT-OCR 2.0 justifies the slower speed.\n\n## Batch Processing Throughput\n\nProcessing 10,000 pages end-to-end, including loading and post-processing:\n\n| Model / GPU             | Total Time | Effective Throughput |\n| ----------------------- | ---------- | -------------------- |\n| PaddleOCR v4 / RTX 5090 | 78 min     | 128 pg/min           |\n| GOT-OCR 2.0 / RTX 5090  | 185 min    | 54 pg/min            |\n| Surya / RTX 5090        | 148 min    | 68 pg/min            |\n\nBatch throughput is slightly lower than peak per-page speed due to overhead from loading pages and writing results. For large-scale document processing projects, see the [document processing throughput benchmark](https://gigagpu.com/document-processing-throughput-gpu/).\n## Cost per Page by Configuration\n\nBased on [GigaGPU hosting](https://gigagpu.com/dedicated-gpu-hosting/) rates, running 24/7:\n\n| Configuration                    | Pages/Month | Cost per Page |\n| -------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------- |\n| PaddleOCR / RTX 3090 ($175/mo)   | 3.67M       | $0.000048     |\n| PaddleOCR / RTX 5090 ($250/mo)   | 6.05M       | $0.000041     |\n| GOT-OCR 2.0 / RTX 5090 ($250/mo) | 2.33M       | $0.000107     |\n\nSelf-hosted OCR costs a fraction of a cent per page, orders of magnitude cheaper than cloud OCR APIs. Detailed cost modelling available in the [OCR cost per 10,000 pages](https://gigagpu.com/ocr-cost-per-10000-pages-gpu/) guide.\n\n### Process Documents at Scale on Your Own Hardware\n\nSelf-hosted OCR with no per-page fees. Proc"

        "# GOT-OCR2\n\n## Overview\n\nThe GOT-OCR2 model was proposed in [General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model](https://huggingface.co/papers/2409.01704) by Haoran Wei, Chenglong Liu, Jinyue Chen, Jia Wang, Lingyu Kong, Yanming Xu, Zheng Ge, Liang Zhao, Jianjian Sun, Yuang Peng, Chunrui Han, Xiangyu Zhang.\n\nThe abstract from the paper is the following:\n\n*Traditional OCR systems (OCR-1.0) are increasingly unable to meet people’snusage due to the growing demand for intelligent processing of man-made opticalncharacters. In this paper, we collectively refer to all artificial optical signals (e.g., plain texts, math/molecular formulas, tables, charts, sheet music, and even geometric shapes) as \"characters\" and propose the General OCR Theory along with an excellent model, namely GOT, to promote the arrival of OCR-2.0. The GOT, with 580M parameters, is a unified, elegant, and end-to-end model, consisting of a high-compression encoder and a long-contexts decoder.\nAs an OCR-2.0 model, GOT can handle all the above \"characters\" under various OCR tasks. On the input side, the model supports commonly used scene- and document-style images in slice and whole-page styles. On the output side, GOT can generate plain or formatted results (markdown/tikz/smiles/kern) via an easy prompt. Besides, the model enjoys interactive OCR features, i.e., region-level recognition guided by coordinates or colors. Furthermore, we also adapt dynamic resolution and multipage OCR technologies to GOT for better practicality. In experiments, we provide sufficient results to prove the superiority of our model.*\n\n<img src=\"https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/transformers/model_doc/got_ocr_overview.png\"\nalt=\"drawing\" width=\"600\"/>\n\n GOT-OCR2 training stages. Taken from the original paper. \n\nTips:\nGOT-OCR2 works on a wide range of tasks, including plain document OCR, scene text OCR, formatted document OCR, and even OCR for tables, charts, mathematical formulas, geometric shapes, molecular formulas and sheet music. While this implementation of the model will only output plain text, the outputs can be further processed to render the desired format, with packages like `pdftex`, `mathpix`, `matplotlib`, `tikz`, `verovio` or `pyecharts`.\nThe model can also be used for interactive OCR, where the user can specify the region to be recognized by providing the coordinates or the color of the region's bounding box.\n\nThis model was contributed by [yonigozlan](https://huggingface.co/yonigozlan).\nThe original code can be found [here](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0).\n\n## Usage example\n\n### Plain text inference\n\n```python\nfrom transformers import AutoModelForImageTextToText, AutoProcessor\n\nmodel = AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained(\"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2."

        "user=MVZrGkYAAAAJ&hl=en) , [Yuang Peng](https://scholar.google.com.hk/citations?user=J0ko04IAAAAJ&hl=zh-CN&oi=ao) , Chunrui Han, [Xiangyu Zhang](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yuB-cfoAAAAJ&hl=en)\n\nimage/jpeg\n\n## Usage\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs. Requirements tested on python 3.10：\n\n```\ntorch==2.0.1\ntorchvision==0.15.2\ntransformers==4.37.2\ntiktoken==0.6.0\nverovio==4.3.1\naccelerate==0.28.0\n```\n\n```\n`from  modelscope  import  AutoModel, AutoTokenizer\n tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained( 'stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR2_0' , trust_remote_code= True )\n model = AutoModel.from_pretrained( 'stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR2_0' , trust_remote_code= True , low_cpu_mem_usage= True , device_map= 'cuda' , use_safetensors= True , pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id)\n model = model. eval ().cuda()\n # input your test image image_file =  'xxx.jpg' # plain texts OCR res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type= 'ocr' )\n # format texts OCR: # res = model.\nchat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format') # fine-grained OCR: # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='ocr', ocr_box='') # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format', ocr_box='') # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='ocr', ocr_color='') # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format', ocr_color='') # multi-crop OCR: # res = model.chat_crop(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='ocr') # res = model.chat_crop(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format') # render the formatted OCR results: # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format', render=True, save_render_file = './demo.html') print (res)\n`\n```\n\nMore details about 'ocr\\_type', 'ocr\\_box', 'ocr\\_color', and 'render' can be found at our GitHub.\nOur training codes are available at our Its core advantages lie in extreme speed and extensive compatibility.\n\n**Supported Languages:** Default support for Chinese and English recognition. For other supported languages, please refer to the documentation: [Model List](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/model_list/) .\n\n**Project Origin:** Considering that [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) still has room for optimization in engineering aspects, we innovatively converted the models in PaddleOCR into the highly compatible ONNX format to simplify and accelerate the inference deployment of OCR models on various terminal devices.\nFurthermore, we achieved seamless cross-platform porting based on multiple programming languages such as Python, C++, Java, and C#, enabling developers to get started easily and integrate efficiently.\n\n**Name Implication:** The name \"RapidOCR\" embodies our core expectations for the product: **Rapid** (simple operation, fast response), **Good & Economical** (low resource consumption, high cost-effectiveness), and **Intelligent** (achieving accurate and efficient recognition relying on deep learning technology). We focus on leveraging the advantages of artificial intelligence to create compact yet powerful models, relentlessly pursuing speed while ensuring excellent recognition results.\n\n**User Guide:**\n\n* **Direct Deployment:** If the models provided in this repository meet your needs, simply refer to the [Official Documentation](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/quickstart/) to quickly complete the deployment and usage of RapidOCR.\n\n...\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\n### Contributing\n\nContributing\n\nActivity\n\nCustom properties\n\n### Stars\n\n**7k** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**58** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**664** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases 53\n\nRelease v3.9.0 Latest Jun 23, 2026\n\n\\+ 52 releases\n\n## Sponsor this project\n\n* buy_me_a_coffee [buymeacoffee.com/ **swhl**](https://buymeacoffee.com/swhl)\n* <https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/sponsor/>\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 75\\.8%\n* Jupyter Notebook 24\\.0%\n* Makefile 0\\.2%\n\n## Footer"

This release sets the PP-OCRv6 det small detection model and rec small recognition model as the default models. Benchmark metrics demonstrate that PP-OCRv6 delivers significantly better performance than PP-OCRv4.\n2. The total size of the whl package has increased from the previous 15 MB to 29 MB, resulting from the larger file sizes of the det small and rec small models.\n3. This version currently supports inference with only two engines: ONNXRuntime and OpenVINO. Support for Paddle, Torch, MNN and TensorRT will be added incrementally in subsequent releases.\n\n### 补充说明\n\n1. 该版本将 PP-OCRv6 的 det small 模型和 rec small 识别模型作为了 **默认模型** 。因为从指标来看，PP-OCRv6 效果远高于 PP-OCRv4。\n2. 整体 whl 包从之前的 15 MB → 29M，因为 det small 和 rec small 模型变大了。\n3.\n该版本目前 **仅支持了 ONNXRuntime 和 OpenVINO 两个推理引擎的推理** ，后续会逐步支持 Paddle, Torch, MNN 和 T ensorRT。\n\n* * *\n\n### 🚀 Features\n\n* feat(python): support PP-OCRv6 (onnxruntime + openvino, det + rec) by [@jaminmei](https://github.com/jaminmei) in [\\#692](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/pull/692)\n\n### 🎉 Contributors\n\n* Components for other languages have been migrated to separate repositories.\n\nFor the complete workflow on contributing to Python development, please refer to: **Python CONTRIBUTING** .\n\n### 🎖 Code Contributors\n\n[](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/graphs/contributors)\n\n### 🌟 Sponsors & Backers\n\nRapidOCR is an Apache2.0-licensed open source project with its ongoing development made possible entirely by the support of these awesome backers. If you'd like to join them, please consider [sponsoring RapidOCR's development](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/sponsor/) .\n\n#### Sponsors\n|Sponsors |Application |Introduction |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n|[](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/releases/download/v1.1.0/Quicker.jpg) |[Quicker](https://getquicker.net/) |Your fingertip toolbox |\n\n#### Backers\n\n|[](https://github.com/zg-young)  \n[@zg-young](https://github.com/zg-young) |[](https://github.com/Eunsolfs)  \n[@Eunsolfs](https://github.com/Eunsolfs) |[](https://github.com/youzzhang)  \n[@youzzhang](https://github.com/youzzhang) |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n\n### 📜 Citation\n\nIf you find this project useful in your research, please consider cite:\n\n```\n@misc { RapidOCR 2021,\n    title = { {Rapid OCR}: OCR Toolbox } ,\n    author = { RapidAI Team } ,\n    howpublished = { \\url{https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR} } ,\n    year = { 2021 } \n}\n```\n\n### ⭐️ Stargazers over time\n\n[Stargazers over time](https://starchart.cc/RapidAI/RapidOCR)\n\n### ⚖️ License\nThe copyright of the OCR model is held by Baidu, while the copyrights of all other engineering scripts are retained by the repository's owner.\n\nThis project is released under the Apache 2.0 license .\n\n## Footer"

      "url": "https://awesome.ecosyste.ms/projects/github.com/rapidai/rapidtable",

        "82.30\n  \n        88.05\n  \n        85.08\n  \n  \n  \n  \n        Ours (SynText)\n  \n        80.68\n  \n        85\n  \n  \n        82.97\n  \n        12.68\n  \n  \n  \n        Ours (MLT-17)\n  \n        84.54\n  \n        86.62\n  \n        85.57\n  \n        12.31\n\n```\n\n#### 可视化结果\n\nMethodsFPSSegLink [26]70.086d>77.08.9PixelLink [4]73.283.077.8TextSnake [18]73.983.278.31.1TextField [37]75.987.481.35.2MSR[38]76.787.87.481.7FTSN [3]77.187.682.0LSE[30]81.784.282.9<>CRAFT [2]78.288.282.98.6MCN[16]798883ATRR>[35]82.185.283.6PAN [34]83.884.484.130.2DB[12]79.291.584.932.0DRRG[41]82.3088.0585.08Ours (SynText)80.688582.9712.68Ours (MLT-17)84.5486.6285.5712.31\n\n### 与[TableStructureRec](https://github.com/RapidAI/TableStructureRec)关系\n\nTableStructureRec库是一个表格识别算法的集合库，当前有`wired_table_rec`有线表格识别算法和`lineless_table_rec`无线表格识别算法的推理包。\n\nRapidTable是整理自PP-Structure中表格识别部分而来。由于PP-Structure较早，这个库命名就成了`rapid_table`。\n\n总之，RapidTable和TabelStructureRec都是表格识别的仓库。大家可以都试试，哪个好用用哪个。\n由于每个算法都不太同，暂时不打算做统一处理。\n\n关于表格识别算法的比较，可参见[TableStructureRec测评](https://github.com/RapidAI/TableStructureRec)\n\n### 更新日志\n\n#### 2024.12.30 update\n\n- 支持Unitable模型的表格识别，使用pytorch框架\n\n#### 2024.11.24 update\n\n- 支持gpu推理，适配 rapidOCR 单字识别匹配,支持逻辑坐标返回及可视化\n\n#### 2024.10.13 update\n\n- 补充最新paddlex-SLANet-plus 模型(paddle2onnx原因暂不能支持onnx)\n\n#### 2023-12-29 v0.1.3 update\n\n- 优化可视化结果部分\n\n#### 2023-12-27 v0.1.2 update\n\n- 添加返回cell坐标框参数\n  \n- 完善可视化函数\n\n#### 2023-07-17 v0.1.0 update\n\n- 将`rapidocr_onnxruntime`部分从`rapid_table`中解耦合出来，给出选项是否依赖，更加灵活。\n\n- 增加接口输入参数`ocr_result`：\n  \n    - 如果在调用函数时，事先指定了`ocr_result`参数值，则不会再走OCR。其中`ocr_result`格式需要和`rapidocr_onnxruntime`返回值一致。\n  \n    - 如果未指定`ocr_result`参数值，但是事先安装了`rapidocr_onnxruntime`库，则会自动调用该库，进行识别。\n- 如果`ocr_result`未指定，且`rapidocr_onnxruntime`未安装，则会报错。必须满足两个条件中一个。\n\n#### 2023-07-10 v0.0.13 updata\n\n- 更改传入表格还原中OCR的实例接口，可以传入其他OCR实例，前提要与`rapidocr_onnxruntime`接口一致\n\n#### 2023-07-06 v0.0.12 update\n\n- 去掉返回表格的html字符串中的``元素，便于后续统一。\n  \n- 采用Black工具优化代码\n```\n\nEcosyste.ms\n\nTools and open datasets to support, sustain, and secure critical digital infrastructure.\n\nCode: [AGPL-3](https://github.com/ecosyste-ms/documentation/blob/main/LICENSE) — Data: [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)\n\n[](https://github.com/ecosyste-ms) [](https://mastodon.social/@ecosystems) [](https://opencollective.com/ecosystems)\n\n### Supported by\n\n[Schmidt Futures](https://www.schmidtfutures.org)\n\n[Open Source Collective](https://oscollective.org)\n\n[Become a sponsor](https://opencollective.com/ecosystems)\n\n* **[About](https://ecosyste.ms)**\n* **[Blog](https://blog.ecosyste.ms)**\n* **[Contact](https://mastodon.social/@ecosystems)**\n* **[Privacy](https://ecosyste.ms/privacy)**"

      "title": "RapidOCR vs Tesseract：2025年OCR工具性能深度测评",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "RapidOCR vs Tesseract：2025年OCR工具性能深度测评\n你是否还在为OCR识别准确率低、多语言支持差、部署流程复杂而烦恼？ 本文将通过实测对比RapidOCR与Tesseract两款主流OCR工具，帮助你快速选择最适合业务场景的解决方案。 读完本文你将了解：两款工具的核心差异、多场景准确率对比、性能测试数据及最佳部署"
      ]

      "url": "https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/",
      "title": "RapidOCR 开源 OCR 工具 - RapidOCR 文档 - rapidai.github.io",

      "excerpts": [
        "跳转至\n\n🎉 最新更新日志： v3.9.0\n\nRapidOCR 文档\n\nRapidOCR 开源 OCR 工具\n\n正在初始化搜索引擎\n\n[RapidAI/RapidOCR](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR \"前往仓库\")\n\n[](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCRDocs/blob/main/docs/index.md \"编辑此页\") [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCRDocs/raw/main/docs/index.md \"查看本页的源代码\")\n\n# 概览\n\n**_信创级开源 OCR - 为世界内容安全贡献力量_**\n\n[](https://clawhub.ai/rapidai/rapidocr) [](https://huggingface.co/spaces/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3) [](https://www.modelscope.cn/studios/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3.0.0/summary) [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/RapidAI/RapidOCR/blob/main/assets/RapidOCRDemo.ipynb) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/graphs/contributors) [](https://pepy.tech/project/rapidocr) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/rapidocr/) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/stargazers) [SemVer2.0](https://semver.org/) [](https://github.com/psf/black)\n\n### 📝 简介 ¶\n\nRapidOCR 是一款完全开源免费、支持离线快速部署的多平台多语言 OCR 工具，以极致的速度与广泛的兼容性为核心优势。\n\n**支持语言：** 默认支持中英文识别。其他支持的语言，参见文档： [模型列表](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/model_list/)\n**项目缘起：** 鉴于 [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) 在工程化方面仍有优化空间，为简化并加速 OCR 模型在各类终端设备上的推理部署，我们创新性地将 PaddleOCR 中的模型转换为高度兼容的 ONNX 格式，并基于 Python, C++, Java, C# 等多种编程语言，实现了跨平台的无缝移植，让开发者能够轻松上手、高效集成。\n\n**名称寓意：** RapidOCR 这一名称承载着我们对产品的核心期待——轻快（操作简便、响应迅速）、好省（资源占用低、成本效益高）且智能（依托深度学习技术，实现精准高效的识别）。我们专注于发挥人工智能的优势，打造小巧而强大的模型，始终将速度作为不懈追求，同时确保卓越的识别效果。\n\n**使用指南：**\n\n* **直接部署** ：若本仓库提供的模型已满足您的需求，只需参考 [快速开始](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/quickstart/) 即可快速完成 RapidOCR 的部署与使用。\n* **定制化微调** ：若现有模型无法满足特定场景需求，您可在 PaddleOCR 基础上，利用自有数据进行微调，再将优化后的模型应用于 RapidOCR 的部署流程，实现个性化定制。\n如果您觉得本项目对您的工作或学习有所帮助，恳请您不吝赐予一颗 ⭐ Star，给予我们宝贵的支持与鼓励！\n\n### 整体框架 ¶\n\n```\n---\nconfig:\n  fontFamily: \"LXGW WenKai Screen\"\n---\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph Step\n    direction TB\n    C(Text Det) --> D(Text Cls) --> E(Text Rec)\n    end\n\n    A[/OurSelf Dataset/] --> B(PaddleOCR) --Train--> Step --> F(PaddleOCRModelConverter)\n    F --ONNX--> G{RapidOCR Deploy\\n<b>Python/C++/Java/C#</b>}\n    G --> H(Windows x86/x64) & I(Linux) & J(Android) & K(Web) & L(Raspberry Pi)\n\n    click B \"https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR\" _blank\n    click F \"https://github.com/RapidAI/PaddleOCRModelConverter\" _blank\n```\n\n### 🎥 效果展示 ¶\n\nDemo\n\n### 👥 谁在使用？( [更多](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/discussions/286) ) ¶\n\n* [Docling](https://github.com/docling-project/docling)\n* [CnOCR](https://github.com/breezedeus/CnOCR)\n* [api-for-open-llm](https://github.com/xusenlinzy/api-for-open-llm)\n* [arknights-mower](https://github.com/ArkMowers/arknights-mower)\n* [pensieve](https://github.com/arkohut/pensieve)\n* [genshin\\_artifact\\_auxiliary](https://github.com/SkeathyTomas/genshin_artifact_auxiliary)\n* [ChatLLM](https://github.com/yuanjie-ai/ChatLLM)\n* [langchain](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain)"

        "**[Big news!](https://blog.tidelift.com/tidelift-joins-sonar?utm_source=libraries&utm_medium=referral) Sonar has entered a definitive agreement to acquire Tidelift!**\n\nToggle navigation \n\n* \n\n    + GitHub\n    + GitLab\n    + Bitbucket\n    + By logging in you accept  \n        our terms of service  \n        and privacy policy\n\n# rapidocr-onnxruntime  \nRelease\n1\\.4.4\n\nA cross platform OCR Library based on OnnxRuntime.\n\n[Homepage](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/rapidocr-onnxruntime/1.4.4/) Python\n\n* * *\n\nKeywords License\n    Apache-2.0\nInstall\n    `pip install rapidocr-onnxruntime==1.4.4`\n\n* * *\n\n### Documentation\n\n**_Open source OCR for the security of the digital world_**\n\n[](https://clawhub.ai/rapidai/rapidocr) [](https://huggingface.co/spaces/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3) [](https://www.modelscope.cn/studios/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3.0.0/summary) [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/RapidAI/RapidOCR/blob/main/assets/RapidOCRDemo.\nipynb) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/graphs/contributors) [](https://pepy.tech/project/rapidocr) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/rapidocr/) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/stargazers) [SemVer2.0](https://semver.org/) [](https://github.com/psf/black)\n\nJoin our [Discord](https://discord.gg/33eyQJq498)\n\n[简体中文](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/blob/main/README-CN.md) | English\n\n### 📝 Introduction\n\nRapidOCR is a completely open-source, free OCR tool that supports multi-platform, multi-language operation and rapid offline deployment. Its core advantages lie in extreme speed and extensive compatibility.\n\n**Supported Languages:** Default support for Chinese and English recognition. For other supported languages, please refer to the documentation: [Model List](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/model_list/) .\n\n**Project Origin:** Considering that [PaddleOCR](https://github.\ncom/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) still has room for optimization in engineering aspects, we innovatively converted the models in PaddleOCR into the highly compatible ONNX format to simplify and accelerate the inference deployment of OCR models on various terminal devices. Furthermore, we achieved seamless cross-platform porting based on multiple programming languages such as Python, C++, Java, and C#, enabling developers to get started easily and integrate efficiently.\n\n**Name Implication:** The name \"RapidOCR\" embodies our core expectations for the product: **Rapid** (simple operation, fast response), **Good & Economical** (low resource consumption, high cost-effectiveness), and **Intelligent** (achieving accurate and efficient recognition relying on deep learning technology). We focus on leveraging the advantages of artificial intelligence to create compact yet powerful models, relentlessly pursuing speed while ensuring excellent recognition results.\n\n**User Guide:**"

      "title": "Swift OCR Free Library - Scan, Recognize, or Extract Text from Images",

        "1. [Products](https://products.fileformat.com/)\n2. [OCR](https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/)\n3. [Swift](https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/swift/)\n4. SwiftyTesseract\n\n# Free Swift OCR Library to Perform OCR on Scanned Images & PDFs\n\n## Open Source Swift Optical Character Recognition (OCR) Library allows to Scan, Recognize, and Extract Text from Camera Photos Scanned Images & PDFs for free inside iOS and macOS\n\n## **What is SwiftyTesseract?**\n\nSwiftyTesseract is a premier, open-source OCR library designed specifically for the Swift ecosystem, empowering software developers to seamlessly integrate sophisticated optical character recognition into their iOS and macOS applications. By leveraging the powerful, industry-proven Tesseract OCR engine, it provides an intuitive and streamlined Swift interface for accurately extracting text from images, scanned documents, and various digital media.\nThis makes it an ideal foundation for developers building everything from simple text-reading utilities to complex data processing tools that require reliable text scanning and recognition capabilities within the Apple environment.\n\nThis library simplifies OCR integration by wrapping Tesseract's functionality into a straightforward and developer-friendly API. It offers extensive versatility right out of the box, including robust support for multiple languages—a critical feature for creating international applications. To ensure high accuracy, SwiftyTesseract incorporates essential image preprocessing capabilities that enhance text clarity before recognition. Furthermore, developers retain fine-grained control by customizing key Tesseract configuration options, such as the OCR engine mode and page segmentation mode.\nIts combination of ease of use, multi-language support, and deep customizability makes SwiftyTesseract a uniquely versatile and powerful tool for software developers tackling a wide spectrum of text recognition projects.\n\n* At a Glance\n* Platform Independence\n* Supported File Formats\n\n### At A Glance\n\nAn overview of SwiftyTesseract features.\n\nFeatures Overview\n\n* Make OCR Operations\n* Add OCR Capabilities\n* Recognize Image text\n* Image to Text Export\n* Recognize Font Text\n* Search PDF\n* Over 100 Languages\n* Create OCR apps\n* Save to browser\n* Extract Text\n* Multi-threading Support\n* Recognize images text\n\nSwiftyTesseract\n\nSwiftyTesseract supports popular image file formats listed below.\n\nReader\n\n* [PNG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/png/) , [JPEG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/jpeg/) , [BMP](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/bmp/) , [TIFF](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/tiff/) , TGA, DICOM\n\nWriter\n\n* [PNG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/png/) , [JPEG](https://docs.\nfileformat.com/image/jpeg/) , [BMP](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/bmp/) , [TIFF](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/tiff/)\n\nSwiftyTesseract\n\n### Platform Independence\n\nSwiftyTesseract can work with any Swift-based programming language\n\n* Swift 4.1 and above.\n\nSwiftyTesseract\n\nPrevious Next\n\n## Getting Started with SwiftyTesseract\n\nThe recommend way to install SwiftyTesseract is using CocoaPods. Please use the following command for a smooth installation.\n\n### **Install SwiftyTesseractvia CocoaPods**\n\n```\n pod 'SwiftyTesseract'\n```\n\nYou can also install it manually; download the latest release files directly from [GitHub](https://github.com/SwiftyTesseract/SwiftyTesseract/) repository.\n\n## Perform OCR on an Image via Swift Library\n\nThe open source SwiftyTesseract library makes it easy for software developers to load various types of images and perform OCR operation according to their needs inside Swift applications. You can use an image from your app's assets or any other source."
      ]

It is very fast, accurate and much less finicky.\n\n* * *\n\n[Deprecated](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/498de0e49d68c5f9c06a7ac7cc3077e81e0ef5fc97d977b5f041b6c7c2610d22/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f5374617475732d446570726563617465642d637269746963616c) [Carthage compatible](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/89a0af6192d5500d7a5b04e3b9fd8b5bee0a9dbd9467558a8025c0c2d8fd0e95/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f43617274686167652d636f6d70617469626c652d3442433531442e7376673f7374796c653d666c6174) [CocoaPods Compatible](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/cdab4b68646fbd17ab7149e5096b89754a551141817be0679730c38c3f26ce84/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f636f636f61706f64732f762f53776966744f43522e737667) [Platform](https://camo.githubusercontent.\ncom/b3f612c0e5fc72d00210c4dfe5cbd1428b3c6e797b93071378f68ddd659ef096/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f636f636f61706f64732f702f53776966744f43522e7376673f7374796c653d666c6174)\n\n# SwiftOCR\n\nSwiftOCR is a fast and simple OCR library written in Swift. It uses a neural network for image recognition.\nAs of now, SwiftOCR is optimized for recognizing short, one line long alphanumeric codes (e.g. DI4C9CM). We currently support iOS and OS X.\n\n## Features\n\n* Easy to use training class\n* High accuracy\n* Great default image preprocessing\n* Fast and accurate character segmentation algorithm\n* Add support for lowercase characters\n* Add support for connected character segmentation\n\n## Why should I choose SwiftOCR instead of Tesseract?\n\nThis is a really good question.\n\nIf you want to recognize normal text like a poem or a news article, go with Tesseract, but if you want to recognize short, alphanumeric codes (e.g.\ngift cards), I would advise you to choose SwiftOCR because that's where it exceeds.\n\nTesseract is written in C++ and over 30 years old. To use it you first have to write a Objective-C++ wrapper for it. The main issue that's slowing down Tesseract is the way memory is managed. Too many memory allocations and releases slow it down.\n\nI did some testing on over 50 difficult images containing alphanumeric codes. The results where astonishing. SwiftOCR beat Tesseract in every category.\n\n| |SwiftOCR |Tesseract |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n|Speed |0\\.08 sec. |0\\.63 sec. |\n|Accuracy |97\\.7% |45\\.2% |\n|CPU |~30% |~90% |\n|Memory |45 MB |73 MB |\n\n## How does it work?\n\n1. Input image is thresholded (binarized).\n2. Characters are extracted from the image, using a technique called [Connected-component labeling](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Connected-component_labeling) .\n3. Separated characters are converted into numbers, which are then fed into the neural network.\n\n## How to use it?\n\n...\n\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n```\n\n**NOTE** : This software depends on other packages that may be licensed under different open source licenses.\n\n## About\n\nFast and simple OCR library written in Swift\n\n### Topics\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**4\\.6k** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**150** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**477** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases 4\n\n1\\.2.1 Latest May 8, 2020\n\n\\+ 3 releases\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Swift 99\\.1%\n* Other 0\\.9%\n\n## Footer"
      ]

      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nDrcKarim / **SwiftOCRKit** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 0\n* Star 21\n\n# DrcKarim/SwiftOCRKit\n\nmain\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[16 Commits](https://github.com/DrcKarim/SwiftOCRKit/commits/main/)\n\n 16 Commits |\n|Sources/ SwiftOCRKit |Sources/ SwiftOCRKit | | |\n|Tests |Tests | | |\n|.gitignore |.gitignore | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|Package.swift |Package.swift | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|screenshot.png |screenshot.png | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* MIT license\n\n# SwiftOCRKit\nSwiftOCRKit is a lightweight, Apple-native Swift package that provides clean, async/await wrappers around Apple Vision for on-device OCR (Optical Character Recognition) on iOS and macOS.\n\nThe goal of the library is to simplify Vision's text recognition APIs while keeping everything:\n\n* **Apple-native** \\- Built on Apple Vision framework\n* **Privacy-friendly** \\- 100% on-device processing, no network calls\n* **Easy to integrate** \\- Simple async/await API\n* **Production-ready** \\- Proper error handling and configuration options\n\nSwiftOCRKit Screenshot\n\n## Features\n\n**Optical Character Recognition (OCR)**\n\n* Extract text from UIImage, NSImage, or CGImage\n* Async/await API\n* Configurable recognition levels (.accurate or .fast)\n* Language correction support\n* Custom language selection\n* Minimum text height filtering\n* Comprehensive error handling\n\n## Requirements\n\n* iOS 15.0+\n* macOS 12.0+\n* Swift 5.7+\n* Xcode 14.0+\n\n## Installation\n\n### Swift Package Manager\n\nAdd SwiftOCRKit using Xcode:\n\n1.\n**File → Add Packages**\n2. Enter the repository URL:\n   \n   ```\n   https://github.com/DrcKarim/SwiftOCRKit\n   ```\n3. Add the package to your target\n\nOr add it to your `Package.swift` :\n\n```\ndependencies: [ \n    . package ( url : \" https://github.com/DrcKarim/SwiftOCRKit \" , from : \" 1.0.0 \" ) \n ]\n```\n\n## Usage\n\nImport the library:\n\n```\nimport SwiftOCRKit\n```\n\n### Basic OCR Example\n\n```\n// iOS\n let image = UIImage ( named : \" document \" ) !\n let text = try await VisionOCR . recognizeText ( from : image ) \n print ( text ) \n\n// macOS\n let image = NSImage ( named : \" document \" ) !\n let text = try await VisionOCR . recognizeText ( from : image ) \n print ( text )\n```\n\n### Advanced OCR with Configuration\n\n```\nlet config = VisionOCRConfiguration ( \n    recognitionLevel : . accurate ,        // or .fast for speed\n    usesLanguageCorrection : true ,       // improves accuracy\n    minimumTextHeight : 0.02 ,            // filter small text\n\n...\n\n* Submit pull requests with improvements\n* Suggest new Vision-based features\n\nPlease keep changes focused and consistent with the existing API style.\n\n## License\n\nMIT License  \nCopyright (c) 2026 **Made with ❤️ by [Karim Bouchaane](https://github.com/DrcKarim)**\n\n## About\n\nFast and simple OCR library written in Swift clean async/await AI Vision utilities for iOS & macOS built on Apple Vision and Core ML\n\n### Topics\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nMIT license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**21** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases 11\n\nREmove folder Latest Feb 7, 2026\n\n\\+ 10 releases\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Swift 100\\.0%\n\n## Footer"

      "url": "https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/swift/evil",
      "title": "Read Numbers from ID Cards, Bank Cards & Documents via Free Swift OCR API",
      "publish_date": "2026-06-10",
      "excerpts": [
        "1. [Products](https://products.fileformat.com/)\n2. [OCR](https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/)\n3. [Swift](https://products.fileformat.com/ocr/swift/)\n4. Evil\n\n# Free Swift OCR API to Read ID & Bank Cards Numbers\n\n## Open Source Top Swift Optical Character Recognition (OCR) Library for Recognizing & Reading Numbers from ID Cards, Bank Cards, House Numbers & Documents via Free Swift API.\n\n## **What is Evil?**\n\nFor software developers building on Apple platforms, integrating Optical Character Recognition (OCR) into iOS and macOS applications is streamlined with the open-source Evil library. This Swift-based solution harnesses the on-device power of Apple's Vision framework to provide a high-level API for extracting text from images with remarkable efficiency.\nDesigned as a developer-first tool, Evil simplifies the complex process of adding robust OCR capabilities, enabling you to quickly recognize text from sources like bank cards, ID cards, or door numbers directly within your Swift projects.\n\nThe Evil library excels at extracting structured data by combining Swift-native performance with exceptional flexibility through Core ML model (.mlmodel) integration. This model-based architecture allows developers to deploy pre-trained models for immediate use or train and implement custom models tailored to specific recognition tasks. By offering simple APIs alongside flexible model deployment, Evil delivers a streamlined, high-performance OCR experience that is perfectly suited for creating sophisticated, on-device text extraction features in your mobile and desktop applications for iOS and macOS.\n\n* At a Glance\n* Platform Independence\n* Supported File Formats\n\n### At A Glance\n\nAn overview of Evil features.\n\nFeatures Overview\n* Add OCR Operations\n* Add OCR Capabilities\n* Recognize Image Text\n* Convet images of Text\n* Recognized Font Text\n* Search PDF\n* Over 100 Languages\n* Create OCR apps\n* Save to Browser\n* Extract Text\n* Multi-threading Support\n* Recognize Images Text\n\nEvil\n\nEvil supports popular image file formats listed below.\n\nReader\n\n* [PNG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/png/) , [JPEG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/jpeg/) , [BMP](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/bmp/) , [TIFF](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/tiff/) , TGA, DICOM\n\nWriter\n\n* [PNG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/png/) , [JPEG](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/jpeg/) , [BMP](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/bmp/) , [TIFF](https://docs.fileformat.com/image/tiff/)\n\nEvil\n\n### Platform Independence\n\nEvil can work with any Swift-based programming language\n\n* iOS 11.0+ / macOS 10.13+\n* Swift 4 and above.\n\nEvil\n\nPrevious Next\n\n## Getting Started with Evil\n\nThe recommend way to install Evil is using CocoaPods.\nPlease use the following command for a smooth installation.\n\n### **Install Evil Library via CocoaPods**\n\n```\n pod 'evil'\n```\n\n### **Install Evil Library via GitHub**\n\n```\n git clone https://github.com/getomni-ai/zerox.git\n```\n\nYou can also install it manually; download the latest release files directly from [GitHub](https://github.com/evilgix/Evil) repository.\n\n## Recognize Numbers in Images via Swift API\n\nBuilt entirely in Swift, Evil supports both iOS (11.0+) and macOS (10.13+), making it versatile for native Apple ecosystem development. The open source Evil library offers complete support for number recognition from structured data inside Swift applications. The primary goal of the Evil library is to make text recognition from images, such as bank cards, ID cards, and even door numbers, as straightforward as possible for software developers."
      ]

      "url": "https://qiita.com/shu223/items/f9c82b0b4e7bdb4e3c96",
      "title": "Swiftで書かれたOCRライブラリ「SwiftOCR」をiOS実機で試してみた #機械学習 - Qiita",
      "publish_date": "2016-08-25",
      "excerpts": [
        "154\n\nGo to list of users who liked\n\n144\n\n[](https://b.hatena.ne.jp/entry/s/qiita.com/shu223/items/f9c82b0b4e7bdb4e3c96 \"Hatena Bookmark\")\n\nAdd to Hatena Bookmark\n\nmore\\_horiz\n\ninfo\n\nMore than 5 years have passed since last update.\n\nDay 8 iOS Advent Calendar2016\n\n@shu223 (Shuichi Tsutsumi) in エンジニアと人生コミュニティ\n\n# Swiftで書かれたOCRライブラリ「SwiftOCR」をiOS実機で試してみた\n\n* iOS\n* 画像処理\n* 機械学習\n* DeepLearning\n* Swift\n\n154\n\nLast updated at 2016-08-25 Posted at 2016-08-25\n\nOCRエンジンをつくるのはそれなりに大変そうだし専門知識も必要そうなので、それがよほどのコア技術となるような会社／プロダクトでもない限りあまり自前実装をすることはなくて、どこかのライブラリを買ってくるか、オープンソースのものを使うか、ということがほとんどだと思います。で、 **iOSで無料で使えるOCRエンジン、となると実質 [Tesseract](https://github.com/tesseract-ocr/tesseract) 一択だった** のではないでしょうか。\n\nそんな状況の中、最近出てきたのが、 **Swiftで書かれたオープンソースのOCRライブラリ** 「SwiftOCR」です。\n\n* **Swift** 製\n* **高精度**\n* **高速**\n* iOS と OS X で使用可能\n\nをうたっていて、ライセンスはApache License, Version 2.0です。\n\n**画像認識にはニューラルネットワークを利用** しているとのこと。\n\nそしてREADMEに載っている以下の表がなんとも魅力的です。\n\n|SwiftOCR | Tesseract  \n\\------------ |------------ | -------------  \nSpeed | 0.08 sec.\n\n...\n\nixlib=rb-4.0.0&auto=format&gif-q=60&q=75&s=20d0a83656392c5e6a28f9ae1c6877a3)\n\n（\"TSUISUMSHUICHI\" 近い、が、間違えている）\n\nあれ、領域を最初から絞ってあげてるという好条件なのに、 **間違ってるし、全然ロバストじゃない** （ちょっと動くと認識結果がコロコロ変わる）・・・\n\nちなみにこの名刺のフォントはちょっと変わってますが、 **Core Image + Tesseractでかなりいい精度で認識** できてました。\n\nここでハタと気付きました。\n\nSwiftOCR.swift\n\n```\ninternal var recognizableCharacters = \"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789\"\n```\n\nサンプルのデフォルトでは **認識対象文字列に小文字アルファベットが入ってなかった** ということに。。\n\nというわけで、と小文字を追加して再挑戦・・・\n\nSwiftOCR.swift\n\n```\ninternal var recognizableCharacters = \"ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789\"\n```\n\n・・・と思いましたが、そのすぐ上にコメントでこう書いてありました。\n\nSwiftOCR.swift\n\n```\n///It **must** be in the **same order** as the network got trained\n```\n\n**学習（トレーニング）時と同じ順序** じゃないといけないよと。。 ¹\n\n##  学習（モデル作成）\n\nというわけで、リポジトリに同梱されている SwiftOCR Training というOS Xで動作する学習ツールでネットワークをつくりなおしました。\n\nBuild & Runで次のようなウィンドウが起動します。\n\n[](https://qiita-user-contents.imgix.net/https%3A%2F%2Fqiita-image-store.s3.amazonaws.\n\n...\n\nTesseractではもっとちゃんと認識できた的なことをちらっと上で書きましたが、そのときはTesseractの性能どうこうよりも、 **二値化の閾値を動的に計算** する処理を入れたときに精度にグッと改善した記憶があります。\n\nで、今回のCameraサンプルでは、ソースをさらっと見た限りだとGPUImageでグレースケール化してるだけっぽいので、まずその点でロバスト性を大きく欠いているのではないかという気がします。\n\nあとちゃんと見てないですが文字領域の切り出しがうまくいってるのかも気になります。そこはCore ImageのText Detectorでも補完できるので。\n\nそれとネットワークをTrainingする際に、自分の環境に入っているフォントが一通り列挙されて、それをもとに学習データが自動生成されるっぽかったですが、そんなんでいいのかなと。所詮個人の環境なので古今東西のフォントを網羅してるわけじゃないですし。それよりはちゃんとした人（専門家）がちゃんとしたデータでちゃんとつくったであろうTesseractのモデルの方が良さそうだなと。\n\n##  おわりに\n\n今回は残念ながら精度が出なかったわけですが、誤解しないでほしいのは、 **本記事ではあくまでサンプルをちょこっと試しただけ** であって、 **精度改善の余地はいくらでもある** 、ということです。本記事ではTesseractと同条件での比較も行っていませんし。\n\n上で書いたように\n* 学習をちゃんとする\n* 前処理をちゃんとする\n\nというだけで大いに改善するかもしれないですし、非常に可能性のあるライブラリだと思います。\n\n仕事でOCR的なことが必要になったら改めてもっとちゃんと触ってみようと思います。\n\n##  こちらもどうぞ\n\n* [Swiftで書かれた人工知能・機械学習ライブラリ「Swift-AI」をiOSで動かしてみる - Qiita](http://qiita.com/shu223/items/b8119de62209d1b8d78a)\n* [TensorFlowの学習済みモデルを拾ってきてiOSで利用する - Qiita](http://qiita.com/shu223/items/a2808e9dfccbd23387ff)\n* [機械学習はじめの一歩に役立つ記事のまとめ - Qiita](http://qiita.com/shu223/items/9cbde46c0690f1952750)\n* [iOSと機械学習](http://d.hatena.ne.jp/shu223/20141212/1418300463)\n\n1. 結果のラベルの順番が変わってしまうということだと思います。 ↩\n2. チマチマ打つのは面倒なのでstoryboardをいじりました。 ↩\n3. つくりなおした学習データがちゃんと効いてそうな結果だとは思います。 ↩\n\n154\n\nGo to list of users who liked\n\n144\n\ncomment 0\n\nGo to list of comments\n\nRegister as a new user and use Qiita more conveniently\n\n1. You get articles that match your needs\n2. You can efficiently read back useful information\n3. You can use dark theme\n\n[What you can do with signing up](https://help.qiita.com/ja/articles/qiita-login-user)\n\n \n\n154\n\nGo to list of users who liked\n\n144\n\nmore\\_horiz\n\nHow developers code is here."

    {
      "url": "https://transloadit.com/devtips/ocr-in-ios-a-swift-developer-s-guide",
      "title": "OCR in iOS: a Swift developer's guide | Transloadit",
      "publish_date": "2025-02-05",
      "excerpts": [
        "* Posts\n* Tags\n* DevTimes\n* Case Studies\n* DevTips\n* Press\n* Jobs\n\n##### On this page:\n\n1. Requirements\n2. Setup\n3. Core OCR implementation\n4. Camera integration\n5. Results display\n6. Performance optimization\n7. Testing\n8. Troubleshooting\n\nLast updated:\n\n# Building OCR capabilities in Swift for iOS\n\nTim Koschützki\n\nCo-founder\n · \nBerlin, Germany\n · Show bio ·\n\n[](https://twitter.com/tim_kos) [](https://github.com/tim-kos)\n\nTim is a skilled programmer with close to 20 years of experience in web and application development.\nPartway through his career, he became a core contributor to the CakePHP framework. Tim wears many\nhats here at Transloadit. This includes working on our API services, Robots and scaling algorithms,\nsupervising customer support, handling customer billing, and much more. Tim has a passion for German\nEngineering™, which is reflected clearly in his work for Transloadit and [his open source projects](https://github.com/tim-kos) .\nOutside the digital world, Tim is also a\nlicensed pyrotechnician. So when he's not causing fireworks on your screen, he can often be found\ncreating spectacular displays that light up the skies.\n\nOptical Character Recognition (OCR) brings powerful capabilities to mobile applications, enabling\ntext extraction from images and documents. In this guide, we demonstrate how to integrate OCR into\niOS using the SwiftyTesseract SDK combined with modern image processing techniques for optimal\naccuracy.\n\n## Requirements\n\n* iOS 13.0 or later (full Apple Silicon support)\n* Xcode 14.0+\n* Swift 5.5+\n\n## Setup\n\nAdd SwiftyTesseract to your project using Swift Package Manager:\n\n```\n// Package.swift \ndependencies : [ . package ( url : \"https://github.com/SwiftyTesseract/SwiftyTesseract.git\" , . upToNextMajor ( from : \"4.0.1\" ) ) ]\n```\n\n## Core OCR implementation\n\nCreate an OCR processor that leverages the SwiftyTesseract SDK for text extraction:\n\n```\nimport Vision import SwiftyTesseract import UIKit class OCRProcessor { private let  tesseract : Tesseract init ( ) throws { \n        tesseract  = try Tesseract ( language : . english ) } func extractText ( from image : UIImage ) async throws -> String { return try await  tesseract . performOCR ( on :  image ) } func preprocessImage ( _  image : UIImage ) throws -> UIImage { guard let  cgImage  =  image . cgImage  else { throw OCRError . processingFailed\n         } let  ciImage  = CIImage ( cgImage :  cgImage ) let  context  = CIContext ( ) guard let  filter  = CIFilter ( name : \"CIColorControls\" ) else { throw OCRError . processingFailed\n         } \n        filter . setValue ( ciImage ,  forKey : kCIInputImageKey ) \n        filter . setValue ( 1.1 ,  forKey : kCIInputContrastKey ) \n        filter . setValue ( 0.0 ,  forKey : kCIInputBrightnessKey ) \n        filter . setValue ( 0.0 ,  forKey : kCIInputSaturationKey ) guard let  outputImage  =  filter ."

Zero GPU required, LaTeX math support, concurrent processing, resume from breakpoint.\n\n## Contents\n\n* Features\n* Use Cases\n* Why GLM?\n  \n    + Pricing\n    + Comparison Summary\n    + Why Cloud API?\n* Quick Start\n* Studio Prototype\n* Output Structure\n* Verification and Failure Semantics\n* Common Failure Modes and Recommended Handling\n* Recommended Workflow for Personal Knowledge Pipelines\n* Reference-book Metadata and Downstream Note Workflow\n* Clean Junk Images\n* Configuration\n* Requirements\n* Known Limitations\n* License\n* Appendix: Detailed Benchmark Test Results\n* Appendix: API Pricing Details\n\n## Features\n\n* **PDF / PPT / PPTX -> Markdown** : segment-based OCR with failure-safe fallback ( `PDF upload -> per-page image -> native PDF text fallback` )\n* **Handwriting recognition** : `--handwrite` mode uses ZhipuAI handwriting OCR API for handwritten content\n* **Concurrent processing** : when a file has multiple segments, processes 2 segments in parallel (GLM-OCR API max concurrency is 2)\n* **Long screenshot support** : auto-splits tall images (e.g. chat screenshots) into overlapping segments (text OCR only, no image extraction)\n* **Resume from breakpoint** : already-completed segments are skipped on re-run\n* **Image extraction** : embedded images are saved to `images/` subfolder\n* **Explicit failure reporting** : segments that still fail after fallback are recorded in `_failed_segments/*.failed.json` and are never silently treated as complete\n* **Strict validation** : `verify_ocr.py` checks directory/md/page coverage/failure reports, and `audit_ocr_integrity.py` catches legacy mixed-output problems\n* **Clean output tree** : converted PPT PDFs are cached under `_cache/ppt_pdf/` instead of being mixed into `output/`\n* **Markdown cleanup at the OCR boundary** : normalizes OCR math delimiters so `$ 2x+1 $` becomes `$2x+1$` before downstream note generation\n* **Suspicious math-command audit** : can emit a report of suspicious commands still left after cleanup, so the remaining OCR edge cases are explicit\n* **Visual-block suppression** : filters header/footer strips, page-number corners, logos, watermarks, and glyph-fragment crops before they enter `images/`"

It introduces Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) loss and stable full-task reinforcement learning to improve training efficiency, recognition accuracy, and generalization.\nThe model integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder. Combined with a two-stage pipeline of layout analysis and parallel recognition based on PP-DocLayout-V3, GLM-OCR delivers robust and high-quality OCR performance across diverse document layouts.\n\n**Key Features**\n\n* **State-of-the-Art Performance** : Achieves a score of 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking #1 overall, and delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks, including formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.\n* **Optimized for Real-World Scenarios** : Designed and optimized for practical business use cases, maintaining robust performance on complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals, and other challenging real-world layouts.\n* **Efficient Inference** : With only 0."

      "url": "https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/1792/local-glm-ocr-pdf-markdown-obsidian-llm-wiki",
      "title": "Local GLM-OCR: Convert PDF to Markdown and Build Your AI Knowledge Base with Obsidian (2026 Guide)",

        "Skip to content Skip to navigation Skip to footer\n\nP. Pillitteri\n\n[](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/1782/how-to-use-github-beginner-guide-2026 \"Previous\") [](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/1802/best-claude-skills-mobile-development-2026 \"Next\")\n\n# Local GLM-OCR: Convert PDF to Markdown and Build Your AI Knowledge Base with Obsidian (2026 Guide)\n\n##### Z.AI GLM-OCR runs locally with 0.9B parameters and beats Gemini 3 Pro. Practical guide to convert PDFs to Markdown and build a private AI knowledge base with Obsidian and Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern.\n\nA 0.9 billion parameter model has just outperformed Gemini 3 Pro and GPT-5.2 on the reference benchmark for document parsing, OmniDocBench V1.5, scoring 94.62 points against rivals one hundred times its size. It is called **GLM-OCR** , the Chinese laboratory Z.\nAI (formerly Zhipu AI) released it on February 3, 2026 with an MIT license, it runs on a video card with 4 GB of VRAM, and it converts a 47-page PDF into clean Markdown in under 30 seconds. In this guide I show you the exact stack I use every day to turn PDFs, slides, and screenshots into a queryable personal knowledge base, built on the Obsidian vault and managed entirely by AI following the _LLM Wiki_ pattern that Andrej Karpathy made famous in April 2026. The result is a memory that organizes itself, lives on your disk without sending a single byte to the cloud, and grows every time you drop in a new document.\n\nGLM-OCR by Z.AI converts PDFs to Markdown at 1.86 pages per second, Obsidian turns them into a queryable wiki\n\n> Introducing GLM-OCR: SOTA performance, optimized for complex document understanding. With only 0.\n9B parameters, GLM-OCR delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks, including formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.\n> \n> — Z.ai (@Zai\\_org) [February 3, 2026](https://twitter.com/Zai_org/status/2018520052941656385)\n\n## What GLM-OCR Is and Why Such a Small Model Changes the Rules\n\n**GLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR (Optical Character Recognition) model developed by the Chinese laboratory Z.AI** and available as open weights on Hugging Face since February 2026. Its strength is not the size; in fact, with just 0.9 billion parameters it occupies less than 2 GB on disk and runs on a consumer 4 GB VRAM graphics card in FP16 precision. The surprise comes from the [OmniDocBench V1.5](https://github.com/opendatalab/OmniDocBench) benchmark, the de facto standard for document parsing published by OpenDataLab: GLM-OCR closed at 94.62 points, surpassing both closed-source giants like Gemini 3 Pro and GPT-5."

      "url": "https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/03/15/zhipu-ai-introduces-glm-ocr-a-0-9b-multimodal-ocr-model-for-document-parsing-and-key-information-extraction-kie/",
      "title": "Zhipu AI Introduces GLM-OCR: A 0.9B Multimodal OCR Model for ...",
      "publish_date": "2026-03-15",
      "excerpts": [
        "Search\n\n[Home](https://www.marktechpost.com/) [Technology](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/ \"View all posts in Technology\") [Artificial Intelligence](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/artificial-intelligence/ \"View all posts in Artificial Intelligence\") Zhipu AI Introduces GLM-OCR: A 0.9B Multimodal OCR Model for Document Parsing...\n\n* [Technology](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/)\n* [Artificial Intelligence](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/artificial-intelligence/)\n* [Editors Pick](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/editors-pick/)\n* [Language Model](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/artificial-intelligence/language-model/)\n* [New Releases](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/editors-pick/new-releases/)\n* [OCR](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/artificial-intelligence/language-model/ocr/)\n* [Uncategorized](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/uncategorized/)\n# Zhipu AI Introduces GLM-OCR: A 0.9B Multimodal OCR Model for Document Parsing and Key Information Extraction (KIE)\n\nBy\n\n[Asif Razzaq](https://www.marktechpost.com/author/6flvq/)\n\n\\-\n\nMarch 15, 2026\n\n**Why Document OCR Still Remains a Hard Engineering Problem** ? What does it take to make OCR useful for real documents instead of clean demo images? And can a compact multimodal model handle parsing, tables, formulas, and structured extraction without turning inference into a resource bonfire?\n\nThat is the problem targeted by **GLM-OCR** , introduced by researchers from **Zhipu AI and Tsinghua University** . The research team presents GLM-OCR as a **0\\.9B-parameter compact multimodal model** for document understanding. It combines a **0\\.4B CogViT visual encoder** , a lightweight cross-modal connector, and a **0\\.5B GLM language decoder** . The stated goal is to balance document recognition quality with lower latency and lower computational cost than larger multimodal systems.\nTraditional OCR systems are often good at plain text transcription, but they struggle when documents contain mixed layouts, tables, formulas, code blocks, seals, and structured fields. Recent multimodal large language models improve document understanding, but the research team argue that their size and standard autoregressive decoding make them expensive for edge deployment and large-scale production. GLM-OCR is positioned as a smaller system built for these deployment constraints rather than as a general-purpose vision-language model adapted to OCR as an afterthought.\n\n### **A Compact Architecture Built for OCR Workloads**\n\nA key technical point for this research is the use of **Multi-Token Prediction (MTP)** . Standard autoregressive decoding predicts one token at a time, which is not ideal for OCR-style tasks where outputs are often deterministic and locally structured. GLM-OCR instead predicts multiple tokens per step."

        "glm-ocr :latest\n\n5\\.6M Downloads Updated 4 months ago\n\n## GLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture.\n\nGLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture.\n\nCancel\n\nvision tools\n\nCLI cURL Python JavaScript\n\n[Documentation](https://github.com/ollama/ollama-python) [Documentation](https://github.com/ollama/ollama-js)\n\n```\nollama run glm-ocr\n```\n\n```\ncurl http://localhost:11434/api/chat \\\n  -d '{\n    \"model\": \"glm-ocr\",\n    \"messages\": [{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"Hello!\"}]\n  }'\n```\n\n```\nfrom ollama import chat\n\nresponse = chat(\n    model= 'glm-ocr' ,\n    messages=[{ 'role' : 'user' , 'content' : 'Hello!' }],\n)\n print (response.message.content)\n```\n\n```\nimport ollama from 'ollama' \n\n const response = await ollama.chat({\n  model: 'glm-ocr' ,\n  messages: [{role: 'user' , content: 'Hello!' }],\n})\nconsole.log(response.message.content)\n```\n\n## Details\nUpdated 4 months ago\n\n4 months ago\n\n6effedd0dc8a · 2.2GB ·\n\nmodel\n\narch glmocr\n\n·\n\nparameters 1\\.11B\n\n·\n\nquantization F16\n\n2\\.2GB\n\nparams\n\n{\n\"temperature\": 0\n}\n\n18B\n\n## Readme\n\nlogo.svg\n\nGLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture. The model integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder.\n\nimage.png\n\n### Usage\n\n#### Text recognition\n\n```\nollama run glm-ocr Text Recognition: ./image.png\n```\n\n#### Table recognition\n\n```\nollama run glm-ocr Table Recognition: ./image.png\n```\n\n#### Figure recognition\n\n```\nollama run glm-ocr Figure Recognition: ./image.png\n```\n\n### Key features\n\n* **State-of-the-Art Performance** : Achieves a score of 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking #1 overall, and delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks,\nincluding formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.\n* **Optimized for Real-World Scenarios** : Designed and optimized for practical business use cases, maintaining robust performance on complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals, and other challenging real-world layouts.\n* **Efficient Inference** : With only 0.9B parameters, GLM-OCR supports deployment via vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama, significantly reducing inference latency and compute cost, making it ideal for high-concurrency services and edge deployments.\n* **Easy to Use** : Fully open-sourced and equipped with a comprehensive [SDK](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR) and inference toolchain, offering simple installation, one-line invocation, and smooth integration into existing production pipelines.\n\nWrite Preview\n\n!logo.svg\nGLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture."

    {
      "url": "https://aindev.ru/news/619-zhipu-ai-glm-ocr",
      "title": "Zhipu AI представила GLM-OCR: мультимодальную OCR-модель для анализа документов",
      "publish_date": "2026-03-15",
      "excerpts": [
        "Перейти к содержимому\n\n[AInDev.ru Разработка в эпоху ИИ](https://aindev.ru)\n\n[Generative AI](https://aindev.ru/category/generative-ai) 15\\.03.2026 11:47\n\n# Zhipu AI представила GLM-OCR: мультимодальную OCR-модель для анализа документов\n\nКомпания Zhipu AI представила GLM-OCR — мультимодальную модель с 900 миллионами параметров, специализирующуюся на анализе документов и извлечении ключевых данных (KIE). Разработка базируется на архитектуре General Language Model (GLM) и объединяет классические методы оптического распознавания символов с современными подходами к глубокому пониманию естественного языка.\n\n## Технологические особенности реализации\n\nОсновная задача GLM-OCR заключается в обработке сложных визуальных структур, типичных для повседневного документооборота. Благодаря мультимодальному подходу модель анализирует не только текстовое содержимое, но и визуальную составляющую файла.\nПроще говоря, алгоритм учитывает взаимное расположение элементов на странице, что позволяет корректно интерпретировать таблицы, списки и маркированные перечни, сохраняя логические связи между данными.\n\nНа практике это означает, что модель эффективно работает с разнородными задачами: от распознавания текста на отсканированных копиях и PDF-файлах до структурирования информации из нестандартных форматов. Технически модель оптимизирована для оперативного выполнения вычислений, что критически важно при работе с массивами данных в таких требовательных к точности сферах, как финансовый сектор, юриспруденция, медицина и государственное управление.\n\n## Функциональные возможности и языковая экспертиза\n\nВ архитектуру заложена поддержка нескольких языков, что расширяет границы применения системы в глобальном контексте.\nВ отличие от традиционных решений, направленных на простое распознавание символов, GLM-OCR акцентирована на извлечении конкретных сущностей — имён, дат, финансовых показателей и специфических параметров, заданных пользователем.\n\nЕсли коротко, Zhipu AI переходит от стандартного OCR к созданию интеллектуальных систем анализа, которые воспринимают документ как единое целое, а не как набор разрозненных букв. Внедрение подобных решений значительно снижает потребность в ручном вводе данных и оптимизирует рабочие процессы, автоматизируя верификацию и систематизацию информации в документах сложной верстки.\n\nДанный подход отражает актуальный тренд в разработке искусственного интеллекта — создание специализированных моделей, способных оперировать одновременно текстовыми и графическими данными для достижения максимальной точности."
      ]

      "title": "GLM-OCR — компактная модель для распознавания сложных документов — ИИ: События — NeuraBooks",
      "publish_date": "2026-03-26",

\"Поделиться в VK\")\n\nОпубликовано 3 февраля 2026\n# GLM-OCR — компактная модель для распознавания сложных документов\n\nGLM-OCR: маленькая модель, которая читает документы лучше больших\n\nНовая       "url": "https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/model_doc/glm_ocr",
      "title": "GLM-OCR · Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# GLM-OCR\n\n## Overview\n\n[GLM-OCR](https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-OCR) is a multimodal OCR (Optical Character Recognition) model designed for complex document understanding from [Z.ai](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR). The model combines a CogViT visual encoder (pre-trained on large-scale image-text data), a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder.\n\nKey features of GLM-OCR include:\n- **Lightweight**: Only 0.9B parameters while achieving state-of-the-art performance (94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5)\n- **Multi-task**: Excels at text recognition, formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction\n- **Multi-modal**: Processes document images for text, formula, and table extraction\n\nThis model was contributed by the [zai-org](https://huggingface.co/zai-org) team.\nThe original code can be found [here](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR).\n\n## Usage example\n\n### Single image inference\n\n```python\nfrom transformers import AutoProcessor, GlmOcrForConditionalGeneration\n\nmodel_id = \"zai-org/GLM-OCR\"\n\nprocessor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(model_id)\nmodel = GlmOcrForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(\n    model_id,\n    device_map=\"auto\",\n)\n\nmessages = [\n    {\n        \"role\": \"user\",\n        \"content\": [\n            {\"type\": \"image\", \"url\": \"https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/transformers/tasks/car.jpg\"},\n            {\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"Text Recognition:\"},\n        ],\n    }\n]\n\ninputs = processor.apply_chat_template(\n    messages,\n    tokenize=True,\n    add_generation_prompt=True,\n    return_dict=True,\n    return_tensors=\"pt\",\n).to(model.device)\n\noutput = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=512)\nprint(processor.decode(output[0], skip_special_tokens=True))\n```\n\n### Batch inference\n\nThe model supports batching multiple images for efficient processing.\n\n```python\nfrom transformers import AutoProcessor, GlmOcrForConditionalGeneration\n\nmodel_id = \"zai-org/GLM-OCR\"\n\nprocessor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(model_id)\nmodel = GlmOcrForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained(\n    model_id,\n    device_map=\"auto\",\n)\n\n# First document\nmessage1 = [\n    {\n        \"role\": \"user\",\n        \"content\": [\n            {\"type\": \"image\", \"url\": \"https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/transformers/tasks/car.jpg\"},\n            {\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"Text Recognition:\"},\n        ],\n    }\n]\n\n# Second document\nmessage2 = [\n    {\n        \"role\": \"user\",\n        \"content\": [\n            {\"type\": \"image\", \"url\": \"https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/bee.jpg\"},\n            {\"type\": \"text\", \"text\": \"Text Recognition:\"},\n        ],\n    }\n]\n\nmessages = [message1, message2]\n\ninputs = processor.apply_chat_template(\n    messages,\n    tokenize=True,\n    add_generation_prompt=True,"
      ]
    },

The model combines a CogViT visual encoder (pre-trained on large-scale image-text data), a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder.\n\nKey features of GLM-OCR include:\n\n* **Lightweight** : Only 0.9B parameters while achieving state-of-the-art performance (94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5)\n* **Multi-task** : Excels at text recognition, formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction"

Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) triplet paradigm, which simplifies the multi-tool pipeline of modular approaches while avoiding the inefficiency of using large multimodal models for full-page approximately 1.6% drop in performance.\n    * On olmOCR-Bench, MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B outperforms Nanonets-OCR-3B by 7.3%.\n* On OmniDocBench, MonkeyOCR-pro-3B achieves the best overall performance on
