        "13032) [Source_code](https://github.com/rednote-hilab/dots.mocr) [Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gkyDZtWGfh8) [Youtube](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BxH5KD5lDSM) [Wechat](https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/EHNrk5SSCOReyTm7uv6nsg) [HyperAI Demo](https://hyper.ai/cn/notebooks/50071)\n> \n> \n\n## News\n\n* `2026.04.01` 🚀 dots.mocr achieves the best open-source score on [MDPBench](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.28130) , a 17-language document parsing benchmark!\n* `2026.03.15` 🚀 We release [dots.mocr](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.13032) Multimodal OCR: Parse Anything from Documents.\n* `2026.01.30` 🚀 We release [MonkeyDoc](https://www.modelscope.cn/datasets/zenosai/MonkeyDoc) and provide the necessary details of our data generation pipeline.\n* `2025.11.14` 🚀 We release [MonkeyOCR-v1.5](https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.10390) Technical Report, achieving the best document parsing performance to date. [Demo](https://aiwrite.wps.cn/pdf/parse/web/) .\n* `2025.07.10` 🚀 We release [MonkeyOCR-pro-1.\n2B](https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B) , a leaner and faster version model that outperforms our previous 3B version in accuracy, speed, and efficiency.\n* `2025.06.12` 🚀 The model’s trending on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/models?sort=trending) \\#2.\n* `2025.06.05` 🚀 We release [MonkeyOCR](https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR) , an English and Chinese documents parsing model.\n\n## Introduction\n\nMonkeyOCR adopts a Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) triplet paradigm, which simplifies the multi-tool pipeline of modular approaches while avoiding the inefficiency of using large multimodal models for full-page document processing.\n\n1. MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B surpasses MonkeyOCR-3B by 7.4% on Chinese documents.\n2. MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B delivers approximately a 36% speed improvement over MonkeyOCR-pro-3B, with approximately 1.6% drop in performance.\n3. On olmOCR-Bench, MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B outperforms Nanonets-OCR-3B by 7.3%.\n4.\nOn OmniDocBench, MonkeyOCR-pro-3B achieves the best overall performance on both English and Chinese documents, outperforming even closed-source and extra-large open-source VLMs such as Gemini 2.0-Flash, Gemini 2.5-Pro, Qwen2.5-VL-72B, GPT-4o, and InternVL3-78B.\n\nSee detailed results below.\n\n### Comparing MonkeyOCR with closed-source and extra large open-source VLMs.\n\n## Inference Speed (Pages/s) on Different GPUs and [PDF](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1geumlJmVY7UUKdr8324sYZ0FHSAElh7m?usp=sharing) Page Counts\n\n|Model |GPU |50 Pages |100 Pages |300 Pages |500 Pages |1000 Pages |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|MonkeyOCR-pro-3B |3090 |0\\.492 |0\\.484 |0\\.497 |0\\.492 |0\\.496 |\n|A6000 |0\\.585 |0\\.587 |0\\.609 |0\\.598 |0\\.608 |\n|H800 |0\\.923 |0\\.768 |0\\.897 |0\\.930 |0\\.891 |\n|4090 |0\\.972 |0\\.969 |1\\.006 |0\\.986 |1\\.006 |\n|MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B |3090 |0\\.615 |0\\.660 |0\\.677 |0\\.687 |0\\.683 |\n|A6000 |0\\.709 |0\\.786 |0\\.825 |0\\.829 |0\\.825 |\n|H800 |0\\.965 |1\\.082 |1\\.101 |1\\."

      "title": "MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition ...",

      "excerpts": [
        "Title: MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition-Relation Triplet Paradigm\n\nURL Source: https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218\n\nPublished Time: Fri, 06 Jun 2025 00:58:28 GMT\n\nMarkdown Content:\nZhang Li 1, Yuliang Liu 1,†, Qiang Liu 2, Zhiyin Ma 1, Ziyang Zhang 1, \n\nShuo Zhang 1, Zidun Guo 1, Jiarui Zhang 2, Xinyu Wang 1, Xiang Bai 1\n1 Huazhong University of Science and Technology, 2 Kingsoft Office\n\n###### Abstract\n\nWe introduce MonkeyOCR, a vision-language model for document parsing that advances the state of the art by leveraging a Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) triplet paradigm. This design simplifies what would otherwise be a complex multi-tool pipeline (as in MinerU’s modular approach) and avoids the inefficiencies of processing full pages with giant end-to-end models (e.g., large multimodal LLMs like Qwen-VL). In SRR, document parsing is abstracted into three fundamental questions – “Where is it?” (structure), “What is it?\n” (recognition), and “How is it organized?” (relation) – corresponding to layout analysis, content identification, and logical ordering. This focused decomposition balances accuracy and speed: it enables efficient, scalable processing without sacrificing precision. To train and evaluate this approach, we introduce the MonkeyDoc (the most comprehensive document parsing dataset to date), with 3.9 million instances spanning over ten document types in both Chinese and English. Experiments show that MonkeyOCR outperforms MinerU by an average of 5.1%, with particularly notable improvements on challenging content such as formulas (+15.0%) and tables (+8.6%). Remarkably, our 3B-parameter model surpasses much larger and top-performing models, including Qwen2.5-VL (72B) and Gemini 2.5 Pro, achieving state-of-the-art average performance on English document parsing tasks. In addition, MonkeyOCR processes multi-page documents significantly faster (0.84 pages per second compared to 0.\n65 for MinerU and 0.12 for Qwen2.5-VL-7B). The 3B model can be efficiently deployed for inference on a single NVIDIA 3090 GPU. Code and models will be released at [https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR).\n\n![Image 1: Refer to caption](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1/x1.png)\n\nFigure 1: Performance comparison of MonkeyOCR and other SOTA models on OmniDocBench[[33](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1#bib.bib33)]. “Overall” represents the comprehensive evaluation across nine document types in OmniDocBench.\n\n1 Introduction\n--------------\n\nDocument parsing is a foundational technology that transforms unstructured, multimodal content, including text, tables, images, formulas, and more, within various document formats into structured, machine-readable information."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR",
      "title": "echo840/MonkeyOCR · Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue) [](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/issues?q=is%3Aissue+is%3Aclosed) [](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/blob/main/LICENSE.txt) [](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR)\n\n> **MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition-Relation Triplet Paradigm**  \n> Zhang Li, Yuliang Liu, Qiang Liu, Zhiyin Ma, Ziyang Zhang, Shuo Zhang, Zidun Guo, Jiarui Zhang, Xinyu Wang, Xiang Bai  \n> [](https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.05218)  [](https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR) [](https://modelscope.cn/models/l1731396519/MonkeyOCR) [](https://openbayes.com/console/public/tutorials/91ESrGvEvBq) [](http://vlrlabmonkey.xyz:7685/)\n> \n> \n\n##  Introduction\n\nMonkeyOCR adopts a Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) triplet paradigm, which simplifies the multi-tool pipeline of modular approaches while avoiding the inefficiency of using large multimodal models for full-page document processing.\n\n1. MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B surpasses MonkeyOCR-3B by 7.4% on Chinese documents.\n2.\nMonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B delivers approximately a 36% speed improvement over MonkeyOCR-pro-3B, with approximately 1.6% drop in performance.\n3. On olmOCR-Bench, MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B outperforms Nanonets-OCR-3B by 7.3%.\n4. On OmniDocBench, MonkeyOCR-pro-3B achieves the best overall performance on both English and Chinese documents, outperforming even closed-source and extra-large open-source VLMs such as Gemini 2.0-Flash, Gemini 2.5-Pro, Qwen2.5-VL-72B, GPT-4o, and InternVL3-78B.\n\nSee detailed results below.\n\n###  Comparing MonkeyOCR with closed-source and extra large open-source VLMs.\n\n[](https://zimgs.com/i/EKhkhY)\n\n##  Inference Speed (Pages/s) on Different GPUs and [PDF](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1geumlJmVY7UUKdr8324sYZ0FHSAElh7m?usp=sharing) Page Counts\n\n|Model |GPU |50 Pages |100 Pages |300 Pages |500 Pages |1000 Pages |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|MonkeyOCR-pro-3B |3090 |0\\.492 |0\\.484 |0\\.497 |0\\.492 |0\\.496 |\n|A6000 |0\\.585 |0\\.587 |0\\.609 |0\\.598 |0\\.608 |\n\n...\n\n##  Supported Hardware\n\nDue to the limited types of GPUs available to us, we may not be able to provide highly accurate hardware specifications. We've tested the model on GPUs such as the 3090, 4090, A6000, H800, A100, and even the 4060 with 8GB of VRAM (suitable for deploying quantized 3B model and 1.2B model). We are very grateful for the feedback and contributions from the open-source community, who have also successfully run the model on [50-series GPUs](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/issues/90) , [H200](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/issues/151) , [L20](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/issues/133) , [V100](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/issues/144) , [2080 Ti](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/pull/1) and [npu](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR/pull/226/files) .\n\n##  News\n\n* `2025.07.10` 🚀 We release [MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B](https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR-pro-1."
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1",
      "title": "MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition ...",
      "publish_date": "2025-06-05",
      "excerpts": [
        "For datasets that provide only region-level bounding boxes without reading order information, we leverage expert models to generate region-level reading order annotations automatically. Specifically, we utilize PPOCR [ [17](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1.bib17) ] for line-wise text recognition within each region, obtain text line positions, and then apply LayoutReader [ [44](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1.bib44) ] to predict the reading order of these lines. The region-level order is determined by aggregating the predicted order of all text lines within each region. Through this approach, we generate 78,000 additional region-level annotations, further enriching the diversity and coverage of our dataset.\n\n## 4 MonkeyOCR\n\nRefer to caption Figure 5: The overall architecture of MonkeyOCR.\nThe system adopts a Structure-Recognition-Relation framework, consisting of structure detection, which locates and classifies semantic regions; block-level content recognition, which extracts structured information from each region in parallel; and relation prediction, which determines the logical reading order of the detected elements.\n\nThe proposed method, MonkeyOCR , addresses the fundamental limitations of both pipeline-based and end-to-end document parsing approaches by introducing a modular yet globally optimized Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) framework. As illustrated in Figure [5](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1.F5 \"Figure 5 ‣ 4 MonkeyOCR ‣ MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition-Relation Triplet Paradigm\") , we decompose the document parsing process into three relatively independent but tightly integrated stages: structure detection , block-level content recognition , and relation prediction .\nThis design aims to mitigate the cumulative error typically observed in pipeline toolchains, while also improving inference efficiency by reducing the context length compared to monolithic end-to-end models.\n\nIn the first stage, a YOLO-based [ [49](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.05218v1.bib49) ] document layout detector processes the input image $I\\in\\mathbb{R}^{H\\times W\\times 3}$ , producing a set of bounding boxes $B=\\{b_{1},b_{2},\\dots,b_{n}\\}$ and their corresponding element types $T=\\{t_{1},t_{2},\\dots,t_{n}\\}$ . Each bounding box $b_{i}=(x_{1i},y_{1i},x_{2i},y_{2i})$ represents the spatial coordinates of the $i$ \\-th element, and the element type $t_{i}\\in\\{\\text{text},\\text{table},\\text{formula},\\text{figure},\\dots\\}$ specifies the category of the detected element.\n\nFor the second stage, we perform block-level content recognition in parallel."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://ai-bot.cn/monkeyocr",
      "title": "MonkeyOCR - 华中科技联合金山办公推出的文档解析模型 | AI工具集",
      "publish_date": "2025-06-09",
      "excerpts": [
        "[首页](https://ai-bot.cn/) _•_ [AI工具](https://ai-bot.cn/ai-tools/) _•_ [AI项目和框架](https://ai-bot.cn/ai-research/) _•_ MonkeyOCR – 华中科技联合金山办公推出的文档解析模型\n\n# MonkeyOCR – 华中科技联合金山办公推出的文档解析模型\n\n[AI工具](https://ai-bot.cn/ai-tools/) 1年前 (2025)发布 [AI小集](https://ai-bot.cn/author/7/ \"AI小集\") 0 5\n\n## MonkeyOCR是什么\n\nMonkeyOCR 是华中科技大学联合金山办公（Kingsoft Office）推出的文档解析模型，模型支持高效地将非结构化文档内容转换为结构化信息。基于精确的布局分析、内容识别和逻辑排序，显著提升文档解析的准确性和效率。与传统方法相比，MonkeyOCR在处理复杂文档（如包含公式和表格的文档）时表现出色，平均性能提升5.1%，在公式和表格解析上分别提升15.0%和8.6%。模型在多页文档处理速度上表现出色，达到每秒0.84页，远超其他同类工具。MonkeyOCR支持多种文档类型，包括学术论文、教科书和报纸等，适用多种语言，为文档数字化和自动化处理提供强大的支持。\n\nMonkeyOCR\n\n## MonkeyOCR的主要功能\n\n* **文档解析与结构化** ：将各种格式的文档（如PDF、图像等）中的非结构化内容（包括文本、表格、公式、图像等）转换为结构化的机器可读信息。\n* **多语言支持** ：支持多种语言，包括中文和英文。\n* **高效处理复杂文档** ：在处理复杂文档（如包含公式、表格、多栏布局等）时表现出色。\n* **快速多页文档处理** ：高效处理多页文档，处理速度达到每秒0.84页，显著优于其他工具（如MinerU每秒0.65页，Qwen2.5-VL-7B每秒0.12页）。\n* **灵活的部署与扩展** ：支持在单个NVIDIA 3090 GPU上高效部署，满足不同规模的需求。\n\n## MonkeyOCR的技术原理\n* **结构-识别-关系（SRR）三元组范式** ：基于YOLO的文档布局检测器，识别文档中的关键元素（如文本块、表格、公式、图像等）的位置和类别。对每个检测到的区域进行内容识别，用大型多模态模型（LMM）进行端到端的识别，确保高精度。基于块级阅读顺序预测机制，确定检测到的元素之间的逻辑关系，重建文档的语义结构。\n* **MonkeyDoc数据集** ：MonkeyDoc是迄今为止最全面的文档解析数据集，包含390万个实例，涵盖中文和英文的十多种文档类型。数据集基于多阶段管道构建，整合精心的手动标注、程序化合成和模型驱动的自动标注。用在训练和评估MonkeyOCR模型，确保在多样化和复杂的文档场景中具有强大的泛化能力。\n* **模型优化与部署** ：用AdamW优化器和余弦学习率调度，结合大规模数据集进行训练，确保模型在精度和效率之间的平衡。基于LMDeploy工具，MonkeyOCR能在单个NVIDIA 3090 GPU上高效运行，支持快速推理和大规模部署。\n\n## MonkeyOCR的项目地址\n\n* **GitHub仓库** ： <https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR>\n* **HuggingFace模型库** ： <https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR>\n* **arXiv技术论文** ： <https://arxiv.org/pdf/2506.05218>\n* **在线体验Demo** ： <http://vlrlabmonkey.xyz:7685/>\n\n## MonkeyOCR的应用场景\n\n* **自动化业务流程** ：企业内部文档处理，如合同、报表、发票等，实现数据自动提取和结构化，提高效率，减少人工干预。\n* **数字存档** ：图书馆、档案馆等机构对纸质文档进行数字化存档，便于长期保存和检索。\n* **智能教育** ：教育机构对教材、试卷、学术论文等进行解析，提取内容用于在线学习平台或教学资源库。\n* **医疗记录管理** ：医院对病历、检查报告等医疗文档进行解析，提取关键信息用于电子病历系统，提高数据管理效率。\n* **学术研究** ：科研人员对大量学术文献进行解析，提取关键信息用于文献综述和数据分析，辅助研究工作。\n\n[\\# AI工具](https://ai-bot.cn/ai-tools/ \"查看更多文章\") [\\# AI项目和框架](https://ai-bot.cn/ai-research/ \"查看更多文章\")\n\n© 版权声明\n\n本站文章版权归 [AI工具集](https://ai-bot.cn/) 所有，未经允许禁止任何形式的转载。\n\n[上一篇 #### 智谱CoCo - 智谱推出的首个企业级超级助手Agent](https://ai-bot.cn/aiworker-coco/)\n\n[下一篇 #### Playmate - 趣丸科技团队推出的人脸动画生成框架](https://ai-bot.cn/playmate/)\n\n#### 相关文章\n\n[即梦AI](https://jimeng.jianying.com/ai-tool/home/?utm_medium=aitools&utm_source=aibot&utm_campaign=null&utm_content=hw_jm_aibot)\n\n## [暂无评论](https://ai-bot.cn/monkeyocr/)\n\n再想想 发表评论\n\n暂无评论...\n\n[AI工具集 AI工具集](https://ai-bot.cn         "Tutorial Introduction\n\n[Build](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR)\n\nMonkeyOCR is a document parsing model open-sourced on June 5, 2025, by Huazhong University of Science and Technology in collaboration with Kingsoft Office. The model efficiently converts unstructured document content into structured information. Based on accurate layout analysis, content recognition, and logical sorting, it significantly improves the accuracy and efficiency of document parsing. Compared to traditional methods, MonkeyOCR performs exceptionally well in processing complex documents (such as those containing formulas and tables), achieving an average performance improvement of 5.11 TP3T, and improvements of 15.01 TP3T and 8.61 TP3T in formula and table parsing, respectively. The model excels in processing multi-page documents, achieving 0.84 pages per second, far exceeding other similar tools.\nMonkeyOCR supports various document types, including academic papers, textbooks, and newspapers, and is compatible with multiple languages, providing powerful support for document digitization and automated processing. Related research papers are available. [MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition-Relation Triplet Paradigm](https://hyper.ai/papers/2506.05218) .\n\nKey features:\n\n* Document parsing and structuring: Convert unstructured content (including text, tables, formulas, images, etc.) in documents of various formats (such as PDF, images, etc.) into structured machine-readable information.\n* Multi-language support: Supports multiple languages, including Chinese and English.\n* Efficiently handle complex documents: It performs well when processing complex documents (such as those containing formulas, tables, multi-column layouts, etc.).\n* Fast multi-page document processing: Efficiently process multi-page documents with a processing speed of 0."

        "[](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio) [](https://github.com/newreleasesio) Pricing  \n\n# github miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR [2\\.58](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/releases/tag/2.58)\n\nPandaOCR.v2.58\n\non [GitHub](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR)\n\nlatest releases: 2\\.72 , 2\\.71 , 2\\.69 ...\n\n5 years ago\n\n#### 更新日志：\n\n* 增加 表格识别（只支持API版，目前内置百度表格API接口，识别后不会直接显示，将显示表格下载地址，右键菜单可下载）\n* 增加 多个OCR（演示版：灵云OCR | 飞桨OCR | 合合OCR | 网校OCR）\n* 增加 灵云翻译（演示版）\n* 增加 划词复制 配置项（0=禁用 | 1=启用 | 配合启用监听文本并翻译得到划词翻译功能）\n* 增加 强制显示窗口 配置项（0=禁用 | 1=启用 | 启用后将在识别完成后强行显示主窗口）\n* 增加 恢复焦点窗口 配置项（0=禁用 | 1=焦点窗口 | 2=顶端窗口 | 启用后将在识别后恢复识别前的焦点窗口）\n* 增加 切换引擎接口的同时将重新识别或翻译\n* 调整 讯飞OCR更改为多语种版（自动识别语言，支持日文）\n* 调整 因公式和表格识别与文字识别不属于同一类型，所以分开为单独的两个选择项目，但API接口使用和OCR同样的ID和KEY配置\n\n#### 特别声明：\n\n* 本工具一直只在Github发布和更新，目前并没有所谓PandaOCR官网或熊猫OCR官网，从其他网站下载的PandaOCR请自行验证安全性！\n#### 文件下载（因新增配置项，升级前建议先删除或备份旧版CONFIG.ini配置文件）：\n\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzous.com/b015f3htg>\n* 百度网盘： [https://pan.baidu.com/s/1DecMneZWU5XVz7AMyW\\_rJw](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1DecMneZWU5XVz7AMyW_rJw) 提取码：s75w\n* 微云网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/5DllWfF>\n\nCheck out latest releases or  \nreleases around miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR 2.58\n\nDon't miss a new PandaOCR release\n\nNewReleases is sending notifications on new releases.\n\nGet notifications\n\n* About\n* [News](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio)\n* Contact\n\n* Terms of Service\n* Privacy Policy"
      ]

        "[](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio) [](https://github.com/newreleasesio) Pricing  \n\n# github miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR [2\\.69](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/releases/tag/2.69)\n\nPandaOCR.v2.69\n\non [GitHub](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR)\n\nlatest releases: 2\\.72 , 2\\.71\n\n5 years ago\n\n#### 更新日志：\n\n* 修复 腾讯翻译 API接口\n* 修复 弹窗自动隐藏\n\n#### 特别说明：\n\n* 🥇由于演示接口存在未知风险，日后会陆续将演示接口移除，此工具一直以来也都在强调大家应申请使用更快更稳定的私用API接口！\n* 🥇所以请及时转到私用API接口以免到时一脸懵逼可能影响您的日常使用！ [接口申请教程](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/wiki/%E6%BC%94%E7%A4%BA%E7%89%88%E5%92%8CAPI%E7%89%88%E5%BC%95%E6%93%8E%E6%8E%A5%E5%8F%A3)\n* 🥇此次更新还保留了一些演示接口作为过渡使用，但下个版本预计会移除90%的演示接口！\n* 🥇目前全新重构的专业版已发布，我个人精力有限，这里的普通版承诺会保持维护API接口和修复重大BUG，但不再增加新功能！ [PandaOCR.Pro专业版](https://gitee.com/DDDDDGOOO/PandaOCR.Pro)\n* 如果遇到无法运行或其他杀软误报问题，请参考这里： [参考链接](https://support.qq.com/products/322047/faqs-more/=58619)\n* 你知道的，我没有忘记\n\n#### 文件下载：\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzoui.com/b015f3htg>\n* 百度网盘： <https://pan.baidu.com/s/11a0BBVriXMMLhi0dV3Srsg> 提取码: 5azr\n* 微云网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/5DllWfF>\n\nCheck out latest releases or  \nreleases around miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR 2.69\n\nDon't miss a new PandaOCR release\n\nNewReleases is sending notifications on new releases.\n\nGet notifications\n\n* About\n* [News](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio)\n* Contact\n\n* Terms of Service\n* Privacy Policy"

        "[](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio) [](https://github.com/newreleasesio) Pricing  \n\n# miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR [2\\.56](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/releases/tag/2.56)\n\nPandaOCR.v2.56\n\non [GitHub](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR)\n\nlatest releases: 2\\.72 , 2\\.71 , 2\\.69 ...\n\n5 years ago\n\n#### 更新日志：\n\n* 增加 微软公式识别（演示版引擎）\n* 增加 Yandex识别（演示版且不稳定）\n* 增加 微软翻译（Edge浏览器内置翻译）\n* 增加 百度智能 | 图像处理 | 风格转换功能（图像区右键菜单）\n* 增加 以图搜图功能（图像区右键菜单）\n* 增加 解二维码功能（图像区右键菜单）\n* 增加 粘贴图片功能（图像区右键菜单）\n* 增加 文本纠错功能（文本区右键菜单）\n* 增加 粘贴文本功能（文本区右键菜单）\n* 修复 一些其他小问题\n\n#### 文件下载（从较旧版本升级前建议先删除或备份旧版CONFIG.ini配置文件）：\n\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzous.com/b015f3htg>\n* 百度网盘： [https://pan.baidu.com/s/1DecMneZWU5XVz7AMyW\\_rJw](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1DecMneZWU5XVz7AMyW_rJw) 提取码：s75w\n* 微云网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/5DllWfF>\n\nCheck out latest releases or  \nreleases around miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR 2.56\n\nDon't miss a new PandaOCR release\n\nNewReleases is sending notifications on new releases.\nGet notifications\n\n* About\n* [News](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio)\n* Contact\n\n* Terms of Service\n* Privacy Policy"

      "url": "https://www.rjgcz.com/3161.html",
      "title": "多功能OCR识别、翻译、朗读工具 PandaOCR v5.58 中文绿色版【软件个锤子·R1481】 - 软件个锤子网",
      "publish_date": "2025-06-01",
      "excerpts": [
        "# 多功能OCR识别、翻译、朗读工具 PandaOCR v5.58 中文绿色版【软件个锤子·R1481】\n\n* [Windows](https://www.rjgcz.com/windows) [办公效率](https://www.rjgcz.com/windows/%e5%8a%9e%e5%85%ac%e6%95%88%e7%8e%87-windows) [文件管理](https://www.rjgcz.com/windows/%e6%96%87%e4%bb%b6%e7%ae%a1%e7%90%86)\n* 来源： [软件个锤子](https://www.rjgcz.com/?p=3161&preview=true)\n* 25年6月1日\n* \n* 编辑\n\n前往下载\n\n[](https://www.rjgcz.com/users/1)\n\n**软件个锤子**\n\n取消关注 关注 私信\n\nPandaOCR是一个高效的OCR智能识别工具，具备多个识别引擎，能精准处理文本、公式和表格等复杂内容，并支持多平台翻译和朗读功能。\n\n多功能OCR识别、翻译、朗读工具 PandaOCR v5.58 中文绿色版【软件个锤子·R1481】\n\n## 多样化识别模式\n\n* **区域截图识别：** 类似于QQ截图，选择区域即可识别。\n* **图片识别：** 支持将图片文件转换成可编辑的文档格式。\n* **剪贴板识别：** 自动处理复制到剪贴板的图片内容。\n* **固定区域识别：** 针对指定屏幕区域进行持续识别。\n* **快捷操作：** 支持快捷键和屏幕边角触发功能，快速启动截图和识别。\n\n## 内置识别和翻译引擎\n\n包含多种OCR和翻译引擎，提供全面的语言处理能力：\n\n* OCR引擎：包括搜狗OCR、腾讯OCR、百度OCR等多种国内外知名OCR服务。\n* 翻译引擎：搜狗翻译、腾讯翻译、百度翻译及其他多种翻译服务。\n* 朗读引擎：涵盖搜狗、腾讯和百度的多种朗读选项。\n\n## 使用场景和推荐\nPandaOCR适用于广泛的文本识别和语言翻译场景，推荐使用搜狗引擎进行OCR识别，腾讯或搜狗进行翻译，搜狗或腾讯进行文本朗读。讯飞引擎在处理短文本时表现优异，长文本识别则需谨慎选择。\n\n## 自定义API使用\n\n用户可以通过以下链接申请自己的API ID和KEY，自定义PandaOCR的功能：\n\n* 搜狗OCR/翻译: <http://deepi.sogou.com>\n* 百度OCR/翻译: <https://cloud.baidu.com>\n* 腾讯OCR/翻译: <https://ai.qq.com>\n* 有道OCR/翻译: <http://ai.youdao.com>\n\n完成申请后，编辑程序目录下的 _CONFIG.ini_ 文件，按需设置API ID和KEY，重启软件后即可使用新配置。\n\n免费通道下载区\n\n**1**\n\n多通道高速下载区 一键下载历史版本\n\n**2**\n\n* \n* \n* \n* \n* \n*\n\n下载权限\n\n查看\n\n* ￥\n  \n  免费下载\n  \n  评论并刷新后下载\n  \n  登录后下载\n\n查看演示\n\n* {{attr.name}}：\n\n您当前的等级为\n\n登录后免费下载 登录 小黑屋反思中，不准下载！ 评论后刷新页面下载 评论 支付 以后下载 请先 登录 您今天的下载次数（ 次）用完了，请明天再来 支付积分 以后下载 立即支付 支付 以后下载 立即支付 您当前的用户组不允许下载 [升级会员](https://www.rjgcz.com/vips)\n\n您已获得下载权限 您可以每天下载资源 次，今日剩余 次\n\n**免责声明：** 本站所有资源均收集自互联网，分享目的仅供学习参考，并不贩卖资源，资源版权归该资源的合法拥有者所有，请您在下载后24小时内删除。\n若本站发布的内容侵犯到您的合法权益，请立即联系43404810@qq.com及时做删除处理！\n\n**海报分享** **{{postData.favorites\\_isset ? '已收藏' : '收藏'}}**\n\n[OCR识别](https://www.rjgcz.com/tag/ocr%e8%af%86%e5%88%ab) [文本朗读](https://www.rjgcz.com/tag/%e6%96%87%e6%9c%ac%e6%9c%97%e8%af%bb) [翻译](https://www.rjgcz.com/tag/%e7%bf%bb%e8%af%91)\n\n[](https://www.rjgcz.com/18819.html)\n\n[办公效率](https://www.rjgcz.com/windows/%e5%8a%9e%e5%85%ac%e6%95%88%e7%8e%87-windows)\n\n## [批量文件重命名软件 大笨狗更名器 v4.56 免费版【软件个锤子·R3275】](https://www.rjgcz.com/18819.html)\n\n2025-4-27 15:28:53\n\n[办公效率](https://www.rjgcz.com/windows/%e5%8a%9e%e5%85%ac%e6%95%88%e7%8e%87-windows) [办公效率](https://www.rjgcz.com/macos/%e5%8a%9e%e5%85%ac%e6%95%88%e7%8e%87)\n\n## [屏幕设备秒变副屏 Deskreen v2.0.4 免费开源 支持手机/电脑/电视 支持Windows/macOS/Linux【软件个锤子·R4125】](https://www.rjgcz.com/20435.html)\n\n2025-6-7 10:45:56\n\n[](https://www.rjgcz.com/20435.html)\n\n查看演示\n\n{{m.name}} ： {{m.value}}\n\n您的下载权限 查看全部权限\n\n* ￥\n  \n  免费下载\n  \n  今天剩余0次下载\n  \n  评论后下载\n  \n  登录后下载\n\n登录后下载： 登录 小黑屋反思中！ 评论后下载 评论 请先登录 今天剩余0次下载 ￥ [升级会员](https://www.rjgcz.com/vips)\n\n您已获得下载权限 今日还有{{item.current\\_user.can.count}}次免费下载\n\n## 最新发布\n\n* TOP1\n## 微信全自动聊天机器人 V9 支持自定义从某网站搜索资源自动发送 附视频使用教程 | 软件个锤子 | R5050\n  \n  6月21日\n  \n  [](https://www.rjgcz.com/13589.html)\n* TOP2\n  \n  ## ImageX备份还原 v1.0.2026.0619 中文绿色便携版 | 软件个锤子 | R5059\n  \n  6月21日\n  \n  [](https://www.rjgcz.com/16026.html)\n* ## 重复图片查找删除软件 Duplicate Photos Fixer Pro v1.3.1086.1046 Win / Mac 中文特别版 | 软件个锤子 | R5058\n  \n  6月21日\n  \n  [](https://www.rjgcz.com/12272.html)\n* ##     {
      "url": "https://www.wuleba.com/1531.html",
      "title": "PandaOCR 2.72 绿色版（免费全能OCR图文识别工具） - 吾乐吧软件站",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "- [主页](https://www.wuleba.com)\n - [办公软件](https://www.wuleba.com/windows/office)\n - [图形图像](https://www.wuleba.com/windows/graphic)\n\n# PandaOCR 2.72 绿色版（免费全能OCR图文识别工具）\n\n[吾乐吧软件站](https://www.wuleba.com/author/wuleba) • 2022-04-16 • [办公软件](https://www.wuleba.com/windows/office) , [图形图像](https://www.wuleba.com/windows/graphic) • 2961 阅读\n\n## 软件介绍\n\nPandaOCR（又称熊猫OCR）是一款个人开发的免费多功能OCR图文识别软件，堪称最强OCR工具。提供大量识别引擎、翻译引擎、朗读引擎、公式识别、表格识别、多规则截图识别等全功能，支持高分辨率屏幕。\n\n## 软件截图\n\n[](https://www.wuleba.com/wp-content/uploads/20220327170619.jpg)\n\n## 功能特色\n\n• 支持识别引擎：搜狗OCR/API+腾讯OCR/API+百度OCR/API+有道OCR/API+阿里OCR/API+京东OCR+华为OCR+网易OCR+讯飞OCR+金山OCR+灵云OCR+飞桨OCR+合合OCR+网校OCR/API+易道OCR+薪火OCR+必应OCR+SpaceOCR/API+YandexOCR。\n\n...\n\n[免费全能OCR图文识别工具PandaOCR       "title": "PandaOCR Pro(多功能OCR文字识别软件)v5.58 免费版 - 下载集",

        "[下载集](https://www.xzji.com/ \"下载集\") / [电脑软件](https://www.xzji.com/soft/ \"电脑软件\") / [图形软件](https://www.xzji.com/soft/tuxing/ \"图形软件\") / [图片转换](https://www.xzji.com/soft/tupian/ \"图片转换\") / PandaOCR Pro(OCR文字识别软件)\n\nPandaOCR Pro(OCR文字识别软件) 5.58\n\n# PandaOCR Pro(OCR文字识别软件) 5.58\n\n支持OCR文字识别，还支持翻译和朗读\n\n版本\n\nv5.58\n\n大小\n\n12\\.3 MB\n\n语言\n\n简体中文\n\n系统\n\nWin All\n\n授权\n\n免费版\n\n更新\n\n2025-05-31\n\n厂商\n\n[PandaOCR](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro \"PandaOCR\")\n\n本地下载 12\\.3 MB · 高速直链 报错反馈\n\n**普通下载入口 · 非广告按钮**\n\n软件介绍 软件截图 下载地址 相关版本 同类推荐 相关阅读\n\n**[PandaOCR Pro](https://www.xzji.com/soft/1078.html \"PandaOCR Pro\") (熊猫OCR识别工具)** 是一款图片转文字识别软件，支持识别文本、翻译文本，识别引擎分别是百度OCR+有道OCR+TXOCR+搜狗OCR，翻译引擎是谷歌翻译+ [百度翻译](https://www.xzji.com/k/baidufanyi/ \"百度翻译\") \\+有道翻译+TX翻译+爱词霸翻译+搜狗翻译+必应翻译，还支持朗读，截取识别固定区域，并且支持右侧小弹窗，方便快速查看识别/翻译内容!可以直接从图片中识别文字，也可以截图识别，如果文字是英文，还可以将识别出的文字直接翻译成中文，并且朗读给用户听。\n\n### 功能特色\n\n1、支持识别引擎：百度OCR/API+腾讯OCR/API+搜狗OCR+有道OCR。"

|README.md | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n\n## PandaOCR - 多功能OCR图文识别+翻译+朗读+弹窗+公式+表格+图床+搜图+二维码\n\n<VER：2.72>\n\n##### 2022-03-16 更新\n\n* * *\n\n# [推荐使用最新的PandaOCR.Pro专业版（点此跳转）](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro#%E4%B8%8B%E8%BD%BD)\n\n#### 专业版本：\n\n* 🥇目前全新重构的PandaOCR.Pro专业版已发布，它更快速稳定、接口更丰富、操作更简单，推荐您使用！ [查看PandaOCR.Pro专业版](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro)\n* 针对当前普通版的说明：\n* 会继续维护普通版，保留所有接口但不再增加新功能，专业版的版本号之所以从5.x开始，正是考虑到普通版以后还会有更新所以保留了一段版本号。\n* 您可以继续像以往那样免费使用普通版，不用担心推出专业版后导致普通版停用，如果有更高需求可以尝试专业版。\n\n...\n\n* 支持智能合并修正文本，让排版更合理，并提高翻译质量\n* 支持设置最多十条固定截图规则，每条规则都能有自己的快捷键\n* 支持监听图像和文本复制操作，快速识别图像文本或翻译复制文本\n* 支持简单的窗口汉化功能，帮助翻译纯英文类软件界面文字\n* 还有一些奇怪的没有列在程序界面上的功能，可以编辑程序目录下的CONFIG.INI配置文件试试\n\n* * *\n\n#### 使用技巧：\n\n* 将鼠标移到各功能组件或按钮上会显示简单的悬停提示帮助你理解程序操作。\n* 配置文件内已添加各功能注释说明，如果想要实现某种功能但在程序界面上没找到相关设置，可以先翻一翻配置文件或许它已经在那里等着你（程序目录下CONFIG.ini即是配置文件）。\n* 如果你有两块屏幕，请勾选“高级截图方式”以解决无法截取第二块屏的问题。\n* 如果你是高分屏或修改了系统DPI缩放，可能出现截图不全或弹窗位置偏移的问题，此时你需要在此程序文件的属性中取消系统DPI设置。\n* 程序界面上存在的设置多数是可以实时生效的不需要频繁点击保存按钮，比如设置语言、更换引擎此类。\n* 有时手工更改了配置文件又不想重启软件可以试试右键点击界面左上角图标重载配置，不要点左键。\n* 从演示版引擎临时更改为API版引擎可以右键点击引擎选择组合框。\n* 鼠标党如果觉得按快捷键识别麻烦可以把鼠标移到屏幕左上角来触发识别，默认配置已启用，也可以编辑配置文件关闭或改为其他位置（配置项：边角触发截图）。\n* 在截图时按住CTRL键可以临时取消识别，只截图并复制至剪贴板。\n* 在截图时按住ALT键可以临时取消修正文本，当识别图像的文本中不包含任何标点符号时建议这样使用。\n* 在文本区输入文本后按CTRL+回车键会直接翻译，不需要再用鼠标点翻译按钮。\n* 在文本区输入文本后按ALT+回车键会直接朗读。\n* 将图片直接拖入至程序界面上会自动开始识别。\n* 在识别或是朗读进行中如果想中止任务可以双击界面右上方“线程”字样处，或按住空格键的同时点击托盘图标。\n* 如果觉得文本区域过小，可以双击文本区使用大窗口浏览或简单编辑。\n* 有些不太用的上的功能建议关闭以减少识别等待时间，比如朗读文本。\n* 在启用了监听复制功能时临时不想执行识别或翻译可以在复制的同时按住空格键。\n\n* * *\n\n#### 使用教程：\n\n* 官方教程： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/wiki>\n* 游戏机翻： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1Vt4y1U7Es/>\n* 接口申请： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1Vt4y1U7Es?p=2>\n* 基础操作： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1UV411d7zh>\n* 基础操作： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV15k4y1z7Vp>\n\n* * *\n\n#### API版接口申请（默认使用免费的演示版 | [演示版和API版区别](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/wiki/%E6%BC%94%E7%A4%BA%E7%89%88%E5%92%8CAPI%E7%89%88%E5%BC%95%E6%93%8E%E6%8E%A5%E5%8F%A3 \"演示版和API版区别\") ）：\n\n##### 搜狗：\n\n* OCR： <http://deepi.sogou.com> （送100/200元体验金 | 可能需要先充点钱才能用）\n* 翻译： <http://deepi.sogou.com> （送100/200元体验金）\n\n...\n\n* OCR： <https://ai.xueersi.com> （10万次/天免费 | 10秒间隔/次 | 旧站）\n* OCR： <https://ai.100tal.com> （新站）\n\n##### Deepl：\n\n* 翻译： <https://www.deepl.com/translator> （付费且服务器延迟较高）\n\n##### Mathpix：\n\n* 公式： <https://dashboard.mathpix.com> （1000次/月免费）\n\n##### SpaceOCR：\n\n* OCR： <https://ocr.space/OCRAPI> （25000次/月免费）\n\n* * *\n\n#### 隐私安全：\n\n##### 程序：\n\n* 程序本身不包含任何恶意代码，如有杀软误报请自行加入白名单（曾提交火绒人工检测为安全）\n* 程序调用了一个51.la的统计，单纯用来统计使用人数，除此之外不会上传您的任何隐私数据\n* 因功能需要，请使用管理员权限运行，程序会调用键鼠钩子，如“划词复制功能”需要鼠标钩子，为避免某些游戏屏蔽热键导致无法截图而使用了键盘钩子\n* 程序的开发与发布均在ESET NOD32杀软保护的环境中完成，如果使用过程中，您系统中某种“安全”软件称「发现木马」，那么此种情形将考验您的判断力\n\n##### 引擎：\n\n* 本程序不带离线引擎，所调用的识别/翻译/朗读等全部为在线引擎\n* 理论上用户上传到各个引擎的数据都只是缓存数据，通常会在一定时间内清除\n* 但服务器毕竟为各引擎所在公司控制和所有，我无法保证上传数据的绝对安全\n* 所以如果您要处理的数据属于商业或机密数据，请换用其他自己可控的本地私有化产品\n* 如因此发生的任何损失，本人表示概不负责\n\n* * *\n\n#### 特别说明：\n\n* 已知在高分辨率/高分屏下截图功能异常 [[尝试解决方法]](https://github.\ncom/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/issues/17 \"[尝试解决方法]\")\n* 如需使用自己申请的ID和KEY，用文本编辑器打开程序目录下的CONFIG.ini文件，在[数据配置]项，将各版本后面数值设为1（0为演示版），并修改下面相应的ID和KEY\n* 如需反馈API版引擎存在的问题，请主动提供相应的ID和KEY给我测试\n\n* * *\n\n#### 程序下载：\n\n* 更新日志： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/releases>\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzoui.com/b015f3htg>\n* 百度网盘： <https://pan.baidu.com/s/11a0BBVriXMMLhi0dV3Srsg> 提取码: 5azr\n* 微云网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/5DllWfF>\n\n* * *\n\n#### 问题反馈：\n\n* 论坛反馈： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/issues>\n* QQ群反馈： [https://jq.qq.com/?\\_wv=1027&k=5XjJmJ5](https://jq.qq.com/?_wv=1027&k=5XjJmJ5)\n\n* * *\n\n#### 程序截图：\n\n[默认界面](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/master/images/AeroSnap%E6%88%AA%E5%9B%BE1.png Our model is now available at [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/PaddlePaddle/Unlimited-OCR) .\n* [2026/06/22] 🚀 We present [Unlimited-OCR](https://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) , aiming to push [Deepseek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) one step further.\n\n## Inference\n\n### Transformers\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs. Our model is now available at [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/PaddlePaddle/Unlimited-OCR) .\n* [2026/06/22] 🚀 We present [Unlimited-OCR](https://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) , aiming to push [Deepseek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) one step further.\n\n## Inference\n\n### Transformers\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs. Requirements tested on python 3.12.3 + CUDA12.9：\n\n```\ntorch==2.10.0\ntorchvision==0.25.0\ntransformers==4.57.1\nPillow==12.1.1\nmatplotlib==3.10.8\neinops==0.8.2\naddict==2.4.0\neasydict==1.13\npymupdf==1.27.2.2\npsutil==7.2.2\n```\n\n```\nimport os \n import torch \n from transformers import AutoModel , AutoTokenizer \n\n model_name = 'baidu/Unlimited-OCR' \n\n tokenizer = AutoTokenizer . from_pretrained ( model_name , trust_remote_code = True )\n model = AutoModel . from_pretrained (\n    model_name ,\n    trust_remote_code = True ,\n    use_safetensors = True ,\n    torch_dtype = torch . bfloat16 ,\n)\n model = model . eval (). cuda ()\n\n # ── Single image supports two configs: gundam or base ── \n # gundam: base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True \n # base: base_size=1024, image_size=1024, crop_mode=False \n model . infer (\n    tokenizer ,\n    prompt = '<image>document parsing.' ,\n    image_file = 'your_image.jpg' ,\n    output_path = 'your/output/dir' ,\n    base_size = 1024 , image_size = 640 , crop_mode = True ,\n    max_length = 32768 ,\n    no_repeat_ngram_size = 35 , ngram_window = 128 ,\n    save_results = True ,\n)"

      "url": "https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/6063/unlimited-ocr-baidu-long-pdfs",
      "title": "Unlimited-OCR by Baidu: Open Source OCR for Long PDFs",
      "publish_date": "2026-06-28",
      "excerpts": [
        "Skip to content Skip to navigation Skip to footer\n\nP. Pillitteri\n\n[](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/6049/mistral-ocr-4-document-ai-170-languages \"Previous\") [](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/6077/pokemon-go-scans-military-drones-niantic-vantor \"Next\")\n\n# Unlimited-OCR by Baidu: Open Source OCR for Long PDFs\n\n##### Unlimited-OCR is Baidu's open source OCR model (MIT, 4,663 stars) that parses entire multi-page PDFs in a single pass. It is free: how to use it and how to test it right now.\n\nAnyone who has tried to feed a two-hundred-page contract to an OCR pipeline knows the ritual: the system reads the first page, extracts its text, moves to the second, stitches the pieces together, and only at the end delivers a reconstructed document to drop into a RAG system (Retrieval-Augmented Generation, the technique that pairs a language model's generation with a targeted document search).\nIt works, but it is page-by-page labor, and the model's memory grows with every token produced until a long document becomes more of an engineering problem than a reading one.\n\nUnlimited-OCR, the open source model released by Baidu on June 18, 2026, sets out to remove that very ritual. The stated goal sits right in the repository subtitle: \"Welcome the Era of One-shot Long-horizon Parsing,\" meaning reading a long document in a single pass instead of splitting and stitching it. The project has gathered 4,663 stars on GitHub as of June 24, 2026, ships under the MIT license, and presents itself as a direct evolution of DeepSeek-OCR. In this article I answer the three practical questions that matter: is it free, how do you use it, and how do you try it right now.\n\nUnlimited-OCR by Baidu - open source MIT OCR model for parsing long PDFs in a single pass Unlimited-OCR: one-shot parsing of long documents, MIT license, open weights on Hugging Face.\n\n**TL;DR for the five-minute reader**\n* **Yes, it is free** : MIT license, open weights downloadable from Hugging Face and ModelScope, no API key and no licensing cost.\n* **3 billion parameters** in BF16 format and a 32,768-token context window: it runs on a single mid-range NVIDIA GPU.\n* **Technical novelty** : it keeps the entire document visible to the model but carries only a sliding window of the generated text, so memory does not grow with every output token.\n* **Two ways to use it** : the Transformers library in Python for a single image, or the `infer.py` command-line script for folders and multipage PDFs.\n* **Try it instantly** : the Hugging Face Space demo runs in the browser with nothing to install; alternatively Google Colab or Kaggle with a free GPU.\n\n## What Unlimited-OCR Is and Where It Comes From\n\nUnlimited-OCR is a vision-language OCR (Optical Character Recognition, the optical recognition of characters) model: it takes the image of a page as input and returns structured text, including tables and layout."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr",
      "title": "Unlimited-OCR - One-Shot Long-Document OCR with Constant-Time Inference - Aitoolnet",
      "publish_date": "2026-06-24",
      "excerpts": [
        "A financial analyst reviewing a 40-page annual report traditionally faces cross-page table fragmentation—revenue tables split across page boundaries, footnote references disconnected from their source cells, footnotes spanning pages with no structural linkage. With Unlimited-OCR, the entire report is processed in a single forward pass: tables maintain their HTML structure end-to-end, footnote cells are linked to their parent columns, and all formulas are emitted as LaTeX without manual re-entry. The result is an instantly queryable structured document with zero post-processing overhead.\n\nA development team building an internal knowledge base from a 200-page technical specification encounters per-page loops, coordinate-to-text alignment scripts, and custom logic for every table and formula variant. With Unlimited-OCR, a single API call processes the full specification and returns Markdown with HTML tables and LaTeX formulas.\nOutput is immediately indexed in a vector database or ingested into a downstream pipeline—no external调度器, no page boundary handling, no custom post-parsing logic.\n\n## Conclusion\n\nThe competitive divide in document AI is no longer per-character accuracy—it's whether a system can treat a document as a continuous semantic system rather than a collection of isolated pages. Unlimited-OCR shifts the paradigm from \"page-level recognition\" to \"document-level memory,\" with constant-time inference that removes the computational penalty previously associated with long documents. When the cost of processing a 40-page contract equals the cost of processing a 2-page memo, entire categories of workflow automation become economically viable. For teams building enterprise document pipelines, the ability to ingest long, high-density documents in one shot—without degrading memory or latency—changes what \"scalable\" means.\nExplore the open-source implementation at `baidu/Unlimited-OCR` on Hugging Face or GitHub.\n\n* * *\n\n## More information on Unlimited-OCR\n\nLaunched\n\nPricing Model\n\nFree\n\nStarting Price\n\nGlobal Rank\n\nFollow\n\nMonth Visit\n\n<5k\n\nUnlimited-OCR was manually vetted by our editorial team and was first featured on 2026-06-24.\n\nAitoolnet Featured banner Get Embed code\n\nShare it:\n\n[](https://facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr) [](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet/?text=&url=https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr) [](https://www.tumblr.com/widgets/share/tool?posttype=link&title=&caption=&content=https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr&canonicalUrl=https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr&shareSource=tumblr_share_button)  [](https://pinterest.com/pin/create/button/?url=https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr&media=https://www.aitoolnet.com/unlimitedocr&description=) [](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https://www.aitoolnet."

## Goal
Maintain a commercially-safe OCR pipeline that produces fully searchable, MRC-compressed PDFs from any input PDF.

## Constraints & Preferences
- All dependencies must be business-friendly (Apache 2.0, BSD, MIT, EPL 2.0 test-only, GPL+CPE for JDK) — no GPL, no AGPL, no copyleft that would force source disclosure.
- All dependencies install locally into project subdirectories; entire stack is self-contained and portable by copying the folder.
- Pure-Java segmentation (no Leptonica native dependency for the core algorithm).
- `./gradlew build` must NOT run tests; tests run separately via `./gradlew test`.
- `temp/` directory in project root added to `.gitignore`.

## Progress
### Done
- Fixed `TestPdfGenerator` compilation: `PDAnnotationTextMarkup` constructor is `protected` in PDFBox 3.0.6, replaced with `PDAnnotationUnderline()`; removed unused imports.
- Added `generateTestPdfs` Gradle task to `build.gradle.kts`.
- Deleted `tests/generate_test_pdfs.py` (PyMuPDF) and purged all PyMuPDF references from `install-deps.sh`, `README.md`, `.gitignore`, `tests/TESTING.md`.
- Verified all runtime dependencies: PDFBox 3.0.6 (Apache 2.0), picocli 4.7.6 (Apache 2.0), commons-logging (Apache 2.0). Test-only: JUnit Jupiter (EPL 2.0), opentest4j (EPL 2.0). Native: Tesseract (Apache 2.0), Leptonica (BSD 2-Clause), jbig2enc (Apache 2.0), tessdata (Apache 2.0), OpenJDK 21 Adoptium (GPL 2.0 + Classpath Exception). No AGPL, no GPL (without exception), no proprietary components.
- Cleaned up `NOTICE.md` and `README.md` with accurate dependency list and restructured sections.
- Cleaned up `bootstrap.sh`: removed dead Tess4J/lept4j JNA symlinks and unnecessary `libtesseract-dev` package.
- Expanded language bundles in `bootstrap.sh` from 7 to 20 (added ita, por, rus, ara, hin, kor, nld, swe, pol, tur, heb, vie, tha).
- Added PDF/A-2b output support (`--pdfa` flag, XMP metadata, sRGB OutputIntent, optional font embedding via `pdf.pdfa.fontPath` setting).
- Added `--threads` CLI flag and `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` setting for concurrency control.
- Fixed OCR subprocess deadlock in `OCREngine.ocr()`: replaced blocking `readLine()` stdout loop with a daemon thread drainer, ensuring `process.waitFor(120s)` timeout is always reachable.
- Increased OCR subprocess timeout from 30s to 120s.
- Default `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` set to 1 (sequential OCR).
- `build.gradle.kts`: removed `check` from `build` task dependencies — tests no longer run during `./gradlew build`.
- Updated settings.jsonc, CLI help text, and README for all new settings.
- Fixed `--threads N` CLI flag to override OCR threads too: `ocrMax = threads != null ? segThreads : Math.min(segThreads, settings.getInt(...))`.
- Added `stdin` redirect to `/dev/null` in `ProcessBuilder` (precaution against subprocess deadlock).
- Added `process.destroyForcibly()` in `InterruptedException` catch block to prevent orphaned Tesseract zombies when `Future.cancel(true)` interrupts a worker thread.
- Restructured temp directory: `temp/{inputName}-{nanotime}/` holds grayscale page PNGs and Tesseract TSV output. Cleans up in `finally`.
- Changed `OCREngine.ocr(BufferedImage)` → `OCREngine.ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)`: reads pre-saved grayscale PNG from disk, no in-memory conversion or temp file creation per call.
- **FIXED** `PDFAssembler.java` black-background bug: removed `invertBinaryImage()` call on foreground mask. Original mask (text=black, background=white) maps correctly to CCITT G4 (0=white, 1=black) and ImageMask (1=draw black, 0=transparent). Inversion caused background pixels to draw black over everything.
- **FIXED** `JBIG2Compressor.java` to read `jbig2enc.flags` from `Settings.getInstance().getString("jbig2enc.flags", "-p -s")` instead of hardcoding `"-p", "-s"` — the settings key is now authoritative.
- Created `docs/similar-projects-comparison.md` with analysis of OCRmyPDF, archive-pdf-tools, pdf2pdfocr, Docling, PaddleOCR, and olmOCR.
- Researched 13 additional projects (EasyOCR, surya, docTR, Umi-OCR, DeepSeek-OCR, GOT-OCR2.0, RapidOCR, GLM-OCR, MonkeyOCR, PandaOCR, Unlimited-OCR, SwiftOCR, llama_index) — data collected, comparison document needs updating.
- Fixed test compilation errors in 5 test files that called the old `engine.ocr(BufferedImage)` signature:
  - `OCREngineTest.java`: added `ocrPage()` helper saving pages to temp dir, calls `engine.ocr(pageIndex, tempDir)`.
  - `PDFAssemblerTest.java`: added `ocrPages()` helper, replaced 5 stream-based OCR blocks.
  - `MetadataPreserverTest.java`: added `ocrPages()` helper, replaced 3 OCR stream blocks.
  - `PipelineIntegrationTest.java`: changed `processOcr()` to accept OCREngine and save-to-temp-dir pattern, updated 3 call sites.
  - `OcrAccuracyTest.java`: added temp directory management to `evaluateDocument()`, saving pages before OCR loop with cleanup in `finally`.
- All tests compile and SettingsTest + JBIG2CompressorTest pass (8/8).

### In Progress
- (none)

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- PDFBox 3.0.6 over iText (AGPL) to keep license business-friendly.
- Tesseract CLI via subprocess over Tess4J/JNA to avoid native-library version mismatches.
- OCR concurrency defaults to 1 (sequential) because Tesseract is I/O- and memory-bound; concurrent instances slow each other even with sufficient RAM (16 GB) and small traineddata (4 MB).
- `--threads N` CLI flag overrides both segmentation and OCR threads; `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` setting only applies when `--threads` is omitted.
- Segmentation uses a separate thread pool from OCR (CPU-bound vs subprocess-bound, different optimal concurrency).
- `process.waitFor(timeout)` used with a daemon thread draining stdout via `InputStream.read()` (not `readLine()`), so the timeout is always reachable even if the subprocess hangs.
- MRC mode uses Lossless PNG background + CCITT G4 foreground mask (no inversion) + invisible text. Non-MRC uses JPEG background + invisible text only.
- Pages saved as grayscale PNGs to `temp/` immediately after extraction — OCR threads read from disk instead of sharing mutable `BufferedImage` objects.
- Adoptium API URL set to `eclipse` (Temurin) distribution.
- `./gradlew build` excludes tests; `./gradlew test` runs tests separately.
- `jbig2enc.flags` setting in `settings.jsonc` is now the single source of truth for JBIG2 encoding arguments — `JBIG2Compressor` reads it via `Settings.getInstance()` instead of hardcoding.

## Next Steps
- Update `docs/similar-projects-comparison.md` with the 13 newly researched projects (EasyOCR, surya, docTR, Umi-OCR, DeepSeek-OCR, GOT-OCR2.0, RapidOCR, GLM-OCR, MonkeyOCR, PandaOCR, Unlimited-OCR, SwiftOCR, llama_index) to complete coverage.

## Critical Context
- `PDFAssemblerTest` and `PipelineIntegrationTest` hang/time out due to slow OCR in full pipeline integration tests (pre-existing, not introduced by recent changes).
- `OCREngine.ocr()` blocking `readLine()` loop was the root cause of the perceived concurrent deadlock — the 30s `waitFor` timeout was never reached because the main thread was stuck draining stdout.
- Concurrent Tesseract processes from the shell work correctly (verified: 2, 3, 4 parallel instances all succeed). The hang was caused entirely by Java's blocking I/O on stdout, not by Tesseract itself.
- **MRC black-background bug** was a CCITT/ImageMask inversion: `invertBinaryImage()` swapped text and background pixel roles. Original mask (text=black=CCITT 1, background=white=CCITT 0) maps correctly without inversion — CCITT 1 → ImageMask 1 → draws black (text), CCITT 0 → transparent (background).
- On the test system: 16 GB RAM, Intel Core i7-5600U (2 cores / 4 threads), `eng.traineddata` is 4.0 MB. Sequential OCR takes ~10-12 seconds per page under low system load; under high load (opencode, VS Code, Firefox, Gradle daemon) it degrades to 26-106s per page.
- System load of 8+ on 2-core CPU causes Tesseract slowdowns that can exceed the 120s timeout.
- `OCREngine` default tesseract path: `native/linux/tesseract`; `Settings.java` default: `./native/linux/tesseract` — both resolve to the same path functionally.
- `docs/similar-projects-comparison.md` currently covers 6 projects; 13 more have been researched and need to be integrated.

## Relevant Files
- `build.gradle.kts`: Java 21, PDFBox 3.0.6, picocli 4.7.6, Shadow plugin; `build` task excludes tests.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TrulyFreeOCR.java`: CLI entry point with `--threads`, `--pdfa`, `--no-mrc` flags; creates `temp/{inputName}-{nanotime}/`, saves grayscale page PNGs, concurrent pipeline with separate thread pools for segmentation and OCR.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/OCREngine.java`: Subprocess-based OCR `ocr(int pageIndex, File tempDir)` with daemon thread stdout drainer, stdin redirect, 120s timeout, `destroyForcibly()` on both timeout and interrupt.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssembler.java`: MRC assembly — Lossless PNG background + CCITT G4 foreground mask (no inversion, uses original binary mask directly) + invisible text layer.
- `src/main/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/JBIG2Compressor.java`: Now reads `jbig2enc.flags` from `Settings.getInstance()`, splits by whitespace, passes as args to SubprocessRunner.
- `bootstrap.sh`: Downloads 20 language bundles via loop; installs system Tesseract/Leptonica; builds jbig2enc from source.
- `settings.jsonc`: Key settings — `pipeline.mrc.enabled`, `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` (default 1), `pdf.pdfa.enabled` (default false), `pdf.pdfa.fontPath`, `jbig2enc.flags` (default `"-p -s"`).
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/TestPdfGenerator.java`: Java replacement for PyMuPDF PDF generator.
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/OCREngineTest.java`: Tests updated to use `ocrPage()` helper with temp-dir save-then-OCR pattern.
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PDFAssemblerTest.java`: Tests updated with `ocrPages()` helper; 5 OCR stream blocks replaced.
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/MetadataPreserverTest.java`: Tests updated with `ocrPages()` helper.
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/pipeline/PipelineIntegrationTest.java`: `processOcr()` updated to save-to-temp-dir pattern.
- `src/test/java/com/trulyfreeocr/eval/OcrAccuracyTest.java`: `evaluateDocument()` updated with temp-dir management for page OCR.
- `docs/similar-projects-comparison.md`: Comparison of 6 projects (needs expansion to include 13 more).

<content>
1: # Similar Projects Comparison
2: 
3: ## Overview
4: 
5: A survey of open-source tools that produce searchable, compressed PDFs from scanned
6: documents or input PDFs.  The focus is on license compatibility, MRC compression,
7: self-contained deployment, and Java portability.
8: 
9: ---
10: 
11: ## Project Matrix
12: 
13: | Feature | TrulyFreeOCR | OCRmyPDF | archive-pdf-tools | pdf2pdfocr | Docling | PaddleOCR |
14: |---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
15: | **Language** | Java 21 (pure) | Python | Python | Python | Python | Python / C++ |
16: | **License** | Apache 2.0 | MPL-2.0 | AGPL-3.0 | Apache 2.0 | MIT | Apache 2.0 |
17: | **GitHub Stars** | — | ~29k | ~140 | ~300 | ~62k | ~84k |
18: | **OCR Engine** | Tesseract 5 CLI | Tesseract 5 | Tesseract 5 | Tesseract | Tesseract / pluggable | PaddleOCR (own) |
19: | **MRC Compression** | Yes (PNG bg + JBIG2/G4 fg) | Yes (via `--optimize`) | Yes (JPEG2000 + JBIG2) | No | No (extraction only) | No |
20: | **Self-Contained** | Yes (single JAR) | No (Python + Ghostscript + Tesseract) | No (Python + system deps) | No | No (Python + models) | No (Python + GPU) |
21: | **PDF/A** | Yes (2b) | Yes (1b/2b/3b) | Yes (3b) | No | Focused on extraction | No |
22: | **No Native Deps** | Only Tesseract subprocess | Ghostscript + Tesseract | Kakadu/OpenJPEG + MuPDF | pdftk + qpdf + ghostscript | Models downloaded at runtime | PyTorch / GPU |
23: | **Image Preprocessing** | Otsu + background normalization | Deskew, clean, deskew | Sauvola binarization | None | Vision-based layout models | DL-based |
24: | **Concurrent OCR** | Optional (configurable threads) | Yes (multi-core) | Yes | Yes | Yes (batching) | Yes (GPU batch) |
25: | **Language Support** | 20 languages (tessdata) | 100+ languages | 100+ languages | 100+ languages | 100+ languages | 100+ languages |
26: | **CLI Interface** | picocli | argparse | Custom | argparse | Typer | Custom |
27: | **Active Development** | Yes | Yes (v17.7.0, Jun 2026) | Stale (last release 2021) | Low (last commit 2023) | Yes (v2.x, weekly) | Yes (v3.7.0, Jun 2026) |
28: 
29: ---
30: 
31: ## Detailed Analysis
32: 
33: ### OCRmyPDF (ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF)
34: - **License**: MPL-2.0 — business-friendly, allows closed-source integration if
35:   modifications to OCRmyPDF itself are published.
36: - **Strengths**: Gold standard for PDF OCR.  Battle-tested on millions of PDFs.
37:   Rich preprocessing (deskew, clean, deskew, rotate).  Full PDF/A support.
38:   100+ languages.  Plugin architecture (AppleOCR, EasyOCR, PaddleOCR backends).
39: - **Weaknesses**: Python + system dependencies (Ghostscript, Tesseract).  Not
40:   self-contained.  MPL-2.0 requires source disclosure of any modifications to
41:   OCRmyPDF code itself.  Ghostscript is AGPL (used as subprocess, not linked).
42: - **Relevance**: Directly comparable in output quality and feature set.  Our
43:   advantage is Java portability (single JAR) and Apache 2.0 license (no
44:   publication requirement).
45: 
46: ### archive-pdf-tools (internetarchive/archive-pdf-tools)
47: - **License**: AGPL-3.0 — **not compatible** with commercial use without
48:   disclosing entire application source.
49: - **Strengths**: Mature MRC compression with JPEG2000.  Proven at scale (6M PDFs
50:   in 2021).  PDF/A-3b and PDF/UA support.  Mask viewing tools.
51: - **Weaknesses**: AGPL-3.0 is a non-starter for commercial use.  Requires
52:   system JPEG2000 codec (Kakadu or OpenJPEG).  Python + complex dependency tree.
53:   Stale (last release 2021).
54: - **Relevance**: Shows that MRC + OCR at Internet Archive scale is feasible.
55:   Our MRC approach (PNG bg + JBIG2/G4 fg) is simpler and avoids JPEG2000
56:   licensing issues.
57: 
58: ### pdf2pdfocr (LeoFCardoso/pdf2pdfocr)
59: - **License**: Apache 2.0 — same as ours.
60: - **Strengths**: Simple, straightforward.  No re-encoding of source images when
61:   possible.  GUI available.  Cross-platform (Windows batch script included).
62: - **Weaknesses**: No MRC compression.  No PDF/A.  No image preprocessing.
63:   Low community (303 stars).  1.2.0 "marapurense" release never formalized.
64: - **Relevance**: Demonstrates the simplest possible OCR sandwich approach.
65:   Our tool adds MRC compression and full PDF/A for a material improvement.
66: 
67: ### Docling (docling-project/docling)
68: - **License**: MIT — permissive.
69: - **Strengths**: IBM Research quality.  Vision-based layout analysis (avoids
70:   pixel-level OCR for clean PDFs).  TableFormer for table extraction.  30x faster
71:   than OCR for born-digital PDFs.  Huge ecosystem (LangChain, LlamaIndex).
72:   61k+ stars.
73: - **Weaknesses**: Focused on *extraction* to Markdown/JSON, not on producing
74:   compressed searchable PDFs.  Heavy model downloads (GBs).  Python-only.
75:   Requires PyTorch for local inference.
76: - **Relevance**: Complementary — Docling is for RAG pipelines, not for
77:   producing archive-quality PDF output.  If we ever add a structure-preserving
78:   extraction mode, Docling's approach (vision models for layout) would be the
79:   direction.
80: 
81: ### PaddleOCR (PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR)
82: - **License**: Apache 2.0.
83: - **Strengths**: SOTA accuracy (reported better than Tesseract for many
84:   scripts).  GPU-accelerated.  84k+ stars.  LLM-ready output (JSON/Markdown).
85:   Mature document parsing pipeline.
86: - **Weaknesses**: Python + PyTorch/PaddlePaddle runtime.  GPU strongly
87:   recommended for practical throughput.  Model downloads are large.  No
88:   searchable PDF output (focused on text extraction).
89: - **Relevance**: PaddleOCR is the best OCR *engine* available today.  If we
90:   ever add a pluggable OCR backend, PaddleOCR CLI (via subprocess, like
91:   Tesseract) would be a natural second option for higher accuracy.
92: 
93: ### olmOCR (allenai/olmOCR)
94: - **License**: Apache 2.0 (model weights, code).
95: - **Strengths**: VLM-based OCR that preserves reading order, handles tables,
96:   equations, handwriting.  Designed for high-throughput (estimated $178/1M
97:   pages).
98: - **Weaknesses**: Requires GPU (FP8 inference).  7B parameter model.  Very new
99:   (2025/2026).  No searchable PDF output — text-only.
100: - **Relevance**: VLM-based OCR is the emerging paradigm.  Once these models
101:   become efficient enough to run on CPU, a subprocess-based integration would
102:   be straightforward.
103: 
104: ---
105: 
106: ## Why TrulyFreeOCR Exists
107: 
108: | Problem | Existing Tools | Our Solution |
109: |---|---|---|
110: | Commercial-safe license | OCRmyPDF (MPL-2.0) requires publishing modifications; archive-pdf-tools is AGPL | Apache 2.0 — no disclosure obligations |
111: | Self-contained deployment | All alternatives require Python + system libs + model downloads | Single fat JAR + bundled native binaries + bootstrap script |
112: | Java/enterprise integration | Python tools need subprocess calls from Java code | Native Java API, embeddable in Maven/Gradle projects |
113: | MRC compression | Only OCRmyPDF and archive-pdf-tools offer it, with complex deps | PNG bg + JBIG2/G4 fg with clean fallback |
114: | No Ghostscript dependency | OCRmyPDF requires Ghostscript (AGPL) | Pure PDFBox — no AGPL anywhere in the stack |
115: 
116: ## Potential Future Directions
117: 
118: 1. **Pluggable OCR backends** — Add PaddleOCR CLI as an alternative to
119:    Tesseract (subprocess-based, same pattern).
120: 2. **Deskew/clean preprocessing** — Add Leptonica-based deskew and text
121:    cleaning via subprocess (similar to OCRmyPDF's `--deskew`).
122: 3. **JPEG2000 background** — Add optional JPEG2000 background compression
123:    via OpenJPEG subprocess for better compression ratios than PNG.
124: 4. **Docling-style extraction** — A `--extract-json` mode that outputs
125:    structured Markdown/JSON for RAG pipelines.

(End of file - total 125 lines)
</content>
```

        "Search `⌘K`\n\nSuggest Edit \n\n \n\nFact-checked by Grok 3 months ago\n\n# EasyOCR\n\nAra Eve Leo Sal\n\n1x\n\nEasyOCR is an open-source Python library designed for optical character recognition (OCR), enabling the extraction of text from images with support for over 80 languages and various writing scripts, including Latin , Chinese , Arabic , Devanagari , and Cyrillic . [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() Developed and maintained by Jaided AI, the library was initially released through a GitHub commit on March 14, 2020, providing a ready-to-use solution for both natural scene text and dense document text. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() EasyOCR distinguishes itself with its straightforward installation via pip and minimal code requirements for implementation, making it accessible for developers in AI applications requiring text extraction from visual data. [[[3]]()]() [[[2]]()]() Key features include customizable models for specific use cases and compatibility with popular deep learning frameworks. [[[1]]()]()\n## Overview\n\n### Introduction to EasyOCR\n\nEasyOCR is an open-source Python library designed for optical character recognition (OCR), enabling the extraction of text from images, including both natural scene text and dense document text. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() Developed and maintained by Jaided AI, it provides a ready-to-use solution with minimal setup requirements, supporting acceleration on both GPU and CPU hardware for efficient performance across various computing environments. [[[1]]()]() The library integrates deep learning models such as CRAFT for text detection and CRNN for text recognition, facilitating accurate processing of diverse text inputs. [[[1]]()]() [[[4]]()]() Initiated through the creation of its GitHub repository on March 14, 2020, EasyOCR quickly gained traction as an accessible tool for developers and researchers in computer vision.\n[[[1]]()]() Its primary purpose is to democratize OCR capabilities by offering straightforward implementation without extensive configuration, making it suitable for applications ranging from document digitization to real-time text analysis. [[[2]]()]() Supporting over 80 languages and scripts, including Latin , Chinese , Arabic , Devanagari , and Cyrillic , EasyOCR stands out for its multilingual versatility in handling global text datasets. [[[1]]()]() This broad coverage, combined with its open-source nature, has positioned it as a key resource in AI-driven projects requiring robust text extraction from varied sources.\n\n### Key Features\n\nEasyOCR distinguishes itself through its extensive support for over 80 languages and scripts, encompassing diverse writing systems such as Latin, Chinese, Arabic, Devanagari, and Cyrillic.\n\n...\n\n[[[1]]()]() While native JSON output is not explicitly provided, the structured Python list format can be easily converted to JSON for integration into applications. [[[1]]()]() For interactive testing, a web-based demo is available on Hugging Face Spaces, powered by Gradio , enabling users to upload images and experiment with OCR functionality online. [[[1]]()]()\n\n## History and Development\n\n### Origins and Initial Release\n\nEasyOCR was developed by Jaided AI, a company specializing in optical character recognition (OCR) services and AI solutions. [[[1]]()]() The project originated as an open-source Python library aimed at providing a straightforward and effective tool for text extraction from images, with an emphasis on broad language support from its inception. [[[1]]()]() The initial public release occurred via a GitHub commit on March 14, 2020, marking the repository's launch under the Apache 2.0 license ."
      ]

        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# easyocr 1.7.2\n\npip install easyocr Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: Sep 24, 2024\n\nEnd-to-End Multi-Lingual Optical Character Recognition (OCR) Solution\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for rkcosmos from gravatar.com rkcosmos\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Homepage](https://github.com/jaidedai/easyocr)\n* [Download](https://github.com/jaidedai/easyocr.git)\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License:** Apache License 2.0\n* **Author:** Rakpong Kittinaradorn\n* Tags ocr , optical , character , recognition , deep , learning , neural , network\n\n###### Classifiers\n\n* **Development Status**\n  \n    + 5 - Production/Stable\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/easyocr/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\n# EasyOCR\n\n[PyPI Status](https://badge.fury.io/py/easyocr) [license](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR/blob/master/LICENSE) [Open in Colab](https://colab.to/easyocr) [Tweet](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Check%20out%20this%20awesome%20library:%20EasyOCR%20https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR) [Twitter](https://twitter.com/JaidedAI)\n\nReady-to-use OCR with 80+ [supported languages](https://www.jaided.ai/easyocr) and all popular writing scripts including: Latin, Chinese, Arabic, Devanagari, Cyrillic, etc.\n\n[Try Demo on our website](https://www.jaided.ai/easyocr)\n\nIntegrated into [Huggingface Spaces 🤗](https://huggingface.co/spaces) using [Gradio](https://github.com/gradio-app/gradio) . Try out the Web Demo: [Hugging Face Spaces](https://huggingface.co/spaces/tomofi/EasyOCR)\n\n## What's new\n\n* 24 September 2024 - Version 1.7.2\n  \n    + Fix several compatibilities\n* [Read all release notes](https://github.\ncom/JaidedAI/EasyOCR/blob/master/releasenotes.md)\n\n## What's coming next\n\n* Handwritten text support\n\n## Examples\n\nexample\n\nexample2\n\nexample3\n\n## Installation\n\nInstall using `pip`\n\nFor the latest stable release:\n\n```\npip install easyocr\n```\n\nFor the latest development release:\n\n```\npip install git+https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR.git\n```\n\nNote 1: For Windows, please install torch and torchvision first by following the official instructions here <https://pytorch.org> . On the pytorch website, be sure to select the right CUDA version you have. If you intend to run on CPU mode only, select `CUDA = None` .\n\nNote 2: We also provide a Dockerfile [here](https://github.com/JaidedAI/EasyOCR/blob/master/Dockerfile) .\n\n## Usage\n\n```\nimport easyocr \n reader = easyocr . Reader ([ 'ch_sim' , 'en' ]) # this needs to run only once to load the model into memory \n result = reader . readtext ( 'chinese.jpg' )\n```\n\n...\n\nTraining script is provided by [@gmuffiness](https://github.com/gmuffiness) .\n\nThe recognition model is a CRNN ( [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/1507.05717) ). It is composed of 3 main components: feature extraction (we are currently using [Resnet](https://arxiv.org/abs/1512.03385) ) and VGG, sequence labeling ( [LSTM](https://www.bioinf.jku.at/publications/older/2604.pdf) ) and decoding ( [CTC](https://www.cs.toronto.edu/~graves/icml_2006.pdf) ). The training pipeline for recognition execution is a modified version of the [deep-text-recognition-benchmark](https://github.com/clovaai/deep-text-recognition-benchmark) framework. (Thanks [@ku21fan](https://github.com/ku21fan) from [@clovaai](https://github.com/clovaai) ) This repository is a gem that deserves more recognition.\n\nBeam search code is based on this [repository](https://github.com/githubharald/CTCDecoder) and his [blog](https://towardsdatascience.com/beam-search-decoding-in-ctc-trained-"

      "title": "[Feature]: Alternative AI OCR \"surya\" as opposed to EasyOCR, Just found it today and it dominated the accuracy and speed of Tesseract & EasyOCR · Issue #1330 · ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF · GitHub",
      "publish_date": "2024-06-11",
      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nocrmypdf / **OCRmyPDF** Public\n\n* \n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 2\\.3k\n* Star 33\\.8k\n\n# [Feature]: Alternative AI OCR \"surya\" as opposed to EasyOCR, Just found it today and it dominated the accuracy and speed of Tesseract & EasyOCR \\#1330\n\nNew issue\n\nCopy link\n\nNew issue\n\nCopy link\n\nClosed\n\nClosed\n\n[[Feature]: Alternative AI OCR \"surya\" as opposed to EasyOCR, Just found it today and it dominated the accuracy and speed of Tesseract & EasyOCR]() \\#1330\n\nCopy link\n\nAssignees\n\njbarlow83\n\nLabels\n\n[enhancement](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22enhancement%22)\n\n[@abclution](https://github.com/abclution)\n\n## Description\n\n[@abclution](https://github.com/abclution)\n\n[abclution](https://github.com/abclution)\nopened [on Jun 11, 2024](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/issues/1330)\n\nIssue body actions\n\n### Describe the proposed feature\n\n[@jbarlow83](https://github.com/jbarlow83) First off, thanks for this excellent software.\n\nI wanted to share this amazing AI OCR I came across today, surya  \n<https://github.com/VikParuchuri/surya>\n\nIts accuracy and speed were miles ahead of both Tesseract and EasyOCR.\n\nI tried to use the EasyOCR plugin, but I had similar issues as reported on the plugin issues page as other users and was in the end unable to use it as part of the OCRmyPDF integrated workflow. This may be due to non Nvidia hardware, but I was able to use EasyOCR outside of OCRmyPDF.\n\nFirst off, surya has the same problem as EasyOCR, no hOCR output built in for easy plug in play alignment of detected text. It does however export some clearly and simply defined json that I believe a surya json output to hOCR converter would be enough.\n\nInstallation of surya is painless enough.\nAs well as it includes a streamlit demo gui to test it quickly.  \nI will put some images here that I was unable to get Tesseract to find any text (red text on pink background, lol) even after pre-processing.\n\nScaled version of the image sent to surya  \n[image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/10130237/338735549-34a2c4d8-0695-49ae-9d34-f3f4ddbc23c3.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.\n\n...\n\nDue to how surya detects the text correctly and the reading order correctly text can indexed for search and in databases more correctly.\n\nAnyways, just thought I would share what I found.\n\nReactions are currently unavailable\n\n## Metadata\n\n## Metadata\n\n### Assignees\n\n* @jbarlow83 jbarlow83\n\n### Labels\n\n[enhancement](https://github.com/ocrmypdf/OCRmyPDF/issues?q=state%3Aopen%20label%3A%22enhancement%22)\n\n### Type\n\nNo type\n\n### Fields\n\n[Give feedback](https://github.com/orgs/community/discussions/189141)\n\nNo fields configured for issues without a type.\n\n### Projects\n\nNo projects\n\n### Milestone\n\nNo milestone\n\n### Relationships\n\nNone yet\n\n### Development\n\nNo branches or pull requests\n\n## Issue actions\n\n## Footer"
      ]
    },

        "ocr-surya: Image-to-Text model — overview, use cases ...\nThe critical constraint before using this model: it operates under a GPL-3.0 code license and uses a modified OpenRAIL-M license for model weights—free for research, personal use, and startups under $2M funding/revenue, but commercial use requires a license from Datalab."

      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/surya-ocr",
      "title": "surya-ocr · PyPI",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Skip to main content Switch to mobile version\n\nPyPI\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\nSearch PyPI Search\n\n# surya-ocr 0.20.0\n\npip install surya-ocr Copy PIP instructions\n\nLatest release\n\nReleased: May 27, 2026\n\nOCR, layout, reading order, and table recognition in 90+ languages.\n\n### Verified details\n\n_These details have been [verified by PyPI](https://docs.pypi.org/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for vikp from gravatar.com vikp\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Repository](https://github.com/datalab-to/surya)\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License:** Apache-2.0\n* **Author:** Vik Paruchuri\n* Tags ocr , pdf , tables , text detection , text recognition\n* **Requires:** Python <4, >=3.10\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/surya-ocr/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\nDatalab Logo\n\n# Datalab\n**State of the Art models for Document Intelligence**\n\n[Code License](https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0) [Model License](https://www.datalab.to/pricing) [Discord](https://discord.gg/KuZwXNGnfH)\n\n[Homepage](https://www.datalab.to) [Docs](https://documentation.datalab.to) [Datalab Playground](https://www.datalab.to/playground)\n\n* * *\n\n# Surya\n\nSurya is a 650M param OCR model with these features:\n\n* Accuracy - scores 83.3% on [olmOCR-bench](https://huggingface.co/datasets/allenai/olmOCR-bench) (top under 3B params)\n* Speed - throughput of 5 pages/s on an RTX 5090\n* Multilingual - scores 87.2% on an internal benchmark set of 91 languages (more here )\n* Layout analysis (table, image, header, etc.) with reading order\n* Table recognition (rows + columns)\n\nWe also ship smaller models for line-level text detection and ocr error detection. It works on a range of documents (see usage and benchmarks ).\n\n## Try Datalab's Managed Platform\nOur managed platform runs both Surya, and variants of our highest accuracy model, [Chandra](https://github.com/datalab-to/chandra) .\n\nGet started with **$5 in free credits** —  (takes under 30 seconds) or try our free [public playground](https://www.datalab.to/playground?utm_source=gh-surya) .\n\n## Model Information\n\n|Detection |OCR |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n\n|Layout |Table Recognition |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n\nSurya is named for the [Hindu sun god](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surya) , who has universal vision.\n\n## Examples\n\nEach row links to five annotated views of the same page: text-line detection, OCR, layout, reading order, and (when present) table recognition.\n\n|Name |Detection |OCR |Layout |Order |Table Rec |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Newspaper |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Textbook |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Tax Form |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Handwritten Notes |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Corporate Doc |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n# Commercial usage\n\nThe Surya code is licensed under Apache 2.0. The model weights use a modified AI Pubs Open Rail-M license (free for research, personal use, and startups under $5M funding/revenue). For broader commercial licensing of the model weights, visit our pricing page [here](https://www.datalab.to/pricing?utm_source=gh-surya) .\n\n# Installation\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\n\n## Inference backend prerequisites\n\nSurya auto-spawns the server on first use, and you need `vllm` (NVIDIA GPU) or `llama.cpp` (CPU / Apple Silicon):\n\n* **NVIDIA GPU:** [Docker](https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/) plus the [NVIDIA Container Toolkit](https://docs.nvidia.com/datacenter/cloud-native/container-toolkit/latest/install-guide.html) .\n* **CPU / Apple Silicon:** the `llama-server` binary from llama.cpp:\n  \n  ```\n  brew install llama.cpp # macOS \n   # or grab a release from https://github.com/ggml-org/llama.cpp/releases\n  ```\n\n## Upgrading from Surya v1"
      ]

      "title": "\n      https://github.com/datalab-to/surya | Ecosyste.ms: Awesome\n    ",

        "| Name             |              Detection              |                                      OCR |                                     Layout |                                       Order |                                    Table Rec |\n  \n| Japanese         | Image | Image | Image | Image | Image |\n  \n| Chinese          | Image  |  Image |  Image |  Image |                                              |\n  \n| Hindi            |  Image   |    Image |    Image |    Image |                                              |\n  \n| Arabic           |  Image  |   Image |   Image |   Image |                                              |\n  \n| Chinese + Hindi  | Image | Image | Image | Image |                                              |\n| Presentation     |   Image   |     Image |     Image |     Image |     Image |\n  \n| Scientific Paper |  Image   |    Image |    Image |    Image |    Image |\n  \n| Scanned Document | Image  |  Image |  Image |  Image |  Image |\n  \n| New York Times   |   Image    |      Image |      Image |        Image |                                              |\n  \n| Scanned Form     |  Image   |    Image |    Image |    Image | Image |\n  \n| Textbook         | Image | Image | Image |   Image |                                              |\n\n# Hosted API\n\nThere is a hosted API for all surya models available [here](https://www.datalab.to?utm_source=gh-surya):\n\n- Works with PDF, images, word docs, and powerpoints\n  \n- Consistent speed, with no latency spikes\n  \n- High reliability and uptime\n\n# Commercial usage\n\nOur model weights use a modified AI Pubs Open Rail-M license (free for research, personal use, and startups under $2M funding/revenue) and our code is GPL.\nFor broader commercial licensing or to remove GPL requirements, visit our pricing page [here](https://www.datalab.to/pricing?utm_source=gh-surya).\n\n# Installation\n\nYou'll need python 3.10+ and PyTorch. You may need to install the CPU version of torch first if you're not using a Mac or a GPU machine.  See [here](https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/) for more details.\n\nInstall with:\n\n```shell\n  \npip install surya-ocr\n  \n```\n\nModel weights will automatically download the first time you run surya.\n\n# Usage\n\n- Inspect the settings in `surya/settings.py`.  You can override any settings with environment variables.\n  \n- Your torch device will be automatically detected, but you can override this.  For example, `TORCH_DEVICE=cuda`.\n\n## Interactive App\n\nI've included a streamlit app that lets you interactively try Surya on images or PDF files.  Run it with:\n\n```shell\n  \npip install streamlit pdftext\n  \nsurya_gui\n  \n```\n\n## OCR (text recognition)\n\n...\n\ncom/huggingface/transformers) from huggingface\n  \n- [CRAFT](https://github.com/clovaai/CRAFT-pytorch), a great scene text detection model\n\nThank you to everyone who makes open source AI possible.\n\n# Citation\n\nIf you use surya (or the associated models) in your work or research, please consider citing us using the following BibTeX entry:\n\n```bibtex\n  \n@misc{paruchuri2025surya,\n  \n  author       = {Vikas Paruchuri and Datalab Team},\n  \n  title        = {Surya: A lightweight document OCR and analysis toolkit},\n  \n  year         = {2025},\n  \n  howpublished = {\\url{https://github.com/VikParuchuri/surya}},\n  \n  note         = {GitHub repository},\n  \n}\n```\n\nEcosyste.ms\n\nTools and open datasets to support, sustain, and secure critical digital infrastructure.\n\nCode: [AGPL-3](https://github.com/ecosyste-ms/documentation/blob/main/LICENSE) — Data: [CC BY-SA 4.0](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/)\n\n[](https://github.com/ecosyste-ms) [](https://mastodon."
