    {
      "url": "https://dev.to/aairom/using-suryaocr-with-docling-1d9k",
      "title": "Using SuryaOCR with Docling - DEV Community",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "DEV Community\n\n[Powered by Algolia](https://www.algolia.com/developers/?utm_source=devto&utm_medium=referral)\n\n \n\n## DEV Community\n\nAdd reaction\n\nLike Unicorn Exploding Head Raised Hands Fire\n\nJump to Comments Save Boost\n\nCopy link\n\n[Share to X](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=%22Using%20SuryaOCR%20with%20Docling%22%20by%20Alain%20Airom%20%28Ayrom%29%20%23DEVCommunity%20https%3A%2F%2Fdev.to%2Faairom%2Fusing-suryaocr-with-docling-1d9k) [Share to LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle?mini=true&url=https%3A%2F%2Fdev.to%2Faairom%2Fusing-suryaocr-with-docling-1d9k&title=Using%20SuryaOCR%20with%20Docling&summary=Combination%20of%20Docling%20with%20SuryaOCR.%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20%20What%20is%20Surya%20OCR%3F%20%20%20Surya%20is%20a%20high-performance%2C...&source=DEV%20Community) [Share to Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php?u=https%3A%2F%2Fdev.to%2Faairom%2Fusing-suryaocr-with-docling-1d9k) [Share to Mastodon](https://s2f.kytta.dev/?text=https%3A%2F%2Fdev.to%2Faairom%2Fusing-suryaocr-with-docling-1d9k)\n\nReport Abuse\n\nAlain Airom (Ayrom)\n\nAlain Airom (Ayrom)\n\nPosted on Jan 21\n\n# Using SuryaOCR with Docling\n\\# suriyaocr \\# docling \\# documentprocessing \\# gpu\n\nCombination of Docling with SuryaOCR.\n\n[](https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2Fd814yc171ws22qii4ye6.png)\n\n##  What is Surya OCR?\n\nSurya is a high-performance, multilingual document OCR toolkit designed to provide “universal vision” through accurate text detection and recognition across more than 90 languages. Named after the Hindu sun god, the project — available via its [GitHub](https://github.com/datalab-to/surya) repository and [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/surya-ocr/0.2.0/) — offers a comprehensive suite of features including line-level text detection, layout analysis (identifying tables, images, and headers), reading order detection, and LaTeX OCR.\nDeveloped by Vik Paruchuri, Surya stands out for its specialized focus on complex document structures, benchmarking favorably against traditional tools like Tesseract by leveraging modern deep learning architectures to deliver superior accuracy and sophisticated visual understanding for a wide variety of document types.\n\n> Surya Project description  \n> Surya is a document OCR toolkit that does:\n> \n> * Accurate OCR in 90+ languages\n> * Line-level text detection in any language\n> * Table and chart detection (coming soon)\n>   Surya is named for the Hindu sun god, who has universal vision.\n> \n> \n\n[](https://media2.dev.to/dynamic/image/width=800%2Cheight=%2Cfit=scale-down%2Cgravity=auto%2Cformat=auto/https%3A%2F%2Fdev-to-uploads.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fuploads%2Farticles%2F5wd7z30s74xgdtxbzi73.png)\n\n> From Surya PyPi Page\n> \n> \n\n##  The Power of two: Surya and Docling\n\nCombining the precision of **Surya-OCR** with the structural intelligence of Docling creates a powerful pipeline for transforming static documents into machine-readable data.\n\n...\n\nThis combination doesn’t just “read” text — it understands the spatial and logical relationships of headers, tables, and formulas, delivering a high-fidelity Markdown output that is tailor-made for LLM ingestion and RAG architectures. Ultimately, this pairing empowers developers to transform massive, disorganized archives into a searchable, structured, and actionable data lake with unprecedented speed and accuracy.\n\n> > > Thanks for reading <<<\n> > > \n> > > \n> > \n> \n> \n\n##  Links\n\n* Sample Used for this post: [https://docling-project.github.io/docling/examples/suryaocr\\_with\\_custom\\_models/](https://docling-project.github.io/docling/examples/suryaocr_with_custom_models/)\n* Docling Project: <https://github.com/docling-project/docling>\n* Pypi page of Surya-ocr: <https://pypi.org/project/surya-ocr/0.2.0/>\n* Surya GitHub Page: <https://github.com/datalab-to/surya>\n\n## Top comments (0)\n\nSubscribe\n\npic\n\nPersonal Trusted User\n\nCreate template\n\nTemplates let you quickly answer FAQs or store snippets for re-use.\n\nSubmit Preview Dismiss\n\nCode of Conduct • Report abuse\n\nAre you sure you want to hide"
      ]

      "url": "https://github.com/ThinkCode/surya-pdf-ocr",
      "title": "GitHub - ThinkCode/surya-pdf-ocr: OCR, layout analysis ...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## Community\n\n[Discord](https://discord.gg//KuZwXNGnfH) is where we discuss future development.\n\n## Examples\n\n|Name |Detection |OCR |Layout |Order |Table Rec |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Japanese |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Chinese |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Hindi |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Arabic |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Chinese + Hindi |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Presentation |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scientific Paper |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Scanned Document |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|New York Times |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n|Scanned Form |Image |Image |Image |Image |Image |\n|Textbook |Image |Image |Image |Image | |\n\n# Hosted API\n\nThere is a hosted API for all surya models available [here](https://www.datalab.to/) :\n\n* Works with PDF, images, word docs, and powerpoints\n* Consistent speed, with no latency spikes\n* High reliability and uptime\n\n# Commercial usage\nI want surya to be as widely accessible as possible, while still funding my development/training costs. Research and personal usage is always okay, but there are some restrictions on commercial usage.\n\nThe weights for the models are licensed `cc-by-nc-sa-4.0` , but I will waive that for any organization under $5M USD in gross revenue in the most recent 12-month period AND under $5M in lifetime VC/angel funding raised. You also must not be competitive with the [Datalab API](https://www.datalab.to/) . If you want to remove the GPL license requirements (dual-license) and/or use the weights commercially over the revenue limit, check out the options [here](https://www.datalab.to) .\n\n# Installation\n\nYou'll need python 3.9+ and PyTorch. You may need to install the CPU version of torch first if you're not using a Mac or a GPU machine. See [here](https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally/) for more details.\n\nInstall with:\n\n```\npip install surya-ocr\n```\nModel weights will automatically download the first time you run surya.\n\n# Usage\n\n* Inspect the settings in `surya/settings.py` . You can override any settings with environment variables.\n* Your torch device will be automatically detected, but you can override this. For example, `TORCH_DEVICE=cuda` .\n\n## Interactive App\n\nI've included a streamlit app that lets you interactively try Surya on images or PDF files. Run it with:\n\n```\npip install streamlit\nsurya_gui\n```\n\n## OCR (text recognition)\n\nThis command will write out a json file with the detected text and bboxes:\n\n```\nsurya_ocr DATA_PATH\n```\n\n* `DATA_PATH` can be an image, pdf, or folder of images/pdfs\n* `--langs` is an optional (but recommended) argument that specifies the language(s) to use for OCR. You can comma separate multiple languages. Use the language name or two-letter ISO code from [here](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639_language_codes) . Surya supports the 90+ languages found in `surya/languages.py` .\n\n...\n\nOCR, layout analysis, reading order, table recognition in 90+ languages\n\n[www.datalab.to](https://www.datalab.to \"https://www.datalab.to\")\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nGPL-3.0 license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**1** star\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\nNo releases published\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 100\\.0%\n\n## Footer"
      ]

{
  "search_id": "search_323f27f75fc641a3883b7cfef678f6fb",
  "results": [
    {
      "url": "https://github.com/mindee/doctr",
      "title": "GitHub - mindee/doctr: docTR (Document Text Recognition) - a seamless, high-performing & accessible library for OCR-related tasks powered by Deep Learning. · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "python scripts/analyze.py path/to/your/doc.pdf\n```\n\nAll script arguments can be checked using `python scripts/analyze.py --help`\n\n### Minimal API integration\n\nLooking to integrate docTR into your API? Here is a template to get you started with a fully working API using the wonderful [FastAPI](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi) framework.\n\n#### Deploy your API locally\n\nSpecific dependencies are required to run the API template, which you can install as follows:\n\n```\ncd api/\npip install poetry\nmake lock\npip install -r requirements.txt\n```\n\nYou can now run your API locally:\n\n```\nuvicorn --reload --workers 1 --host 0.0.0.0 --port=8002 --app-dir api/ app.main:app\n```\n\nAlternatively, you can run the same server on a docker container if you prefer using:\n\n```\nPORT=8002 docker-compose up -d --build\n```\n\n#### What you have deployed\n\nYour API should now be running locally on your port 8002.\nAccess your automatically-built documentation at <http://localhost:8002/redoc> and enjoy your three functional routes (\"/detection\", \"/recognition\", \"/ocr\", \"/kie\"). Here is an example with Python to send a request to the OCR route:\n\n```\nimport requests \n\n params = { \"det_arch\" : \"db_resnet50\" , \"reco_arch\" : \"crnn_vgg16_bn\" }\n\n with open ( \"/path/to/your/doc.jpg\" , \"rb\" ) as f :\n    files = [  # application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png supported \n        ( \"files\" , ( \"doc.jpg\" , f . read (), \"image/jpeg\" )),\n    ]\n print ( requests . post ( \"http://localhost:8080/ocr\" , params = params , files = files ). json ())\n```\n\n### Example notebooks\n\nLooking for more illustrations of docTR features? You might want to check the [Jupyter notebooks](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/tree/main/notebooks) designed to give you a broader overview.\n\n## Citation\n\nIf you wish to cite this project, feel free to use this [BibTeX](http://www.bibtex.org/) reference:\n\n```\n@misc { doctr2021 ,\ntitle = { docTR: Document Text Recognition } ,\n    author = { Mindee } ,\n    year = { 2021 } ,\n    publisher = { GitHub } ,\n    howpublished = { \\url{https://github.com/mindee/doctr} } \n}\n```\n\n## Contributing\n\nIf you scrolled down to this section, you most likely appreciate open source. Do you feel like extending the range of our supported characters? Or perhaps submitting a paper implementation? Or contributing in any other way?\n\nYou're in luck, we compiled a short guide (cf. [`CONTRIBUTING`](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/contributing/contributing.html) ) for you to easily do so!\n\n## License\n\nDistributed under the Apache 2.0 License. See [`LICENSE`](https://github.com/mindee/doctr?tab=Apache-2.0-1-ov-file) for more information.\n\n## About\n\ndocTR (Document Text Recognition) - a seamless, high-performing & accessible library for OCR-related tasks powered by Deep Learning.\n\n[mindee.github.io/doctr/](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/ \"https://mindee.github.io/doctr/\")\n\n### Topics\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\n### Code of conduct\n\nCode of conduct\n\n### Contributing\n\nContributing\n\nActivity\n\nCustom properties\n\n### Stars\n\n**6\\.1k** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**51** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**649** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases 21\n\nv1"

      "url": "https://github.com/dess-h/mindee-doctr",
      "title": "GitHub - dess-h/mindee-doctr: docTR (Document Text Recognition) - a seamless, high-performing & accessible library for OCR-related tasks powered by Deep Learning. · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "### Minimal API integration\n\nLooking to integrate docTR into your API? Here is a template to get you started with a fully working API using the wonderful [FastAPI](https://github.com/tiangolo/fastapi) framework.\n\n#### Deploy your API locally\n\nSpecific dependencies are required to run the API template, which you can install as follows:\n\n```\ncd api/\npip install poetry\nmake lock\npip install -r requirements.txt\n```\n\nYou can now run your API locally:\n\n```\nuvicorn --reload --workers 1 --host 0.0.0.0 --port=8002 --app-dir api/ app.main:app\n```\n\nAlternatively, you can run the same server on a docker container if you prefer using:\n\n```\nPORT=8002 docker-compose up -d --build\n```\n\n#### What you have deployed\n\nYour API should now be running locally on your port 8002. Access your automatically-built documentation at <http://localhost:8002/redoc> and enjoy your three functional routes (\"/detection\", \"/recognition\", \"/ocr\", \"/kie\"). Here is an example with Python to send a request to the OCR route:\n```\nimport requests \n\n params = { \"det_arch\" : \"db_resnet50\" , \"reco_arch\" : \"crnn_vgg16_bn\" }\n\n with open ( '/path/to/your/doc.jpg' , 'rb' ) as f :\n    files = [  # application/pdf, image/jpeg, image/png supported \n        ( \"files\" , ( \"doc.jpg\" , f . read (), \"image/jpeg\" )),\n    ]\n print ( requests . post ( \"http://localhost:8080/ocr\" , params = params , files = files ). json ())\n```\n\n### Example notebooks\n\nLooking for more illustrations of docTR features? You might want to check the [Jupyter notebooks](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/tree/main/notebooks) designed to give you a broader overview.\n\n## Citation\n\nIf you wish to cite this project, feel free to use this [BibTeX](http://www.bibtex.org/) reference:\n\n```\n@misc { doctr2021 ,\n    title = { docTR: Document Text Recognition } ,\n    author = { Mindee } ,\n    year = { 2021 } ,\n    publisher = { GitHub } ,\n    howpublished = { \\url{https://github.com/mindee/doctr} } \n}\n```\n\n## Contributing\nIf you scrolled down to this section, you most likely appreciate open source. Do you feel like extending the range of our supported characters? Or perhaps submitting a paper implementation? Or contributing in any other way?\n\nYou're in luck, we compiled a short guide (cf. [`CONTRIBUTING`](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/contributing/contributing.html) ) for you to easily do so!\n\n## License\n\nDistributed under the Apache 2.0 License. See [`LICENSE`](https://github.com/mindee/doctr?tab=Apache-2.0-1-ov-file) for more information.\n\n## About\n\ndocTR (Document Text Recognition) - a seamless, high-performing & accessible library for OCR-related tasks powered by Deep Learning.\n\n[mindee.github.io/doctr/](https://mindee.github.io/doctr/ \"https://mindee.github.io/doctr/\")\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**0** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\n20 tags\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*"
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/2025/08/31/doctr-the-fast-open-source-way-to-extract-text-from-documents-and-images-using-ocr/",
      "title": "docTR: The Fast, Open-Source Way to Extract Text from ...",
      "publish_date": "2025-08-31",
      "excerpts": [
        "[Bright Coding](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev)\n\n[Home](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev)\n\n[Home](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev)\n\n1. [Home](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev)\n2. \n3. [Apps](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/category/apps)\n4. \n5. docTR: The Fast, Open-Source Way to Extr...\n\n# docTR: The Fast, Open-Source Way to Extract Text from Documents and Images Using OCR\n\nM\n\nMohamed IDBRAHIM\n\nAug 31, 2025\n\n[Apps](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/category/apps) [Tools](https://www.blog.brightcoding.dev/category/tools)\n\n5 min read\n\ndocTR: The Fast, Open-Source Way to Extract Text from Documents and Images Using OCR\n\n0\n\nEvery year the world produces more than **2\\.5 trillion PDFs, scans, photographs and screenshots** .  \nAlmost all of them contain text that is invisible to software: you can look at a page, but you cannot search it, index it, or pipe it into an analytics pipeline.\nOptical Character Recognition (OCR) is the bridge between those pixels and the structured data world.  \nThis article walks you through **[docTR](https://github.com/mindee/doctr)** an MIT-licensed library that the company **Mindee** open-sourced to make state-of-the-art OCR **as simple as three lines of Python** .\n\n* * *\n\n1. What is docTR?\n\n* * *\n\nFeatureWhat it means for you **End-to-end OCR** Detects where text lives on a page and then reads the characters in one call **PyTorch & TensorFlow** Swap backends without changing a single line of application code **CPU & GPU ready** Runs on a laptop, a server or a cloud GPU with the same API **PDF, image, or URL** Feed it a file path, a list of JPEGs, or even a live web page **Production-grade** Same accuracy as Google Vision / AWS Textract on public benchmarks---\n\n1. How OCR Works in docTR\n\n* * *\n\n[docTR](https://github.com/mindee/doctr) decomposes the problem into two neural-network stages:\n\n1. **Text Detection**\nFinds the bounding boxes of every word, line or block on the page.  \n   Supported algorithms:\n\n* DBNet (Differentiable Binarization)\n* LinkNet\n* FAST (Faster Arbitrarily-Shaped Text Detector)\n\n1. **Text Recognition**  \n   Cuts the detected boxes out of the image and feeds them to a sequence model that reads the characters.  \n   Backends include:\n\n* CRNN (CNN + RNN)\n* SAR (Show, Attend & Read)\n* MASTER\n* ViTSTR & PARSeq (Vision Transformers)\n\nYou can mix and match: for example, `db_resnet50` for detection and `crnn_vgg16_bn` for recognition.  \nIf you do not care about the internals, just call `ocr_predictor(pretrained=True)` and docTR will pick the best defaults.\n\n* * *\n\n1. Installing the Library\n\n* * *\n\n```\n# CPU-only, smallest footprint\npip install python-doctr\n\n# With GPU support, visualization, HTML export\npip install \"python-doctr[viz,html]\" torch torchvision --extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118\n```\n\nNeed the absolute latest code?\n\n```"
      ]
    },

(Document AI, OCR, and Chart Understanding / Models and tools)\n* awesome-context-ai \\- docTR\n  \\- platform | Document text recognition | ![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mindee/doctr) | (OCR & Text Extraction / Context-Relevant MCP Servers)\n\n## [README](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/blob/main/README.md)\n\n```\n          \n\n[![Slack Icon](https://img.shields.io/badge/Slack-Community-4A154B?\nstyle=flat-square&logo=slack&logoColor=white)](https://slack.mindee.com) [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-blue.svg)](LICENSE) ![Build Status](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/workflows/builds/badge.svg) [![Docker Images](https://img.shields.io/badge/Docker-4287f5?style=flat&logo=docker&logoColor=white)](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/pkgs/container/doctr) [![codecov](https://codecov.io/gh/mindee/doctr/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=577MO567NM)](https://codecov.io/gh/mindee/doctr) [![CodeFactor](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/mindee/doctr/badge?s=bae07db86bb079ce9d6542315b8c6e70fa708a7e)](https://www.codefactor.io/repository/github/mindee/doctr) [![Codacy Badge](https://api.codacy.com/project/badge/Grade/340a76749b634586a498e1c0ab998f08)](https://app.codacy.com/gh/mindee/doctr?utm_source=github.com&utm_medium=referral&utm_content=mindee/doctr&utm_campaign=Badge_Grade) [![Doc Status](https://github.com/mindee/doctr/workflows/doc-status/badge."

      "url": "https://mindee.github.io/doctr/index.html",
      "title": "docTR documentation - GitHub Pages",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "docTR documentation\n\nBack to top\n\nView this page\n\n# docTR: Document Text Recognition ¶\n\nState-of-the-art Optical Character Recognition made seamless & accessible to anyone, powered by PyTorch\n\nDocTR provides an easy and powerful way to extract valuable information from your documents:\n\n* 🧾 **for automation** : seamlessly process documents for Natural Language Understanding tasks: we provide OCR predictors to parse textual information (localize and identify each word) from your documents.\n* 👩‍🔬 **for research** : quickly compare your own architectures speed & performances with state-of-art models on public datasets.\n\n## Main Features ¶\n\n* 🤖 Robust 2-stage (detection + recognition) OCR predictors with pretrained parameters\n* ⚡ User-friendly, 3 lines of code to load a document and extract text with a predictor\n* 🚀 State-of-the-art performance on public document datasets, comparable with GoogleVision/AWS Textract\n* ⚡ Optimized for inference speed on both CPU & GPU\n* 🐦 Light package, minimal dependencies\n* 🛠️ Actively maintained by Mindee\n* 🏭 Easy integration (available templates for browser demo & API deployment)\n\n### Supported datasets ¶\n\n* FUNSD from [“FUNSD: A Dataset for Form Understanding in Noisy Scanned Documents”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1905.13538.pdf) .\n* CORD from [“CORD: A Consolidated Receipt Dataset forPost-OCR Parsing”](https://openreview.net/pdf?id=SJl3z659UH) .\n* SROIE from [ICDAR 2019](https://rrc.cvc.uab.es/?ch=13) .\n* IIIT-5k from [CVIT](https://cvit.iiit.ac.in/research/projects/cvit-projects/the-iiit-5k-word-dataset) .\n* Street View Text from [“End-to-End Scene Text Recognition”](http://vision.ucsd.edu/~kai/pubs/wang_iccv2011.pdf) .\n* SynthText from [Visual Geometry Group](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/scenetext/) .\n* SVHN from [“Reading Digits in Natural Images with Unsupervised Feature Learning”](http://ufldl.stanford.edu/housenumbers/nips2011_housenumbers.pdf) .\n* IC03 from [ICDAR 2003](http://www.iapr-tc11.\norg/mediawiki/index.php?title=ICDAR_2003_Robust_Reading_Competitions) .\n* IC13 from [ICDAR 2013](http://dagdata.cvc.uab.es/icdar2013competition/) .\n* IMGUR5K from [“TextStyleBrush: Transfer of Text Aesthetics from a Single Example”](https://github.com/facebookresearch/IMGUR5K-Handwriting-Dataset) .\n* MJSynth from [“Synthetic Data and Artificial Neural Networks for Natural Scene Text Recognition”](https://www.robots.ox.ac.uk/~vgg/data/text/) .\n* IIITHWS from [“Generating Synthetic Data for Text Recognition”](https://github.com/kris314/hwnet) .\n* WILDRECEIPT from [“Spatial Dual-Modality Graph Reasoning for Key Information Extraction”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2103.14470v1.pdf) .\n* COCO-Text dataset from [“COCO-Text: Dataset and Benchmark for Text Detection and Recognition in Natural Images”](https://arxiv.org/pdf/1601.07140v2) .\n\nNext Installation\n\nMade with [Sphinx](https://www.sphinx-doc.org/) and [@pradyunsg](https://pradyunsg.me) 's [Furo](https://github.com/pradyunsg/furo)\n\nOn this page\n* docTR: Document Text Recognition\n  \n    + Main Features\n        \n        - Model zoo\n                  \n            * Text detection models\n            * Text recognition"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://www.mindee.com/",
      "title": "AI Document Processing API - Mindee",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "4\\.8/5 (30+ reviews)\n\n4\\.9/5 (10+ reviews)\n\n# Automate document processing with AI-powered API\n\nFrom simple photos to complex PDFs or handwritten files, our API turn your document data into structured JSON with high‑reliability. Zero model training required. Any alphabets, any languages supported.\n\n[14-day free trial](https://app.mindee.com/signup) Explore platform\n\nTrusted by top-tier teams worldwide\n\nv2-Carlabella\n\nv2-Spendesk\n\nv2 Payfit\n\nv2 Lucca\n\nv2 Circula\n\nv2-Carlabella\n\nv2-Spendesk\n\nv2 Payfit\n\nv2 Lucca\n\nv2 Circula\n\n## Most advanced AI OCR features  \nGetting your document processing to the next level\n\nExtract\n\n Split\n\n Classify\n\n Crop\n\nOur AI-driven OCR API provides high-precision data extraction for all document formats, enabling businesses to automate workflows with speed and total reliability.\n\nAccelerate processing by automatically breaking multi-page uploads into separate documents.\nOur solution detects document boundaries to split batches into distinct records ready for extraction.\n\nAutomate your workflow by sorting incoming documents instantly. Mindee OCR API distinguishes between document types, routing each file to its specific category for streamlined data management.\n\nDigitize multiple documents scanned on a single page with automated detection. Mindee OCR API isolates and crops each item into a standalone file, ensuring every record is processed individually.\n\nPlatform overview\n\nChaining\n\n## Chain your pre-processing & extraction models in a single API call\n\nA single API call to manage, manipulate, detect, and isolate the elements to be processed, then extract each one according to your specific needs. At no additional cost.\n\n## Companies around the world use Mindee’s API\n\nSpendesk Lucca Indy\n\n \n\nLearn more\n\nMaxime Rihouey Lead ML Engineer @ Spendesk\n\nDocuments processed\n\nInvoices\n\nReceipts\n\nFeatures most used\n\nExtract\n\nLearn more\nAntoine Diekmann Head of the Finance BU @ Lucca\n\nDocuments processed\n\nInvoices\n\nReceipts\n\nFeatures most used\n\nExtract\n\nLearn more\n\nBoris Chabilan Lead Product Manager @ Indy\n\nDocuments processed\n\nReceipts\n\nFeatures most used\n\nExtract\n\nAuto-crop\n\nAll customer stories\n\ncontinuous learning model\n\n## Train and custom your extraction model to deal with every edge-case\n\nMaster the complexity of non-standard documents with an architecture built for total adaptability. Our platform moves beyond static extraction by leveraging continuous learning to refine performance.  \n  \nBy integrating RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), you can upload documents to create a dynamic knowledge base of past corrections and specific business contexts. This ensures that even the most unique edge‑cases are handled with precision, turning rare exceptions into standard automated successes through a self-improving feedback loop."
      ]
    },

      "publish_date": "2021-12-07",
      "excerpts": [
        "Dec 7, 2021 8:30 AM Eastern Standard Time\n\n# **Mindee Introduces Advanced Open-Source Optical Character Recognition with docTR**\n\nShare\n\n* * *\n\n_Leveraging deep learning and open-source, Mindee opens up state-of-the-art OCR capabilities to benefit the entire developer community_\n\nSAN FRANCISCO--( [BUSINESS WIRE](https://www.businesswire.com) )-- [Mindee](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fmindee.com%2F%3Futm_source%3D10f%26utm_campaign%3Dgithub1&esheet=52545917&newsitemid=20211207005248&lan=en-US&anchor=Mindee&index=1&md5=d1092f807dad2de9e25d48177687cb3e) , the API-first platform designed for developers to eliminate manual data entry, announced the introduction of docTR, a seamless, high-performing, and accessible open-source library for OCR-related tasks powered by deep learning.\n\nMindee’s [docTR](https://cts.businesswire.com/ct/CT?id=smartlink&url=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.\ncom%2Fmindee%2Fdoctr%2Freleases&esheet=52545917&newsitemid=20211207005248&lan=en-US&anchor=docTR&index=2&md5=908baaf8f0b515347a0082327f8b2e9f) provides optical character recognition with accessibility for the entire developer community. Combining textual parsing through text and object detection and recognition, this open-source repository offers a wider range and complex use cases. Going beyond the textual elements, it provides a holistic view of information encoded in visual forms, including QR codes, barcodes, information in ID pictures, and even logos.\n\nPowered by the machine learning tool of your choice, TensorFlow 2 or PyTorch, DocTR features training capabilities for text detection in documents and images as well as recognition with pretrained parameters. It incorporates a five-line code to load documents, extract text with a predictor, and optimize for very high end-to-end performances, including inference speed on both CPU and GPU."
      ]
    }

      "url": "https://grokipedia.com/page/Umi-OCR",
      "title": "Umi-OCR — Grokipedia",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Search `⌘K`\n\nSuggest Edit \n\n \n\nFact-checked by Grok 2 months ago\n\n# Umi-OCR\n\nAra Eve Leo Sal\n\n1x\n\n**Umi-OCR** is a free and open-source optical character recognition (OCR) software that operates entirely offline, enabling users to extract text from screenshots, images, PDFs, and other documents without any internet connection or data upload, thereby prioritizing privacy and local processing. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() [[[3]]()]() Developed primarily in Python and QML by hiroi-sora and hosted on GitHub under the MIT license, Umi-OCR features a tabbed interface in its current major version 2 series (latest release v2.1.5 as of March 2025) and supports platforms including Windows 7 x64 and later, as well as Linux x64 through dedicated runtime environments. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() The project has gained significant popularity, with over 42,700 stars and 4,200 forks on GitHub.\n[[[1]]()]() Core capabilities include screenshot capture with hotkey-based OCR, batch processing of unlimited images in formats such as JPG, PNG, and TIFF, PDF document recognition with options to generate searchable dual-layer PDFs while ignoring headers, footers, or watermarks, and QR code/barcode scanning and generation across multiple protocols. [[[2]]()]() [[[3]]()]() Additional tools encompass formula recognition (convertible to LaTeX), text post-processing for layout parsing, and multilingual support covering over 100 languages with built-in engines such as PaddleOCR (higher accuracy) and RapidOCR (faster performance). [[[3]]()]() [[[2]]()]() The software provides flexible integration options, including command-line and HTTP interfaces for external use, and emphasizes no installation requirement—users can unzip and run it directly—along with GPU-accelerated rendering and customizable settings for themes, fonts, and OCR plugins.\n[[[2]]()]() While macOS compatibility remains a long-term development goal without current official support, the project focuses on Windows and Linux users seeking efficient, privacy-focused text extraction. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]()\n\n## Overview\n\n### Description\n\nUmi-OCR is a free and open-source optical character recognition (OCR) software that operates entirely offline without requiring an internet connection. [[[2]]()]() This design prioritizes user privacy by keeping all processing local and enables fast performance on sufficient hardware, which can surpass online OCR services in speed. [[[2]]()]() The software focuses on efficient batch text extraction from images, screenshots, and PDF documents, supporting common document handling needs such as excluding watermarks."
      ]

        "Umi-OCR\n\nホーム ダウンロード ブログ 概要 [GitHub](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n\n日本語\n\n# Umi-OCR オフラインOCRソフト（Windows / Linux）\n\nUmi-OCRでスクリーンショット、画像、PDFを100%オフラインで文字認識。アップロード不要。\n\nUmi-OCRをダウンロード [GitHubで見る](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n\n41K+\n\nGitHub Stars\n\n100+\n\n対応言語\n\n100%\n\nOffline\n\n## 強力な機能\n\nテキスト認識に必要なすべてを提供\n\n### スクリーンショットOCR\n\nホットキーで画面の任意の領域からテキストを即座にキャプチャして認識\n\n### バッチ処理\n\n数百枚の画像を一度に高精度で処理、数量制限なし\n\n### PDFスキャン\n\nスキャンしたPDFからテキストを抽出し、検索可能な二層PDFを作成\n\n### QRコード認識\n\n画像からQRコードと19種類のバーコードをスキャンして読み取り\n\n### 数式認識\n\n画像内の数式をLaTeX形式に変換\n\n### 完全オフライン\n\nすべての処理はローカルで実行 - データが外部に送信されることはありません\n\n## よくある質問\n\nUmi-OCRは本当に無料ですか？\n\nはい、Umi-OCRはMITライセンスの完全無料オープンソースソフトウェアです。隠れた料金、サブスクリプション、使用制限、透かしはありません。\n\nインターネット接続は必要ですか？\n\nいいえ、Umi-OCRは完全にオフラインで動作します。すべてのテキスト認識はローカルで実行され、ドキュメントや画像のプライバシーが保護されます。\n\nPaddleバージョンとRapidバージョンの違いは？\n\nPaddleバージョンは高精度で大きなモデル（約128MB）、複雑なドキュメントに最適。Rapidバージョンは高速処理で小さなサイズ（約97MB）、素早い作業に最適。\nどの言語に対応していますか？\n\nUmi-OCRは100以上の言語に対応。英語、中国語、日本語、韓国語、ロシア語、ドイツ語、フランス語、その他ほとんどのヨーロッパ言語を含みます。\n\n手書き文字を認識できますか？\n\nはい、Umi-OCRは印刷文字と手書き文字の両方を認識できます。ただし精度は文字の明瞭さやスタイルによって異なります。\n\nシステム要件は何ですか？\n\nUmi-OCRはWindows 7以降とLinux x64（Debian）で動作。約200MBのディスク容量が必要で、ハイエンドPCでもローエンドPCでも動作します。\n\n## Umi-OCRをダウンロード\n\nお好みのバージョンを選んで、今すぐテキスト認識を始めましょう\n\n[Paddleバージョン (~128MB)](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases/download/v2.1.5/Umi-OCR_Paddle_v2.1.5.7z.exe) [Rapidバージョン (~98MB)](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases/download/v2.1.5/Umi-OCR_Rapid_v2.1.5.7z.exe)\n\n最新バージョン: v2.1.5\n\nUmi-OCR\n\n無料オープンソースのオフラインOCRソフトウェア\n\n[](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n\n### クイックリンク\n\n* ホーム\n* ダウンロード\n* ブログ\n\n### 法的情報\n\n* プライバシーポリシー\n* 利用規約\n\n### リソース\n\n* [GitHub](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR)\n* [Releases](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/releases)\n* [Issues](https://github.com/hiroi-sora/Umi-OCR/issues)\n\nこのウェブサイトはUmi-OCR公式プロジェクトとは関係ありません。\n\nUmi-OCR | Windows・Linux向けオフラインOCRソフト"

        "Umiocr Logo\n\nAbout How To FAQ Blog\n\n# Umi OCR — Free Online OCR Tool, Extract Text from Images & PDFs Instantly\n\n**Umi OCR** is a free online OCR tool that runs **100% in your browser** . Extract text from images, screenshots & scanned PDFs — **50 languages supported** , no uploads, no login, completely free.\n\nLoading OCR engine...\n\nDrop an image or PDF here, click to browse, or paste directly (Ctrl+V)\n\nPNG, JPG, WebP, BMP, or scanned PDF\n\nBrowse Files Take Photo\n\nPreview\n\nBoxes: **0** Detection: **0ms** Recognition: **0ms** Total: **0ms**\n\nExtract Text\n\nExtracted Text Copy\n\n**Umi OCR** (also written as **Umiocr** ) is a free online OCR tool that lets you **extract text from images and scanned PDFs** instantly — no software installation required. Simply upload a JPG, PNG, or scanned PDF and **convert image to text** in seconds, running directly in your browser.\nWhether you need to perform **OCR from a screenshot** , digitize a scanned receipt, extract text from a business card photo, or convert a scanned PDF to editable text — Umi OCR handles it all, 100% locally. Supports **50 languages** including Chinese (Simplified & Traditional), English, Japanese, Korean, French, German, Spanish, and more.\n\nImage to Text PDF OCR Screenshot OCR 50 Languages 100% Browser-Based No Data Upload Free & Unlimited No Login Required\n\nLanguage Support\n\n## 50 Languages Supported by Umi OCR\n\nUmiocr uses a single unified model to recognize text in **50 languages** — no model switching required. From East Asian scripts to Latin-alphabet languages, Umiocr handles them all automatically.\n\n🇨🇳 Simplified Chinese\n\n🇹🇼 Traditional Chinese\n\n🇺🇸 English\n\n🇯🇵 Japanese\n\n🇰🇷 Korean\n\n🇫🇷 French\n\n🇩🇪 German\n\n🇪🇸 Spanish\n\n🇵🇹 Portuguese\n\n🇮🇹 Italian\n\n🇷🇺 Russian\n\n🇸🇦 Arabic\n\n🇮🇳 Hindi\n\n🇳🇱 Dutch\n\n🇵🇱 Polish\n🇸🇪 Swedish\n\n🇳🇴 Norwegian\n\n🇩🇰 Danish\n\n🇫🇮 Finnish\n\n🇨🇿 Czech\n\n🇸🇰 Slovak\n\n🇷🇴 Romanian\n\n🇭🇺 Hungarian\n\n🇺🇦 Ukrainian\n\n🌍 \\+26 More Languages\n\nFeatures\n\n## Why Choose Umi OCR?\n\nFree, private, browser-based OCR that runs entirely on your device — no installs, no uploads, no fees.\n\n### 50 Languages — Image & PDF OCR\n\nUmiocr supports 50 languages in a single unified model: Simplified & Traditional Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, French, German, Spanish, Arabic, Hindi, and 41 more Latin-script and Asian languages — no model switching required.\n\n### 100% Free & Unlimited\n\nUmi OCR is completely free with zero limits. No subscription, no API key, no registration, no hidden fees. Convert images and PDFs to text as many times as you need — forever free for everyone.\n\n### Runs Entirely in Your Browser\n\nThe entire OCR engine runs locally inside your browser tab via ONNX Runtime Web."

Dismiss alert\n\nRamakm / **Deepseek-OCR** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 2\n* Star 0\n\n# Ramakm/Deepseek-OCR\n\nmain\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[3 Commits](https://github.com/Ramakm/Deepseek-OCR/commits/main/)\n\n 3 Commits |\n|configs |configs | | |\n|data |data | | |\n|demo |demo | | |\n|docs |docs | | |\n|evaluation |evaluation | | |\n|models |models | | |\n|preprocess |preprocess | | |\n|scripts |scripts | | |\n|utils |utils | | |\n|ARCHITECTURE.md |ARCHITECTURE.md | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|requirements.txt |requirements.txt | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* MIT license\n\n# DeepSeek-OCR: Contexts Optical Compression\n\n[](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.18234) \n\nA research implementation of **DeepSeek-OCR** based on the paper [\"DeepSeek-OCR: Contexts Optical Compression\"](https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.18234) . This project explores OCR as a compression-decompression task using vision-language models.\n\n## Overview\n\nDeepSeek-OCR compresses text-rich images into compact vision tokens, then decodes them back to text using a language model. Key features:\n\n* **High Compression** : 64-400 vision tokens for documents with 600-5000+ text tokens\n* **Strong Performance** : ~97% accuracy with <10× compression ratio\n\n...\n\n* **Model Weights** : [HuggingFace](https://huggingface.co/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR)\n* **Related Work** :\n  \n    + [DeepSeek-VL](https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.05525) \\- Vision-Language foundation\n    + [Vary](https://arxiv.org/abs/2312.06109) \\- Vision vocabulary\n    + [InternVL](https://arxiv.org/abs/2404.16821) \\- Multi-resolution VLM\n\n## Contributing\n\nContributions welcome! Areas:\n\n* Training scripts with distributed support\n* Gundam (tiling) mode implementation\n* Layout-aware OCR prompts\n* Multilingual evaluation\n* MoE decoder integration\n* More baseline comparisons\n\n## License\n\nMIT License. See LICENSE for details.\n\n## Acknowledgments\n\n* DeepSeek AI team for the original paper and research\n* HuggingFace for transformers library\n* timm library for vision backbones\n* Open-source OCR community (Tesseract, PaddleOCR)\n\n* * *\n\n**Note** : This is a research implementation for educational purposes. For production use, consider the [official DeepSeek-OCR model](https://huggingface."

      "url": "https://deepseekocr.io/",
      "title": "DeepSeek-OCR - Free, Open Source, High Performance OCR Tool",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Effortlessly extract tables, charts, and key figures from dense financial reports, invoices, and bank statements. The high accuracy and layout-aware parsing reduce manual data entry, minimize errors, and accelerate financial workflows and analysis.\n\nFinancial Report Analysis with DeepSeek-OCR\n\nLLM Data Pipelines\n\n## Powering Next-Gen AI with Quality Data\n\nThe ultimate data source for LLMs.\n\nGenerate vast, high-quality datasets for training Large Language Models (LLMs) and Vision-Language Models (VLMs). DeepSeek-OCR's efficiency makes it economically viable to process millions of documents, creating diverse and rich training corpora.\n\nDiverse Document Processing for LLMs\n\nDigital Archiving\n\n## Creating Efficient Digital Libraries\n\nArchive, search, and manage documents.\n\nTransform physical documents into a lightweight, searchable digital archive.\nThe optical compression ensures that storage requirements are minimized while the high-quality text extraction makes every document instantly discoverable.\n\nDigital Archiving with DeepSeek-OCR\n\n## A Breakthrough in Efficiency and Accuracy\n\nDeepSeek-OCR isn't just another text recognition tool. It's a fundamental rethinking of how machines process visual information, built to be both powerful and lightweight.\n\n📦\n\n### Contexts Optical Compression\n\nThe core innovation. We represent high-resolution documents with a fraction of the vision tokens, dramatically reducing computational costs and increasing processing speed.\n\n🎯\n\n### State-of-the-Art Accuracy\n\nAchieve up to 97% OCR precision on complex benchmarks. DeepSeek-OCR surpasses leading models while using significantly fewer resources.\n\n📄\n\n### Versatile Document Parsing\n\nGo beyond plain text. Intelligently extract data from complex layouts, including academic papers, financial charts, chemical formulas, and geometric figures.\n\n🚀\n### Built for Massive Scale\n\nEngineered for real-world deployment, capable of processing over 200,000 pages per day on a single GPU, making it ideal for large-scale data annotation and LLM training.\n\n🌍\n\n### Global Language Recognition\n\nTrained on a vast dataset covering nearly 100 languages, allowing for accurate text extraction from international documents without changing models.\n\n🧠\n\n### Low-Memory Architecture\n\nThe novel DeepEncoder design processes high-resolution images while maintaining low activation memory, enabling deployment on less powerful hardware and reducing operational costs.\n\n## Frequently Asked Questions\n\nWhat is DeepSeek-OCR? ▼\n\nDeepSeek-OCR is a new vision-language model that specializes in recognizing and extracting text and data from documents using an innovative technique called 'Contexts Optical Compression'. What makes 'Contexts Optical Compression' different? ▼"
      ]
    },

      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/deepseek-ocr-cli",
      "title": "deepseek-ocr-cli · PyPI",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "org/project/deepseek-ocr-cli/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\n#  DeepSeek OCR CLI\n\n[PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/deepseek-ocr-cli) [Python 3.10+](https://www.python.org/downloads/) [License: MIT](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)\n\nCommand-line tool for OCR using DeepSeek vision models. Supports Ollama (local) and vLLM (GPU server) backends.\n\n##  Features\n\n* **Multi-backend** : Ollama (local, free) and vLLM (OpenAI-compatible API)\n* Supports PDFs and images (JPG, PNG, WEBP, GIF, BMP, TIFF)\n* Per-document output folders with figures\n* Batch processing with incremental resume (skips already-processed files)\n* Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures\n* Parallel page processing for faster PDF OCR\n* `--dry-run` to preview files before processing\n* Clean markdown output with HTML tables converted to markdown\n\n##  Choosing an OCR tool\nThis is one of five OCR CLI tools with a shared design: clean Markdown output, batch processing, and figure extraction. Pick based on your constraints:\n\n|Tool |Engine |Runs |Cost |Best for |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|**deepseek-ocr-cli** (this repo) |DeepSeek vision |Local (Ollama / vLLM) |Free |General-purpose local OCR with multi-backend flexibility |\n|[gemini-ocr-cli](https://github.com/r-uben/gemini-ocr-cli) |Google Gemini |Cloud API |Free tier / Pay-per-use |Fast cloud OCR with concurrent processing |\n|[marker-ocr-cli](https://github.com/r-uben/marker-ocr-cli) |Marker (Surya + Texify) |Local |Free |Academic papers with equations, tables, complex layouts |\n|[mistral-ocr-cli](https://github.com/r-uben/mistral-ocr-cli) |Mistral OCR API |Cloud API |~$1/1k pages |Structured extraction (tables, headers, footers) |\n|[nougat-ocr-cli](https://github.com/r-uben/nougat-ocr-cli) |Meta Nougat |Local (GPU) |Free |Academic papers, GPU-accelerated batch processing |\n\n##  Requirements\n\n* Python 3.\n10+\n* [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) installed and running (for Ollama backend)\n* `deepseek-ocr` model pulled in Ollama\n\n##  Installation\n\n###  1\\. Install Ollama\n\n```\n# macOS/Linux \nbrew install ollama\n\n # Or download from https://ollama.ai\n```\n\n###  2\\. Pull the DeepSeek-OCR model\n\n```\nollama pull deepseek-ocr\n```\n\n###  3\\. Install the CLI\n\n```\npip install deepseek-ocr-cli\n```\n\n##  Quick Start\n\n```\n# Process a single image \ndeepseek-ocr document.jpg\n\n # Process a PDF \ndeepseek-ocr paper.pdf\n\n # Process all files in a directory \ndeepseek-ocr ./documents/ --recursive\n\n # Preview files without processing \ndeepseek-ocr ./documents/ --dry-run\n\n # Custom output directory \ndeepseek-ocr doc.pdf -o ./results/\n\n # Use vLLM backend \ndeepseek-ocr paper.pdf --backend vllm --vllm-url http://gpu-server:8000/v1\n\n # Parallel processing for faster PDF OCR \ndeepseek-ocr large-document.pdf -w 2 \n\n # Extract and analyze embedded figures \ndeepseek-ocr paper.pdf --analyze-figures"
      ]
    },

Beta\n* **Intended Audience**\n  \n    + Developers\n* **Programming Language**\n  \n    + Python :: 3\n    + Python :: 3.10\n    + Python :: 3.11\n    + Python :: 3.12\n* **Topic**\n  \n    + Scientific/Engineering :: Image Recognition\n    + Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules\n\n[Report project as malware](https://pypi.org/project/deepseek-ocr/submit-malware-report/)\n\n* Project description\n* Project details\n* Release history\n* Download files\n\n## Project description\n\n#  DeepSeek-OCR-SDK\n\n[PyPI version](https://pypi.org/project/deepseek-ocr/) [License: MIT](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT) [Python 3.10+](https://www.python.org/downloads/) [Code style: black](https://github.com/psf/black)\n\nEnglish | 中文\n\n* * *\n\n##  English\n\n###  Overview\n\n**DeepSeek-OCR-SDK** is a simple and efficient Python SDK for the DeepSeek OCR API. It provides a clean, production-ready interface for converting documents (PDF, images) to Markdown text with high accuracy and performance.\n\n###  Key Features\n\n...\n\norg/project_metadata/)_\n\n###### Maintainers\n\nAvatar for bukely from gravatar.com bukely\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **Author:** Chengjie\n\n### Unverified details\n\n_These details have **not** been verified by PyPI_\n\n###### Project links\n\n* [Documentation](https://github.com/BukeLy/DeepSeek-OCR-SDK)\n* [Homepage](https://github.com/BukeLy/DeepSeek-OCR-SDK)\n* [Issues](https://github.com/BukeLy/DeepSeek-OCR-SDK/issues)\n* [Repository](https://github.com/BukeLy/DeepSeek-OCR-SDK)\n\n###### Meta\n\n* **License Expression:** MIT  \n  _[SPDX](https://spdx.org/licenses/) [License Expression](https://spdx.github.io/spdx-spec/v3.0.1/annexes/spdx-license-expressions/)_\n* Tags ocr , deepseek , document-processing , pdf , image-to-text\n* **Requires:** Python >=3.10\n* **Provides-Extra:** `dev`\n\n###### Classifiers\n\n* **Development Status**\n  \n    + 4 - Beta\n* **Intended Audience**\n  \n    + Developers\n* **Programming Language**\n  \n    + Python :: 3\n    + Python :: 3.10\n    + Python :: 3.11\n    + Python :: 3.12\n* **Topic**\n  \n    + Scientific/Engineering :: Image Recognition"

        "com/vllm-project/vllm) team for their help.\n* [2025/10/20]🚀🚀🚀 We release DeepSeek-OCR, a model to investigate the role of vision encoders from an LLM-centric viewpoint.\n\n## Contents\n\n* Install\n* vLLM Inference\n* Transformers Inference\n\n## Install\n\n> Our environment is cuda11.8+torch2.6.0.\n> \n> \n\n1. Clone this repository and navigate to the DeepSeek-OCR folder\n\n```\ngit clone https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR.git\n```\n\n1. Conda\n\n```\nconda create -n deepseek-ocr python=3.12.9 -y\nconda activate deepseek-ocr\n```\n\n1. Packages\n\n* download the vllm-0.8.5 [whl](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/tag/v0.8.5)\n\n```\npip install torch==2.6.0 torchvision==0.21.0 torchaudio==2.6.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu118\npip install vllm-0.8.5+cu118-cp38-abi3-manylinux1_x86_64.whl\npip install -r requirements.txt\npip install flash-attn==2.7.3 --no-build-isolation\n```\n**Note:** if you want vLLM and transformers codes to run in the same environment, you don't need to worry about this installation error like: vllm 0.8.5+cu118 requires transformers>=4.51.1\n\n## vLLM-Inference\n\n* VLLM:\n\n> **Note:** change the INPUT\\_PATH/OUTPUT\\_PATH and other settings in the DeepSeek-OCR-master/DeepSeek-OCR-vllm/config.py\n> \n> \n\n```\ncd DeepSeek-OCR-master/DeepSeek-OCR-vllm\n```\n\n1. image: streaming output\n\n```\npython run_dpsk_ocr_image.py\n```\n\n1. pdf: concurrency ~2500tokens/s(an A100-40G)\n\n```\npython run_dpsk_ocr_pdf.py\n```\n\n1. batch eval for benchmarks\n\n```\npython run_dpsk_ocr_eval_batch.py\n```\n\n**[2025/10/23] The version of upstream [vLLM](https://docs.vllm.ai/projects/recipes/en/latest/DeepSeek/DeepSeek-OCR.html) :**\n\n```\nuv venv\n source .venv/bin/activate\n #  Until v0.11.1 release, you need to install vLLM from nightly build \nuv pip install -U vllm --pre --extra-index-url https://wheels.vllm.ai/nightly\n```\n\n```\nfrom vllm import LLM , SamplingParams \n from vllm .\nmodel_executor . models . deepseek_ocr import NGramPerReqLogitsProcessor \n from PIL import Image \n\n # Create model instance \n llm = LLM (\n    model = \"deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR\" ,\n    enable_prefix_caching = False ,\n    mm_processor_cache_gb = 0 ,\n    logits_processors = [ NGramPerReqLogitsProcessor ]\n)\n\n # Prepare batched input with your image file \n image_1 = Image . open ( \"path/to/your/image_1.png\" ). convert ( \"RGB\" )\n image_2 = Image . open ( \"path/to/your/image_2.png\" ). convert ( \"RGB\" )\n prompt = \"<image> \\n Free OCR.\" \n\n model_input = [\n    {\n        \"prompt\" : prompt ,\n        \"multi_modal_data\" : { \"image\" : image_1 }\n    },\n    {\n        \"prompt\" : prompt ,\n        \"multi_modal_data\" : { \"image\" : image_2 }\n    }\n]\n\n sampling_param = SamplingParams (\n            temperature = 0.0 ,\n            max_tokens = 8192 ,\n            # ngram logit processor args \n            extra_args = dict (\n                ngram_size = 30 ,\n                window_size = 90 ,\n\n...\n\n### License\n\nMIT license\n\nActivity\n\nCustom properties\n\n### Stars\n\n**23\\.4k** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**107** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**2\\.2k** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\nNo releases published\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 100\\.0%\n\n## Footer"

        "\"multi_modal_data\" : { \"image\" : image_2}\n    }\n]\n\nsampling_param = SamplingParams(\n            temperature= 0.0 ,\n            max_tokens= 8192 ,\n             # ngram logit processor args \n            extra_args= dict (\n                ngram_size= 30 ,\n                window_size= 90 ,\n                whitelist_token_ids={ 128821 ,  128822 },   # whitelist: <td>, </td> \n            ),\n            skip_special_tokens= False ,\n        )\n # Generate output \nmodel_outputs = llm.generate(model_input, sampling_param)\n\n # Print output for  output  in  model_outputs:\n     print (output.outputs[ 0 ].text)\n```\n\n##  Visualizations\n\n| | |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n\n##  Acknowledgement\n\nWe would like to thank [Vary](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/Vary/) , [GOT-OCR2.0](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/) , [MinerU](https://github.com/opendatalab/MinerU) , [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) , [OneChart](https://github.\ncom/LingyvKong/OneChart) , [Slow Perception](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/Slow-Perception) for their valuable models and ideas.\n\nWe also appreciate the benchmarks: [Fox](https://github.com/ucaslcl/Fox) , [OminiDocBench](https://github.com/opendatalab/OmniDocBench) .\n\n##  Citation\n\n```\n@article{wei2025deepseek,\n  title={DeepSeek-OCR: Contexts Optical Compression},\n  author={Wei, Haoran and Sun, Yaofeng and Li, Yukun},\n  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2510.18234},\n  year={2025}\n}\n```\n\nDownloads last month\n    2,219,126\n\nSafetensors [](https://huggingface.co/docs/safetensors)\n\nModel size\n\n3B params\n\nTensor type\n\nBF16\n\n·\n\nFiles info\n\nInference Providers [NEW](https://huggingface.co/docs/inference-providers)\n\nImage-Text-to-Text\n\nThis model isn't deployed by any Inference Provider.       "url": "https://huggingface.co/docs/transformers/model_doc/got_ocr2",
      "title": "GOT-OCR2 · Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# GOT-OCR2\n\n## Overview\n\nThe GOT-OCR2 model was proposed in [General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model](https://huggingface.co/papers/2409.01704) by Haoran Wei, Chenglong Liu, Jinyue Chen, Jia Wang, Lingyu Kong, Yanming Xu, Zheng Ge, Liang Zhao, Jianjian Sun, Yuang Peng, Chunrui Han, Xiangyu Zhang.\n\nThe abstract from the paper is the following:\n\n*Traditional OCR systems (OCR-1.0) are increasingly unable to meet people’snusage due to the growing demand for intelligent processing of man-made opticalncharacters. In this paper, we collectively refer to all artificial optical signals (e.g., plain texts, math/molecular formulas, tables, charts, sheet music, and even geometric shapes) as \"characters\" and propose the General OCR Theory along with an excellent model, namely GOT, to promote the arrival of OCR-2.0. The GOT, with 580M parameters, is a unified, elegant, and end-to-end model, consisting of a high-compression encoder and a long-contexts decoder.\nAs an OCR-2.0 model, GOT can handle all the above \"characters\" under various OCR tasks. On the input side, the model supports commonly used scene- and document-style images in slice and whole-page styles. On the output side, GOT can generate plain or formatted results (markdown/tikz/smiles/kern) via an easy prompt. Besides, the model enjoys interactive OCR features, i.e., region-level recognition guided by coordinates or colors. Furthermore, we also adapt dynamic resolution and multipage OCR technologies to GOT for better practicality. In experiments, we provide sufficient results to prove the superiority of our model.*\n\n<img src=\"https://huggingface.co/datasets/huggingface/documentation-images/resolve/main/transformers/model_doc/got_ocr_overview.png\"\nalt=\"drawing\" width=\"600\"/>\n\n GOT-OCR2 training stages. Taken from the original paper. \n\nTips:\nGOT-OCR2 works on a wide range of tasks, including plain document OCR, scene text OCR, formatted document OCR, and even OCR for tables, charts, mathematical formulas, geometric shapes, molecular formulas and sheet music. While this implementation of the model will only output plain text, the outputs can be further processed to render the desired format, with packages like `pdftex`, `mathpix`, `matplotlib`, `tikz`, `verovio` or `pyecharts`.\nThe model can also be used for interactive OCR, where the user can specify the region to be recognized by providing the coordinates or the color of the region's bounding box.\n\nThis model was contributed by [yonigozlan](https://huggingface.co/yonigozlan).\nThe original code can be found [here](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0).\n\n## Usage example\n\n### Plain text inference\n\n```python\nfrom transformers import AutoModelForImageTextToText, AutoProcessor\n\nmodel = AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained(\"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2."

        "GOT-OCR-2.0-hf: Image-to-Text model — overview, use cases ...\nGOT-OCR-2.0-hf is a unified end-to-end optical character recognition model developed by stepfun-ai that handles plain document OCR, scene text OCR, formatted document OCR, tables, charts, mathematical formulas, geometric shapes, molecular formulas, and sheet music recognition."

      "excerpts": [
        "# \n\nabhinand\n\n/\n\nGOT-OCR-2.0-unofficial\n\nlike 17\n\nSafetensors GOT ocr vision\n\narxiv: 2409\\.01704\n\narxiv: 2312\\.06109\n\nModel card Files Files and versions xet [Community 1](https://huggingface.co/abhinand/GOT-OCR-2.0-unofficial/discussions)\n\nCopy to bucket new\n\n**Note:** ORIGINAL MODEL REPO: <https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0>\n\n* * *\n\n### General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model\n\n[](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/) [](https://arxiv.org/abs/2409.01704) [](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/blob/main/assets/wechat.jpg) [](https://zhuanlan.zhihu.com/p/718163422)\n\n[Haoran Wei\\*](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=J4naK0MAAAAJ&hl=en) , Chenglong Liu\\*, Jinyue Chen, Jia Wang, Lingyu Kong, Yanming Xu, [Zheng Ge](https://joker316701882.github.io/) , Liang Zhao, [Jianjian Sun](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=MVZrGkYAAAAJ&hl=en) , [Yuang Peng](https://scholar.google.com.hk/citations?\nuser=J0ko04IAAAAJ&hl=zh-CN&oi=ao) , Chunrui Han, [Xiangyu Zhang](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yuB-cfoAAAAJ&hl=en)\n\n##  Release\n\n* [2024/9/03]🔥🔥🔥 We open-source the codes, weights, and benchmarks. The paper can be found in this [repo](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/blob/main/GOT-OCR-2.0-paper.pdf) . We also have submitted it to Arxiv.\n* [2024/9/03]🔥🔥🔥 We release the OCR-2.0 model GOT!\n\n[Code License](https://github.com/tatsu-lab/stanford_alpaca/blob/main/LICENSE) [Data License](https://github.com/tatsu-lab/stanford_alpaca/blob/main/DATA_LICENSE)\n\n**Usage and License Notices** : The data, code, and checkpoint are intended and licensed for research use only. They are also restricted to use that follow the license agreement of Vary.\n\n##  Community contributions\n\nWe encourage everyone to develop GOT applications based on this repo. Thanks for the following contributions :\n\n[Colab of GOT](https://colab.research.google.\ncom/drive/1nmiNciZ5ugQVp4rFbL9ZWpEPd92Y9o7p?usp=sharing) ~ contributor： [@Zizhe Wang](https://github.com/PaperPlaneDeemo)\n\n##  Contents\n\n* Install\n* GOT Weights\n* Demo\n* Train\n* Eval\n\n* * *\n\nTowards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model\n\n* * *\n\n##  Install\n\n1. Our environment is cuda11.8+torch2.0.1\n2. Clone this repository and navigate to the GOT folder\n\n```\ngit  clone  https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0.git\n cd 'the GOT folder'\n```\n\n1. Install Package\n\n```\nconda create -n got python=3.10 -y\nconda activate got\npip install -e .\n```\n\n1. Install Flash-Attention\n\n```\npip install ninja\npip install flash-attn --no-build-isolation\n```\n\n##  GOT Weights\n\n* [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1OdDtsJ8bFJYlNUzCQG4hRkUL6V-qBQaN?usp=sharing)\n* [BaiduYun](https://pan.baidu.com/s/1G4aArpCOt6I_trHv_1SE2g) code: OCR2\n\n##  Demo\n\n1. plain texts OCR:\n\n```\npython3 GOT/demo/run_ocr_2.0.py  --model-name  /GOT_weights/  --image-file  /an/image/file.png  --type ocr\n```\n\n1."
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR2_0",
      "title": "stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR2_0 · Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "user=J0ko04IAAAAJ&hl=zh-CN&oi=ao) , Chunrui Han, [Xiangyu Zhang](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yuB-cfoAAAAJ&hl=en)\n\n[image/jpeg](https://cdn-uploads.huggingface.co/production/uploads/6653eee7a2d7a882a805ab95/QCEFY-M_YG3Bp5fn1GQ8X.jpeg)\n\n##  Usage\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs. Requirements tested on python 3.10：\n\n```\ntorch==2.0.1\ntorchvision==0.15.2\ntransformers==4.37.2\ntiktoken==0.6.0\nverovio==4.3.1\naccelerate==0.28.0\n```\n\n```\nfrom  transformers  import  AutoModel, AutoTokenizer\n\ntokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained( 'ucaslcl/GOT-OCR2_0' , trust_remote_code= True )\nmodel = AutoModel.from_pretrained( 'ucaslcl/GOT-OCR2_0' , trust_remote_code= True , low_cpu_mem_usage= True , device_map= 'cuda' , use_safetensors= True , pad_token_id=tokenizer.eos_token_id)\nmodel = model. eval ().cuda()\n\n # input your test image \nimage_file =  'xxx.jpg' # plain texts OCR \nres = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type= 'ocr' )\n\n # format texts OCR: # res = model.\nchat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format') # fine-grained OCR: # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='ocr', ocr_box='') # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format', ocr_box='') # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='ocr', ocr_color='') # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format', ocr_color='') # multi-crop OCR: # res = model.chat_crop(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='ocr') # res = model.chat_crop(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format') # render the formatted OCR results: # res = model.chat(tokenizer, image_file, ocr_type='format', render=True, save_render_file = './demo.html') print (res)\n```\n\nMore details about 'ocr\\_type', 'ocr\\_box', 'ocr\\_color', and 'render' can be found at our GitHub.\nOur training codes are available at our [GitHub](https://github.com/Ucas-HaoranWei/GOT-OCR2.0/) .\n\n##  More Multimodal Projects\n\n👏 Welcome to explore more multimodal projects of our team:\n\n[Vary](https://github.\ncom/Ucas-HaoranWei/Vary) | [Fox](https://github.com/ucaslcl/Fox) | [OneChart](https://github.com/LingyvKong/OneChart)\n\n##  Citation\n\nIf you find our work helpful, please consider citing our papers 📝 and liking this project ❤️！\n\n```\n@article{wei2024general,\n  title={General OCR Theory: Towards OCR-2.0 via a Unified End-to-end Model},\n  author={Wei, Haoran and Liu, Chenglong and Chen, Jinyue and Wang, Jia and Kong, Lingyu and Xu, Yanming and Ge, Zheng and Zhao, Liang and Sun, Jianjian and Peng, Yuang and others},\n  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2409.01704},\n  year={2024}\n}\n@article{liu2024focus,\n  title={Focus Anywhere for Fine-grained Multi-page Document Understanding},\n  author={Liu, Chenglong and Wei, Haoran and Chen, Jinyue and Kong, Lingyu and Ge, Zheng and Zhu, Zining and Zhao, Liang and Sun, Jianjian and Han, Chunrui and Zhang, Xiangyu},\n  journal={arXiv preprint arXiv:2405.14295},\n  year={2024}\n}\n@article{wei2023vary,"

        "GOT-OCR2_0: Image-to-Text model — overview, use cases ...\nGOT-OCR-2.0-hf by stepfun-ai provides a Hugging Face Transformers native implementation versus the trust_remote_code=True approach used here. Choose the HF implementation if you prioritize code transparency and avoiding custom deserialization; choose this version if you need the original architecture exactly as researched."

      "excerpts": [
        "# \n\nstepfun-ai\n\n/\n\nGOT-OCR-2.0-hf\n\nlike 234\n\nFollow\n\nStepFun 2\\.88k\n\nImage-Text-to-Text Transformers Safetensors multilingual got\\_ocr2 got vision-language ocr2.0\n\narxiv: 2409\\.01704\n\narxiv: 2405\\.14295\n\narxiv: 2312\\.06109\n\nLicense: apache-2.0\n\nModel card Files Files and versions xet [Community 11](https://huggingface.co/stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf/discussions)\n\nDeploy\n\nCopy to bucket new\n\nUse this model\n\n### Instructions to use stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf with libraries, inference providers, notebooks, and local apps. Follow these links to get started.\n\n* Libraries\n* Transformers\n  \n  How to use stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf with Transformers:\n  \n  ```\n  # Use a pipeline as a high-level helper\n  from transformers import pipeline\n  \n  pipe = pipeline(\"image-text-to-text\", model=\"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\")\n  ```\n  \n  ```\n  # Load model directly\n  from transformers import AutoProcessor, AutoModelForMultimodalLM\n  \n  processor = AutoProcessor.from_pretrained(\"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\")\nmodel = AutoModelForMultimodalLM.from_pretrained(\"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\")\n  ```\n* Notebooks\n* Google Colab\n* Kaggle\n* Local Apps Settings\n* vLLM\n  \n  How to use stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf with vLLM:\n  \n  ##### Install from pip and serve model\n  \n  ```\n  # Install vLLM from pip:\n  pip install vllm\n  # Start the vLLM server:\n  vllm serve \"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\"\n  # Call the server using curl (OpenAI-compatible API):\n  curl -X POST \"http://localhost:8000/v1/completions\" \\\n  \t-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  \t--data '{\n  \t\t\"model\": \"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\",\n  \t\t\"prompt\": \"Once upon a time,\",\n  \t\t\"max_tokens\": 512,\n  \t\t\"temperature\": 0.5\n  \t}'\n  ```\n  \n  ##### Use Docker\n  \n  ```\n  docker model run hf.co/stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\n  ```\n* SGLang\n  \n  How to use stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf with SGLang:\n  \n  ##### Install from pip and serve model\n  \n  ```\n  # Install SGLang from pip:\n  pip install sglang\n  # Start the SGLang server:\n  python3 -m sglang.launch_server \\\n--model-path \"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\" \\\n      --host 0.0.0.0 \\\n      --port 30000\n  # Call the server using curl (OpenAI-compatible API):\n  curl -X POST \"http://localhost:30000/v1/completions\" \\\n  \t-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  \t--data '{\n  \t\t\"model\": \"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\",\n  \t\t\"prompt\": \"Once upon a time,\",\n  \t\t\"max_tokens\": 512,\n  \t\t\"temperature\": 0.5\n  \t}'\n  ```\n  \n  ##### Use Docker images\n  \n  ```\n  docker run --gpus all \\\n      --shm-size 32g \\\n      -p 30000:30000 \\\n      -v ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface \\\n      --env \"HF_TOKEN=<secret>\" \\\n      --ipc=host \\\n      lmsysorg/sglang:latest \\\n      python3 -m sglang.launch_server \\\n          --model-path \"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\" \\\n          --host 0.0.0.0 \\\n          --port 30000\n  # Call the server using curl (OpenAI-compatible API):\n  curl -X POST \"http://localhost:30000/v1/completions\" \\\n  \t-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  \t--data '{\n  \t\t\"model\": \"stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf\","

        "跳转至\n\n🎉 最新更新日志： v3.9.0\n\nRapidOCR 文档\n\nRapidOCR 开源 OCR 工具\n\n正在初始化搜索引擎\n\n[RapidAI/RapidOCR](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR \"前往仓库\")\n\n[](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCRDocs/blob/main/docs/index.md \"编辑此页\") [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCRDocs/raw/main/docs/index.md \"查看本页的源代码\")\n\n# 概览\n\n**_信创级开源 OCR - 为世界内容安全贡献力量_**\n\n[](https://clawhub.ai/rapidai/rapidocr) [](https://huggingface.co/spaces/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3) [](https://www.modelscope.cn/studios/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3.0.0/summary) [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/RapidAI/RapidOCR/blob/main/assets/RapidOCRDemo.ipynb) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/graphs/contributors) [](https://pepy.tech/project/rapidocr) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/rapidocr/) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/stargazers) [SemVer2.0](https://semver.org/) [](https://github.com/psf/black)\n\n### 📝 简介 ¶\n\nRapidOCR 是一款完全开源免费、支持离线快速部署的多平台多语言 OCR 工具，以极致的速度与广泛的兼容性为核心优势。\n\n**支持语言：** 默认支持中英文识别。其他支持的语言，参见文档： [模型列表](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/model_list/)\n**项目缘起：** 鉴于 [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) 在工程化方面仍有优化空间，为简化并加速 OCR 模型在各类终端设备上的推理部署，我们创新性地将 PaddleOCR 中的模型转换为高度兼容的 ONNX 格式，并基于 Python, C++, Java, C# 等多种编程语言，实现了跨平台的无缝移植，让开发者能够轻松上手、高效集成。\n\n**名称寓意：** RapidOCR 这一名称承载着我们对产品的核心期待——轻快（操作简便、响应迅速）、好省（资源占用低、成本效益高）且智能（依托深度学习技术，实现精准高效的识别）。我们专注于发挥人工智能的优势，打造小巧而强大的模型，始终将速度作为不懈追求，同时确保卓越的识别效果。\n\n**使用指南：**\n\n* **直接部署** ：若本仓库提供的模型已满足您的需求，只需参考 [快速开始](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/quickstart/) 即可快速完成 RapidOCR 的部署与使用。\n* **定制化微调** ：若现有模型无法满足特定场景需求，您可在 PaddleOCR 基础上，利用自有数据进行微调，再将优化后的模型应用于 RapidOCR 的部署流程，实现个性化定制。\n如果您觉得本项目对您的工作或学习有所帮助，恳请您不吝赐予一颗 ⭐ Star，给予我们宝贵的支持与鼓励！\n\n### 整体框架 ¶\n\n```\n---\nconfig:\n  fontFamily: \"LXGW WenKai Screen\"\n---\nflowchart LR\n    subgraph Step\n    direction TB\n    C(Text Det) --> D(Text Cls) --> E(Text Rec)\n    end\n\n    A[/OurSelf Dataset/] --> B(PaddleOCR) --Train--> Step --> F(PaddleOCRModelConverter)\n    F --ONNX--> G{RapidOCR Deploy\\n<b>Python/C++/Java/C#</b>}\n    G --> H(Windows x86/x64) & I(Linux) & J(Android) & K(Web) & L(Raspberry Pi)\n\n    click B \"https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR\" _blank\n    click F \"https://github.com/RapidAI/PaddleOCRModelConverter\" _blank\n```\n\n### 🎥 效果展示 ¶\n\nDemo\n\n### 👥 谁在使用？( [更多](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/discussions/286) ) ¶\n\n* [Docling](https://github.com/docling-project/docling)\n* [CnOCR](https://github.com/breezedeus/CnOCR)\n* [api-for-open-llm](https://github.com/xusenlinzy/api-for-open-llm)\n* [arknights-mower](https://github.com/ArkMowers/arknights-mower)\n* [pensieve](https://github.com/arkohut/pensieve)\n* [genshin\\_artifact\\_auxiliary](https://github.com/SkeathyTomas/genshin_artifact_auxiliary)\n* [ChatLLM](https://github.com/yuanjie-ai/ChatLLM)\n* [langchain](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain)\n* [Langchain-Chatchat](https://github.com/chatchat-space/Langchain-Chatchat)\n* [JamAIBase](https://github.com/EmbeddedLLM/JamAIBase)\n* [PAI-RAG](https://github.com/aigc-apps/PAI-RAG)\n* [ChatAgent\\_RAG](https://github.com/junyuyang7/ChatAgent_RAG)\n* [OpenAdapt](https://github.com/OpenAdaptAI/OpenAdapt)\n*     {
      "url": "https://prompts.brightcoding.dev/blog/rapidocr-the-lightning-fast-ocr-every-developer-needs",
      "title": "RapidOCR: The Lightning-Fast OCR Every Developer Needs",
      "publish_date": "2026-02-01",
      "excerpts": [
        "Enterprises need OCR that handles Chinese, English, and multiple languages simultaneously. Startups require solutions that deploy offline without API costs. Researchers want reproducible results across environments. RapidOCR delivers all three, making it the **fastest-growing OCR toolkit** on GitHub with thousands of stars and millions of downloads.\n\n## Key Features That Set RapidOCR Apart\n\n### **Multi-Backend Inference Architecture**\n\nUnlike monolithic OCR tools, RapidOCR embraces flexibility through four powerful engines:\n\n* **ONNXRuntime** : Universal deployment with optimized CPU/GPU execution\n* **OpenVINO** : Intel hardware acceleration for edge and server scenarios\n* **PaddlePaddle** : Native support for original PaddleOCR models\n* **PyTorch** : Deep learning research and custom model integration\n\nThis modular design lets you **optimize for your specific hardware** without rewriting code.\n\n...\n\nLabels mix English, Chinese, and numeric codes in unpredictable layouts. Their Python-based automation pipeline uses RapidOCR's **batch processing** mode to handle 10,000 images/hour. The toolkit's **text box sorting algorithm** correctly sequences multi-column labels, reducing manual review by 85%. Deployment across Windows warehouses and Linux cloud servers uses identical code.\n\n### **4\\. Content Moderation at Scale**\n\nA social platform moderates user-uploaded memes and screenshots for policy violations. They need **fast, accurate text extraction** to flag harmful content. RapidOCR's **GPU acceleration** via ONNXRuntime CUDA processes images in **150ms** each, enabling real-time moderation for 1M+ daily uploads. The Python API integrates with their PyTorch-based image classification pipeline, creating a unified moderation system.\n\n### **5\\. Academic Research Reproducibility**\n\nA university research team studies historical document digitization.\nThey need **consistent results** across lab workstations and cloud compute clusters. RapidOCR's **deterministic inference** and versioned models ensure identical outputs everywhere. The C++ implementation runs on high-performance computing clusters, while Python notebooks enable student collaboration—both using the same model files.\n\n## Step-by-Step Installation & Setup Guide\n\n### **Prerequisites**\n\n* **Python** : Version 3.6 or higher (3.8+ recommended)\n* **pip** : Latest version for dependency resolution\n* **Operating System** : Linux (Ubuntu 18.04+), Windows 10+, or macOS 10.14+\n* **Hardware** : 2GB RAM minimum, 4GB recommended for large images\n\n### **Core Installation**\n\nInstall RapidOCR with the ONNXRuntime backend (recommended for most users):\n\n```\n# Upgrade pip first\npython -m pip install --upgrade pip\n\n# Install RapidOCR with ONNXRuntime\npip install rapidocr onnxruntime\n```\n\n### **Backend-Specific Installations**\n\nChoose your inference engine based on deployment needs:\n\n```\n# For Intel hardware acceleration (CPUs, VPUs)\npip install rapidocr_openvino\n\n# For PaddlePaddle ecosystem compatibility\npip install rapidocr_paddle\n\n# For PyTorch research workflows\npip install rapidocr_pytorch\n```\n\n### **GPU Acceleration Setup**\n\nFor CUDA-enabled GPUs, install ONNXRuntime GPU:\n\n```\n# Uninstall CPU version first\npip uninstall onnxruntime\n\n# Install GPU version\npip install onnxruntime-gpu\n\n# Verify GPU availability\npython -c \"import onnxruntime; print(onnxruntime.get_device())\"\n```\n\n### **Environment Verification**\n\nCreate a test script to validate installation:\n\n```\n# verify_installation.py\nfrom rapidocr import RapidOCR\nimport sys\n\ntry:\n    engine = RapidOCR()\n    print(\"✅ RapidOCR installed successfully\")\n    print(f\"📦 Backend: ONNXRuntime\")\n    print(f\"🎯 Ready for inference\")\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print(f\"❌ Installation failed: {e}\")\n    sys.exit(1)\n```\n\nRun it: `python verify_installation.py`\n\n### **Docker Deployment**\n\nFor containerized environments:"
      ]
    },

      "excerpts": [
        "**[Big news!](https://blog.tidelift.com/tidelift-joins-sonar?utm_source=libraries&utm_medium=referral) Sonar has entered a definitive agreement to acquire Tidelift!**\n\nToggle navigation \n\n* \n\n    + GitHub\n    + GitLab\n    + Bitbucket\n    + By logging in you accept  \n        our terms of service  \n        and privacy policy\n\n# rapidocr-onnxruntime  \nRelease\n1\\.4.4\n\nA cross platform OCR Library based on OnnxRuntime.\n\n[Homepage](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/rapidocr-onnxruntime/1.4.4/) Python\n\n* * *\n\nKeywords License\n    Apache-2.0\nInstall\n    `pip install rapidocr-onnxruntime==1.4.4`\n\n* * *\n\n### Documentation\n\n**_Open source OCR for the security of the digital world_**\n\n[](https://clawhub.ai/rapidai/rapidocr) [](https://huggingface.co/spaces/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3) [](https://www.modelscope.cn/studios/RapidAI/RapidOCRv3.0.0/summary) [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/RapidAI/RapidOCR/blob/main/assets/RapidOCRDemo.\nipynb) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/graphs/contributors) [](https://pepy.tech/project/rapidocr) [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/rapidocr/) [](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/stargazers) [SemVer2.0](https://semver.org/) [](https://github.com/psf/black)\n\nJoin our [Discord](https://discord.gg/33eyQJq498)\n\n[简体中文](https://github.com/RapidAI/RapidOCR/blob/main/README-CN.md) | English\n\n### 📝 Introduction\n\nRapidOCR is a completely open-source, free OCR tool that supports multi-platform, multi-language operation and rapid offline deployment. Its core advantages lie in extreme speed and extensive compatibility.\n\n**Supported Languages:** Default support for Chinese and English recognition. For other supported languages, please refer to the documentation: [Model List](https://rapidai.github.io/RapidOCRDocs/main/model_list/) .\n\n**Project Origin:** Considering that [PaddleOCR](https://github.\ncom/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) still has room for optimization in engineering aspects, we innovatively converted the models in PaddleOCR into the highly compatible ONNX format to simplify and accelerate the inference deployment of OCR models on various terminal devices. Furthermore, we achieved seamless cross-platform porting based on multiple programming languages such as Python, C++, Java, and C#, enabling developers to get started easily and integrate efficiently.\n\n**Name Implication:** The name \"RapidOCR\" embodies our core expectations for the product: **Rapid** (simple operation, fast response), **Good & Economical** (low resource consumption, high cost-effectiveness), and **Intelligent** (achieving accurate and efficient recognition relying on deep learning technology). We focus on leveraging the advantages of artificial intelligence to create compact yet powerful models, relentlessly pursuing speed while ensuring excellent recognition results.\n\n**User Guide:**"
      ]

The current version has no [OSV.dev](https://osv.dev/) malware reports and no critical or high-severity vulnerabilities.\n\nrapidocr has 3.3M monthly downloads and 7K GitHub stars. Version 3.9.0 supports Python <4,>=3.8, first published in 2025.\n\nIt is actively maintained under a permissive license, last released in Jun 2026.\n\nIt is built on top of colorlog, numpy and omegaconf.       "url": "https://www.toolify.ai/ai-model/zai-org-glm-ocr",
      "title": "GLM-OCR huggingface.co api & zai-org GLM-OCR github AI Model - Toolify",

      "excerpts": [
        "return_dict= True ,\n    return_tensors= \"pt\" \n).to(model.device)\ninputs.pop( \"token_type_ids\" ,  None )\ngenerated_ids = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens= 8192 )\noutput_text = processor.decode(generated_ids[ 0 ][inputs[ \"input_ids\" ].shape[ 1 ]:], skip_special_tokens= False )\n print (output_text)\n```\n\n###### Prompt Limited\n\nGLM-OCR currently supports two types of prompt scenarios:\n\n1. **Document Parsing** – extract raw content from documents. Supported tasks include:\n\n```\n{\n     \"text\" :  \"Text Recognition:\" ,\n     \"formula\" :  \"Formula Recognition:\" ,\n     \"table\" :  \"Table Recognition:\" \n}\n```\n\n1. **Information Extraction** – extract structured information from documents. Prompts must follow a strict JSON schema. For example, to extract personal ID information:\n\n```\n请按下列JSON格式输出图中信息:\n{\n     \"id_number\" :  \"\" ,\n     \"last_name\" :  \"\" ,\n     \"first_name\" :  \"\" ,\n     \"date_of_birth\" :  \"\" ,\n     \"address\" : {\n         \"street\" :  \"\" ,\n\"city\" :  \"\" ,\n         \"state\" :  \"\" ,\n         \"zip_code\" :  \"\" \n    },\n     \"dates\" : {\n         \"issue_date\" :  \"\" ,\n         \"expiration_date\" :  \"\" \n    },\n     \"sex\" :  \"\" \n}\n```\n\n⚠️ Note: When using information extraction, the output must strictly adhere to the defined JSON schema to ensure downstream processing compatibility.\n\n##### GLM-OCR SDK\n\nWe provide an easy-to-use SDK for using GLM-OCR more efficiently and conveniently. please check our github to get more detail.\n\n##### Acknowledgement\n\nThis project is inspired by the excellent work of the following projects and communities:\n\n* PP-DocLayout-V3\n* PaddleOCR\n* MinerU\n\n##### License\n\nThe GLM-OCR model is released under the MIT License.\n\nThe complete OCR pipeline integrates PP-DocLayoutV3 for document layout analysis, which is licensed under the Apache License 2.0. Users should comply with both licenses when using this project.\n\n## Runs of zai-org GLM-OCR on huggingface.co\n\n3\\.3M\n\nTotal runs\n\n0\n\n24-hour runs\n215\\.5K\n\n3-day runs\n\n648\\.8K\n\n7-day runs\n\n\\-2.9M\n\n30-day runs\n\n## More Information About GLM-OCR huggingface.co Model\n\n### More GLM-OCR license Visit here:\n\n<https://choosealicense.com/licenses/mit>\n\n### GLM-OCR huggingface.co\n\nGLM-OCR huggingface.co is an AI model on huggingface.co that provides GLM-OCR's model effect (), which can be used instantly with this zai-org GLM-OCR model. huggingface.co supports a free trial of the GLM-OCR model, and also provides paid use of the GLM-OCR. Support call GLM-OCR model through api, including Node.js, Python, http.\n\n### GLM-OCR huggingface.co Url\n\n<https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-OCR>\n\n### zai-org GLM-OCR online free\n\nGLM-OCR huggingface.co is an online trial and call api platform, which integrates GLM-OCR's modeling effects, including api services, and provides a free online trial of GLM-OCR, you can try GLM-OCR online for free by clicking the link below.\n\n### zai-org GLM-OCR online free url in huggingface.co:\n\n<https://huggingface."
