    {
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/defford/GLM-OCR/blob/main/README.md",
      "title": "README.md · defford/GLM-OCR at main",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# Duplicated from zai-org/GLM-OCR\n\ndefford\n\n/\n\nGLM-OCR\n\nlike 0\n\nImage-to-Text Transformers Safetensors 8 languages glm\\_ocr image-text-to-text\n\narxiv: 2603\\.10910\n\nLicense: mit\n\nModel card Files Files and versions xet [Community](https://huggingface.co/defford/GLM-OCR/discussions)\n\nDeploy\n\nCopy to bucket new\n\nUse this model\n\n### Instructions to use defford/GLM-OCR with libraries, inference providers, notebooks, and local apps. Follow these links to get started.\n\n* Libraries\n* Transformers\n  \n  How to use defford/GLM-OCR with Transformers:\n  \n  ```\n  # Use a pipeline as a high-level helper\n  # Warning: Pipeline type \"image-to-text\" is no longer supported in transformers v5.\n  # You must load the model directly (see below) or downgrade to v4.x with:\n  # 'pip install \"transformers<5.0.0'\n  from transformers import pipeline\n  \n  pipe = pipeline(\"image-to-text\", model=\"defford/GLM-OCR\")\n  ```\n  \n  ```\n  # Load model directly\n  from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForImageTextToText\ntokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(\"defford/GLM-OCR\")\n  model = AutoModelForImageTextToText.from_pretrained(\"defford/GLM-OCR\")\n  ```\n* Notebooks\n* Google Colab\n* Kaggle\n\nmain\n\nGLM-OCR / README.md\n\ndefford's picture\n\ndefford\n\nDuplicate from zai-org/GLM-OCR\n\n[7636b40](https://huggingface.co/defford/GLM-OCR/commit/7636b40aafd0db1e32518392a9397bac3c5a5fd2) 2 months ago\n\npreview code\n\n|\n\nraw\n\nCopy download link\n\n[history](https://huggingface.co/defford/GLM-OCR/commits/main/README.md) blame contribute delete\n\nSafe\n\n8\\.31 kB\n\nmetadata\n\n```\nlicense: mit \n language: \n  - zh \n  - en \n  - fr \n  - es \n  - ru \n  - de \n  - ja \n  - ko \n pipeline_tag: image-to-text \n library_name: transformers\n```\n\n#  GLM-OCR\n\n👋 Join our [WeChat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/wechat.jpg) and [Discord](https://discord.gg/QR7SARHRxK) community  \n📍 Use GLM-OCR's [API](https://docs.z.ai/guides/vlm/glm-ocr)  \n👉 [GLM-OCR SDK](https://github.\ncom/zai-org/GLM-OCR) Recommended  \n📖 [Technical Report](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.10910)\n\n##  Introduction\n\nGLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture. It introduces Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) loss and stable full-task reinforcement learning to improve training efficiency, recognition accuracy, and generalization. The model integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder. Combined with a two-stage pipeline of layout analysis and parallel recognition based on PP-DocLayout-V3, GLM-OCR delivers robust and high-quality OCR performance across diverse document layouts.\n\n**Key Features**\n\n* **State-of-the-Art Performance** : Achieves a score of 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://a2aprotocol.ai/blog/2026-glm-ocr-complete-guide",
      "title": "2026 Complete Guide to GLM-OCR for Next-Gen Document Understanding | A2A Protocol",
      "publish_date": "2026-02-04",
      "excerpts": [
        "> * Use **Markdown + LaTeX** for human review, documentation, and publishing.\n> \n> \n\n> ✅ **Best Practice – Use Precision Mode for High-Stakes Documents**\n> \n> * Turn on **PRECISION\\_MODE\\_ON** for:\n>   \n>     + Legal contracts\n>     + Financial statements\n>     + Regulatory filings\n> * Accept the extra latency in exchange for **maximum accuracy** .[1]\n> \n> \n\n> ⚠️ **Caution – Preprocess Low-Quality Scans**\n> \n> * For low-DPI or heavily skewed scans, apply:\n>   \n>     + Binarization\n>     + De-skewing\n>     + Noise reduction\n> * This helps the visual encoder and improves downstream structure detection.\n> \n> \n\n> 💡 **Pro Tip – Combine with LLMs for End-to-End Automation**\n> \n> * Use GLM-OCR for **reliable structure extraction** .\n> * Then feed its Markdown/JSON output into a general-purpose LLM for:\n>   \n>     + Summaries\n>     + Risk flags\n>     + Q&A and report generation.\n> \n> \n\n* * *\n\n## 🤔 Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)\n### Q1: What makes GLM-OCR different from traditional OCR engines?\n\nGLM-OCR is built as a **multimodal vision-language model** instead of a pure character recognizer. It doesn’t just read characters; it **understands document structure and context** , and generates **semantic outputs** (Markdown, JSON, LaTeX) that are far easier to use in modern AI and data pipelines.[1][2]\n\n* * *\n\n### Q2: Can GLM-OCR handle handwriting and messy scans?\n\nYes, to a significant extent. GLM-OCR uses **contextual perception** and **multi-token prediction** to interpret handwriting and noisy images by looking at surrounding text and document structure.[1] While extreme cases may still require manual correction, it outperforms many traditional OCR tools in **handwritten annotations and marginalia** .\n\n* * *\n\n### Q3: Is GLM-OCR suitable for on-prem or air-gapped deployments?\n\nYes. The model is released as **open weights under the Apache-2.\n0 license** , and documentation highlights support for VLLM/SGLang and local inference, making it suitable for **on-prem, air-gapped, and highly regulated environments** .[1][2][3]\n\n* * *\n\n### Q4: How does GLM-OCR scale to large volumes of documents?\n\nThe **0\\.9B parameter size** is relatively small for a multimodal model, which helps keep inference efficient.[2][3] Official docs report throughput around **1\\.86 pages/second for PDFs** and **0\\.67 images/second** on capable hardware.[2] For large-scale workloads, you can:\n\n* Run multiple instances behind a load balancer.\n* Use VLLM/SGLang for batched inference.\n* Schedule batch jobs for nightly or off-peak processing.\n\n* * *\n\n### Q5: When should I choose GLM-OCR over proprietary cloud OCR (Google, Azure, etc.)?\n\nChoose GLM-OCR when you need:\n\n* **Full control** over data (on-prem, private cloud).\n* **Open-source licensing** and freedom from per-page vendor lock-in.\n* **Rich structure** (Markdown/JSON/LaTeX) rather than just text."
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/1792/local-glm-ocr-pdf-markdown-obsidian-llm-wiki",
      "title": "Local GLM-OCR: Convert PDF to Markdown and Build Your AI ...",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Skip to content Skip to navigation Skip to footer\n\nP. Pillitteri\n\n[](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/1782/how-to-use-github-beginner-guide-2026 \"Previous\") [](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/1802/best-claude-skills-mobile-development-2026 \"Next\")\n\n# Local GLM-OCR: Convert PDF to Markdown and Build Your AI Knowledge Base with Obsidian (2026 Guide)\n\n##### Z.AI GLM-OCR runs locally with 0.9B parameters and beats Gemini 3 Pro. Practical guide to convert PDFs to Markdown and build a private AI knowledge base with Obsidian and Karpathy LLM Wiki pattern.\n\nA 0.9 billion parameter model has just outperformed Gemini 3 Pro and GPT-5.2 on the reference benchmark for document parsing, OmniDocBench V1.5, scoring 94.62 points against rivals one hundred times its size. It is called **GLM-OCR** , the Chinese laboratory Z.\nAI (formerly Zhipu AI) released it on February 3, 2026 with an MIT license, it runs on a video card with 4 GB of VRAM, and it converts a 47-page PDF into clean Markdown in under 30 seconds. In this guide I show you the exact stack I use every day to turn PDFs, slides, and screenshots into a queryable personal knowledge base, built on the Obsidian vault and managed entirely by AI following the _LLM Wiki_ pattern that Andrej Karpathy made famous in April 2026. The result is a memory that organizes itself, lives on your disk without sending a single byte to the cloud, and grows every time you drop in a new document.\n\nGLM-OCR by Z.AI converts PDFs to Markdown at 1.86 pages per second, Obsidian turns them into a queryable wiki\n\n> Introducing GLM-OCR: SOTA performance, optimized for complex document understanding. With only 0.\n9B parameters, GLM-OCR delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks, including formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.\n> \n> — Z.ai (@Zai\\_org) [February 3, 2026](https://twitter.com/Zai_org/status/2018520052941656385)\n\n## What GLM-OCR Is and Why Such a Small Model Changes the Rules\n\n**GLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR (Optical Character Recognition) model developed by the Chinese laboratory Z.AI** and available as open weights on Hugging Face since February 2026. Its strength is not the size; in fact, with just 0.9 billion parameters it occupies less than 2 GB on disk and runs on a consumer 4 GB VRAM graphics card in FP16 precision. The surprise comes from the [OmniDocBench V1.5](https://github.com/opendatalab/OmniDocBench) benchmark, the de facto standard for document parsing published by OpenDataLab: GLM-OCR closed at 94.62 points, surpassing both closed-source giants like Gemini 3 Pro and GPT-5."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-OCR",
      "title": "zai-org/GLM-OCR · Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "The model integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder. Combined with a two-stage pipeline of layout analysis and parallel recognition based on PP-DocLayout-V3, GLM-OCR delivers robust and high-quality OCR performance across diverse document layouts.\n\n**Key Features**\n\n* **State-of-the-Art Performance** : Achieves a score of 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking #1 overall, and delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks, including formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.\n* **Optimized for Real-World Scenarios** : Designed and optimized for practical business use cases, maintaining robust performance on complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals, and other challenging real-world layouts.\n* **Efficient Inference** : With only 0.\n9B parameters, GLM-OCR supports deployment via vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama, significantly reducing inference latency and compute cost, making it ideal for high-concurrency services and edge deployments.\n* **Easy to Use** : Fully open-sourced and equipped with a comprehensive [SDK](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR) and inference toolchain, offering simple installation, one-line invocation, and smooth integration into existing production pipelines.\n\n##  Performance\n\n* Document Parsing & Information Extraction\n\n[image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/docparse.png)\n\n* Real-World Scenarios Performance\n\n[image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/realworld.png)\n\n* Speed Test\n\nFor speed, we compared different OCR methods under identical hardware and testing conditions (single replica, single concurrency), evaluating their performance in parsing and exporting Markdown files from both image and PDF inputs.\nResults show GLM-OCR achieves a throughput of 1.86 pages/second for PDF documents and 0.67 images/second for images, significantly outperforming comparable models.\n\n[image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/speed.png)\n\n##  Usage\n\n###  Official SDK\n\nFor document parsing tasks, we strongly recommend using our [official SDK](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR) .\nCompared with model-only inference, the SDK integrates PP-DocLayoutV3 and provides a complete, easy-to-use pipeline for document parsing, including layout analysis and structured output generation. This significantly reduces the engineering overhead required to build end-to-end document intelligence systems.\n\nNote that the SDK is currently designed for document parsing tasks only. For information extraction tasks, please refer to the following section and run inference directly with the model.\n\n###  vLLM\n\n1. run\n\n```\npip install -U vllm --extra-index-url https://wheels.vllm.ai/nightly\n```"
      ]

    {
      "url": "https://grokipedia.com/page/GLM-OCR",
      "title": "GLM-OCR — Grokipedia",
      "publish_date": "2026-02-03",
      "excerpts": [
        "By prioritizing \"compact size, high accuracy\" via proprietary techniques such as the CogViT visual encoder and deep scene optimization, GLM-OCR aims to solve real-world business pain points in complex document parsing and provide an open-source, practical solution for diverse industries. [[[2]]()]() [[[4]]()]()\n\n### Development team\n\nGLM-OCR was developed by Zhipu AI, also known as Z.ai. [[[5]]()]() Zhipu AI is the organization responsible for the ChatGLM family of large language models as well as the broader GLM series, including multimodal advancements. [[[5]]()]() The model is open-sourced under the zai-org organization on both Hugging Face and GitHub, where the official repository and model weights are hosted. [[[1]]()]() [[[3]]()]() This aligns with Zhipu AI's practice of releasing GLM-related models and techniques publicly through these platforms. [[[5]]()]()\n\n### Release history\nGLM-OCR was open-sourced in early February 2026 via the GitHub repository zai-org/GLM-OCR, marking its initial public release under the MIT License for the model and Apache License 2.0 for the code. [[[3]]()]() The model was simultaneously made available on Hugging Face under zai-org/GLM-OCR, where it includes the full model weights and integration instructions. [[[1]]()]() At launch, Z.ai introduced access through its cloud API (Zhipu MaaS API), enabling usage without local hardware via API endpoints documented at the Z.ai developer portal. [[[2]]()]() Self-hosted deployment options were provided from the outset, with explicit support for Ollama alongside vLLM and SGLang for local inference. [[[3]]()]() [[[1]]()]() No subsequent major version releases or updates have been documented beyond the initial rollout.\n\n## Architecture\n\n### Visual encoder\nThe visual encoder in GLM-OCR is CogViT, which serves as the primary component for processing visual inputs such as images and document pages. [[[1]]()]() [[[2]]()]() CogViT is pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, enabling robust extraction of document layout features and textual content from complex visual arrangements, including intricate layouts, tables, formulas, and mixed text-image elements. [[[1]]()]() This pre-training supports high-fidelity representation of visual information, contributing to the model's ability to handle diverse real-world document challenges such as varied fonts , seals , handwriting, and multilingual text. [[[2]]()]() [[[1]]()]() GLM-OCR is built on the GLM-V encoder-decoder architecture , in which CogViT functions as the visual encoder. [[[1]]()]()\n\n### Cross-modal connector\n\nGLM-OCR employs a lightweight cross-modal connector to bridge the visual and language components of its architecture."
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://docs.z.ai/guides/vlm/glm-ocr",
      "title": "GLM-OCR - Overview - Z.AI DEVELOPER DOCUMENT",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "> ## Documentation Index\n> Fetch the complete documentation index at: https://docs.z.ai/llms.txt\n> Use this file to discover all available pages before exploring further.\n\n# GLM-OCR\n\n## Overview\n\nGLM-OCR is a lightweight professional OCR model with parameters as small as 0.9B, yet it achieves state-of-the-art performance across multiple capabilities. It sets a new benchmark for document parsing with its “small size and high accuracy.” Key features include:\n\n* **Performance SOTA**: Scored 94.62 points to top OmniDocBench V1.5 and achieved current best performance across **multiple mainstream document understanding benchmarks** including tables and formulas at launch.\n* **Optimized for Real-World Scenarios**: Delivers stable, leading accuracy in complex environments like code documentation, intricate tables, and stamp recognition. Maintains exceptional recognition precision even with complex layouts, diverse fonts, or mixed text-image content.\n* **Efficient and Cost-Effective**: With just 0.9B parameters, supports VLLM and SGLang deployment, significantly reducing inference latency and computational overhead.\n\n<CardGroup cols={3}>\n  <Card title=\"Input Modality\" icon=\"arrow-down-right\" color=\"#ffffff\">\n    - PDF, images (JPG, PNG)\n    - Single image ≤ 10MB, PDF ≤ 50MB\n    - Maximum support: 100 pages\n  </Card>\n\n  <Card title=\"Output Modality\" icon=\"arrow-down-left\" color=\"#ffffff\">\n    Text / Image Links / MD Documents\n  </Card>\n\n  <Card title=\"Supported Language\" icon=\"language\" color=\"#ffffff\">\n    Support Chinese, English, French, Spanish, Russian, German, Japanese, Korean, etc.\n  </Card>\n</CardGroup>\n\n<Tip>\n  For detailed pricing information on GLM-OCR, please visit the [Pricing Page](/guides/overview/pricing).\n</Tip>\n\n## Usage\n\n<AccordionGroup>\n  <Accordion title=\"Text Recognition\">"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://pypi.org/project/glmocr/",
      "title": "glmocr · PyPI",
      "publish_date": "2026-04-07",
      "excerpts": [
        "* For multiple independent documents, call the endpoint multiple times (one document per request).\n\n### Modular Architecture\n\nGLM-OCR uses composable modules for easy customization:\n\n|Component |Description |\n| --- | --- |\n|`PageLoader` |Preprocessing and image encoding |\n|`OCRClient` |Calls the GLM-OCR model service |\n|`PPDocLayoutDetector` |PP-DocLayout layout detection |\n|`ResultFormatter` |Post-processing, outputs JSON/Markdown |\n\nYou can extend the behavior by creating custom pipelines:\n\n```\nfrom glmocr.dataloader import PageLoader \n from glmocr.ocr_client import OCRClient \n from glmocr.postprocess import ResultFormatter \n\n class MyPipeline : \n  def __init__ ( self , config ): \n    self . page_loader = PageLoader ( config ) \n    self . ocr_client = OCRClient ( config ) \n    self . formatter = ResultFormatter ( config ) \n\n  def process ( self , request_data ): \n    # Implement your own processing logic \n    pass\n```\n\n## Star History\n\n[](https://www.star-history.com/?\nrepos=zai-org%2FGLM-OCR&type=date&legend=top-left)\n\n## Acknowledgement\n\nThis project is inspired by the excellent work of the following projects and communities:\n\n* [PP-DocLayout-V3](https://huggingface.co/PaddlePaddle/PP-DocLayoutV3)\n* [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR)\n* [MinerU](https://github.com/opendatalab/MinerU)\n\n## License\n\nThe Code of this repo is under Apache License 2.0.\n\nThe GLM-OCR model is released under the MIT License.\n\nThe complete OCR pipeline integrates [PP-DocLayoutV3](https://huggingface.co/PaddlePaddle/PP-DocLayoutV3) for document layout analysis, which is licensed under the Apache License 2.0. Users should comply with both licenses when using this project.\n\n## Citation\n\nIf you find GLM-OCR useful in your research, please cite our technical report:\n\n```\n@misc { duan2026glmocrtechnicalreport , \n title = {GLM-OCR Technical Report} ,"
      ]
    },
    {
      "url": "https://huggingface.co/zai-org/GLM-OCR/blob/main/README.md",
      "title": "README.md · zai-org/GLM-OCR at main - Hugging Face",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "GLM-OCR is a multimodal OCR model for complex document understanding, built on the GLM-V encoder–decoder architecture. It introduces Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) loss and stable full-task reinforcement learning to improve training efficiency, recognition accuracy, and generalization. The model integrates the CogViT visual encoder pre-trained on large-scale image–text data, a lightweight cross-modal connector with efficient token downsampling, and a GLM-0.5B language decoder. Combined with a two-stage pipeline of layout analysis and parallel recognition based on PP-DocLayout-V3, GLM-OCR delivers robust and high-quality OCR performance across diverse document layouts.\n\n**Key Features**\n\n* **State-of-the-Art Performance** : Achieves a score of 94.62 on OmniDocBench V1.5, ranking #1 overall, and delivers state-of-the-art results across major document understanding benchmarks, including formula recognition, table recognition, and information extraction.\n* **Optimized for Real-World Scenarios** : Designed and optimized for practical business use cases, maintaining robust performance on complex tables, code-heavy documents, seals, and other challenging real-world layouts.\n* **Efficient Inference** : With only 0.9B parameters, GLM-OCR supports deployment via vLLM, SGLang, and Ollama, significantly reducing inference latency and compute cost, making it ideal for high-concurrency services and edge deployments.\n* **Easy to Use** : Fully open-sourced and equipped with a comprehensive [SDK](https://github.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR) and inference toolchain, offering simple installation, one-line invocation, and smooth integration into existing production pipelines.\n\n##  Performance\n\n* Document Parsing & Information Extraction\n\n[image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/docparse.png)\n\n* Real-World Scenarios Performance\n\n[image](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/zai-org/GLM-OCR/refs/heads/main/resources/realworld."
      ]
    }

      "title": "GitHub - bdim404/Monkey-OCR-GUI: Monkey OCR GUI is a cross-platform graphical tool based on Monkey OCR API. Monkey OCR GUI 是一个基于 Monkey OCR API 的跨平台图形化工具。 · GitHub",

      "excerpts": [
        "English | 简体中文\n\n* * *\n\n## 📖 Project Introduction\n\nMonkey OCR GUI is a cross-platform graphical tool based on Monkey OCR API, designed to provide a clean, efficient, and user-friendly OCR interface. The tool supports text recognition, formula extraction, table parsing, and complete document recognition, with powerful features including multi-threaded concurrent processing, real-time progress tracking, result preview, and export.\n\n### Core Features\n\n* 🎯 **Multi-Mode Recognition** : Supports four recognition modes: text, formula (LaTeX), table (HTML), and document (Markdown)\n* 📄 **Multi-Format Support** : Supports PDF, PNG, JPG, JPEG format files\n* 🔄 **Page Navigation** : PDF multi-page file support with page browsing and selective processing\n* ⚡ **High Performance** : Intelligent multi-threaded concurrent processing, automatically adjusts thread count based on system load\n* 📊 **Real-time Progress** : Real-time progress bar and detailed log output\n\n...\n\n* Exponential backoff strategy\n* Maximum 3 retry attempts\n\n#### 4\\. Error Handling\n\n* Fine-grained exception classification\n* Detailed error logging\n* User-friendly prompts\n\n#### 5\\. Resource Management\n\n* Automatic temporary file cleanup\n* Startup cleanup of expired cache\n* Exit cleanup of all temporary resources\n\n## ❓ FAQ\n\n### Q1: What to do if API connection fails?\n\n**A** : Check the following:\n\n1. Confirm API service is running\n2. Check if URL format is correct (e.g., `http://localhost:8000` )\n3. Confirm firewall is not blocking the connection\n4. Check log output for detailed error information\n\n### Q2: Why is processing slow?\n\n**A** : Possible reasons:\n\n1. High network latency\n2. Insufficient system resources (CPU/memory)\n3. Processing large files or many pages\n4. High API server load\n\nTry:\n\n* Reduce concurrent page count\n* Process large files in batches\n* Upgrade system hardware\n\n### Q3: What file formats are supported?\n\n**A** : Currently supported:\n\n* **Images** : PNG, JPG, JPEG\n* **Documents** : PDF (multi-page support)\n\n### Q4: How to change interface language?\n\n**A** : The application automatically detects system language. You can manually switch by modifying the configuration file:\n\n```\n\"ui\" : {\n    \"language\" : \"zh_CN\"  # or \"en_US\" \n}\n```\n\n### Q5: Where are exported files saved?\n\n**A** : A file selection dialog will pop up during export, allowing you to freely choose the save location.\n\n### Q6: How to update to the latest version?\n\n**A** :\n\n```\ngit pull origin main\npip install -r requirements.txt --upgrade\n```\n\n## 📄 License\n\nThis project is licensed under the MIT License .\n\n## 🙏 Acknowledgments\n\n* [Monkey OCR API](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR) \\- MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition-Relation Triplet Paradigm\n* [CustomTkinter](https://github.com/TomSchimansky/CustomTkinter) \\- Modern Tkinter interface library\n* [PyMuPDF](https://github.com/pymupdf/PyMuPDF) \\- High-performance PDF processing library\n\n## About\nMonkey OCR GUI is a cross-platform graphical tool based on Monkey OCR API. [Demo](https://aiwrite.wps.cn/pdf/parse/web/) .\n* `2025.07.10` 🚀 We release [MonkeyOCR-pro-1.\n2B](https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B) , a leaner and faster version model that outperforms our previous 3B version in accuracy, speed, and efficiency.\n* `2025.06.12` 🚀 The model’s trending on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/models?sort=trending) \\#2.\n* `2025.06.05` 🚀 We release [MonkeyOCR](https://huggingface.co/echo840/MonkeyOCR) , an English and Chinese documents parsing model.\n\n## Introduction\n\nMonkeyOCR adopts a Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) triplet paradigm, which simplifies the multi-tool pipeline of modular approaches while avoiding the inefficiency of using large multimodal models for full-page document processing.\n\n1. MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B surpasses MonkeyOCR-3B by 7.4% on Chinese documents.\n2. MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B delivers approximately a 36% speed improvement over MonkeyOCR-pro-3B, with approximately 1.6% drop in performance.\n3. On olmOCR-Bench, MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B outperforms Nanonets-OCR-3B by 7.3%.\n4.\nOn OmniDocBench, MonkeyOCR-pro-3B achieves the best overall performance on both English and Chinese documents, outperforming even closed-source and extra-large open-source VLMs such as Gemini 2.0-Flash, Gemini 2.5-Pro, Qwen2.5-VL-72B, GPT-4o, and InternVL3-78B.\n\nSee detailed results below.\n\n### Comparing MonkeyOCR with closed-source and extra large open-source VLMs.\n\n## Inference Speed (Pages/s) on Different GPUs and [PDF](https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1geumlJmVY7UUKdr8324sYZ0FHSAElh7m?usp=sharing) Page Counts\n\n|Model |GPU |50 Pages |100 Pages |300 Pages |500 Pages |1000 Pages |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|MonkeyOCR-pro-3B |3090 |0\\.492 |0\\.484 |0\\.497 |0\\.492 |0\\.496 |\n|A6000 |0\\.585 |0\\.587 |0\\.609 |0\\.598 |0\\.608 |\n|H800 |0\\.923 |0\\.768 |0\\.897 |0\\.930 |0\\.891 |\n|4090 |0\\.972 |0\\.969 |1\\.006 |0\\.986 |1\\.006 |\n|MonkeyOCR-pro-1.2B |3090 |0\\.615 |0\\.660 |0\\.677 |0\\.687 |0\\.683 |\n|A6000 |0\\.709 |0\\.786 |0\\.825 |0\\.829 |0\\.825 |\n|H800 |0\\.965 |1\\.082 |1\\.101 |1\\."

    {
      "url": "https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1",
      "title": "MonkeyOCR v1.5 Technical Report: Unlocking Robust Document Parsing for Complex Patterns",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Furthermore, to address tables that span across multiple pages or columns, the framework automatically identifies and merges these segments by combining rule-based matching with BERT-based semantic discrimination, thereby reconstructing the complete table structure.\n\nExperimental results show that MonkeyOCR v1.5 achieves state-of-the-art performance on the widely adopted OmniDocBench v1.5 benchmark, surpassing the previous best-performing methods PPOCR-VL and MinerU 2.5 by 1.0% and 2.2%, respectively, in overall performance. Notably, MonkeyOCR v1.5 demonstrates stronger robustness in complex scenarios. On newspaper documents characterized by dense text and intricate layouts, it achieves the best performance among all competitors. Moreover, on the OCRFlux-complex dataset, our method significantly outperforms the previous state-of-the-art PPOCR-VL by 8.2%. Beyond accuracy improvement, MonkeyOCR v1.\n5 further extends its capability to handle embedded image recovery, cross-page table reconstruction, and multi-column table merging, showcasing strong generalization in complex real-world document parsing scenarios.\n\n## 2 MonkeyOCR v1.5\n\nWe propose MonkeyOCR v1.5, a vision-language-based document parsing framework designed for robust and efficient OCR in complex, real-world layouts. Compared with the previous version, v1.5 simplifies the pipeline into two stages and introduces a visual-consistency-based reinforcement learning paradigm that improves table recognition accuracy without relying on large-scale manual annotations. In addition, to address challenges such as embedded images and cross-page or cross-column table merging that other methods struggle with (Tab. [1](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1.T1 \"Table 1 ‣ 2 MonkeyOCR v1.5 ‣ MonkeyOCR v1.\n5 Technical Report: Unlocking Robust Document Parsing for Complex Patterns\") ), we incorporate an image-decoupled and type-guided table recognition module. Together, these advancements make MonkeyOCR v1.5 a scalable and high-fidelity OCR system well-suited for heterogeneous document understanding.\n\n|Models |Inserted Image |Cross-page Table Merging |Cross-column Table Merging |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Det.&Rec. |RpHdrCont. |NoHdrCont. |SplitCont. |RpHdrCont. |NoHdrCont. |SplitCont. |\n|MinerU2.5 [ [18](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1.bib18) ] |× |✓ |✓ |× |× |× |× |\n|MonkeyOCR [ [13](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1.bib13) ] |× |× |× |× |× |× |× |\n|OCRFlux [ [2](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1.bib2) ] |× |✓ |✓ |× |× |× |× |\n|dotsOCR [ [25](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1.bib25) ] |× |× |× |× |× |× |× |\n|PaddleOCR-VL [ [5](https://arxiv.org/html/2511.10390v1.bib5) ] |✓ |× |× |× |× |× |× |\n|MonkeyOCR v1."
      ]

      "url": "https://github.com/rednote-hilab/dots.mocr",
      "title": "GitHub - rednote-hilab/dots.mocr: Multimodal OCR: Parse Anything from Documents · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "* README\n* MIT license\n\n# dots.mocr\n\n[**🖥️ Live Demo**](https://dotsocr.xiaohongshu.com) | [**📄 arXiv**](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.13032v1) | **💬 WeChat** | [**📕 rednote**](https://www.xiaohongshu.com/user/profile/683ffe42000000001d021a4c) | [**🐦 X**](https://x.com/rednotehilab)\n\n## Introduction\n\nDesigned for comprehensive parsing, **dots.mocr** seamlessly recognizes both diverse human scripts and structured graphical content. Its core capabilities encompass grounding, recognition, semantic understanding, and interactive dialogue.\n\n## News\n\n* `2026.3.19` 🚀 We present [dots.mocr](https://huggingface.co/rednote-hilab/dots.mocr) . Beyond achieving state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in standard multilingual document parsing among models of comparable size, **dots.mocr** excels at converting structured graphics (e.g., charts, UI layouts, scientific figures and etc.) directly into SVG code.\nAdditionally, the model retains comparable performance in general vision tasks to Qwen3-VL-4B. Simultaneously, we are releasing [dots.mocr-svg](https://huggingface.co/rednote-hilab/dots.mocr-svg) , a variant specifically optimized for robust image-to-SVG parsing tasks. More information can be found [in the paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.13032v1) .\n\n## Evaluation\n\n### 1\\. Document Parsing\n\n#### 1\\.1 Elo Score of different bench between latest models\n\n|models |olmOCR-Bench |OmniDocBench (v1.5) |XDocParse |Average |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|MonkeyOCR-pro-3B |895\\.0 |811\\.3 |637\\.1 |781\\.1 |\n|GLM-OCR |884\\.2 |972\\.6 |820\\.7 |892\\.5 |\n|PaddleOCR-VL-1.5 |897\\.3 |997\\.9 |866\\.4 |920\\.5 |\n|HuanyuanOCR |997\\.6 |1003\\.9 |951\\.1 |984\\.2 |\n|dots.ocr |1041\\.1 |1027\\.2 |1190\\.3 |1086\\.2 |\n|**dots.mocr** |**1104\\.4** |**1059\\.0** |**1210\\.7** |**1124\\.7** |\n|Gemini 3 Pro |1180\\.4 |1128\\.0 |1323\\.7 |1210\\.7 |\n\n> **Notes:**\n> \n> * Results for Gemini 3 Pro, PaddleOCR-VL-1.\n5, and GLM-OCR were obtained via APIs, while HuanyuanOCR results were generated using local inference.\n> * The Elo score evaluation was conducted using Gemini 3 Flash. The prompt can be found at: Elo Score Prompt . These results are consistent with the findings on [ocrarena](https://www.ocrarena.ai/battle) .\n> \n> \n\n#### 1\\.2 olmOCR-bench\n\n|Model |ArXiv |Old scans math |Tables |Old scans |Headers & footers |Multi column |Long tiny text |Base |Overall |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|Mistral OCR API |77\\.2 |67\\.5 |60\\.6 |29\\.3 |93\\.6 |71\\.3 |77\\.1 |99\\.4 |72\\.0±1.1 |\n|Marker 1.10.1 |83\\.8 |66\\.8 |72\\.9 |33\\.5 |86\\.6 |80\\.0 |85\\.7 |99\\.3 |76\\.1±1.1 |\n|MinerU 2.5.4\\* |76\\.6 |54\\.6 |84\\.9 |33\\.7 |96\\.6 |78\\.2 |83\\.5 |93\\.7 |75\\.2±1.1 |\n|DeepSeek-OCR |77\\.2 |73\\.6 |80\\.2 |33\\.3 |96\\.1 |66\\.4 |79\\.4 |99\\.8 |75\\.7±1.0 |\n|Nanonets-OCR2-3B |75\\.4 |46\\.1 |86\\.8 |40\\.9 |32\\.1 |81\\.9 |93\\.0 |99\\.6 |69\\.5±1.1 |\n|PaddleOCR-VL\\* |85\\.7 |71\\.0 |84\\.1 |37\\.8 |97\\."

      "url": "https://beta.hyper.ai/en/notebooks/40710",
      "title": "MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing Based on the structure-recognition-relation Triple Paradigm | Notebooks | HyperAI",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## 1\\. Tutorial Introduction\n\n[Build](https://github.com/Yuliang-Liu/MonkeyOCR)\n\nMonkeyOCR is a document parsing model open-sourced on June 5, 2025, by Huazhong University of Science and Technology in collaboration with Kingsoft Office. The model efficiently converts unstructured document content into structured information. Based on accurate layout analysis, content recognition, and logical sorting, it significantly improves the accuracy and efficiency of document parsing. Compared to traditional methods, MonkeyOCR performs exceptionally well in processing complex documents (such as those containing formulas and tables), achieving an average performance improvement of 5.11 TP3T, and improvements of 15.01 TP3T and 8.61 TP3T in formula and table parsing, respectively. The model excels in processing multi-page documents, achieving 0.84 pages per second, far exceeding other similar tools.\nMonkeyOCR supports various document types, including academic papers, textbooks, and newspapers, and is compatible with multiple languages, providing powerful support for document digitization and automated processing. Related research papers are available. [MonkeyOCR: Document Parsing with a Structure-Recognition-Relation Triplet Paradigm](https://hyper.ai/papers/2506.05218) .\n\nKey features:\n\n* Document parsing and structuring: Convert unstructured content (including text, tables, formulas, images, etc.) in documents of various formats (such as PDF, images, etc.) into structured machine-readable information.\n* Multi-language support: Supports multiple languages, including Chinese and English.\n* Efficiently handle complex documents: It performs well when processing complex documents (such as those containing formulas, tables, multi-column layouts, etc.).\n* Fast multi-page document processing: Efficiently process multi-page documents with a processing speed of 0.\n84 pages per second, significantly better than other tools (such as MinerU 0.65 pages per second Qwen2.5-VL-7B 0.12 pages per second).\n* Flexible deployment and expansion: Supports efficient deployment on a single NVIDIA 3090 GPU to meet needs of different scales.\n\nTechnical principle:\n\n* Structure-Recognition-Relation (SRR) triplet paradigm: A document layout detector based on YOLO that identifies the location and category of key elements in a document (such as text blocks, tables, formulas, images, etc.). Content recognition is performed on each detected region, and end-to-end recognition is performed using a large multi-morphic model (LMM) to ensure high accuracy. Based on a block-level reading order prediction mechanism, the logical relationship between the detected elements is determined to reconstruct the semantic structure of the document.\n* MonkeyDoc dataset: MonkeyDoc is the most comprehensive document parsing dataset to date, containing 3."
      ]
    }

| | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n\n# 介绍\n\n* 由于旧版PandaOCR在制作时急于增加各种功能而忽视了代码中的各种执行逻辑和对后续维护或添加新功能的不良影响，导致现在的代码堆积成了一座屎山，每次看到源代码都感到满头大汉，与其去翻那座大山，干脆重来，所以又花不少时间重构了一个全新版本，同时为避免与之前的版本混淆，取名PandaOCR.Pro专业版。\n* 与PandaOCR不同的是，PandaOCR.Pro主要针对使用私用API版接口的人群，演示接口由于普遍不够稳定且经常需要修复终归不会是长久之计。\n* PandaOCR.\nPro同样是免费使用，但它有一个激活专业版本状态，在未激活状态下您可以使用除高级功能外的全部功能，同时识别或翻译等功能可使用演示接口和百度API版接口，普通使用基本足够！\n* 激活专业版后不受任何限制，您可以使用全部功能，包括全部已接入的API接口和高级功能，如：图文批量识别、调用本地接口、屏幕对照翻译、屏幕显示按键、按键音效播放、快捷短语翻译等！\n* 如果您喜欢并愿意支持本工具继续提供更好的功能或体验可以考虑激活专业版，也可以单纯捐助，谢谢！\n\n# 特性\n\n* 重构了整个程序，优化了程序逻辑，所以它理论上应该更稳定，更简洁和更快速。\n* 保留了PandaOCR上已有的大部分功能，同时也精简了少许可能会影响性能且不太常用的功能。\n* 增加了调用本地接口高级功能，专为开发者准备 [调用本地接口教程](https://www.showdoc.com.cn/PandaOCR/11558471145804815)\n* 增加了多种离线OCR引擎调用能力，满足各种识别需求。\n* 增加了一些朋友需要的按OCR位置进行排版的功能，虽然目前还只是实验性，但日后还有优化空间。\n* 优化了行首缩进功能，旧版强行在行首添加多个空格蹩脚实现，现在改由编辑框原生显示，提升识别速度且更美观。\n* 优化了边角触发截图，解决在旧版中容易误触发的问题。\n* 优化了程序界面，使之看上去更精简干净，且基本保留了旧版的样式和操作方式，老人们可以无缝切换。\n* 优化了截图组件2，现在你可以在截图时快速选择应该使用哪类引擎识别，如搜图，扫码，上传图床或识别公式，而不用在程序界面频繁切换。\n* 优化了设置操作，多数设置可直接在程序界面中切换或修改，减少频繁手工编辑配置文件，为新手在使用上提供便利。\n* 优化了预览弹窗，并可以调整弹窗本身、弹窗文字、弹窗按钮的位置或样式。\n* 优化了图像编码，专业版使用更好的GDI转码图像，解决在旧版中因转码两次导致图像质量降低而影响文字识别或漏字。\n* 修复了PandaOCR上恢复焦点窗口时常失效的老问题。\n\n# 下载\n\n* 最新版本：5.59\n* 更新日期：2026-06-01\n* 更新日志： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro/releases>\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzoui.com/b016ct8ob> 提取码：8888\n* 百度网盘： <https://pan.baidu.com/s/1gaUeeETeGwvNb5-PUYjxqQ> 提取码: kpgh\n* 腾讯网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/jpS7MkHB>\n\n# 教程\n\n* <https://www.showdoc.com.cn/PandaOCR>\n* <https://support.qq.com/products/322047/faqs-more>\n* <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro/wiki>\n\n# 预览\n\n#### 如果看不到图请到备用主页查看： <https://gitee.com/DDDDDGOOO/PandaOCR.Pro>\n\n* 截图界面：\n* [](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro/main/images/%E9%A2%84%E8%A7%8802.jpg)\n* 精简窗口：\n*       "url": "https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR",
      "title": "PandaOCR - 多功能OCR图文识别+翻译+朗读 ... - GitHub",
      "publish_date": "2022-03-16",
      "excerpts": [
        "* 在截图时按住CTRL键可以临时取消识别，只截图并复制至剪贴板。\n* 在截图时按住ALT键可以临时取消修正文本，当识别图像的文本中不包含任何标点符号时建议这样使用。\n* 在文本区输入文本后按CTRL+回车键会直接翻译，不需要再用鼠标点翻译按钮。\n* 在文本区输入文本后按ALT+回车键会直接朗读。\n* 将图片直接拖入至程序界面上会自动开始识别。\n* 在识别或是朗读进行中如果想中止任务可以双击界面右上方“线程”字样处，或按住空格键的同时点击托盘图标。\n* 如果觉得文本区域过小，可以双击文本区使用大窗口浏览或简单编辑。\n* 有些不太用的上的功能建议关闭以减少识别等待时间，比如朗读文本。\n* 在启用了监听复制功能时临时不想执行识别或翻译可以在复制的同时按住空格键。\n\n* * *\n\n#### 使用教程：\n\n* 官方教程： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/wiki>\n* 游戏机翻： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1Vt4y1U7Es/>\n* 接口申请： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1Vt4y1U7Es?p=2>\n* 基础操作： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1UV411d7zh>\n* 基础操作： <https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV15k4y1z7Vp>\n\n* * *\n\n#### API版接口申请（默认使用免费的演示版 | [演示版和API版区别](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/wiki/%E6%BC%94%E7%A4%BA%E7%89%88%E5%92%8CAPI%E7%89%88%E5%BC%95%E6%93%8E%E6%8E%A5%E5%8F%A3 \"演示版和API版区别\") ）：\n\n##### 搜狗：\n\n* OCR： <http://deepi.sogou.com> （送100/200元体验金 | 可能需要先充点钱才能用）\n* 翻译： <http://deepi.sogou.com> （送100/200元体验金）\n##### 腾讯：\n\n* OCR： <https://cloud.tencent.com/> （1000次/月免费 | 腾讯云接口）\n* OCR： <https://ai.qq.com/v1/> （免费 | 腾讯AI旧版接口）\n* OCR： <https://open.youtu.qq.com/> （500次/天免费 | 腾讯优图接口）\n* 翻译： <https://cloud.tencent.com> （5百万字符/月免费 | 腾讯云接口）\n* 翻译： <https://ai.qq.com/v1/> （免费，不推荐，质量不如腾讯云 | 腾讯AI旧版接口）\n* 公式： <https://cloud.tencent.com/> （1000次/月免费 | 腾讯云接口）\n* 表格： <https://cloud.tencent.com/> （1000次/月免费 | 腾讯云接口）\n\n##### 百度：\n\n* OCR： <https://cloud.baidu.com> （50000次/天免费）\n* 翻译： <http://api.fanyi.baidu.com> （免费）\n* 公式： <https://cloud.baidu.com> （1000次免费）\n* 表格： <https://cloud.baidu.com> （50次/天免费）\n\n##### 有道：\n\n* OCR： <http://ai.youdao.com> （送50元体验金）\n* 翻译： <http://ai.youdao.com> （送50元体验金）\n\n##### 阿里：\n\n* OCR： <https://market.aliyun.com/products/57124001/cmapi020020.html> （通用版 | 500次/免费）\n* OCR： <https://market.aliyun.com/products/57124001/cmapi028554.html> （高精度 | 500次/免费）\n* OCR： <https://market.aliyun.com/products/57124001/cmapi00040832.html> （手写版 | 500次/免费）\n* OCR： <https://market.aliyun.com/products/57124001/cmapi00040847.html> （多语种 | 500次/免费）\n* 表格： <https://market.aliyun.com/products/57124001/cmapi024968.html> （500次/免费）\n\n##### 讯飞：\n\n* OCR： <https://www.xfyun.cn/services/textRecg> （10万次/免费）\n* 公式： <https://www.xfyun.cn/services/formula-discern>\n\n##### 网校（学而思/好未来）：\n* OCR： <https://ai.xueersi.com> （10万次/天免费 | 10秒间隔/次 | 旧站）\n* OCR： <https://ai.100tal.com> （新站）\n\n##### Deepl：\n\n* 翻译： <https://www.deepl.com/translator> （付费且服务器延迟较高）\n\n##### Mathpix：\n\n* 公式： <https://dashboard.mathpix.com> （1000次/月免费）\n\n##### SpaceOCR：\n\n* OCR： <https://ocr.space/OCRAPI> （25000次/月免费）\n\n* * *\n\n#### 隐私安全：\n\n##### 程序：\n\n* 程序本身不包含任何恶意代码，如有杀软误报请自行加入白名单（曾提交火绒人工检测为安全）\n* 程序调用了一个51.la的统计，单纯用来统计使用人数，除此之外不会上传您的任何隐私数据\n* 因功能需要，请使用管理员权限运行，程序会调用键鼠钩子，如“划词复制功能”需要鼠标钩子，为避免某些游戏屏蔽热键导致无法截图而使用了键盘钩子\n* 程序的开发与发布均在ESET NOD32杀软保护的环境中完成，如果使用过程中，您系统中某种“安全”软件称「发现木马」，那么此种情形将考验您的判断力\n\n##### 引擎：\n\n* 本程序不带离线引擎，所调用的识别/翻译/朗读等全部为在线引擎\n* 理论上用户上传到各个引擎的数据都只是缓存数据，通常会在一定时间内清除\n* 但服务器毕竟为各引擎所在公司控制和所有，我无法保证上传数据的绝对安全\n* 所以如果您要处理的数据属于商业或机密数据，请换用其他自己可控的本地私有化产品\n* 如因此发生的任何损失，本人表示概不负责\n\n* * *\n\n#### 特别说明：\n\n* 已知在高分辨率/高分屏下截图功能异常 [[尝试解决方法]](https://github.\ncom/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/issues/17 \"[尝试解决方法]\")\n* 如需使用自己申请的ID和KEY，用文本编辑器打开程序目录下的CONFIG.ini文件，在[数据配置]项，将各版本后面数值设为1（0为演示版），并修改下面相应的ID和KEY\n* 如需反馈API版引擎存在的问题，请主动提供相应的ID和KEY给我测试\n\n* * *\n\n#### 程序下载：\n\n* 更新日志： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/releases>\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzoui.com/b015f3htg>\n* 百度网盘： <https://pan.baidu.com/s/11a0BBVriXMMLhi0dV3Srsg> 提取码: 5azr\n* 微云网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/5DllWfF>\n\n* * *\n\n#### 问题反馈：\n\n* 论坛反馈： <https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/issues>\n* QQ群反馈： [https://jq.qq.com/?\\_wv=1027&k=5XjJmJ5](https://jq.qq.com/?_wv=1027&k=5XjJmJ5)\n\n* * *\n\n#### 程序截图：\n\n[默认界面](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/master/images/AeroSnap%E6%88%AA%E5%9B%BE1.png \"默认界面\")\n\n[暗色界面](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/master/images/AeroSnap%E6%88%AA%E5%9B%BE3.png \"暗色界面\")\n\n## About\n\nPandaOCR - 多功能OCR图文识别+翻译+朗读+弹窗+公式+表格+图床+搜图+二维码\n\n### The reason why the version number of the professional version starts from 5.x is that the normal version will be updated in the future, so a period of version number is reserved.\nYou can continue to use the regular version for free as before, without worrying about deactivating the regular version after the launch of the professional version. If you have higher needs, you can try the professional version. You can also use the Baidu API interface without activation. Support shortcut keys and screen corner trigger screenshot recognition function, convenient and fast.\n\n### Features\n\n* Multifunctional OCR Image and Text Recognition\n* Translation and reading\n* Pop-up window\n* Formula and form\n* Image bed, image search, and QR code\n* Supports interception and identification of fixed areas, suitable for Japanese game consoles or raw meat subtitles\n\n### Project Samples\n\nPandaOCR Screenshot 1\n\n### Project Activity\n\n[](https://sourceforge.net/auth/?return_to=%2Fprojects%2Fpandaocr.mirror%2F \"Follow PandaOCR - Requires login\") \n\nSee All Activity >\n\n### Categories\n\nMachine Learning , OCR\n\n### Follow PandaOCR\n\n[PandaOCR Web Site](https://github."

        "[](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio) [](https://github.com/newreleasesio) Pricing  \n\n# github miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR [2\\.69](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/releases/tag/2.69)\n\nPandaOCR.v2.69\n\non [GitHub](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR)\n\nlatest releases: 2\\.72 , 2\\.71\n\n5 years ago\n\n#### 更新日志：\n\n* 修复 腾讯翻译 API接口\n* 修复 弹窗自动隐藏\n\n#### 特别说明：\n\n* 🥇由于演示接口存在未知风险，日后会陆续将演示接口移除，此工具一直以来也都在强调大家应申请使用更快更稳定的私用API接口！\n* 🥇所以请及时转到私用API接口以免到时一脸懵逼可能影响您的日常使用！ [接口申请教程](https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR/wiki/%E6%BC%94%E7%A4%BA%E7%89%88%E5%92%8CAPI%E7%89%88%E5%BC%95%E6%93%8E%E6%8E%A5%E5%8F%A3)\n* 🥇此次更新还保留了一些演示接口作为过渡使用，但下个版本预计会移除90%的演示接口！\n* 🥇目前全新重构的专业版已发布，我个人精力有限，这里的普通版承诺会保持维护API接口和修复重大BUG，但不再增加新功能！ [PandaOCR.Pro专业版](https://gitee.com/DDDDDGOOO/PandaOCR.Pro)\n* 如果遇到无法运行或其他杀软误报问题，请参考这里： [参考链接](https://support.qq.com/products/322047/faqs-more/=58619)\n* 你知道的，我没有忘记\n\n#### 文件下载：\n* 蓝奏网盘： <https://miao520.lanzoui.com/b015f3htg>\n* 百度网盘： <https://pan.baidu.com/s/11a0BBVriXMMLhi0dV3Srsg> 提取码: 5azr\n* 微云网盘： <https://share.weiyun.com/5DllWfF>\n\nCheck out latest releases or  \nreleases around miaomiaosoft/ PandaOCR 2.69\n\nDon't miss a new PandaOCR release\n\nNewReleases is sending notifications on new releases.\n\nGet notifications\n\n* About\n* [News](https://twitter.com/newreleasesio)\n* Contact\n\n* Terms of Service\n* Privacy Policy"

      "url": "https://github.com/miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro/wiki/%E7%99%BE%E5%BA%A6OCR%E6%8E%A5%E5%8F%A3%E7%94%B3%E8%AF%B7%E6%95%99%E7%A8%8B",
      "title": "百度OCR接口申请教程 · miaomiaosoft/PandaOCR.Pro Wiki · GitHub",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nmiaomiaosoft / **PandaOCR.Pro** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 129\n* Star 1\\.9k\n\n# 百度OCR接口申请教程\n\nJump to bottom\n\nmiao\\_miao edited this page Apr 30, 2021 · 3 revisions\n\n### 免费额度：\n\n百度云最近新发公告将对免费额度进行调整，大意：在2021年5月27日前登陆百度云实名认证（应该不用扫脸）并使用过该接口可保持原有的每天50000次额度，否则超时未实名降为每月200次，超时实名降为每月1000次，请正在或将要使(bai)用(piao)百度OCR API版接口的同学注意。\n\n完整公告： <https://dwz.cn/RxUDwxuW>\n\n### 价格参考：\n\n<https://ai.baidu.com/ai-doc/OCR/9k3h7xuv6>\n\n### 教程开始：\n\n1. 打开页面： <https://ai.baidu.com/tech/ocr/> 点击“立即使用”\n\n1. 注册或使用已有百度帐号登陆\n2. 点击创建应用\n\n1. 信息可随意填写，点击“立即创建”\n\n1. 创建完毕后点击“查看应用详情”\n\n1. 复制或记下红框内容\n\n1. 运行“PandaOCR.Pro”点击设置按钮，再切换至接口页面，将“百度OCR版本”后面的数值改为1，再将“接口”后的文本框中填写上面你申请到的“API Key”和“Secret Key”\n\n1. 接口填写格式：API Key | Secret Key，假设API Key为123，Secret Key为888，则填写“123 | 888”\n2. 填写后关闭设置窗口，点击保存按钮并重启软件生效\n\n### 其他说明：\n当前已内置百度OCR版本：1=通用识别 2=高精度识别\n\n如需添加其他接口可以向作者反馈，谢谢！\n\n## Wiki pages Pages 2\n\n* Home\n* 百度OCR接口申请教程\n  \n    + 免费额度：\n    + 价格参考：\n    + 教程开始：\n    + 其他说明：\n\n### Clone this wiki locally\n\n## Footer"
      ]

        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nhaoyitedaniu / **unlimited-ocr** Public\n\nforked from baidu/Unlimited-OCR\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 0\n* Star 1\n\n# haoyitedaniu/unlimited-ocr\n\nmain\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[4 Commits](https://github.com/haoyitedaniu/unlimited-ocr/commits/main/)\n\n 4 Commits |\n|assets |assets | | |\n|wheel |wheel | | |\n|.gitignore |.gitignore | | |\n|CONTRIBUTING.md |CONTRIBUTING.md | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|Unlimited-OCR.pdf |Unlimited-OCR.pdf | | |\n|infer.py |infer.py | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* Contributing\n* License\n\nBaidu Inc.\n\n* * *\n# Unlimited OCR Works\n\n[GitHub](https://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR)\n\n[arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23050) [Twitter Follow](https://x.com/Baidu_Inc)\n\n### Welcome the Era of One-shot Long-horizon Parsing.\n\nUnlimited OCR overview\n\n## Release\n\n* [2026/06/23] 📄 Our paper is now available on [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23050) .\n* [2026/06/23] 🤝 Thanks to the ModelScope community for their support. Our model is now available at [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/PaddlePaddle/Unlimited-OCR) .\n* [2026/06/22] 🚀 We present [Unlimited-OCR](https://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) , aiming to push [Deepseek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) one step further.\n\n## Inference\n\n### Transformers\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs. Requirements tested on python 3.12.3 + CUDA12.9：\n\n```\ntorch==2.10.0\ntorchvision==0.25.0\ntransformers==4.57.1\nPillow==12.1.1\nmatplotlib==3.10.8\neinops==0.8.2\naddict==2.4.0\neasydict==1.13\npymupdf==1.27.2.2\npsutil==7.2.2\n```\n\n```\nimport os \n import torch \n from transformers import AutoModel , AutoTokenizer \n\n model_name = 'baidu/Unlimited-OCR' \n\n tokenizer = AutoTokenizer . from_pretrained ( model_name , trust_remote_code = True )\n model = AutoModel . from_pretrained (\n    model_name ,\n    trust_remote_code = True ,\n    use_safetensors = True ,\n    torch_dtype = torch . bfloat16 ,\n)\n model = model . eval (). cuda ()\n\n # ── Single image supports two configs: gundam or base ── \n # gundam: base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True \n # base: base_size=1024, image_size=1024, crop_mode=False \n model . infer (\n    tokenizer ,\n    prompt = '<image>document parsing.' ,\n    image_file = 'your_image.jpg' ,\n    output_path = 'your/output/dir' ,\n    base_size = 1024 , image_size = 640 , crop_mode = True ,\n    max_length = 32768 ,\n    no_repeat_ngram_size = 35 , ngram_window = 128 ,\n    save_results = True ,\n)\n\n...\n\nLong-horizon OCR demo\n\n## Acknowledgement\n\nWe would like to thank [Deepseek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) , [Deepseek-OCR-2](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR-2) , [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) for their valuable models and ideas.\n\n## Citation\n\n```\n@misc { yin2026unlimitedocrworks ,\n      title = { Unlimited OCR Works } , \n      author = { Youyang Yin and Huanhuan Liu and YY and Qunyi Xie and Chaorun Liu and Shiqi Yang and Shaohua Wang and Zhanlong Liu and Hao Zou and Jinyue Chen and Shu Wei and Jingjing Wu and Mingxin Huang and Zhen Wu and Guibin Wang and Tengyu Du and Lei Jia } ,\n      year = { 2026 } ,\n      eprint = { 2606.23050 } ,\n      archivePrefix = { arXiv } ,\n      primaryClass = { cs.CV } ,\n      url = { https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23050 } , \n}\n```\n\n## About\n\nUnlimited OCR Works: Welcome the Era of One-shot Long-horizon Parsing.\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nMIT license\n\n### Contributing\n\nContributing\n\nActivity\n### Stars\n\n**1** star\n\n### Watchers\n\n**0** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\nNo releases published\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributo"

        "# \n\nbaidu\n\n/\n\nUnlimited-OCR\n\nlike 361\n\nFollow\n\nBAIDU 2\\.58k\n\nModel card Files Files and versions xet [Community 1](https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR/discussions)\n\nDeploy\n\nCopy to bucket new\n\nUse this model\n\n### Instructions to use baidu/Unlimited-OCR with libraries, inference providers, notebooks, and local apps. Follow these links to get started.\n\n* Libraries\n* Transformers\n  \n  How to use baidu/Unlimited-OCR with Transformers:\n  \n  ```\n  # Use a pipeline as a high-level helper\n  from transformers import pipeline\n  \n  pipe = pipeline(\"image-text-to-text\", model=\"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\", trust_remote_code=True)\n  ```\n  \n  ```\n  # Load model directly\n  from transformers import AutoModel\n  model = AutoModel.from_pretrained(\"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\", trust_remote_code=True, dtype=\"auto\")\n  ```\n* Notebooks\n* Google Colab\n* Kaggle\n* Local Apps Settings\n* vLLM\n  \n  How to use baidu/Unlimited-OCR with vLLM:\n  \n  ##### Install from pip and serve model\n  \n  ```\n  # Install vLLM from pip:\npip install vllm\n  # Start the vLLM server:\n  vllm serve \"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\"\n  # Call the server using curl (OpenAI-compatible API):\n  curl -X POST \"http://localhost:8000/v1/completions\" \\\n  \t-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  \t--data '{\n  \t\t\"model\": \"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\",\n  \t\t\"prompt\": \"Once upon a time,\",\n  \t\t\"max_tokens\": 512,\n  \t\t\"temperature\": 0.5\n  \t}'\n  ```\n  \n  ##### Use Docker\n  \n  ```\n  docker model run hf.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR\n  ```\n* SGLang\n  \n  How to use baidu/Unlimited-OCR with SGLang:\n  \n  ##### Install from pip and serve model\n  \n  ```\n  # Install SGLang from pip:\n  pip install sglang\n  # Start the SGLang server:\n  python3 -m sglang.launch_server \\\n      --model-path \"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\" \\\n      --host 0.0.0.0 \\\n      --port 30000\n  # Call the server using curl (OpenAI-compatible API):\n  curl -X POST \"http://localhost:30000/v1/completions\" \\\n  \t-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  \t--data '{\n  \t\t\"model\": \"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\",\n\"prompt\": \"Once upon a time,\",\n  \t\t\"max_tokens\": 512,\n  \t\t\"temperature\": 0.5\n  \t}'\n  ```\n  \n  ##### Use Docker images\n  \n  ```\n  docker run --gpus all \\\n      --shm-size 32g \\\n      -p 30000:30000 \\\n      -v ~/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface \\\n      --env \"HF_TOKEN=<secret>\" \\\n      --ipc=host \\\n      lmsysorg/sglang:latest \\\n      python3 -m sglang.launch_server \\\n          --model-path \"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\" \\\n          --host 0.0.0.0 \\\n          --port 30000\n  # Call the server using curl (OpenAI-compatible API):\n  curl -X POST \"http://localhost:30000/v1/completions\" \\\n  \t-H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  \t--data '{\n  \t\t\"model\": \"baidu/Unlimited-OCR\",\n  \t\t\"prompt\": \"Once upon a time,\",\n  \t\t\"max_tokens\": 512,\n  \t\t\"temperature\": 0.5\n  \t}'\n  ```\n* Docker Model Runner\n  \n  How to use baidu/Unlimited-OCR with Docker Model Runner:\n  \n  ```\n  docker model run hf.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR\n  ```\n\nmain\n\nUnlimited-OCR / LICENSE\n\nHYPERUU's picture\n\nHYPERUU\n\nInitial commit\n[d549bb9](https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR/commit/d549bb9d6a055dbe291408916d66acc2cd5920f6) 1 day ago\n\nRaw\n\nDownload with hf CLI\n\nCopy download link\n\n[History](https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR/commits/main/LICENSE) Blame Contribute Delete\n\n1\\.06 kB\n\n| |MIT License |\n| --- | --- |\n| | |\n| |Copyright (c) 2026 Baidu |\n| | |\n| |Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy |\n| |of this software and associated documentation files (the \"Software\" ), to deal |\n| |in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights |\n| |to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell |\n| |copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is |\n| |furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions: |\n| | |\n| |The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all |\n| |copies or substantial portions of the Software. |\n| | |\n| |THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED \"AS IS\" , WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR |\n| |IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, |\n| |FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONI"

Our model is now available at [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/PaddlePaddle/Unlimited-OCR) .\n* [2026/06/22] 🚀 We present [Unlimited-OCR](https://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) , aiming to push [Deepseek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) one step further.\n\n## Inference\n### Transformers\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs. Requirements tested on python 3.12.3 + CUDA12.9：\n\n```\ntorch==2.10.0\ntorchvision==0.25.0\ntransformers==4.57.1\nPillow==12.1.1\nmatplotlib==3.10.8\neinops==0.8.2\naddict==2.4.0\neasydict==1.13\npymupdf==1.27.2.2\npsutil==7.2.2\n```\n\n```\nimport os \n import torch \n from transformers import AutoModel , AutoTokenizer \n\n model_name = 'baidu/Unlimited-OCR' \n\n tokenizer = AutoTokenizer . from_pretrained ( model_name , trust_remote_code = True )\n model = AutoModel . from_pretrained (\n    model_name ,\n    trust_remote_code = True ,\n    use_safetensors = True ,\n    torch_dtype = torch . bfloat16 ,\n)\n model = model . eval (). cuda ()\n\n # ── Single image supports two configs: gundam or base ── \n # gundam: base_size=1024, image_size=640, crop_mode=True \n # base: base_size=1024, image_size=1024, crop_mode=False \n model . infer (\n    tokenizer ,\n    prompt = '<image>document parsing.' ,\n    image_file = 'your_image.jpg' ,\n\n...\n\norg/abs/2606.23050 } , \n}\n```\n\n## About\n\nNo description, website, or topics provided.\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nMIT license\n\n### Contributing\n\nContributing\n\nActivity\n\nCustom properties\n\n### Stars\n\n**1** star\n\n### Watchers\n\n**1** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**0** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases\n\n3 tags\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Python 98\\.1%\n* Shell 1\\.9%\n\n## Footer"

    {
      "url": "https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/6063/unlimited-ocr-baidu-long-pdfs",
      "title": "Unlimited-OCR by Baidu: Open Source OCR for Long PDFs",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "Skip to content Skip to navigation Skip to footer\n\nP. Pillitteri\n\n[](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/6049/mistral-ocr-4-document-ai-170-languages \"Previous\") [](https://pasqualepillitteri.it/en/news/6077/pokemon-go-scans-military-drones-niantic-vantor \"Next\")\n\n# Unlimited-OCR by Baidu: Open Source OCR for Long PDFs\n\n##### Unlimited-OCR is Baidu's open source OCR model (MIT, 4,663 stars) that parses entire multi-page PDFs in a single pass. It is free: how to use it and how to test it right now.\n\nAnyone who has tried to feed a two-hundred-page contract to an OCR pipeline knows the ritual: the system reads the first page, extracts its text, moves to the second, stitches the pieces together, and only at the end delivers a reconstructed document to drop into a RAG system (Retrieval-Augmented Generation, the technique that pairs a language model's generation with a targeted document search).\nIt works, but it is page-by-page labor, and the model's memory grows with every token produced until a long document becomes more of an engineering problem than a reading one.\n\nUnlimited-OCR, the open source model released by Baidu on June 18, 2026, sets out to remove that very ritual. The stated goal sits right in the repository subtitle: \"Welcome the Era of One-shot Long-horizon Parsing,\" meaning reading a long document in a single pass instead of splitting and stitching it. The project has gathered 4,663 stars on GitHub as of June 24, 2026, ships under the MIT license, and presents itself as a direct evolution of DeepSeek-OCR. In this article I answer the three practical questions that matter: is it free, how do you use it, and how do you try it right now.\n\nUnlimited-OCR by Baidu - open source MIT OCR model for parsing long PDFs in a single pass Unlimited-OCR: one-shot parsing of long documents, MIT license, open weights on Hugging Face.\n\n**TL;DR for the five-minute reader**\n* **Yes, it is free** : MIT license, open weights downloadable from Hugging Face and ModelScope, no API key and no licensing cost.\n* **3 billion parameters** in BF16 format and a 32,768-token context window: it runs on a single mid-range NVIDIA GPU.\n* **Technical novelty** : it keeps the entire document visible to the model but carries only a sliding window of the generated text, so memory does not grow with every output token.\n* **Two ways to use it** : the Transformers library in Python for a single image, or the `infer.py` command-line script for folders and multipage PDFs.\n* **Try it instantly** : the Hugging Face Space demo runs in the browser with nothing to install; alternatively Google Colab or Kaggle with a free GPU.\n\n## What Unlimited-OCR Is and Where It Comes From\n\nUnlimited-OCR is a vision-language OCR (Optical Character Recognition, the optical recognition of characters) model: it takes the image of a page as input and returns structured text, including tables and layout.\n\n...\n\ncom/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) (official repository, MIT license, 4,663 stars)\n* [Model on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) (downloadable weights)\n* [Hugging Face Space demo](https://huggingface.co/spaces/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) (test in the browser)\n* [DeepSeek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) (the project Unlimited-OCR derives from)\n* [PaddleOCR](https://github.com/PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR) (cited in the project credits)\n\nShare\n\n* * *\n\nRate this article\n\n★ ★ ★ ★ ★\n\nThanks for your rating!\n\n* * *\n\n### Related Articles\n\n[Domyn and the 400-Billion-Parameter Open Source European AI Model AI News & Tendenze \\#ai news \\#ai tools #### Domyn and the 400-Billion-Parameter Open Source European AI Model](https://pasqualepillitteri."
      ]

        "Unlimited-OCR: Image-to-Text model — overview, use cases ...\nOverview Unlimited-OCR by baidu is an end-to-end optical character recognition model designed for long-horizon document parsing with support for single images, multi-page documents, and PDFs. The model uses a vision-language architecture that processes images up to 1024×1024 pixels (or 640 pixels in \"gundam\" mode with cropping) and generates text outputs up to 32,768 tokens, making it"

It is now available at [Hugging Face Spaces](https://huggingface.co/spaces/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) .\n* [2026/06/23] 📄 Our paper is now available on [arXiv](https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.23050) .\n* [2026/06/23] 🤝 Thanks to the [ModelScope community](https://github.\ncom/modelscope) for their support. Our model is now available at [ModelScope](https://modelscope.cn/models/PaddlePaddle/Unlimited-OCR) .\n* [2026/06/22] 🚀 We present [Unlimited-OCR](https://github.com/baidu/Unlimited-OCR) , aiming to push [Deepseek-OCR](https://github.com/deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR) one step further.\n\n## Inference\n\n### Transformers\n\nInference using Huggingface transformers on NVIDIA GPUs.       "title": "GitHub - NMAC427/SwiftOCR: Fast and simple OCR library written in Swift · GitHub",

      "excerpts": [
        "## Navigation Menu\n\nToggle navigation\n\nAppearance settings\n\nSearch or jump to...\n\n# Search code, repositories, users, issues, pull requests...\n\nAppearance settings\n\nResetting focus\n\nNMAC427 / **SwiftOCR** Public\n\n* Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings\n* Fork 477\n* Star 4\\.6k\n\n# NMAC427/SwiftOCR\n\nmaster\n\nBranches Tags\n\n \n\nGo to file\n\nCode\n\nOpen more actions menu\n\n## Folders and files\n\n|Name |Name |Last commit message |Last commit date |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- |\n|## Latest commit\n\n## History\n\n[166 Commits](https://github.com/NMAC427/SwiftOCR/commits/master/)\n\n 166 Commits |\n|example |example | | |\n|framework |framework | | |\n|.gitattributes |.gitattributes | | |\n|.gitignore |.gitignore | | |\n|LICENSE |LICENSE | | |\n|README.md |README.md | | |\n|SwiftOCR.podspec |SwiftOCR.podspec | | |\n|View all files |\n\n## Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n* Apache-2.0 license\n\n### ⛔️ This Project is deprecated and no longer gets maintained!\n\n...\n\ncom/b3f612c0e5fc72d00210c4dfe5cbd1428b3c6e797b93071378f68ddd659ef096/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f636f636f61706f64732f702f53776966744f43522e7376673f7374796c653d666c6174)\n\n# SwiftOCR\n\nSwiftOCR is a fast and simple OCR library written in Swift. It uses a neural network for image recognition.\nAs of now, SwiftOCR is optimized for recognizing short, one line long alphanumeric codes (e.g. DI4C9CM). We currently support iOS and OS X.\n\n## Features\n\n* Easy to use training class\n* High accuracy\n* Great default image preprocessing\n* Fast and accurate character segmentation algorithm\n* Add support for lowercase characters\n* Add support for connected character segmentation\n\n## Why should I choose SwiftOCR instead of Tesseract?\n\nThis is a really good question.\n\nIf you want to recognize normal text like a poem or a news article, go with Tesseract, but if you want to recognize short, alphanumeric codes (e.g.\n\n...\n\nSwiftOCR is available through CocoaPods. To install it, simply add the following line to your Podfile:\n\n`pod 'SwiftOCR'`\n\nIf you ever used Tesseract you know how exhausting it can be to implement OCR into your project.\nSwiftOCR is the exact opposite of Tesseract. It can be implemented using **just 6 lines of code** .\n\n```\nimport SwiftOCR\n\n let swiftOCRInstance = SwiftOCR ( ) \n    \nswiftOCRInstance . recognize ( myImage ) { recognizedString in \n    print ( recognizedString ) \n }\n```\n\nTo improve your experience with SwiftOCR you should set your Build Configuration to `Release` .\n\n#### Training\n\nTraining SwiftOCR is pretty easy. There are only a few steps you have to do, before it can recognize a new font.\n\nThe easiest way to train SwiftOCR is using the training app that can be found under `/example/OS X/SwiftOCR Training` . First select the fonts you want to train from the list. After that, you can change the characters you want to train in the text field.\n\n...\n\nSince SwiftOCR uses a neural network for the recognition, this isn't a real problem. A NN is flexible like a human brain and can recognize even the most distorted image (most of the time).\n\n## TODO\n\n* Port to [GPUImage 2](https://github.com/BradLarson/GPUImage2)\n\n## Dependencies\n\n* [Swift-AI](https://github.com/collinhundley/Swift-AI)\n* [GPUImage](https://github.com/BradLarson/GPUImage)\n* [Union-Find](https://github.com/hollance/swift-algorithm-club/tree/master/Union-Find)\n\n## License\n\n```\nThe code in this repository is licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the \"License\");\nyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.\nYou may obtain a copy of the License at\n\n   http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0\n\nUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software\ndistributed under the License is distributed on an \"AS IS\" BASIS,\nWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.\nSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and\nlimitations under the License.\n```\n\n**NOTE** : This software depends on other packages that may be licensed under different open source licenses.\n\n## About\n\nFast and simple OCR library written in Swift\n\n### Topics\n\n### Resources\n\nReadme\n\n### License\n\nApache-2.0 license\n\nActivity\n\n### Stars\n\n**4\\.6k** stars\n\n### Watchers\n\n**150** watching\n\n### Forks\n\n**477** forks\n\nReport repository\n\n## Releases 4\n\n1\\.2.1 Latest May 8, 2020\n\n\\+ 3 releases\n\n## Packages\n\n## Contributors\n\n* \n* \n*\n\n## Languages\n\n* Swift 99\\.1%\n* Other 0\\.9%\n\n## Footer"

        "# Making sure you're not a bot!\n\nLoading...\n\nYou are seeing this because the administrator of this website has set up Anubis to protect the server against the scourge of AI companies aggressively scraping websites. This can and does cause downtime for the websites, which makes their resources inaccessible for everyone.\n\nAnubis is a compromise. Anubis uses a Proof-of-Work scheme in the vein of Hashcash, a proposed proof-of-work scheme for reducing email spam. The idea is that at individual scales the additional load is ignorable, but at mass scraper levels it adds up and makes scraping much more expensive.\n\nUltimately, this is a placeholder solution so that more time can be spent on fingerprinting and identifying headless browsers (EG: via how they do font rendering) so that the challenge proof of work page doesn't need to be presented to users that are much more likely to be legitimate.\nPlease note that Anubis requires the use of modern JavaScript features that plugins like JShelter will disable. Please disable JShelter or other such plugins for this domain.\n\nProtected by [Anubis](https://github.com/TecharoHQ/anubis) From [Techaro](https://techaro.lol) . Repository files navigation\n\n* README\n## 📄 PDF-to-Markdown Benchmark for RAG Pipeline\n\n[](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/8ade7aa7794286744e80c85a211c7f0b6882c059eb17d0ac4a5e263cbfe44c6e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f507974686f6e2d332e31302532422d626c75653f6c6f676f3d707974686f6e) [](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/e12662ebbecdfb371cce9128b62bb058c2dddecdf138b8784c8f8ab681bf4cd7/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f5241472d506970656c696e652d6f72616e6765) [](https://camo.githubusercontent.com/033195cf70fea10daea78293a043862a4f06bb58bb6c7c736b3804c59ed0f15e/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f6769746875622f6c6963656e73652f6a756c696168616861682f7064662d7261672d65787472616374696f6e2d62656e63686d61726b)\n\n**A comprehensive evaluation of OCR and PDF parsing tools for LLM-based Retrieval-Augmented Generation (2024-2026).** `Python` `RAG` `OCR` `LlamaIndex` `Docling` `ColPali`\n\n* * *\n\n## 🏗️ Architecture Overview\n\n```\nflowchart TD\n\tA[PDF File] --> B[PDF Parser / OCR Tool]\n\tB --> C[Markdown/Text Output]\n\tC --> D[Chunking & Embedding]\n\tD --> E[Vector DB]\n\tE --> F[LLM RAG Pipeline]\n```\n\nLoading\n\n...\n\n* **極慢** : `Nougat` , `Marker` (若無高階 GPU)\n\n### 3\\. RAG 適用性 (Clean Output)\n\nRAG 最需要的是 **結構化且帶有語意的文本** (如 Markdown 標題層級)。\n\n* **最佳選擇** : `LlamaParse` 、 `Docling` 、 `Marker` 。直接輸出 Markdown，保留標題層級 (#, ##) 和表格結構。\n* **傳統工具問題** : `pypdf` 、 `PyMuPDF` 輸出為 \"文字流\"，表格亂碼，干擾檢索。\n\n* * *\n\n## 4\\. How to Run the Benchmark\n\n安裝必要套件：\n\n```\npip install llama-parse llama-index docling\n```\n\n執行範例：\n\n```\npython scripts/demo_llama_parse.py\n```\n\n你可以根據 `scripts/demo_llama_parse.py` 修改，對比多種工具的輸出。\n\n* * *\n\n## 5\\. Recommendation Logic\n\n* 追求精準度 → **LlamaParse** / **Docling**\n* 追求開源/本地化 → **Docling** / **Marker**\n* 追求速度 → **PyMuPDF**\n\n* * *\n\n## 6\\. FAQ & Notes\n\n**Q: 哪一類型 PDF 是分水嶺？**\n\n> 多欄論文、含複雜表格的年報、掃描型 PDF（需 OCR）、數學公式密集的學術論文，這些都是高階工具與傳統工具的分水嶺。"

Created by Jerry Liu and first...\n\nLast reviewed\n\nJun 21, 2026\n\nSources\n\n34 citations\n\nReview status\n\nSource-backed\n\nRevision\n\nv9 · 6,081 words\n\nArticle outline 17\n\n## Improve this article\n\nAdd missing citations, update stale details, or suggest a clearer explanation.\n\nSuggest edit\n\nOn this page 17\n\n* When was LlamaIndex released and who founded it?\n* Jerry Liu\n* Simon Suo\n* Is LlamaIndex open source?\n* Core Concepts\n* Documents\n* Nodes\n* Indices\n* RAG Pipeline\n* Agents\n* Workflows\n* LlamaHub (Data Connectors)\n* How does LlamaIndex differ from LangChain?\n* LlamaCloud\n* Agentic Document Workflows\n* Technical Architecture\n* Knowledge Graphs\n* Evaluation and Observability\n* How much funding has LlamaIndex raised?\n* What is LlamaIndex used for?\n* Current State (2025-2026)\n* References\n\nShow detailed outline\n\nAI Wiki AI Wiki\n\nThe free encyclopedia of artificial intelligence.     {
      "url": "https://www.marktechpost.com/2026/03/19/llamaindex-releases-liteparse-a-cli-and-typescript-native-library-for-spatial-pdf-parsing-in-ai-agent-workflows",
      "title": "LlamaIndex Releases LiteParse: A CLI and TypeScript-Native Library for Spatial PDF Parsing in AI Agent Workflows - MarkTechPost",
      "publish_date": "2026-03-20",
      "excerpts": [
        "com/category/technology/artificial-intelligence/language-model/ocr/)\n* [Open Source](https://www.marktechpost.com/category/technology/open-source/)\n\n# LlamaIndex Releases LiteParse: A CLI and TypeScript-Native Library for Spatial PDF Parsing in AI Agent Workflows\n\nBy\n\n[Asif Razzaq](https://www.marktechpost.com/author/6flvq/)\n\n\\-\n\nMarch 19, 2026\n\nIn the current landscape of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), the primary bottleneck for developers is no longer the large language model (LLM) itself, but the data ingestion pipeline. For software developers, converting complex PDFs into a format that an LLM can reason over remains a high-latency, often expensive task.\n\nLlamaIndex has recently introduced **LiteParse** , an open-source, local-first document parsing library designed to address these friction points.\nUnlike many existing tools that rely on cloud-based APIs or heavy Python-based OCR libraries, LiteParse is a TypeScript-native solution built to run entirely on a user’s local machine. It serves as a ‘fast-mode’ alternative to the company’s managed LlamaParse service, prioritizing speed, privacy, and spatial accuracy for agentic workflows.\n\n### **The Technical Pivot: TypeScript and Spatial Text**\n\nThe most significant technical distinction of LiteParse is its architecture. While the majority of the AI ecosystem is built on Python, LiteParse is written in **TypeScript** (TS) and runs on **Node.js** . It utilizes **PDF.js** (specifically `pdf.js-extract` ) for text extraction and **Tesseract.js** for local optical character recognition (OCR).\n\nBy opting for a TypeScript-native stack, LlamaIndex team ensures that LiteParse has zero Python dependencies, making it easier to integrate into modern web-based or edge-computing environments.\nIt is available as both a command-line interface (CLI) and a library, allowing developers to process documents at scale without the overhead of a Python runtime.\n\nThe library’s core logic stands on **Spatial Text Parsing** . Most traditional parsers attempt to convert documents into Markdown. However, Markdown conversion often fails when dealing with multi-column layouts or nested tables, leading to a loss of context. LiteParse avoids this by projecting text onto a spatial grid. It preserves the original layout of the page using indentation and white space, allowing the LLM to use its internal spatial reasoning capabilities to ‘read’ the document as it appeared on the page.\n\n### **Solving the Table Problem Through Layout Preservation**\n\nA recurring challenge for AI devs is extracting tabular data. Conventional methods involve complex heuristics to identify cells and rows, which frequently result in garbled text when the table structure is non-standard."
      ]
    },

      "url": "https://hokai.io/hub/tools/llamaindex",
      "title": "LlamaIndex: AI Agents for Document OCR + Workflows | HokAI",
      "publish_date": null,
      "excerpts": [
        "# LlamaIndex: AI Agents for Document OCR + Workflows\n\nLast updated: 2026-06-14\n\nLlamaIndex processes 1B+ documents with agentic OCR and document AI. Open-source MIT license. LlamaCloud includes 10,000 free credits/month.\n\nNotebookLM (Google) is a free AI research assistant for analyzing documents and generating insights. Offers audio overviews of source materials with no subscription required.\n\n## About LlamaIndex\n\nLlamaIndex is a comprehensive AI data framework and platform designed to help developers build production-ready agentic applications over enterprise data. It combines an open-source framework with managed cloud services (LlamaCloud) and specialized tools like LlamaParse for document processing, extraction, indexing, and retrieval. The platform powers complete enterprise automation across 1B+ documents processed, with 25M+ monthly package downloads.\nLlamaIndex serves as the orchestration layer connecting large language models with proprietary data sources—including APIs, PDFs, databases, and structured data—enabling developers to build RAG applications, knowledge assistants, and autonomous agents. The framework is optimized for handling complex, unstructured documents including tables, images, handwritten text, and nested layouts. LlamaIndex is available in both Python and TypeScript, with deep integrations into the broader AI ecosystem including 300+ integration packages, 150+ data connectors, 40+ LLM providers, and 40+ vector databases.\n\n## Pricing\n\nFree tier: Open-source framework (MIT licensed) with no platform fees; LlamaCloud free tier includes 10,000 credits/month (~1,000 pages). LlamaParse uses credit system: 1,000 credits = $1.25. Fast tier costs ~$1/1,000 pages; Cost Effective ~$3/1,000 pages; Agentic ~$15/1,000 pages; Agentic Plus ~$12/1,000 pages.\nNo public pricing for Starter/Pro subscription tiers (contact sales required).\n\n## Key Features\n\n* **LlamaParse - Agentic OCR** : Industry-leading document parsing for 90+ unstructured file types with support for embedded images, complex layouts, multi-page tables, and handwritten notes. Layout-aware parsing preserves document structure with page citations and confidence scores.\n* **Multi-Tier Parsing Strategy** : Four parsing tiers (Fast, Cost Effective, Agentic, Agentic Plus) that trade off speed and cost for accuracy, with optional version pinning for production stability.\n* **Structured Data Extraction** : Schema-based, LLM-powered extraction agents that turn unstructured content into structured insights with confidence scores and page citations for enterprise-grade accuracy.\n* **End-to-End Workflows** : Event-driven, async-first workflow engine that orchestrates multi-step AI processes, agents, and document pipelines with stateful pause/resume and error correction capabilities."
      ]
