1: package com.trulyfreeocr.pipeline;
2: 
3: import java.awt.image.BufferedImage;
4: import java.io.File;
5: import java.io.IOException;
6: import java.io.InputStream;
7: import java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets;
8: import java.util.List;
9: 
10: import org.apache.pdfbox.Loader;
11: import org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName;
12: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocument;
13: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDDocumentCatalog;
14: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPage;
15: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.PDPageContentStream;
16: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDRectangle;
17: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDFont;
18: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType0Font;
19: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.PDType1Font;
20: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts;
21: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.color.PDOutputIntent;
22: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.CCITTFactory;
23: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.JPEGFactory;
24: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.LosslessFactory;
25: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.image.PDImageXObject;
26: import org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.graphics.state.RenderingMode;
27: import org.apache.pdfbox.util.Matrix;
28: 
29: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.PageResult;
30: import com.trulyfreeocr.model.TextBlock;
31: import com.trulyfreeocr.util.Settings;
32: 
33: /**
34:  * Re-assembles a searchable PDF from per-page inputs:
35:  *   - Source PDF (copied for page dimensions / media boxes)
36:  *   - Cleaned background images
37:  *   - Binary foreground masks (CCITT G4 compressed stencil overlay)
38:  *   - OCR results (invisible text layer)
39:  *
40:  * MRC-like layout (per page):
41:  *   1. Background layer: cleaned page image (Lossless PNG for now).
42:  *   2. Foreground mask: CCITT G4 compressed binary mask, drawn as a
43:  *      PDF ImageMask stencil in black — reveals only the text pixels
44:  *      on top of the background.
45:  *   3. Text layer: invisible OCR text (RenderingMode.NEITHER = Tr 3),
46:  *      selectable and searchable but not visible.
47:  *
48:  * Rendering mode NEITHER makes text invisible on screen/print while
49:  * keeping it selectable and searchable.
50:  *
51:  * Coordinate transformation:
52:  *   Page images at 300 DPI → PDF user space at 72 DPI.
53:  *   scaleX = pageWidth_pts  / imageWidth_px
54:  *   scaleY = pageHeight_pts / imageHeight_px
55:  *   Y is flipped: image origin is top-left, PDF origin is bottom-left.
56:  */
57: public class PDFAssembler {
58: 
59:     private final PDType1Font font;
60:     private final float minFontSize;
61:     private final MetadataPreserver preserver = new MetadataPreserver();
62:     private File pdfaFontFile;
63: 
64:     public PDFAssembler() {
65:         this("HELVETICA", 1f);
66:     }
67: 
68:     public PDFAssembler(String fontName, float minFontSize) {
69:         Standard14Fonts.FontName resolved;
70:         try {
71:             resolved = Standard14Fonts.FontName.valueOf(fontName);
72:         } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {
73:             resolved = Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA;
74:         }
75:         this.font = new PDType1Font(resolved);
76:         this.minFontSize = minFontSize;
77:     }
78: 
79:     public void setPdfaFont(File fontFile) {
80:         this.pdfaFontFile = fontFile;
81:     }
82: 
83:     /**
84:      * Builds a searchable PDF with MRC-like foreground mask overlay.
85:      *
86:      * When foregroundMasks is provided, each mask is CCITT G4 compressed
87:      * and drawn as a PDF ImageMask stencil in black on top of the background.
88:      * This reproduces the original text pixels at full sharpness, while
89:      * the background layer carries the cleaned (de-speckled) page image.
90:      *
91:      * @param sourcePdf       Original PDF (for per-page media boxes).
92:      * @param backgrounds     Per-page cleaned background images.
93:      * @param foregroundMasks Per-page binary foreground masks (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY,
94:      *                        black=text, white=background), or null to skip.
95:      * @param ocrResults      Per-page OCR results.
96:      * @param usePdfa         Enable PDF/A-2b output with XMP metadata.
97:      * @return A new PDDocument with foreground mask overlay and searchable text.
98:      */
99:     public PDDocument assemble(File sourcePdf,
100:                                List<BufferedImage> backgrounds,
101:                                List<BufferedImage> foregroundMasks,
102:                                List<PageResult> ocrResults,
103:                                boolean usePdfa) throws IOException {
104:         PDDocument output = new PDDocument();
105:         List<PDPage> outPages = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
106: 
107:         try (PDDocument source = Loader.loadPDF(sourcePdf)) {
108:             int pageCount = source.getNumberOfPages();
109:             for (int i = 0; i < pageCount; i++) {
110:                 PDPage sourcePage = source.getPage(i);
111:                 PDRectangle mediaBox = sourcePage.getMediaBox();
112: 
113:                 BufferedImage bgImage = backgrounds.get(i);
114:                 PageResult ocr = ocrResults.get(i);
115: 
116:                 PDPage outPage = new PDPage(mediaBox);
117:                 output.addPage(outPage);
118:                 outPages.add(outPage);
119: 
120:                 try (PDPageContentStream cs = new PDPageContentStream(output, outPage)) {
121:                     float pageW = mediaBox.getWidth();
122:                     float pageH = mediaBox.getHeight();
123: 
124:                     // Layer 1: background image
125:                     PDImageXObject bgXObject;
126:                     if (foregroundMasks == null) {
127:                         bgXObject = JPEGFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage, 0.85f);
128:                     } else {
129:                         bgXObject = LosslessFactory.createFromImage(output, bgImage);
130:                     }
131:                     cs.drawImage(bgXObject, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
132: 
133:                     // Layer 2: foreground mask as CCITT G4 stencil overlay
134:                     if (foregroundMasks != null && i < foregroundMasks.size()) {
135:                         BufferedImage fgMask = foregroundMasks.get(i);
136:                         PDImageXObject fgCCITT = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, fgMask);
137:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
138:                         fgCCITT.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
139:                         cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
140:                         cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
141:                     }
142: 
143:                     // Layer 3: invisible OCR text
144:                     float scaleX = pageW / ocr.getWidth();
145:                     float scaleY = pageH / ocr.getHeight();
146:                     PDFont pageFont = (usePdfa && pdfaFontFile != null && pdfaFontFile.exists())
147:                             ? PDType0Font.load(output, pdfaFontFile)
148:                             : font;
149:                     cs.beginText();
150:                     cs.setFont(pageFont, minFontSize);
151:                     cs.setRenderingMode(RenderingMode.NEITHER);
152: 
153:                     for (TextBlock tb : ocr.getTextBlocks()) {
154:                         float x = tb.getBbox().x * scaleX;
155:                         float y = pageH - (tb.getBbox().y + tb.getBbox().height) * scaleY;
156:                         float fontSize = Math.max(tb.getBbox().height * scaleY, minFontSize);
157:                         cs.setFont(pageFont, fontSize);
158:                         cs.setTextMatrix(Matrix.getTranslateInstance(x, y));
159:                         cs.showText(tb.getWord());
160:                     }
161:                     cs.endText();
162:                 }
163:             }
164: 
165:             // Copy metadata from source to output
166:             preserver.preserve(source, output, outPages);
167: 
168:             if (usePdfa) {
169:                 addPdfaMetadata(output);
170:             }
171:         }
172: 
173:         return output;
174:     }
175: 
176:     private void addPdfaMetadata(PDDocument doc) throws IOException {
177:         PDDocumentCatalog catalog = doc.getDocumentCatalog();
178: 
179:         // PDF/A-2b XMP metadata
180:         String xmp = """
181:                 <?xpacket begin="\\uFEFF" id="W5M0MpCehiHzreSzNTczkc9d"?>
182:                 <x:xmpmeta xmlns:x="adobe:ns:meta/">
183:                   <rdf:RDF xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#">
184:                     <rdf:Description rdf:about=""
185:                       xmlns:pdfaid="http://www.aiim.org/pdfa/ns/id/">
186:                       <pdfaid:part>2</pdfaid:part>
187:                       <pdfaid:conformance>B</pdfaid:conformance>
188:                     </rdf:Description>
189:                     <rdf:Description rdf:about=""
190:                       xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/">
191:                       <dc:format>application/pdf</dc:format>
192:                     </rdf:Description>
193:                   </rdf:RDF>
194:                 </x:xmpmeta>
195:                 <?xpacket end="w"?>""".stripIndent();
196: 
197:         org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDMetadata metadata =
198:                 new org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.common.PDMetadata(doc);
199:         metadata.importXMPMetadata(xmp.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8));
200:         catalog.setMetadata(metadata);
201: 
202:         // sRGB output intent
203:         try (InputStream srgbStream = getClass().getResourceAsStream("/sRGB Color Space Profile.icm")) {
204:             if (srgbStream != null) {
205:                 PDOutputIntent intent = new PDOutputIntent(doc, srgbStream);
206:                 intent.setInfo("sRGB IEC61966-2.1");
207:                 intent.setOutputCondition("sRGB IEC61966-2.1");
208:                 intent.setOutputConditionIdentifier("sRGB IEC61966-2.1");
209:                 intent.setRegistryName("http://www.color.org");
210:                 catalog.addOutputIntent(intent);
211:             }
212:         }
213:     }
214: 
215: }

16: /**
17:  * Compresses a binary foreground mask via jbig2enc subprocess,
18:  * falling back to CCITT Group 4 fax encoding via PDFBox when
19:  * the native binary is unavailable.
20:  *
21:  * jbig2enc flags:
22:  *   -p  — Produce a global symbol dictionary (shared across pages).
23:  *   -s  — Refine smearing to improve symbol matching.
24:  */
25: public class JBIG2Compressor {
26: 
27:     private final String nativeDir;
28: 
29:     public JBIG2Compressor() {
30:         this("native");
31:     }
32: 
33:     public JBIG2Compressor(String nativeDir) {
34:         this.nativeDir = nativeDir;
35:     }
36: 
37:     /**
38:      * Holds compressed foreground-mask data.
39:      *
40:      * isJbig2  true = JBIG2 format (from jbig2enc); false = CCITT G4 fallback.
41:      * data     The compressed byte stream.
42:      * width    Original mask width  in pixels.
43:      * height   Original mask height in pixels.
44:      */
45:     public static class CompressionResult {
46:         private final boolean isJbig2;
47:         private final byte[] data;
48:         private final int width;
49:         private final int height;
50: 
51:         public CompressionResult(byte[] data, int width, int height, boolean isJbig2) {
52:             this.data = data;
53:             this.width = width;
54:             this.height = height;
55:             this.isJbig2 = isJbig2;
56:         }
57: 
58:         public boolean isJbig2() { return isJbig2; }
59:         public byte[] getData() { return data; }
60:         public int getWidth() { return width; }
61:         public int getHeight() { return height; }
62:     }
63: 
64:     /**
65:      * Attempts JBIG2 compression via jbig2enc; falls back to CCITT G4 on failure.
66:      *
67:      * @param foregroundMask  Binary image (TYPE_BYTE_BINARY, black=foreground).
68:      * @return CompressionResult with either JBIG2 or CCITT G4 data.
69:      */
70:     public CompressionResult compress(BufferedImage foregroundMask) throws IOException {
71:         File jbig2enc = findJbig2enc();
72:         if (jbig2enc != null) {
73:             try {
74:                 return compressWithJbig2enc(jbig2enc, foregroundMask);
75:             } catch (IOException e) {
76:                 // jbig2enc failed → fall through to fallback
77:             }
78:         }
79:         return compressWithFallback(foregroundMask);
80:     }
81: 
82:     /**
83:      * Locates the jbig2enc binary for the current OS.
84:      *
85:      * Searches native/{os}/jbig2enc (or jbig2enc.exe on Windows).
86:      * The base path can be overridden via system property "tfocr.native.dir"
87:      * (defaults to "native").
88:      *
89:      * @return The executable File, or null if not found.
90:      */
91:     private File findJbig2enc() {
92:         Os os = PlatformUtils.detectOs();
93:         String dirName;
94:         switch (os) {
95:             case WINDOWS: dirName = "win"; break;
96:             case MAC: dirName = "mac"; break;
97:             default: dirName = "linux";
98:         }
99:         File exe = new File(nativeDir + "/" + dirName, os == Os.WINDOWS ? "jbig2enc.exe" : "jbig2enc");
100:         if (exe.canExecute()) return exe;
101:         return null;
102:     }
103: 
104:     /**
105:      * Runs jbig2enc and concatenates global sym + page jbig2 into one byte array.
106:      *
107:      * PDF JBIG2Decode streams require global segments (symbol dictionary)
108:      * followed by page data — exactly the .sym + .jbig2 layout that
109:      * jbig2enc produces.
110:      */
111:     private CompressionResult compressWithJbig2enc(File jbig2enc, BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
112:         File tempPng = File.createTempFile("tfocr-jbig2-", ".png");
113:         tempPng.deleteOnExit();
114:         javax.imageio.ImageIO.write(mask, "png", tempPng);
115: 
116:         try {
117:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
118: 
119:             String flags = Settings.getInstance().getString("jbig2enc.flags", "-p -s");
120:             java.util.List<String> args = new java.util.ArrayList<>();
121:             for (String f : flags.split("\\s+")) {
122:                 if (!f.isEmpty()) args.add(f);
123:             }
124:             args.add(tempPng.getAbsolutePath());
125: 
126:             SubprocessRunner.Result result = SubprocessRunner.run(jbig2enc, args, -1);
127:             if (result.getExitCode() != 0) {
128:                 throw new IOException("jbig2enc failed: " + result.getStderrString());
129:             }
130: 
131:             File symFile = new File(prefix + ".sym");
132:             File jbig2File = new File(prefix + ".jbig2");
133: 
134:             byte[] symData = symFile.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(symFile.toPath()) : new byte[0];
135:             byte[] jbig2Data = jbig2File.exists() ? java.nio.file.Files.readAllBytes(jbig2File.toPath()) : new byte[0];
136: 
137:             byte[] combined = new byte[symData.length + jbig2Data.length];
138:             System.arraycopy(symData, 0, combined, 0, symData.length);
139:             System.arraycopy(jbig2Data, 0, combined, symData.length, jbig2Data.length);
140: 
141:             return new CompressionResult(combined, mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), true);
142:         } finally {
143:             String prefix = tempPng.getAbsolutePath().replaceAll("\\.png$", "");
144:             for (String ext : new String[]{".png", ".sym", ".jbig2"}) {
145:                 File f = new File(prefix + ext);
146:                 if (f.exists()) f.delete();
147:             }
148:         }
149:     }
150: 
151:     /**
152:      * Fallback: encodes the mask as CCITT Group 4 fax using PDFBox's CCITTFactory.
153:      */
154:     private CompressionResult compressWithFallback(BufferedImage mask) throws IOException {
155:         // Create a throwaway PDDocument to use CCITTFactory
156:         try (PDDocument doc = new PDDocument()) {
157:             PDImageXObject ccitt = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(doc, mask);
158:             return new CompressionResult(extractStreamBytes(ccitt), mask.getWidth(), mask.getHeight(), false);
159:         }
160:     }
161: 
162:     private static byte[] extractStreamBytes(PDImageXObject image) throws IOException {
163:         try (java.io.InputStream is = image.createInputStream()) {
164:             return is.readAllBytes();
165:         }
166:     }
167: }

stream.setInt(COSName.BITS_PER_COMPONENT, 1);

stream.setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);

Let me write the code:

```java
private PDImageXObject createJbig2ImageXObject(PDDocument doc, JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result) throws IOException {
    COSStream stream = doc.getDocument().createCOSStream();
    try (OutputStream os = stream.createOutputStream(COSName.JBIG2_DECODE)) {
        os.write(result.getData());
    }
    stream.setInt(COSName.WIDTH, result.getWidth());
    stream.setInt(COSName.HEIGHT, result.getHeight());
    stream.setInt(COSName.BITS_PER_COMPONENT, 1);
    stream.setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
    return new PDImageXObject(stream, doc.getResourceCache());
}
```

Wait, `doc.getDocument()` returns `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSDocument`. Let me check if COSDocument has `createCOSStream()`... Yes, it does in PDFBox 3.x.

    cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
    cs.drawImage(fgCCITT, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
}
```

The `CCITTFactory.createFromImage()` creates an image with CCITT compression. The mask is applied by setting ImageMask=true.

Actually, I just want to double check: does `COSStream` have the right constructor/methods in PDFBox 3.0.6?

In PDFBox 3.0.x:
- `COSStream` is in `org.apache.pdfbox.cos`
- It has `createOutputStream(COSBase filters)` which creates a filtered stream that compresses when closed
- It has `setInt(COSName key, int value)`, `setBoolean(COSName key, boolean value)`, `setItem(COSName key, COSBase value)`
- `COSName.JBIG2_DECODE` should exist

And `PDImageXObject`:
- Constructor: `PDImageXObject(COSStream cosStream, PDResourceCache cache)`
- `doc.getResourceCache()` returns the resource cache

Let me implement it. I'll also need to handle the case where `doc.getDocument()` returns the COSDocument to create the COSStream.

Let me also consider: do I need to set `BITS_PER_COMPONENT` for the JBIG2 image? For an ImageMask (binary image), BPC should be 1. And ImageMask=true should be sufficient.

Actually, for ImageMask in PDF, you only need `/ImageMask true` along with `/Width` and `/Height`. `/BitsPerComponent` is implicitly 1 for ImageMasks. But it doesn't hurt to set it.

```

Modify the foreground mask block (lines 134-141):
```java
// Layer 2: foreground mask as CCITT G4 stencil overlay
if (foregroundMasks != null && i < foregroundMasks.size()) {
    BufferedImage fgMask = foregroundMasks.get(i);
    PDImageXObject fgImage;
    if (compressor != null) {
        JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result = compressor.compress(fgMask);
        if (result.isJbig2()) {
            fgImage = createJbig2ImageXObject(output, result);
        } else {
            fgImage = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, fgMask);
        }
    } else {
        fgImage = CCITTFactory.createFromImage(output, fgMask);
    }
    fgImage.getCOSObject().setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
    fgImage.getCOSObject().removeItem(COSName.COLORSPACE);
    cs.setNonStrokingColor(0f, 0f, 0f);
    cs.drawImage(fgImage, 0, 0, pageW, pageH);
}
```

Add helper method:
```java
private PDImageXObject createJbig2ImageXObject(PDDocument doc, JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result) throws IOException {
    COSStream stream = doc.getDocument().createCOSStream();
    try (OutputStream os = stream.createOutputStream(COSName.JBIG2_DECODE)) {
        os.write(result.getData());
    }
    stream.setInt(COSName.WIDTH, result.getWidth());
    stream.setInt(COSName.HEIGHT, result.getHeight());
    stream.setInt(COSName.BITS_PER_COMPONENT, 1);
    stream.setBoolean(COSName.IMAGE_MASK, true);
    return new PDImageXObject(stream, doc.getResourceCache());
}
```

And add imports for COSStream and OutputStream.

Then in `TrulyFreeOCR.java`, set the compressor:
```java
assembler.setCompressor(compressor);
```

}

OK let me try the simplest thing that could work:

```java
private static PDImageXObject createJbig2ImageXObject(PDDocument doc, JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result) throws IOException {
    PDImageXObject img = new PDImageXObject(doc);
    img.setWidth(result.getWidth());
    img.setHeight(result.getHeight());
    img.setBitsPerComponent(1);
    img.setStencil(true);
    // Set the filter to JBIG2Decode
    img.getCOSObject().setItem(COSName.FILTER, COSName.JBIG2_DECODE);
    // Write compressed data to the underlying stream
    COSStream cosStream = (COSStream) img.getCOSObject();
    try (OutputStream os = cosStream.createOutputStream(COSName.JBIG2_DECODE)) {
        os.write(result.getData());
    }
    return img;
}
```

Looking at PDXObject, `getCOSObject()` typically returns `stream.getCOSObject()` which is the COSStream itself (since PDStream wraps a COSStream and its getCOSObject returns the underlying COSStream).

Actually, `PDStream` has `getCOSObject()` that returns `COSStream`. So `img.getCOSObject()` should return the COSStream.

```java
private static PDImageXObject createJbig2ImageXObject(PDDocument doc, JBIG2Compressor.CompressionResult result) throws IOException {
    // Write JBIG2 data through PDStream with JBIG2Decode filter
    // Since PDFBox has no JBIG2 encoder, data passes through as-is
    PDStream pdStream = new PDStream(doc);
    try (OutputStream os = pdStream.createOutputStream(COSName.JBIG2_DECODE)) {
        os.write(result.getData());
    }
    PDImageXObject img = new PDImageXObject(pdStream, null);
    img.setWidth(result.getWidth());
    img.setHeight(result.getHeight());
    img.setBitsPerComponent(1);
    img.setStencil(true);
    return img;
}
```

Both compilation and test compilation pass. Bug 1 is fixed.

**What changed:**
- `PDFAssembler` now accepts a `JBIG2Compressor` via `setCompressor()`.
- In `assemble()`, when a compressor is available, each foreground mask is compressed via `compressor.compress()`. If the result is JBIG2, the data is injected as a PDImageXObject with `/Filter /JBIG2Decode` and `/ImageMask true`. If the fallback (CCITT G4) is used, `CCITTFactory.createFromImage()` is used as before.
- `TrulyFreeOCR.call()` calls `assembler.setCompressor(compressor)` before `runPipeline()`, wiring the previously dead-code JBIG2Compressor into the pipeline.

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

Now for Bug 3: Thread flag cap logic.

From the review: "Concurrency CLI flaw — `--threads` sets `ocrMax = segThreads` ignoring `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` setting; CLI help promises that setting acts as upper bound."

Looking at the current code in `TrulyFreeOCR.java`:

```java
int segThreads = threads != null ? threads : Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors();
int ocrMax = threads != null ? segThreads : Math.min(segThreads, settings.getInt("pipeline.ocr.maxThreads", 1));
```

The issue:
- When `--threads N` is passed: `segThreads = N`, `ocrMax = segThreads = N` — ignores `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` setting.
- When `--threads` is not passed: `segThreads = #CPUs`, `ocrMax = Math.min(segThreads, maxThreadsSetting)` — correct.

The CLI help says: `--threads: Threads for segmentation (default: # CPUs). OCR limited to pipeline.ocr.maxThreads (default 1).`

So when `--threads` is passed, `ocrMax` should be `Math.min(segThreads, settings.getInt("pipeline.ocr.maxThreads", 1))` — same as the non-`--threads` case.

The fix: remove the conditional and always apply the cap.

```java
int segThreads = threads != null ? threads : Runtime.getRuntime().availableProcessors();
int ocrMax = Math.min(segThreads, settings.getInt("pipeline.ocr.maxThreads", 1));
```

This way, regardless of whether `--threads` is passed or not, OCR threads are always capped by `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads`. Switching on `--threads` only affects segmentation threads.

</details>

Now for Bug 3. The issue is in `TrulyFreeOCR.java` lines 105-106:

```java
int ocrMax = threads != null ? segThreads : Math.min(segThreads, settings.getInt("pipeline.ocr.maxThreads", 1));
```

When `--threads` is passed, `ocrMax` is set to `segThreads` directly, bypassing the `pipeline.ocr.maxThreads` cap. The fix: always apply the cap regardless of whether `--threads` was provided.

126:         int i = skipWhitespace(input, 0);
127:         if (i >= input.length() || input.charAt(i) != '{') return result;
128:         i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1);
129: 
130:         while (i < input.length()) {

143:             i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 1);

152:                 if (valEnd < 0) break;

154:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, valEnd + 1);

157:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 4);
158:             } else if (c == 'f' && input.startsWith("false", i)) {
159:                 value = "false";
160:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, i + 5);
161:             } else if (c == 'n' && input.startsWith("null", i)) {
162:                 value = "null";

168:                         || input.charAt(valEnd) == '.' || input.charAt(valEnd) == '-'
169:                         || input.charAt(valEnd) == '+' || input.charAt(valEnd) == 'e'
170:                         || input.charAt(valEnd) == 'E')) {
171:                     valEnd++;
172:                 }
173:                 if (valEnd == i) break;
174:                 value = input.substring(i, valEnd);
175:                 i = skipWhitespace(input, valEnd);
176:             }
177:             result.put(key, value);
178:         }
179:         return result;
180:     }

182:     private static int skipWhitespace(String s, int pos) {
183:         while (pos < s.length() && Character.isWhitespace(s.charAt(pos))) pos++;

196:                 i++;
197:             }

<details>
<summary>Thinking</summary>

The bug is clear. `stripComments()` at lines 101-122 searches for `//` and `/*` patterns without checking if it's inside a string literal. So a value like `"path": "//server/tessdata"` would be corrupted: the `//` after `"` starts the scan would be misinterpreted as a line comment.

The fix: I need to track whether the current position is inside a double-quoted string. When inside a string, characters should be appended literally without comment processing. I need to handle escape sequences too (though `findStringEnd` already does that for `parseJson`, for `stripComments` I only need to skip over strings).

The approach: when I encounter a `"` character (and it's not escaped), enter a "string mode" where characters are appended literally until the closing `"` (accounting for backslash escapes).

Let me rewrite `stripComments`:

```java
private static String stripComments(String input) {
    StringBuilder out = new StringBuilder(input.length());
    int i = 0;
    while (i < input.length()) {
        char c = input.charAt(i);
        
        // Track whether we're inside a string literal
        if (c == '"') {
            out.append(c);
            i++;
            while (i < input.length()) {
                char sc = input.charAt(i);
                out.append(sc);
                i++;
                if (sc == '\\' && i < input.length()) {
                    out.append(input.charAt(i));
                    i++;
                } else if (sc == '"') {
                    break;
                }
            }
            continue;
        }
        
        if (i + 1 < input.length() && c == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/') {
            // line comment — skip to end of line
            i += 2;
            while (i < input.length() && input.charAt(i) != '\n') i++;
            continue;
        }
        if (i + 1 < input.length() && c == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '*') {
            // block comment — skip to */
            i += 2;
            while (i + 1 < input.length() && !(input.charAt(i) == '*' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/')) i++;
            i += 2; // skip */
            continue;
        }
        out.append(c);
        i++;
    }
    return out.toString();
}
```

This handles:
1. `"` enters string literal mode — characters are appended as-is until closing `"` (with `\\` escape support)
2. `//` outside strings → line comment
3. `/*` outside strings → block comment

This is a clean fix. Let me implement it.

</details>

The bug is in `stripComments()` — it searches for `//` and `/*` unconditionally, even inside string literals. A value like `"path": "//server/tessdata"` would have everything after `//` stripped. The fix: skip over string literals before checking for comment markers.

  "newString": "    private static String stripComments(String input) {\n        StringBuilder out = new StringBuilder(input.length());\n        int i = 0;\n        while (i < input.length()) {\n            char c = input.charAt(i);\n\n            // Skip over string literals so comment markers inside values aren't stripped\n            if (c == '\"') {\n                out.append(c);\n                i++;\n                while (i < input.length()) {\n                    char sc = input.charAt(i);\n                    out.append(sc);\n                    i++;\n                    if (sc == '\\\\' && i < input.length()) {\n                        out.append(input.charAt(i));\n                        i++;\n                    } else if (sc == '\"') {\n                        break;\n                    }\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if (i + 1 < input.length() && c == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/') {\n                // line comment — skip to end of line\n                i += 2;\n                while (i < input.length() && input.charAt(i) != '\\n') i++;\n                continue;\n            }\n            if (i + 1 < input.length() && c == '/' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '*') {\n                // block comment — skip to */\n                i += 2;\n                while (i + 1 < input.length() && !(input.charAt(i) == '*' && input.charAt(i + 1) == '/')) i++;\n                i += 2; // skip */\n                continue;\n            }\n            out.append(c);\n            i++;\n        }\n        return out.toString();\n    }"
}

All 4 bugs from `docs/project-review-by-Antigravity.md` are now fixed:

| # | Bug | Fix |
|---|-----|-----|
| 1 | JBIG2 not wired | `assembler.setCompressor(compressor)` in `TrulyFreeOCR.java`; assembler uses compressor per-page in Layer 2, injects JBIG2 data as `PDImageXObject` with `/Filter /JBIG2Decode` |
| 2 | Text matrix drift | Replaced `newLineAtOffset(x,y)` + `showText` + `newLineAtOffset(-x,-y)` with `setTextMatrix(Matrix.getTranslateInstance(x, y))` before each word — absolute positioning |
| 3 | Thread cap bypass | Changed `ocrMax = threads != null ? segThreads : Math.min(...)` to unconditional `Math.min(segThreads, maxThreadsSetting)` |
| 4 | JSONC comment stripping | Added string-literal awareness to `stripComments()` so `//` and `/*` inside values are not stripped |
